   151→  const [searchTerm, setSearchTerm] = useState('');
   152→  const [collapsedUsers, setCollapsedUsers] = useState<Set<string>>(new Set());
   153→
   154→  // Inline editing state
   155→  const [editingCell, setEditingCell] = useState<EditingCell>(null);
   156→  const [editingValue, setEditingValue] = useState<string>('');
   157→
   158→  // Form state
   159→  const [isFormOpen, setIsFormOpen] = useState(false);
   160→  const [editingId, setEditingId] = useState<string | null>(null);
   161→  const [deletingId, setDeletingId] = useState<string | null>(null);
   162→  const [submitting, setSubmitting] = useState(false);
   163→  const [form, setForm] = useState({
   164→    task_name: '',
   165→    assigned_to: '' as string,
   166→    due_date: undefined as Date | undefined,
   167→    frequency: '' as string,
   168→    task_type: '' as string,
   169→    link: '',
   170→    latest_comment: '',
   171→  });
   172→
   173→  useEffect(() => {
   174→    fetchTasks();
   175→    UserService.getAllUsers().then((users) => {
   176→      setTeamMembers(
   177→        users
   178→          .filter(u => u.role !== 'client')
   179→          .map(u => ({ id: u.id, name: u.name || u.email, email: u.email, role: u.role, profile_photo_url: u.profile_photo_url || null }))
   180→      );
   181→    });
   182→  }, []);
   183→
   184→  const fetchTasks = async () => {
   185→    setLoading(true);
   186→    const { data } = await supabase
   187→      .from('tasks')
   188→      .select('*')
   189→      .order('sort_order', { ascending: true })
   190→      .order('created_at', { ascending: false });
   191→    setTasks(data || []);
   192→    setLoading(false);
   193→  };
   194→
   195→  // Build a map of user id -> profile photo
   196→  const userPhotoMap = useMemo(() => {
   197→    const map: Record<string, string | null> = {};
   198→    teamMembers.forEach(m => { map[m.id] = m.profile_photo_url; });
   199→    return map;
   200→  }, [teamMembers]);
   201→
   202→  // Tab counts
   203→  const oneTimeCount = useMemo(() =>
   204→    tasks.filter(t => t.frequency === 'one-time' && t.task_type !== 'Client SOP').length,
   205→    [tasks]
   206→  );
   207→  const recurringCount = useMemo(() =>
   208→    tasks.filter(t => t.frequency !== 'one-time' && t.task_type !== 'Client SOP').length,
   209→    [tasks]
   210→  );
   211→  const clientSopCount = useMemo(() =>
   212→    tasks.filter(t => t.task_type === 'Client SOP').length,
   213→    [tasks]
   214→  );
   215→
   216→  // Filter tasks by tab + search
   217→  const filtered = useMemo(() => {
   218→    let tabFiltered: Task[];
   219→    if (activeTab === 'one-time') {
   220→      tabFiltered = tasks.filter(t => t.frequency === 'one-time' && t.task_type !== 'Client SOP');
   221→    } else if (activeTab === 'recurring') {
   222→      tabFiltered = tasks.filter(t => t.frequency !== 'one-time' && t.task_type !== 'Client SOP');
   223→    } else {
   224→      tabFiltered = tasks.filter(t => t.task_type === 'Client SOP');
   225→    }
   226→
   227→    if (!searchTerm) return tabFiltered.sort((a, b) => a.sort_order - b.sort_order);
   228→
   229→    const term = searchTerm.toLowerCase();
   230→    return tabFiltered.filter(t =>
   231→      t.task_name.toLowerCase().includes(term) ||
   232→      (t.assigned_to_name && t.assigned_to_name.toLowerCase().includes(term)) ||
   233→      (t.latest_comment && t.latest_comment.toLowerCase().includes(term)) ||
   234→      t.task_type.toLowerCase().includes(term) ||
   235→      (t.created_by_name && t.created_by_name.toLowerCase().includes(term))
   236→    ).sort((a, b) => a.sort_order - b.sort_order);
   237→  }, [tasks, activeTab, searchTerm]);
   238→
   239→  // Group filtered tasks by assigned_to
   240→  const groupedByUser = useMemo(() => {
   241→    const groups: { key: string; label: string; tasks: Task[] }[] = [];
   242→    const map = new Map<string, Task[]>();
   243→
   244→    for (const task of filtered) {
   245→      const key = task.assigned_to || '_unassigned';
   246→      if (!map.has(key)) map.set(key, []);
   247→      map.get(key)!.push(task);
   248→    }
   249→
   250→    const sortedKeys = Array.from(map.keys()).sort((a, b) => {
   251→      if (a === '_unassigned') return 1;
   252→      if (b === '_unassigned') return -1;
   253→      const nameA = map.get(a)![0].assigned_to_name || '';
   254→      const nameB = map.get(b)![0].assigned_to_name || '';
   255→      return nameA.localeCompare(nameB);
   256→    });
   257→
   258→    for (const key of sortedKeys) {
   259→      const tasks = map.get(key)!;
   260→      const label = key === '_unassigned' ? 'Unassigned' : (tasks[0].assigned_to_name || 'Unknown');
   261→      groups.push({ key, label, tasks });
   262→    }
   263→
   264→    return groups;
   265→  }, [filtered]);
   266→
   267→  const toggleUserCollapse = (key: string) => {
   268→    setCollapsedUsers(prev => {
   269→      const newSet = new Set(prev);
   270→      if (newSet.has(key)) newSet.delete(key);
   271→      else newSet.add(key);
   272→      return newSet;
   273→    });
   274→  };
   275→
   276→  // --- Inline editing ---
   277→  const startEditing = (taskId: string, field: string, currentValue: string) => {
   278→    setEditingCell({ taskId, field });
   279→    setEditingValue(currentValue);
   280→  };
   281→
   282→  const cancelEditing = () => {
   283→    setEditingCell(null);
   284→    setEditingValue('');
   285→  };
   286→
   287→  const saveInlineEdit = async () => {
   288→    if (!editingCell) return;
   289→    const { taskId, field } = editingCell;
   290→    const value = editingValue.trim() || null;
   291→    setEditingCell(null);
   292→    setEditingValue('');
   293→    setTasks(prev => prev.map(t => t.id === taskId ? { ...t, [field]: value, updated_at: new Date().toISOString() } : t));
   294→    try {
   295→      await supabase.from('tasks').update({
   296→        [field]: value,
   297→        updated_at: new Date().toISOString(),
   298→      }).eq('id', taskId);
   299→    } catch (error) {
   300→      console.error('Error saving:', error);
   301→      await fetchTasks();
   302→    }
   303→  };
   304→
   305→  const saveSelectField = async (taskId: string, field: string, value: string, extra?: Record<string, string | null>) => {
   306→    const updates: Record<string, string | null> = { [field]: value || null, ...extra };
   307→    setTasks(prev => prev.map(t => t.id === taskId ? { ...t, ...updates, updated_at: new Date().toISOString() } : t));
   308→    try {
   309→      await supabase.from('tasks').update({
   310→        ...updates,
   311→        updated_at: new Date().toISOString(),
   312→      }).eq('id', taskId);
   313→    } catch (error) {
   314→      console.error('Error saving:', error);
   315→      await fetchTasks();
   316→    }
   317→  };
   318→
   319→  const saveDateField = async (taskId: string, date: Date | undefined) => {
   320→    if (!date) return;
   321→    const dateStr = toLocalDateString(date);
   322→    setEditingCell(null);
   323→    setEditingValue('');
   324→    setTasks(prev => prev.map(t => t.id === taskId ? { ...t, due_date: dateStr, updated_at: new Date().toISOString() } : t));
   325→    try {
   326→      await supabase.from('tasks').update({
   327→        due_date: dateStr,
   328→        updated_at: new Date().toISOString(),
   329→      }).eq('id', taskId);
   330→    } catch (error) {
   331→      console.error('Error saving:', error);
   332→      await fetchTasks();
   333→    }
   334→  };
   335→
   336→  const openForm = (task?: Task) => {
   337→    if (task) {
   338→      setEditingId(task.id);
   339→      setForm({
   340→        task_name: task.task_name,
   341→        assigned_to: task.assigned_to || '',
   342→        due_date: task.due_date ? new Date(task.due_date + 'T00:00:00') : undefined,
   343→        frequency: task.frequency,
   344→        task_type: task.task_type,
   345→        link: task.link || '',
   346→        latest_comment: task.latest_comment || '',
   347→      });
   348→    } else {
   349→      setEditingId(null);
   350→      setForm({
   351→        task_name: '',
   352→        assigned_to: '',
   353→        due_date: undefined,
   354→        frequency: '',
   355→        task_type: '',
   356→        link: '',
   357→        latest_comment: '',
   358→      });
   359→    }
   360→    setIsFormOpen(true);
   361→  };
   362→
   363→  const handleSubmit = async () => {
   364→    if (!form.task_name.trim() || !form.frequency || !form.task_type) return;
   365→    if (!user?.id || !userProfile) return;
   366→
   367→    setSubmitting(true);
   368→    try {
   369→      const assignedMember = teamMembers.find(m => m.id === form.assigned_to);
   370→      const payload = {
   371→        task_name: form.task_name.trim(),
   372→        assigned_to: form.assigned_to || null,
   373→        assigned_to_name: assignedMember?.name || null,
   374→        due_date: form.due_date ? toLocalDateString(form.due_date) : null,
   375→        frequency: form.frequency,
   376→        task_type: form.task_type,
   377→        link: form.link.trim() || null,
   378→        latest_comment: form.latest_comment.trim() || null,
   379→        updated_at: new Date().toISOString(),
   380→      };
   381→
   382→      if (editingId) {
   383→        const { error } = await supabase
   384→          .from('tasks')
   385→          .update(payload)
   386→          .eq('id', editingId);
   387→        if (error) throw error;
   388→        toast({ title: 'Updated', description: 'Task updated successfully.' });
   389→      } else {
   390→        const { error } = await supabase
   391→          .from('tasks')
   392→          .insert({
   393→            ...payload,
   394→            created_by: user.id,
   395→            created_by_name: userProfile.name || userProfile.email || 'Unknown',
   396→          });
   397→        if (error) throw error;
   398→        toast({ title: 'Created', description: 'Task created successfully.' });
   399→      }
   400→
   401→      setIsFormOpen(false);
   402→      setEditingId(null);
   403→      setForm({ task_name: '', assigned_to: '', due_date: undefined, frequency: '', task_type: '', link: '', latest_comment: '' });
   404→      await fetchTasks();
   405→    } catch (err) {
   406→      console.error('Error saving task:', err);
   407→      toast({ title: 'Error', description: 'Failed to save task.', variant: 'destructive' });
   408→    } finally {
   409→      setSubmitting(false);
   410→    }
   411→  };
   412→
   413→  const handleDelete = async (id: string) => {
   414→    setDeletingId(null);
   415→    setTasks(prev => prev.filter(t => t.id !== id));
   416→    try {
   417→      await supabase.from('tasks').delete().eq('id', id);
   418→    } catch (error) {
   419→      console.error('Error deleting:', error);
   420→      await fetchTasks();
   421→    }
   422→  };
   423→
   424→  const handleReorder = async (taskId: string, direction: 'up' | 'down', groupTasks: Task[]) => {
   425→    const sorted = [...groupTasks].sort((a, b) => a.sort_order - b.sort_order);
   426→    const idx = sorted.findIndex(t => t.id === taskId);
   427→    const swapIdx = direction === 'up' ? idx - 1 : idx + 1;
   428→    if (swapIdx < 0 || swapIdx >= sorted.length) return;
   429→
   430→    const current = sorted[idx];
   431→    const swap = sorted[swapIdx];
   432→
   433→    const currentOrder = current.sort_order;
   434→    const swapOrder = swap.sort_order;
   435→    const newCurrentOrder = currentOrder === swapOrder
   436→      ? (direction === 'up' ? swapOrder - 1 : swapOrder + 1)
   437→      : swapOrder;
   438→    const newSwapOrder = currentOrder === swapOrder
   439→      ? currentOrder
   440→      : currentOrder;
   441→
   442→    setTasks(prev => prev.map(t => {
   443→      if (t.id === current.id) return { ...t, sort_order: newCurrentOrder };
   444→      if (t.id === swap.id) return { ...t, sort_order: newSwapOrder };
   445→      return t;
   446→    }));
   447→
   448→    try {
   449→      await Promise.all([
   450→        supabase.from('tasks').update({ sort_order: newCurrentOrder }).eq('id', current.id),
   451→        supabase.from('tasks').update({ sort_order: newSwapOrder }).eq('id', swap.id),
   452→      ]);
   453→    } catch (error) {
   454→      console.error('Error reordering:', error);
   455→      await fetchTasks();
   456→    }
   457→  };
   458→
   459→  const formatDate = (dateStr: string) => {
   460→    return new Date(dateStr + 'T00:00:00').toLocaleDateString('en-US', { month: 'short', day: 'numeric', year: 'numeric' });
   461→  };
   462→
   463→  const userColors = [
   464→    { bg: 'bg-blue-50', text: 'text-blue-700', border: 'border-blue-200' },
   465→    { bg: 'bg-emerald-50', text: 'text-emerald-700', border: 'border-emerald-200' },
   466→    { bg: 'bg-purple-50', text: 'text-purple-700', border: 'border-purple-200' },
   467→    { bg: 'bg-amber-50', text: 'text-amber-700', border: 'border-amber-200' },
   468→    { bg: 'bg-cyan-50', text: 'text-cyan-700', border: 'border-cyan-200' },
   469→    { bg: 'bg-pink-50', text: 'text-pink-700', border: 'border-pink-200' },
   470→    { bg: 'bg-indigo-50', text: 'text-indigo-700', border: 'border-indigo-200' },
   471→    { bg: 'bg-orange-50', text: 'text-orange-700', border: 'border-orange-200' },
   472→    { bg: 'bg-teal-50', text: 'text-teal-700', border: 'border-teal-200' },
   473→    { bg: 'bg-slate-50', text: 'text-slate-600', border: 'border-slate-200' },
   474→  ];
   475→
   476→  const getColorForIndex = (idx: number) => userColors[idx % userColors.length];
   477→
   478→  const getUserInitials = (name: string) => {
   479→    return name.split(' ').map(w => w[0]).join('').slice(0, 2).toUpperCase();
   480→  };
   481→
   482→  const tableHeader = (
   483→    <thead>
   484→      <tr className="border-b border-gray-200 bg-gray-50/80">
   485→        <th className={`text-left py-3 px-3 font-semibold text-gray-600 text-xs uppercase tracking-wider ${COL.reorder}`}></th>
   486→        <th className={`text-left py-3 px-3 font-semibold text-gray-600 text-xs uppercase tracking-wider ${COL.taskName}`}>Task Name</th>
   487→        <th className={`text-left py-3 px-3 font-semibold text-gray-600 text-xs uppercase tracking-wider ${COL.dueDate}`}>Due Date</th>
   488→        <th className={`text-left py-3 px-3 font-semibold text-gray-600 text-xs uppercase tracking-wider ${COL.comment}`}>Comment</th>
   489→        <th className={`text-left py-3 px-3 font-semibold text-gray-600 text-xs uppercase tracking-wider ${COL.frequency}`}>Frequency</th>
   490→        <th className={`text-left py-3 px-3 font-semibold text-gray-600 text-xs uppercase tracking-wider ${COL.type}`}>Type</th>
   491→        <th className={`text-left py-3 px-3 font-semibold text-gray-600 text-xs uppercase tracking-wider ${COL.link}`}>Link</th>
   492→        <th className={`text-left py-3 px-3 font-semibold text-gray-600 text-xs uppercase tracking-wider ${COL.createdBy}`}>Created By</th>
   493→        <th className={`text-left py-3 px-3 font-semibold text-gray-600 text-xs uppercase tracking-wider ${COL.created}`}>Created</th>
   494→        <th className={`text-right py-3 px-3 ${COL.actions}`}></th>
   495→      </tr>
   496→    </thead>
   497→  );
   498→
   499→  const renderTaskRow = (task: Task, idx: number, sorted: Task[], groupTasks: Task[]) => {
   500→    const isEditingField = (field: string) => editingCell?.taskId === task.id && editingCell?.field === field;
   501→
   502→    return (
   503→      <tr key={task.id} className="border-b border-gray-100 hover:bg-gray-50/50 transition-colors group">
   504→        {/* Reorder arrows */}
   505→        <td className={`py-3 px-3 ${COL.reorder}`}>
   506→          <div className="flex flex-col items-center gap-0.5 opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100 transition-opacity">
   507→            <Button
   508→              variant="ghost"
   509→              size="sm"
   510→              className="w-auto h-auto px-1 py-0 hover:bg-gray-100 disabled:opacity-30"
   511→              onClick={() => handleReorder(task.id, 'up', groupTasks)}
   512→              disabled={idx === 0}
   513→            >
   514→              <ChevronUp className="h-3.5 w-3.5 text-gray-500" />
   515→            </Button>
   516→            <Button
   517→              variant="ghost"
   518→              size="sm"
   519→              className="w-auto h-auto px-1 py-0 hover:bg-gray-100 disabled:opacity-30"
   520→              onClick={() => handleReorder(task.id, 'down', groupTasks)}
   521→              disabled={idx === sorted.length - 1}
   522→            >
   523→              <ChevronDown className="h-3.5 w-3.5 text-gray-500" />
   524→            </Button>
   525→          </div>
   526→        </td>
   527→
   528→        {/* Task Name - inline editable */}
   529→        <td className={`py-3 px-3 ${COL.taskName}`}>
   530→          {isEditingField('task_name') ? (
   531→            <Input
   532→              value={editingValue}
   533→              onChange={(e) => setEditingValue(e.target.value)}
   534→              onBlur={saveInlineEdit}
   535→              onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter') saveInlineEdit(); if (e.key === 'Escape') cancelEditing(); }}
   536→              className="w-full border-none shadow-none p-0 h-auto bg-transparent focus:outline-none focus:ring-0 focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-0 text-sm font-medium text-gray-900"
   537→              style={{ outline: 'none', boxShadow: 'none' }}
   538→              autoFocus
   539→            />
   540→          ) : (
   541→            <span
   542→              className="text-gray-900 font-medium line-clamp-2 cursor-pointer"
   543→              onDoubleClick={() => startEditing(task.id, 'task_name', task.task_name)}
   544→              title="Double-click to edit"
   545→            >
   546→              {task.task_name}
   547→            </span>
   548→          )}
   549→        </td>
   550→
   551→        {/* Due Date - calendar picker inline */}
   552→        <td className={`py-3 px-3 ${COL.dueDate}`}>
   553→          <Popover>
   554→            <PopoverTrigger asChild>
   555→              <button className={`text-sm hover:text-gray-700 cursor-pointer ${getDueDateColor(task.due_date) || 'text-gray-500'}`}>
   556→                {task.due_date ? new Date(task.due_date + 'T00:00:00').toLocaleDateString('en-US', { month: 'short', day: 'numeric' }) : '—'}
   557→              </button>
   558→            </PopoverTrigger>
   559→            <PopoverContent className="w-auto p-0" align="start">
   560→              <Calendar
   561→                mode="single"
   562→                selected={task.due_date ? new Date(task.due_date + 'T00:00:00') : undefined}
   563→                onSelect={(date) => saveDateField(task.id, date)}
   564→                initialFocus
   565→                classNames={{ day_selected: 'text-white hover:text-white focus:text-white' }}
   566→                modifiersStyles={{ selected: { backgroundColor: '#3e8692' } }}
   567→              />
   568→            </PopoverContent>
   569→          </Popover>
   570→        </td>
   571→
   572→        {/* Comment - inline editable textarea */}
   573→        <td className={`py-3 px-3 ${COL.comment}`}>
   574→          {isEditingField('latest_comment') ? (
   575→            <textarea
   576→              value={editingValue}
   577→              onChange={(e) => setEditingValue(e.target.value)}
   578→              onBlur={saveInlineEdit}
   579→              onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Escape') cancelEditing(); }}
   580→              className="w-full min-w-[140px] border-none shadow-none p-0 bg-transparent focus:outline-none focus:ring-0 text-xs resize-none"
   581→              style={{ outline: 'none', boxShadow: 'none' }}
   582→              rows={2}
   583→              autoFocus
   584→            />
   585→          ) : (
   586→            <span
   587→              className="text-gray-600 line-clamp-2 whitespace-pre-wrap cursor-pointer text-xs"
   588→              onDoubleClick={() => startEditing(task.id, 'latest_comment', task.latest_comment || '')}
   589→              title="Double-click to edit"
   590→            >
   591→              {task.latest_comment || '—'}
   592→            </span>
   593→          )}
   594→        </td>
   595→
   596→        {/* Frequency - inline select */}
   597→        <td className={`py-3 px-3 ${COL.frequency}`}>
   598→          <Select value={task.frequency} onValueChange={(v) => saveSelectField(task.id, 'frequency', v)}>
   599→            <SelectTrigger
   600→              className={`border-none shadow-none bg-transparent w-auto h-auto ${frequencyBadge(task.frequency)} px-2 py-1 rounded-md text-xs font-medium inline-flex items-center focus:outline-none focus:ring-0 focus:border-none focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-0 focus-visible:border-none`}
   601→              style={{ outline: 'none', boxShadow: 'none' }}
   602→            >
   603→              <SelectValue />
   604→            </SelectTrigger>
   605→            <SelectContent>
   606→              {FREQUENCIES.map((f) => <SelectItem key={f} value={f}>{FREQUENCY_LABELS[f]}</SelectItem>)}
   607→            </SelectContent>
   608→          </Select>
   609→        </td>
   610→
   611→        {/* Type - inline select */}
   612→        <td className={`py-3 px-3 ${COL.type}`}>
   613→          <Select value={task.task_type} onValueChange={(v) => saveSelectField(task.id, 'task_type', v)}>
   614→            <SelectTrigger
   615→              className={`border-none shadow-none bg-transparent w-auto h-auto ${typeBadge(task.task_type)} px-2 py-1 rounded-md text-xs font-medium inline-flex items-center focus:outline-none focus:ring-0 focus:border-none focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-0 focus-visible:border-none whitespace-nowrap`}
   616→              style={{ outline: 'none', boxShadow: 'none' }}
   617→            >
   618→              <SelectValue />
   619→            </SelectTrigger>
   620→            <SelectContent>
   621→              {TASK_TYPES.map((t) => <SelectItem key={t} value={t}>{t}</SelectItem>)}
   622→            </SelectContent>
   623→          </Select>
   624→        </td>
   625→
   626→        {/* Link - inline editable */}
   627→        <td className={`py-3 px-3 ${COL.link}`}>
   628→          {isEditingField('link') ? (
   629→            <Input
   630→              value={editingValue}
   631→              onChange={(e) => setEditingValue(e.target.value)}
   632→              onBlur={saveInlineEdit}
   633→              onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter') saveInlineEdit(); if (e.key === 'Escape') cancelEditing(); }}
   634→              className="w-full border-none shadow-none p-0 h-auto bg-transparent focus:outline-none focus:ring-0 focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-0 text-xs"
   635→              style={{ outline: 'none', boxShadow: 'none' }}
   636→              autoFocus
   637→            />
   638→          ) : task.link ? (
   639→            <a
   640→              href={task.link}
   641→              target="_blank"
   642→              rel="noopener noreferrer"
   643→              className="text-[#3e8692] hover:underline inline-flex items-center gap-1 text-xs"
   644→              onDoubleClick={(e) => { e.preventDefault(); startEditing(task.id, 'link', task.link || ''); }}
   645→              title="Double-click to edit"
   646→            >
   647→              <ExternalLink className="h-3 w-3" />
   648→              Link
   649→            </a>
   650→          ) : (
   651→            <span
   652→              className="text-gray-400 cursor-pointer text-xs"
   653→              onDoubleClick={() => startEditing(task.id, 'link', '')}
   654→              title="Double-click to edit"
   655→            >
   656→              —
   657→            </span>
   658→          )}
   659→        </td>
   660→
   661→        {/* Created By */}
   662→        <td className={`py-3 px-3 whitespace-nowrap ${COL.createdBy}`}>
   663→          <span className="text-gray-600 text-xs">{task.created_by_name || '—'}</span>
   664→        </td>
   665→
   666→        {/* Created */}
   667→        <td className={`py-3 px-3 whitespace-nowrap ${COL.created}`}>
   668→          <span className="text-gray-500 text-xs">
   669→            {new Date(task.created_at).toLocaleDateString('en-US', { month: 'short', day: 'numeric' })}
   670→          </span>
   671→        </td>
   672→
   673→        {/* Actions */}
   674→        <td className={`py-3 px-3 text-right ${COL.actions}`}>
   675→          <div className="flex items-center justify-end gap-0.5 opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100 transition-opacity">
   676→            <Button variant="ghost" size="sm" className="h-7 w-7 p-0 hover:bg-gray-200" onClick={() => openForm(task)} title="Edit in popup">
   677→              <Expand className="h-3.5 w-3.5 text-gray-500" />
   678→            </Button>
   679→            <Button variant="ghost" size="sm" className="h-7 w-7 p-0 hover:bg-red-50" onClick={() => setDeletingId(task.id)}>
   680→              <Trash2 className="h-3.5 w-3.5 text-red-500" />
   681→            </Button>
   682→          </div>
   683→        </td>
   684→      </tr>
   685→    );
   686→  };
   687→
   688→  const renderTaskTable = (groupTasks: Task[]) => {
   689→    const sorted = [...groupTasks].sort((a, b) => a.sort_order - b.sort_order);
   690→    return (
   691→      <table className="w-full text-sm table-fixed">
   692→        {tableHeader}
   693→        <tbody>
   694→          {sorted.map((task, idx) => renderTaskRow(task, idx, sorted, groupTasks))}
   695→        </tbody>
   696→      </table>
   697→    );
   698→  };
   699→
   700→  return (
   701→    <div className="min-h-[calc(100vh-64px)] w-full bg-gray-50">
   702→      <div className="w-full">
   703→        <div className="space-y-4">
   704→          {/* Header */}
   705→          <div className="w-full bg-white border border-gray-200 shadow-sm p-6">
   706→            <div className="pb-5 border-b border-gray-100 flex flex-row items-center justify-between">
   707→              <div className="flex items-center gap-3">
   708→                <div className="bg-gray-100 p-2 rounded-lg">
   709→                  <ListTodo className="h-6 w-6 text-gray-600" />
   710→                </div>
   711→                <div>
   712→                  <h2 className="text-2xl font-bold text-gray-900">Tasks</h2>
   713→                  <p className="text-sm text-gray-500">Manage team tasks, SOPs, and recurring work</p>
   714→                </div>
   715→              </div>
   716→              <Button
   717→                className="hover:opacity-90"
   718→                style={{ backgroundColor: '#3e8692', color: 'white' }}
   719→                onClick={() => openForm()}
   720→              >
   721→                <Plus className="h-4 w-4 mr-2" />
   722→                Add Task
   723→              </Button>
   724→            </div>
   725→
   726→            {/* Tabs */}
   727→            <div className="pt-4">
   728→              {loading ? (
   729→                <div className="flex gap-2">
   730→                  {[1, 2, 3].map((i) => <Skeleton key={i} className="h-9 w-28 rounded" />)}
   731→                </div>
   732→              ) : (
   733→                <Tabs value={activeTab} onValueChange={setActiveTab}>
   734→                  <TabsList className="bg-gray-100 p-1 h-auto flex-wrap">
   735→                    <TabsTrigger
   736→                      value="one-time"
   737→                      className="data-[state=active]:bg-white data-[state=active]:text-[#3e8692] data-[state=active]:shadow-sm text-sm px-4 py-2"
   738→                    >
   739→                      One-Time
   740→                      <span className="ml-2 text-xs bg-gray-200 text-gray-600 px-1.5 py-0.5 rounded-full">{oneTimeCount}</span>
   741→                    </TabsTrigger>
   742→                    <TabsTrigger
   743→                      value="recurring"
   744→                      className="data-[state=active]:bg-white data-[state=active]:text-[#3e8692] data-[state=active]:shadow-sm text-sm px-4 py-2"
   745→                    >
   746→                      Recurring
   747→                      <span className="ml-2 text-xs bg-gray-200 text-gray-600 px-1.5 py-0.5 rounded-full">{recurringCount}</span>
   748→                    </TabsTrigger>
   749→                    <TabsTrigger
   750→                      value="client-sop"
   751→                      className="data-[state=active]:bg-white data-[state=active]:text-[#3e8692] data-[state=active]:shadow-sm text-sm px-4 py-2"
   752→                    >
   753→                      Client SOP
   754→                      <span className="ml-2 text-xs bg-gray-200 text-gray-600 px-1.5 py-0.5 rounded-full">{clientSopCount}</span>
   755→                    </TabsTrigger>
   756→                  </TabsList>
   757→                </Tabs>
   758→              )}
   759→            </div>
   760→
   761→            {/* Search */}
   762→            <div className="flex flex-wrap items-center gap-3 pt-4">
   763→              <div className="relative flex-1 max-w-sm">
   764→                <Search className="absolute left-3 top-1/2 transform -translate-y-1/2 h-4 w-4 text-gray-400" />
   765→                <Input
   766→                  placeholder="Search tasks, assignees, comments..."
   767→                  className="pl-10 auth-input"
   768→                  value={searchTerm}
   769→                  onChange={(e) => setSearchTerm(e.target.value)}
   770→                />
   771→              </div>
   772→              {searchTerm && (
   773→                <Button variant="ghost" size="sm" onClick={() => setSearchTerm('')}>
   774→                  Clear
   775→                </Button>
   776→              )}
   777→            </div>
   778→          </div>
   779→
   780→          {/* Grouped Tables */}
   781→          {loading ? (
   782→            <div className="p-6 space-y-3">
   783→              {[1, 2, 3, 4].map((i) => <Skeleton key={i} className="h-12 w-full rounded" />)}
   784→            </div>
   785→          ) : filtered.length === 0 ? (
   786→            <div className="w-full bg-white border border-gray-200 shadow-sm">
   787→              <div className="text-center py-16">
   788→                <ClipboardList className="h-12 w-12 text-gray-300 mx-auto mb-3" />
   789→                <p className="text-gray-500 font-medium">
   790→                  {tasks.length === 0 ? 'No tasks yet.' : 'No tasks match your filters.'}
   791→                </p>
   792→                {tasks.length === 0 && (
   793→                  <Button
   794→                    className="mt-4 hover:opacity-90"
   795→                    style={{ backgroundColor: '#3e8692', color: 'white' }}
   796→                    onClick={() => openForm()}
   797→                  >
   798→                    <Plus className="h-4 w-4 mr-2" /> Create Your First Task
   799→                  </Button>
   800→                )}
   801→              </div>
   802→            </div>
   803→          ) : (
   804→            <div className="space-y-4">
   805→              {groupedByUser.map((group, groupIdx) => {
   806→                const isCollapsed = collapsedUsers.has(group.key);
   807→                const colors = group.key === '_unassigned'
   808→                  ? { bg: 'bg-slate-50', text: 'text-slate-600', border: 'border-slate-200' }
   809→                  : getColorForIndex(groupIdx);
   810→                const photoUrl = group.key !== '_unassigned' ? userPhotoMap[group.key] : null;
   811→
   812→                return (
   813→                  <div key={group.key}>
   814→                    {/* Group Header */}
   815→                    <div
   816→                      className={`flex items-center justify-between px-4 py-3 ${colors.bg} ${isCollapsed ? 'rounded-lg' : 'rounded-t-lg'} border ${colors.border} ${isCollapsed ? '' : 'border-b-0'} cursor-pointer select-none transition-all`}
   817→                      onClick={() => toggleUserCollapse(group.key)}
   818→                    >
   819→                      <div className="flex items-center gap-3">
   820→                        {isCollapsed ? (
   821→                          <ChevronRight className={`w-4 h-4 ${colors.text}`} />
   822→                        ) : (
   823→                          <ChevronDown className={`w-4 h-4 ${colors.text}`} />
   824→                        )}
   825→                        {group.key === '_unassigned' ? (
   826→                          <div className="h-7 w-7 rounded-full bg-slate-200 flex items-center justify-center">
   827→                            <User className="h-3.5 w-3.5 text-slate-500" />
   828→                          </div>
   829→                        ) : photoUrl ? (
   830→                          <Image
   831→                            src={photoUrl}
   832→                            alt={group.label}
   833→                            width={28}
   834→                            height={28}
   835→                            className="rounded-full object-cover h-7 w-7"
   836→                          />
   837→                        ) : (
   838→                          <div className={`h-7 w-7 rounded-full bg-[#3e8692]/10 flex items-center justify-center text-[#3e8692] text-xs font-bold`}>
   839→                            {getUserInitials(group.label)}
   840→                          </div>
   841→                        )}
   842→                        <h3 className={`font-semibold ${colors.text}`}>{group.label}</h3>
   843→                        <Badge variant="secondary" className="text-xs font-medium">
   844→                          {group.tasks.length}
   845→                        </Badge>
   846→                      </div>
   847→                    </div>
   848→
   849→                    {/* Group Table */}
   850→                    {!isCollapsed && (
   851→                      <div className="bg-white rounded-b-lg border border-gray-200 border-t-0 overflow-hidden">
   852→                        <div className="overflow-x-auto">
   853→                          {renderTaskTable(group.tasks)}
   854→                        </div>
   855→                      </div>
   856→                    )}
   857→                  </div>
   858→                );
   859→              })}
   860→            </div>
   861→          )}
   862→        </div>
   863→      </div>
   864→
   865→      {/* Add/Edit Dialog */}
   866→      <Dialog open={isFormOpen} onOpenChange={(open) => { if (!open) { setIsFormOpen(false); setEditingId(null); } }}>
   867→        <DialogContent className="sm:max-w-[600px] max-h-[85vh] overflow-hidden">
   868→          <DialogHeader>
   869→            <DialogTitle>{editingId ? 'Edit Task' : 'Add Task'}</DialogTitle>
   870→            <DialogDescription>
   871→              {editingId ? 'Update the task details below.' : 'Create a new task for the team.'}
   872→            </DialogDescription>
   873→          </DialogHeader>
   874→          <div className="space-y-4 max-h-[60vh] overflow-y-auto px-1 pb-4">
   875→            {/* Task Name */}
   876→            <div className="grid gap-2">
   877→              <Label>Task Name <span className="text-red-500">*</span></Label>
   878→              <Input
   879→                value={form.task_name}
   880→                onChange={(e) => setForm({ ...form, task_name: e.target.value })}
   881→                placeholder="Enter task name"
   882→                className="auth-input"
   883→              />
   884→            </div>
   885→
   886→            <div className="grid grid-cols-2 gap-4">
   887→              {/* Assigned To */}
   888→              <div className="grid gap-2">
   889→                <Label>Assigned To</Label>
   890→                <Select value={form.assigned_to} onValueChange={(v) => setForm({ ...form, assigned_to: v })}>
   891→                  <SelectTrigger className="auth-input"><SelectValue placeholder="Select team member" /></SelectTrigger>
   892→                  <SelectContent>
   893→                    {teamMembers.map((m) => <SelectItem key={m.id} value={m.id}>{m.name}</SelectItem>)}
   894→                  </SelectContent>
   895→                </Select>
   896→              </div>
   897→
   898→              {/* Due Date */}
   899→              <div className="grid gap-2">
   900→                <Label>Due Date</Label>
   901→                <Popover>
   902→                  <PopoverTrigger asChild>
   903→                    <Button
   904→                      variant="outline"
   905→                      className="auth-input justify-start text-left font-normal"
   906→                      style={{ borderColor: '#e5e7eb', backgroundColor: 'white', color: form.due_date ? '#111827' : '#9ca3af' }}
   907→                    >
   908→                      <CalendarIcon className="mr-2 h-4 w-4" />

   975→            <Button
   976→              className="hover:opacity-90"
   977→              style={{ backgroundColor: '#3e8692', color: 'white' }}
   978→              onClick={handleSubmit}
   979→              disabled={!form.task_name.trim() || !form.frequency || !form.task_type || submitting}
   980→            >
   981→              {submitting ? (

   983→              ) : editingId ? 'Save Changes' : 'Add Task'}
   984→            </Button>
   985→          </DialogFooter>
   986→        </DialogContent>

   989→      {/* Delete Confirmation */}

   991→        <DialogContent className="max-w-md">
   992→          <DialogHeader>

   997→            <Button variant="outline" onClick={() => setDeletingId(null)}>Cancel</Button>
   998→            <Button variant="destructive" onClick={() => deletingId && handleDelete(deletingId)}>Delete</Button>
   999→          </DialogFooter>
  1000→        </DialogContent>
  1001→      </Dialog>
  1002→    </div>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Now I understand all three changes:

1. **Avatar fallback**: Team page uses `bg-gradient-to-br from-[#3e8692] to-[#2d6470]` for avatars without profile photos. The tasks page currently uses `bg-[#3e8692]/10` with text color `text-[#3e8692]`. I need to match the team page's gradient style with white text.

2. **Type dropdown overflow**: The Select dropdown for task_type gets cut off because the table container has `overflow-hidden`. I need to add `overflow-visible` or use a portal approach. Actually, the SelectContent from radix should already portal. The issue might be the `overflow-hidden` on the table container. Let me check if adding `overflow-visible` or using the `position="popper"` prop on SelectContent would help. Actually radix SelectContent uses a portal by default, so maybe the issue is the `whitespace-nowrap` on the trigger making the trigger itself overflow. 

3. **Type field width**: The `COL.type` is set to `w-[140px]` which should handle most task types, but longer ones like "Research & Analytics" or "Admin & Operations" exceed that width. Since the table uses `table-fixed`, the cell width is strictly enforced and the trigger text gets clipped. The user wants the text to overflow visibly instead of being truncated.

Now I need to add `overflow-visible` to the type cell so the dropdown trigger content can extend beyond its boundaries if needed.

4. **Plus button for quick task creation**: The Pipeline component has a `+` button on each group header that lets you add a new row inline. I should implement the same pattern here - a `+` button on each group header that opens an inline input row at the bottom of the table for quick task creation.

Let me start implementing these changes.
</details></think>

Looking at the screenshot, I can see three issues:

1. **Profile picture** - The profile photos show as small squares/icons rather than proper circular avatars. Looking at the screenshot, "Bolt XBT" and "El Quazo" both have tiny profile images that look broken/wrong. The Image component might not be rendering correctly at the small size, or the image might be getting clipped. I need to check how the Image tag is being used.

2. **Type field overflow** - The "Admin & Operations" type badge is overflowing into the Link column area. The `overflow-visible` I added is making it worse. I need to remove `overflow-visible` and instead make the type badge truncate/clip like the frequency badge does, not overflow.


3. **Reorder not working** - The reorder arrows are set to only appear on hover, but the user has mentioned this issue multiple times, so I need to check if the reorder functionality itself is actually working or if there's a deeper problem with how the drag-and-drop logic is implemented.

Looking at the `handleReorder` function, it swaps `sort_order` values between tasks, but I'm realizing the real issue: when tasks are created with a default `sort_order` of 0 and `handleSaveNewRow` doesn't set an initial sort order, all new tasks start at 0. Since the table sorts by `sort_order` then `created_at` descending, tasks with identical sort orders don't reorder properly when you try to swap them.

But there's probably something simpler happening too—the reorder buttons have `opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100`, so they might not be visible or clickable at all. Let me address the core issues: fix the profile picture rendering using a standard `<img>` tag like the team page does, and simplify the type field to use the same compact badge pattern as frequency instead of allowing overflow.

For the reorder functionality, I need to make the arrows always visible instead of hover-only, and trace through the swap logic more carefully. When all tasks start with `sort_order: 0`, moving one task down should set it to -1 while the other stays at 0, which should re-sort them correctly on the next render—but something in that state update or re-render cycle might be breaking.

Actually, looking at the screenshot more carefully, each group only has a single task, so reordering within a group isn't even possible. The user might be expecting to reorder tasks across different groups instead.

Re-reading their message about not being able to change the order of fields—I think they mean the task rows themselves, not columns. The reorder arrows are hidden by default with opacity set to zero, so the user probably hasn't even noticed them. I should make those arrows visible and verify the reordering logic is working correctly.

Since each group in their screenshot only has one task, the reorder arrows would be disabled anyway due to the index checks. I could either enable global reordering across groups or just make it clearer that reordering is per-group. The quickest fix is to simply show the arrows by default so users can discover the feature.

Now I'm looking at the profile picture issue—the images appear as tiny thumbnails in the screenshot. The Next.js Image component with those dimensions might need better styling, so I'll switch to a regular img tag instead.

Actually, I notice the Avatar component is already imported at the top of the file, so I should use that for consistency with how the team page handles avatars—it manages fallbacks automatically and renders more reliably.

So my plan is to fix the profile pictures by using the Avatar component, remove the overflow-visible from the type field so it truncates like the frequency column, and make the reorder arrows always visible instead of hiding them on hover.

Implementing these changes now.
</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The user is saying the reorder still doesn't work. Let me think about this more carefully.

Looking at the screenshot, each group only has 1 task:
- "Bolt XBT" has 1 task
- "El Quazo" has 1 task

With only 1 task per group, both up and down arrows are disabled (idx === 0 means up is disabled, idx === sorted.length - 1 where length is 1 means idx 0 === 0, so down is also disabled).

So the reorder arrows are both disabled for every single task because each group only has 1 task! The user can't reorder anything.

The user probably wants to reorder tasks globally (across groups), not just within a group. 

I think the real issue is that scoping reorder to individual groups only makes sense when there are multiple tasks per group. Since the user clearly expects reordering to work, I should probably change it so tasks can be reordered across the entire tab instead of being locked within their user groups. I'm realizing the solution is to apply reordering across the entire filtered task list rather than scoping it to individual groups—when a user clicks the arrows, it swaps sort_order with the adjacent task globally, and then each group displays its tasks sorted by that same sort_order value. This way even single-task groups can be reordered relative to other groups. Refactoring the table rendering to flatten the task list and use global indices instead of group-relative ones, so the reorder logic can properly handle swaps across group boundaries.
</details></think>

   426→  const handleReorder = async (taskId: string, direction: 'up' | 'down', groupTasks: Task[]) => {
   427→    const sorted = [...groupTasks].sort((a, b) => a.sort_order - b.sort_order);
   428→    const idx = sorted.findIndex(t => t.id === taskId);
   429→    const swapIdx = direction === 'up' ? idx - 1 : idx + 1;
   430→    if (swapIdx < 0 || swapIdx >= sorted.length) return;
   431→
   432→    const current = sorted[idx];
   433→    const swap = sorted[swapIdx];
   434→

   436→    const swapOrder = swap.sort_order;

   438→      ? (direction === 'up' ? swapOrder - 1 : swapOrder + 1)
   439→      : swapOrder;

   441→      ? currentOrder

   457→      await fetchTasks();
   458→    }

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-02-12 15:53:54Z)**_

Build passes. Here's what was wrong and how it's fixed:

**The reorder wasn't working because it was scoped per-group.** With only 1 task per user group (as shown in your screenshot), both up and down arrows were always disabled since there was nothing to swap within the group.

Now reordering works **globally across all tasks in the current tab**:
- The arrows use the task's position in the full tab list, not just within its group
- Clicking down on "ClickUp Migration Strategy" (Bolt XBT) will swap its sort_order with "Update KOLs Stats" (El Quazo), moving it below
- The order persists to the database via `sort_order` updates

---

Addd also field order for the table headers.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-02-12 15:54:39Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The user wants to be able to reorder the table columns (headers). They want to change the order of columns like Task Name, Due Date, Comment, Frequency, Type, Link, Created By, Created - and have that order persist.

Let me think about how to implement this. I need to:

1. Store column order - probably in localStorage so it persists across sessions
2. Make column headers draggable or have some way to reorder them
3. Apply the column order to both headers and row cells

Let me look at how to implement this cleanly. I'll use a state array that defines the column order, and render headers/cells based on that order. For reordering, I could use drag-and-drop or simple arrow buttons. Given the existing patterns in the app, I think a simpler approach would work - maybe left/right arrows on hover, or drag handles.


Since dnd-kit is already available in the project from the pipeline page, I could use that for drag-and-drop. But that might be overkill - a simpler approach would be to add a settings dialog where users can reorder columns, backed by localStorage to persist the changes.

Actually, I'll go with native HTML5 drag-and-drop on the headers since it's lightweight and doesn't require additional dependencies. I'll set up a `columnOrder` state that reads from localStorage on initialization, make the headers draggable, and update both the header and row rendering to respect the column order. When the order changes, I'll save it back to localStorage.

Defining the columns...
</details></think>

   686→          {isEditingField('link') ? (
   687→            <Input
   688→              value={editingValue}
   689→              onChange={(e) => setEditingValue(e.target.value)}
   690→              onBlur={saveInlineEdit}
   691→              onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter') saveInlineEdit(); if (e.key === 'Escape') cancelEditing(); }}
   692→              className="w-full border-none shadow-none p-0 h-auto bg-transparent focus:outline-none focus:ring-0 focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-0 text-xs"
   693→              style={{ outline: 'none', boxShadow: 'none' }}
   694→              autoFocus
   695→            />
   696→          ) : task.link ? (
   697→            <a
   698→              href={task.link}
   699→              target="_blank"
   700→              rel="noopener noreferrer"
   701→              className="text-[#3e8692] hover:underline inline-flex items-center gap-1 text-xs"
   702→              onDoubleClick={(e) => { e.preventDefault(); startEditing(task.id, 'link', task.link || ''); }}
   703→              title="Double-click to edit"
   704→            >
   705→              <ExternalLink className="h-3 w-3" />
   706→              Link
   707→            </a>
   708→          ) : (
   709→            <span
   710→              className="text-gray-400 cursor-pointer text-xs"
   711→              onDoubleClick={() => startEditing(task.id, 'link', '')}
   712→              title="Double-click to edit"
   713→            >
   714→              —
   715→            </span>
   716→          )}
   717→        </td>
   718→
   719→        {/* Created By */}
   720→        <td className={`py-3 px-3 whitespace-nowrap ${COL.createdBy}`}>
   721→          <span className="text-gray-600 text-xs">{task.created_by_name || '—'}</span>
   722→        </td>
   723→
   724→        {/* Created */}
   725→        <td className={`py-3 px-3 whitespace-nowrap ${COL.created}`}>
   726→          <span className="text-gray-500 text-xs">
   727→            {new Date(task.created_at).toLocaleDateString('en-US', { month: 'short', day: 'numeric' })}
   728→          </span>
   729→        </td>
   730→
   731→        {/* Actions */}
   732→        <td className={`py-3 px-3 text-right ${COL.actions}`}>
   733→          <div className="flex items-center justify-end gap-0.5 opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100 transition-opacity">
   734→            <Button variant="ghost" size="sm" className="h-7 w-7 p-0 hover:bg-gray-200" onClick={() => openForm(task)} title="Edit in popup">
   735→              <Expand className="h-3.5 w-3.5 text-gray-500" />
   736→            </Button>
   737→            <Button variant="ghost" size="sm" className="h-7 w-7 p-0 hover:bg-red-50" onClick={() => setDeletingId(task.id)}>
   738→              <Trash2 className="h-3.5 w-3.5 text-red-500" />
   739→            </Button>
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-02-12 15:56:58Z)**_

Build passes. Here's what was added:

**Draggable column reordering** - Each table header now has a grip handle (⠿) and can be dragged to reorder. Simply drag a column header and drop it onto another header position to swap their order. The reorder/actions columns stay fixed at first/last.

The column order is **persisted to localStorage** (`tasks-column-order`), so it remembers your preferred layout across page refreshes and sessions.

 `./app/tasks/page.tsx`

```text
   195→
   196→  const handleColumnDrop = () => {
   197→    const from = dragColRef.current;
   198→    const to = dragOverColRef.current;
   199→    dragColRef.current = null;
   200→    dragOverColRef.current = null;
   201→    if (!from || !to || from === to) return;
   202→
   203→    setColumnOrder(prev => {
   204→      const newOrder = [...prev];
   205→      const fromIdx = newOrder.indexOf(from);
   206→      const toIdx = newOrder.indexOf(to);
   207→      newOrder.splice(fromIdx, 1);
   208→      newOrder.splice(toIdx, 0, from);
   209→      localStorage.setItem(COLUMN_ORDER_KEY, JSON.stringify(newOrder));
```
</details></tool-use>

## Rich Text Editor Components Found

I've identified all rich text editor implementations in the codebase. Here's the complete summary:

### Package Dependency
- **Dependency**: `react-quill` version `^2.0.0` (in package.json, line 75)

### Files Using Rich Text Editors

#### 1. **Custom RichTextEditor Component** (Reusable Wrapper)
**File**: `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/components/ui/rich-text-editor.tsx`

**Pattern**:
```typescript
import dynamic from 'next/dynamic';
import { useMemo } from 'react';
import 'react-quill/dist/quill.snow.css';

const ReactQuill = dynamic(() => import('react-quill'), {
  ssr: false,
  loading: () => <div className="h-[200px] bg-gray-50 rounded-lg animate-pulse" />,
});
```

**Configuration**:
```typescript
const modules = {
  toolbar: [
    [{ header: [1, 2, 3, false] }],
    ['bold', 'italic', 'underline', 'strike'],
    [{ color: [] }, { background: [] }],
    [{ list: 'ordered' }, { list: 'bullet' }],
    [{ align: [] }],
    ['link'],
    ['clean'],
  ],
};

const formats = [
  'header',
  'bold',
  'italic',
  'underline',
  'strike',
  'color',
  'background',
  'list',
  'bullet',
  'align',
  'link',
];
```

**Usage**:
```typescript
<RichTextEditor
  value={value}
  onChange={onChange}
  modules={quillModules}
  formats={formats}
  placeholder={placeholder}
/>
```

**Styling**: Custom CSS styling for `.ql-container`, `.ql-toolbar`, `.ql-editor` with rounded corners, gray background, and teal focus color (#3e8692).

---

#### 2. **ReportTabContent Component**
**File**: `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/components/campaign/ReportTabContent.tsx`

**Pattern**: Uses the custom RichTextEditor wrapper component

**Import**:
```typescript
import { RichTextEditor } from '@/components/ui/rich-text-editor';
```

**Usage** (around line 331):
```typescript
<RichTextEditor
  value={customMessage}
  onChange={onCustomMessageChange}
  placeholder="Add a custom message that will be displayed in the public report..."
/>
```

---

#### 3. **Clients Page**
**File**: `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/clients/page.tsx`

**Pattern**: Direct dynamic import with SSR disabled

**Import** (lines 30-33):
```typescript
import dynamic from 'next/dynamic';

const ReactQuill = dynamic(() => import('react-quill'), { ssr: false });
import 'react-quill/dist/quill.snow.css';
```

**Configuration** (lines 1083-1092):
```typescript
const quillModules = {
  toolbar: [
    [{ 'header': [1, 2, 3, false] }],
    ['bold', 'italic', 'underline'],
    [{ 'list': 'ordered'}, { 'list': 'bullet' }],
    [{ 'indent': '-1'}, { 'indent': '+1' }],
    ['link'],
    ['clean']
  ],
};
```

**Usage** (lines 2402-2422):
- Meeting note content editor (line 2402)
- Action items editor (line 2415)

Both use `theme="snow"` with custom CSS styling for `.meeting-note-editor-wrapper`.

---

#### 4. **SOPs Page**
**File**: `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/sops/page.tsx`

**Pattern**: Direct dynamic import with SSR disabled

**Import** (lines 22-26):
```typescript
import dynamic from 'next/dynamic';

// Dynamically import ReactQuill to avoid SSR issues
const ReactQuill = dynamic(() => import('react-quill'), { ssr: false });
import 'react-quill/dist/quill.snow.css';
```

**Configuration** (lines 117-126):
```typescript
const quillModules = {
  toolbar: [
    [{ 'header': [1, 2, 3, false] }],
    ['bold', 'italic', 'underline'],
    [{ 'list': 'ordered'}, { 'list': 'bullet' }],
    [{ 'indent': '-1'}, { 'indent': '+1' }],
    ['link'],
    ['clean']
  ],
};
```

**Usage** (line 954):
```typescript
<ReactQuill
  theme="snow"
  value={formData.content}
  onChange={(value) => setFormData({ ...formData, content: value })}
  modules={quillModules}
  placeholder="Write the SOP steps here..."
  className="bg-white"
/>
```

---

### Summary Table

| File | Editor Type | Import Method | Theme | Toolbar Features | Notable Features |
|------|-------------|----------------|-------|------------------|------------------|
| `components/ui/rich-text-editor.tsx` | ReactQuill Wrapper | Dynamic (SSR disabled) | snow | Headers, bold, italic, underline, strike, color, background, lists, alignment, links, clean | Reusable component, custom styling, loading skeleton |
| `components/campaign/ReportTabContent.tsx` | RichTextEditor (wrapped) | Static import | snow | (via wrapper) | Custom message for public reports |
| `app/clients/page.tsx` | ReactQuill Direct | Dynamic (SSR disabled) | snow | Headers, bold, italic, underline, lists, indent, links, clean | Meeting notes & action items |
| `app/sops/page.tsx` | ReactQuill Direct | Dynamic (SSR disabled) | snow | Headers, bold, italic, underline, lists, indent, links, clean | SOP content editing |

All implementations use the "snow" theme (flat design) and disable SSR to avoid hydration issues.

     1→'use client';

     3→import dynamic from 'next/dynamic';

     7→const ReactQuill = dynamic(() => import('react-quill'), {
     8→  ssr: false,
     9→  loading: () => <div className="h-[200px] bg-gray-50 rounded-lg animate-pulse" />,
    10→});
    11→
    12→interface RichTextEditorProps {
    13→  value: string;
    14→  onChange: (value: string) => void;
    15→  placeholder?: string;
    16→  className?: string;
    17→}
    18→
    19→const modules = {
    20→  toolbar: [
    21→    [{ header: [1, 2, 3, false] }],
    22→    ['bold', 'italic', 'underline', 'strike'],
    23→    [{ color: [] }, { background: [] }],
    24→    [{ list: 'ordered' }, { list: 'bullet' }],
    25→    [{ align: [] }],
    26→    ['link'],
    27→    ['clean'],
    28→  ],
    29→};
    30→
    31→const formats = [
    32→  'header',
    33→  'bold',
    34→  'italic',
    35→  'underline',
    36→  'strike',
    37→  'color',
    38→  'background',
    39→  'list',
    40→  'bullet',
    41→  'align',
    42→  'link',
    43→];
    44→
    45→export function RichTextEditor({
    46→  value,
    47→  onChange,
    48→  placeholder = 'Write something...',
    49→  className = '',
    50→}: RichTextEditorProps) {
    51→  const quillModules = useMemo(() => modules, []);
    52→
    53→  return (
    54→    <div className={`rich-text-editor ${className}`}>
    55→      <ReactQuill
    56→        theme="snow"
    57→        value={value}
    58→        onChange={onChange}
    59→        modules={quillModules}
    60→        formats={formats}
    61→        placeholder={placeholder}
    62→      />
    63→      <style jsx global>{`
    64→        .rich-text-editor .ql-container {
    65→          min-height: 150px;
    66→          font-size: 14px;
    67→          border-bottom-left-radius: 0.5rem;
    68→          border-bottom-right-radius: 0.5rem;
    69→        }
    70→        .rich-text-editor .ql-toolbar {
    71→          border-top-left-radius: 0.5rem;
    72→          border-top-right-radius: 0.5rem;
    73→          background: #f9fafb;
    74→        }
    75→        .rich-text-editor .ql-editor {
    76→          min-height: 150px;
    77→        }
    78→        .rich-text-editor .ql-container.ql-snow,
    79→        .rich-text-editor .ql-toolbar.ql-snow {
    80→          border-color: #e5e7eb;
    81→        }
    82→        .rich-text-editor .ql-container:focus-within {
    83→          border-color: #3e8692;
    84→          box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px rgba(62, 134, 146, 0.2);
    85→        }
    86→        .rich-text-editor .ql-toolbar.ql-snow + .ql-container.ql-snow:focus-within {
    87→          border-color: #3e8692;
    88→        }
    89→      `}</style>

## Rich Text Editor Components Found

I've identified all rich text editor implementations in the codebase. Here's the complete summary:

### Package Dependency
- **Dependency**: `react-quill` version `^2.0.0` (in package.json, line 75)

### Files Using Rich Text Editors

#### 1. **Custom RichTextEditor Component** (Reusable Wrapper)
**File**: `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/components/ui/rich-text-editor.tsx`

**Pattern**:
```typescript
import dynamic from 'next/dynamic';
import { useMemo } from 'react';
import 'react-quill/dist/quill.snow.css';

const ReactQuill = dynamic(() => import('react-quill'), {
  ssr: false,
  loading: () => <div className="h-[200px] bg-gray-50 rounded-lg animate-pulse" />,
});
```

**Configuration**:
```typescript
const modules = {
  toolbar: [
    [{ header: [1, 2, 3, false] }],
    ['bold', 'italic', 'underline', 'strike'],
    [{ color: [] }, { background: [] }],
    [{ list: 'ordered' }, { list: 'bullet' }],
    [{ align: [] }],
    ['link'],
    ['clean'],
  ],
};

const formats = [
  'header',
  'bold',
  'italic',
  'underline',
  'strike',
  'color',
  'background',
  'list',
  'bullet',
  'align',
  'link',
];
```

**Usage**:
```typescript
<RichTextEditor
  value={value}
  onChange={onChange}
  modules={quillModules}
  formats={formats}
  placeholder={placeholder}
/>
```

**Styling**: Custom CSS styling for `.ql-container`, `.ql-toolbar`, `.ql-editor` with rounded corners, gray background, and teal focus color (#3e8692).

---

#### 2. **ReportTabContent Component**
**File**: `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/components/campaign/ReportTabContent.tsx`

**Pattern**: Uses the custom RichTextEditor wrapper component

**Import**:
```typescript
import { RichTextEditor } from '@/components/ui/rich-text-editor';
```

**Usage** (around line 331):
```typescript
<RichTextEditor
  value={customMessage}
  onChange={onCustomMessageChange}
  placeholder="Add a custom message that will be displayed in the public report..."
/>
```

---

#### 4. **SOPs Page**
**File**: `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/sops/page.tsx`

**Pattern**: Direct dynamic import with SSR disabled

**Import** (lines 22-26):
```typescript
import dynamic from 'next/dynamic';

// Dynamically import ReactQuill to avoid SSR issues
const ReactQuill = dynamic(() => import('react-quill'), { ssr: false });
import 'react-quill/dist/quill.snow.css';
```

**Configuration** (lines 117-126):
```typescript
const quillModules = {
  toolbar: [
    [{ 'header': [1, 2, 3, false] }],
    ['bold', 'italic', 'underline'],
    [{ 'list': 'ordered'}, { 'list': 'bullet' }],
    [{ 'indent': '-1'}, { 'indent': '+1' }],

  ],
};
```

**Usage** (line 954):
```typescript
<ReactQuill
  theme="snow"
  value={formData.content}
  onChange={(value) => setFormData({ ...formData, content: value })}
  modules={quillModules}
  placeholder="Write the SOP steps here..."
  className="bg-white"
/>
```

### Summary Table

| File | Editor Type | Import Method | Theme | Toolbar Features | Notable Features |
|------|-------------|----------------|-------|------------------|------------------|
| `components/ui/rich-text-editor.tsx` | ReactQuill Wrapper | Dynamic (SSR disabled) | snow | Headers, bold, italic, underline, strike, color, background, lists, alignment, links, clean | Reusable component, custom styling, loading skeleton |
| `components/campaign/ReportTabContent.tsx` | RichTextEditor (wrapped) | Static import | snow | (via wrapper) | Custom message for public reports |
| `app/clients/page.tsx` | ReactQuill Direct | Dynamic (SSR disabled) | snow | Headers, bold, italic, underline, lists, indent, links, clean | Meeting notes & action items |
| `app/sops/page.tsx` | ReactQuill Direct | Dynamic (SSR disabled) | snow | Headers, bold, italic, underline, lists, indent, links, clean | SOP content editing |

All implementations use the "snow" theme (flat design) and disable SSR to avoid hydration issues.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-02-12 16:13:49Z)**_

Build passes. Here's what was added:

**Status field:**
- New `status` column in the database with values: `to_do`, `paused`, `in_progress`, `ready_for_feedback`, `complete`
- Shows as a colored icon to the left of the task name in the table - click it to get a dropdown to change status
  - To Do = gray circle
  - In Progress = blue play icon
  - Paused = amber pause icon
  - Ready for Feedback = purple message icon
  - Complete = green check icon
- Also editable in the popup dialog as a select dropdown

**Rich text description:**
- Replaced the plain textarea with the project's `RichTextEditor` (React Quill) in the popup dialog
- Supports headers, bold, italic, underline, strikethrough, colors, lists, alignment, and links
- Tooltip on task name renders the HTML description properly on hover

---

```text
Perfect! I found the changelog-to-Telegram integration. Let me compile all the findings now with complete information.

## Summary of Telegram Bot Integration and Changelog Sending

I've found comprehensive Telegram bot integration in the KOL Campaign Manager codebase, including changelog sending to Telegram. Here are the exact file paths and implementation details:

### **Environment Configuration**
**File:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/.env.local`

```
# Telegram Bot Configuration (for form submission notifications)
# Get bot token from @BotFather on Telegram
# Get chat ID by adding bot to your group/channel and sending a message
# Thread ID is optional - use for sending to specific topics in groups
TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=7996189688:AAF73zNcxoStIGaNxzYdV_75XYd0LhQ6fZw
TELEGRAM_TERMINAL_CHAT_ID=-1002681460515
TELEGRAM_TERMINAL_THREAD_ID=2
```

---

### **1. Core Telegram Service Library**
**File:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/telegramService.ts`

This is the main service class that handles all Telegram operations:

**Key Methods:**
- `sendMessage(text, parseMode)` - Sends text messages to the configured terminal chat
- `sendFormSubmissionNotification(formName, formId, submittedBy, responseData)` - Sends form submission notifications
- `sendTestMessage()` - Sends a test message to verify bot is connected
- `sendToChat(chatId, text, parseMode)` - Sends to a specific chat ID
- `registerWebhook(webhookUrl)` - Registers webhook with Telegram API
- `deleteWebhook()` - Removes the webhook
- `getWebhookInfo()` - Gets current webhook status

**API Endpoint Used:** `https://api.telegram.org/bot{botToken}/sendMessage`

**Features:**
- HTML and Markdown formatting support
- Thread ID support for forum topics in groups
- Webhook secret token verification
- Environment variable runtime reading (avoiding serverless caching)

---

### **2. Telegram Send API Route**
**File:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/api/telegram/send/route.ts`

**Endpoint:** `POST /api/telegram/send`

**Request Body:**
```typescript
{
  message: string,
  chatId?: string,
  threadId?: number,
  useTerminalChat?: boolean
}
```

**Features:**
- Routes messages to terminal chat or operations chat
- Stores sent messages in Supabase `telegram_messages` table
- Defaults to HH Operations chat (`-1002636253963`)
- Database storage for message tracking

**Operations Chat ID:** `-1002636253963`

---

### **3. Telegram Webhook Route (Incoming Messages)**
**File:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/api/telegram/webhook/route.ts`

**Endpoint:** `POST /api/telegram/webhook`

**Key Features:**
- Receives updates from Telegram when messages are sent in group chats
- Handles commands (messages starting with `/`)
- Built-in `/test` command that sends chat/thread IDs to Andy Lee (Telegram ID: `6281931733`)
- Extracts Telegram and Twitter/X links from KOL chat messages
- Forwards social links to Content thread (`-1002636253963`, thread ID `4280`)
- Updates CRM opportunities' `last_message_at`, `last_team_message_at`, or `last_reply_at` fields
- Tracks chats in `telegram_chats` table
- Stores messages in `telegram_messages` table

**Content Thread Details:**
- Chat ID: `-1002636253963`
- Thread ID: `4280`
- Purpose: Receives forwarded KOL social links

---

### **4. Changelog to Telegram Integration**
**File:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/admin/changelog/page.tsx`

**Lines 110-123, 138-151, 202-214:**

When a changelog is published (either newly created or updated from draft), it automatically sends a notification to Telegram:

```typescript
// Send Telegram notification if switching from draft to published
if (!wasPublished && formData.is_published) {
  try {
    const notificationMessage = `<b>New Changelog Published</b>\n\n<b>Version:</b> v${formData.version}\n<b>Title:</b> ${formData.title}\n\n${formData.content.substring(0, 500)}${formData.content.length > 500 ? '...' : ''}`;

    await fetch('/api/telegram/send', {
      method: 'POST',
      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
      body: JSON.stringify({ message: notificationMessage })
    });
  } catch (telegramErr) {
    console.error('Failed to send Telegram notification:', telegramErr);
  }
}
```

**How it works:**
1. When a changelog status changes from draft to published, it sends an HTML-formatted message
2. Message includes version, title, and first 500 characters of content
3. Messages are sent via `/api/telegram/send` endpoint
4. Telegram errors are logged but don't prevent the changelog from being saved
5. Only super_admin role can create/publish changelogs

---

### **5. Telegram Delete Message Route**
**File:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/api/telegram/delete/route.ts`

**Endpoint:** `POST /api/telegram/delete`

**Request Body:**
```typescript
{
  chatId: string,
  messageId: string
}
```

**API Endpoint Used:** `https://api.telegram.org/bot{botToken}/deleteMessage`

---

### **6. Telegram Test Route**
**File:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/api/telegram/test/route.ts`

**Endpoint:** `GET /api/telegram/test`

Sends a test message: "✅ Telegram Bot Connected! Your Holo Hive form submission notifications are now active."

---

### **7. Webhook Management Route**
**File:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/api/telegram/webhook/manage/route.ts`

**Endpoints:**
- `GET /api/telegram/webhook/manage` - Get webhook status
- `POST /api/telegram/webhook/manage` - Register or delete webhook

**Request Body:**
```typescript
{
  action: 'register' | 'delete',
  webhookUrl?: string
}
```

**Webhook Features:**
- Auto-constructs webhook URL from environment
- Requires HTTPS (enforced)
- Supports optional webhook secret token validation
- Configurable allowed updates

---

### **Key Database Tables**

1. **telegram_messages** - Stores all sent and received messages
2. **telegram_chats** - Tracks chat metadata (title, type, message count, last activity)
3. **telegram_commands** - Database-driven bot commands with response and image_url
4. **crm_opportunities** - Updated with message timestamps via webhook

---

### **Telegram Message Flow Summary**

```
Changelog Published
         ↓
Admin publishes changelog in /app/admin/changelog/page.tsx
         ↓
Calls POST /api/telegram/send with formatted message
         ↓
Sends to TELEGRAM_TERMINAL_CHAT_ID (-1002681460515)
         ↓
In thread TELEGRAM_TERMINAL_THREAD_ID (2)
         ↓
Message stored in telegram_messages table
```

All sensitive information (bot token, chat IDs) is stored in `.env.local` and accessed via environment variables at runtime.
agentId: adf4692 (for resuming to continue this agent's work if needed)

duration_ms: 35831</usage>
