```text
     1→/**
     2→ * Telegram Bot Service
     3→ * Sends notifications to Telegram chat rooms
     4→ */
     5→
     6→interface TelegramMessage {
     7→  chat_id: string;
     8→  text: string;
     9→  parse_mode?: 'HTML' | 'Markdown' | 'MarkdownV2';
    10→  disable_web_page_preview?: boolean;
    11→  message_thread_id?: number;
    12→}
    13→
    14→export class TelegramService {
    15→  // Read env vars at runtime via getters to avoid serverless caching issues
    16→  private static get botToken() {
    17→    return process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN;
    18→  }
    19→
    20→  private static get chatId() {
    21→    return process.env.TELEGRAM_TERMINAL_CHAT_ID;
    22→  }
    23→
    24→  private static get threadId() {
    25→    return process.env.TELEGRAM_TERMINAL_THREAD_ID;
    26→  }
    27→
    28→  /**
    29→   * Send a message to the configured Telegram chat
    30→   */
    31→  static async sendMessage(text: string, parseMode: 'HTML' | 'Markdown' = 'HTML'): Promise<boolean> {
    32→    const botToken = this.botToken;
    33→    const chatId = this.chatId;
    34→    const threadId = this.threadId;
    35→
    36→    if (!botToken || !chatId) {
    37→      console.error('[Telegram] Configuration missing:', {
    38→        hasToken: !!botToken,
    39→        hasChatId: !!chatId,
    40→        tokenPrefix: botToken ? botToken.substring(0, 10) + '...' : 'missing',
    41→        chatId: chatId || 'missing'
    42→      });
    43→      return false;
    44→    }
    45→
    46→    try {
    47→      const message: TelegramMessage = {
    48→        chat_id: chatId,
    49→        text,
    50→        parse_mode: parseMode,
    51→        disable_web_page_preview: false,
    52→      };
    53→
    54→      // Add thread ID if configured (for sending to specific topics in groups)
    55→      if (threadId) {
    56→        message.message_thread_id = parseInt(threadId);
    57→      }
    58→
    59→      console.log('[Telegram] Sending message:', {
    60→        chatId: chatId,
    61→        threadId: threadId,
    62→        messageLength: text.length,
    63→        parseMode
    64→      });
    65→
    66→      const response = await fetch(
    67→        `https://api.telegram.org/bot${botToken}/sendMessage`,
    68→        {
    69→          method: 'POST',
    70→          headers: {
    71→            'Content-Type': 'application/json',
    72→          },
    73→          body: JSON.stringify(message),
    74→        }
    75→      );
    76→
    77→      if (!response.ok) {
    78→        const error = await response.json();
    79→        console.error('[Telegram] API error:', {
    80→          status: response.status,
    81→          error
    82→        });
    83→        return false;
    84→      }
    85→
    86→      const result = await response.json();
    87→      console.log('[Telegram] Message sent successfully:', {
    88→        messageId: result.result?.message_id,
    89→        chatId: result.result?.chat?.id
    90→      });
    91→
    92→      return true;
    93→    } catch (error) {
    94→      console.error('[Telegram] Error sending message:', error);
    95→      return false;
    96→    }
    97→  }
    98→
    99→  /**
   100→   * Format and send form submission notification
   101→   */
   102→  static async sendFormSubmissionNotification(
   103→    formName: string,
   104→    formId: string,
   105→    submittedBy?: { name?: string; email?: string },
   106→    responseData?: Record<string, any>
   107→  ): Promise<boolean> {
   108→    // Construct the base URL
   109→    const baseUrl = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL
   110→      ? (process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL.startsWith('http')
   111→          ? process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL
   112→          : `https://${process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL}`)
   113→      : (process.env.VERCEL_URL ? `https://${process.env.VERCEL_URL}` : 'http://localhost:3000');
   114→
   115→    const formUrl = `${baseUrl}/forms/${formId}`;
   116→
   117→    const message = `${formName} Form has been submitted.\n<a href="${formUrl}">View Form</a>`;
   118→
   119→    return this.sendMessage(message);
   120→  }
   121→
   122→  /**
   123→   * Send a test message to verify configuration
   124→   */
   125→  static async sendTestMessage(): Promise<boolean> {
   126→    return this.sendMessage(
   127→      '<b>✅ Telegram Bot Connected!</b>\n\nYour Holo Hive form submission notifications are now active.'
   128→    );
   129→  }
   130→
   131→  // ============================================
   132→  // WEBHOOK MANAGEMENT
   133→  // ============================================
   134→
   135→  /**
   136→   * Register the webhook URL with Telegram
   137→   * Call this once after deploying to set up message tracking
   138→   */
   139→  static async registerWebhook(webhookUrl: string): Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string }> {
   140→    if (!this.botToken) {
   141→      return { success: false, error: 'TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN not configured' };
   142→    }
   143→
   144→    try {
   145→      const params: any = {
   146→        url: webhookUrl,
   147→        allowed_updates: ['message', 'edited_message'],
   148→        drop_pending_updates: true // Don't process old messages
   149→      };
   150→
   151→      // Add secret token for webhook verification if configured
   152→      const webhookSecret = process.env.TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET;
   153→      if (webhookSecret) {
   154→        params.secret_token = webhookSecret;
   155→      }
   156→
   157→      const response = await fetch(
   158→        `https://api.telegram.org/bot${this.botToken}/setWebhook`,
   159→        {
   160→          method: 'POST',
   161→          headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
   162→          body: JSON.stringify(params)
   163→        }
   164→      );
   165→
   166→      const result = await response.json();
   167→
   168→      if (!result.ok) {
   169→        console.error('[Telegram] Webhook registration failed:', result);
   170→        return { success: false, error: result.description };
   171→      }
   172→
   173→      console.log('[Telegram] Webhook registered successfully:', webhookUrl);
   174→      return { success: true };
   175→    } catch (error) {
   176→      console.error('[Telegram] Error registering webhook:', error);
   177→      return { success: false, error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Unknown error' };
   178→    }
   179→  }
   180→
   181→  /**
   182→   * Remove the webhook (stop receiving updates)
   183→   */
   184→  static async deleteWebhook(): Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string }> {
   185→    if (!this.botToken) {
   186→      return { success: false, error: 'TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN not configured' };
   187→    }
   188→
   189→    try {
   190→      const response = await fetch(
   191→        `https://api.telegram.org/bot${this.botToken}/deleteWebhook`,
   192→        { method: 'POST' }
   193→      );
   194→
   195→      const result = await response.json();
   196→
   197→      if (!result.ok) {
   198→        return { success: false, error: result.description };
   199→      }
   200→
   201→      console.log('[Telegram] Webhook deleted successfully');
   202→      return { success: true };
   203→    } catch (error) {
   204→      console.error('[Telegram] Error deleting webhook:', error);
   205→      return { success: false, error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Unknown error' };
   206→    }
   207→  }
   208→
   209→  /**
   210→   * Get current webhook info
   211→   */
   212→  static async getWebhookInfo(): Promise<any> {
   213→    if (!this.botToken) {
   214→      return { error: 'TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN not configured' };
   215→    }
   216→
   217→    try {
   218→      const response = await fetch(
   219→        `https://api.telegram.org/bot${this.botToken}/getWebhookInfo`
   220→      );
   221→
   222→      return await response.json();
   223→    } catch (error) {
   224→      console.error('[Telegram] Error getting webhook info:', error);
   225→      return { error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Unknown error' };
   226→    }
   227→  }
   228→
   229→  /**
   230→   * Send a message to a specific chat (for CRM integration)
   231→   */
   232→  static async sendToChat(
   233→    chatId: string,
   234→    text: string,
   235→    parseMode: 'HTML' | 'Markdown' = 'HTML'
   236→  ): Promise<boolean> {
   237→    if (!this.botToken) {
   238→      console.error('[Telegram] Bot token not configured');
   239→      return false;
   240→    }
   241→
   242→    try {
   243→      const response = await fetch(
   244→        `https://api.telegram.org/bot${this.botToken}/sendMessage`,
   245→        {
   246→          method: 'POST',
   247→          headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
   248→          body: JSON.stringify({
   249→            chat_id: chatId,
   250→            text,
   251→            parse_mode: parseMode
   252→          })
   253→        }
   254→      );
   255→
   256→      if (!response.ok) {
   257→        const error = await response.json();
   258→        console.error('[Telegram] Send to chat error:', error);
   259→        return false;
   260→      }
   261→
   262→      return true;
   263→    } catch (error) {
   264→      console.error('[Telegram] Error sending to chat:', error);
   265→      return false;
   266→    }
   267→  }
   268→}
   269→
```

 `./app/api/telegram/send/route.ts`

```text
     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server';
     2→import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js';
     3→
     4→export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic';
     5→
     6→// Operations chat for internal notifications
     7→const OPERATIONS_CHAT_ID = '-1002636253963';
     8→
     9→// Initialize Supabase client
    10→const supabaseUrl = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL!;
    11→const supabaseKey = process.env.SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY || process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY!;
    12→const supabase = createClient(supabaseUrl, supabaseKey);
    13→
    14→/**
    15→ * Send a message to the HH Operations Telegram chat
    16→ * POST /api/telegram/send
    17→ */
    18→export async function POST(request: NextRequest) {
    19→  try {
    20→    const body = await request.json();
    21→    const { message, chatId, threadId, useTerminalChat } = body;
    22→
    23→    if (!message) {
    24→      return NextResponse.json(
    25→        { error: 'Message is required' },
    26→        { status: 400 }
    27→      );
    28→    }
    29→
    30→    const botToken = process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN;
    31→    if (!botToken) {
    32→      console.error('[Telegram Send] Bot token not configured');
    33→      return NextResponse.json(
    34→        { error: 'Telegram bot not configured' },
    35→        { status: 500 }
    36→      );
    37→    }
    38→
    39→    // Use terminal chat if requested, otherwise use provided chatId or default to operations chat
    40→    const terminalChatId = process.env.TELEGRAM_TERMINAL_CHAT_ID;
    41→    const targetChatId = useTerminalChat && terminalChatId ? terminalChatId : (chatId || OPERATIONS_CHAT_ID);
    42→
    43→    const messagePayload: any = {
    44→      chat_id: targetChatId,
    45→      text: message,
    46→      parse_mode: 'HTML'
    47→    };
    48→
    49→    // Add thread_id for topic-based messaging
    50→    // Use terminal thread ID if using terminal chat, otherwise use provided threadId
    51→    const terminalThreadId = process.env.TELEGRAM_TERMINAL_THREAD_ID;
    52→    const targetThreadId = useTerminalChat && terminalThreadId ? terminalThreadId : threadId;
    53→    if (targetThreadId) {
    54→      messagePayload.message_thread_id = parseInt(targetThreadId);
    55→    }
    56→
    57→    const response = await fetch(
    58→      `https://api.telegram.org/bot${botToken}/sendMessage`,
    59→      {
    60→        method: 'POST',
    61→        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
    62→        body: JSON.stringify(messagePayload)
    63→      }
    64→    );
    65→
    66→    if (!response.ok) {
    67→      const error = await response.json();
    68→      console.error('[Telegram Send] Error:', error);
    69→      return NextResponse.json(
    70→        { error: 'Failed to send message', details: error },
    71→        { status: 500 }
    72→      );
    73→    }
    74→
    75→    const result = await response.json();
    76→    console.log('[Telegram Send] Message sent to chat:', targetChatId);
    77→
    78→    // Store the sent message in the database
    79→    try {
    80→      const botInfo = result.result?.from;
    81→      await supabase.from('telegram_messages').insert({
    82→        chat_id: targetChatId,
    83→        message_id: String(result.result?.message_id),
    84→        from_user_id: botInfo?.id ? String(botInfo.id) : null,
    85→        from_user_name: botInfo?.first_name || 'Bot',
    86→        from_username: botInfo?.username || null,
    87→        text: message,
    88→        message_date: new Date().toISOString()
    89→      });
    90→      console.log('[Telegram Send] Message stored in database');
    91→    } catch (dbError) {
    92→      // Log but don't fail the request if storage fails
    93→      console.error('[Telegram Send] Failed to store message:', dbError);
    94→    }
    95→
    96→    return NextResponse.json({
    97→      success: true,
    98→      message_id: result.result?.message_id
    99→    });
   100→  } catch (error) {
   101→    console.error('[Telegram Send] Error:', error);
   102→    return NextResponse.json(
   103→      { error: 'Internal server error' },
   104→      { status: 500 }
   105→    );
   106→  }
   107→}
   108→
```

```text
     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server';
     2→import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js';
     3→
     4→export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic';
     5→
     6→// Use service role for webhook (no user auth context)
     7→const supabaseAdmin = createClient(
     8→  process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL!,
     9→  process.env.SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY!
    10→);
    11→
    12→// Telegram webhook secret for verification (optional but recommended)
    13→const WEBHOOK_SECRET = process.env.TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET;
    14→
    15→// Content thread in HH Operations for KOL social links
    16→const CONTENT_THREAD_CHAT_ID = '-1002636253963';
    17→const CONTENT_THREAD_ID = 4280;
    18→
    19→/**
    20→ * Telegram Webhook Endpoint
    21→ * POST /api/telegram/webhook
    22→ *
    23→ * Receives updates from Telegram when messages are sent in group chats.
    24→ * Updates the corresponding CRM opportunity's last_message_at field.
    25→ */
    26→export async function POST(request: NextRequest) {
    27→  try {
    28→    // Optional: Verify webhook secret token
    29→    const secretToken = request.headers.get('X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token');
    30→    if (WEBHOOK_SECRET && secretToken !== WEBHOOK_SECRET) {
    31→      console.error('[Telegram Webhook] Invalid secret token');
    32→      return NextResponse.json({ ok: false }, { status: 401 });
    33→    }
    34→
    35→    const update = await request.json();
    36→
    37→    // Log incoming update for debugging
    38→    console.log('[Telegram Webhook] Received update:', JSON.stringify(update, null, 2));
    39→
    40→    // Handle message updates
    41→    if (update.message) {
    42→      await handleMessage(update.message);
    43→    }
    44→
    45→    // Handle edited messages too
    46→    if (update.edited_message) {
    47→      // Don't update timestamp for edits, just acknowledge
    48→      console.log('[Telegram Webhook] Edited message ignored');
    49→    }
    50→
    51→    // Telegram expects a 200 OK response
    52→    return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });
    53→  } catch (error) {
    54→    console.error('[Telegram Webhook] Error processing update:', error);
    55→    // Still return 200 to prevent Telegram from retrying
    56→    return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });
    57→  }
    58→}
    59→
    60→/**
    61→ * Send a message to a Telegram chat
    62→ */
    63→async function sendTelegramMessage(chatId: string, text: string, parseMode: 'HTML' | 'Markdown' = 'HTML') {
    64→  const botToken = process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN;
    65→  if (!botToken) {
    66→    console.error('[Telegram Webhook] Bot token not configured');
    67→    return false;
    68→  }
    69→
    70→  try {
    71→    const response = await fetch(
    72→      `https://api.telegram.org/bot${botToken}/sendMessage`,
    73→      {
    74→        method: 'POST',
    75→        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
    76→        body: JSON.stringify({
    77→          chat_id: chatId,
    78→          text,
    79→          parse_mode: parseMode
    80→        })
    81→      }
    82→    );
    83→
    84→    if (!response.ok) {
    85→      const error = await response.json();
    86→      console.error('[Telegram Webhook] Send message error:', error);
    87→      return false;
    88→    }
    89→
    90→    console.log('[Telegram Webhook] Sent reply to chat:', chatId);
    91→    return true;
    92→  } catch (error) {
    93→    console.error('[Telegram Webhook] Error sending message:', error);
    94→    return false;
    95→  }
    96→}
    97→
    98→/**
    99→ * Send a photo with caption to a Telegram chat
   100→ */
   101→async function sendTelegramPhoto(chatId: string, photoUrl: string, caption: string, parseMode: 'HTML' | 'Markdown' = 'HTML') {
   102→  const botToken = process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN;
   103→  if (!botToken) {
   104→    console.error('[Telegram Webhook] Bot token not configured');
   105→    return false;
   106→  }
   107→
   108→  try {
   109→    const response = await fetch(
   110→      `https://api.telegram.org/bot${botToken}/sendPhoto`,
   111→      {
   112→        method: 'POST',
   113→        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
   114→        body: JSON.stringify({
   115→          chat_id: chatId,
   116→          photo: photoUrl,
   117→          caption,
   118→          parse_mode: parseMode
   119→        })
   120→      }
   121→    );
   122→
   123→    if (!response.ok) {
   124→      const error = await response.json();
   125→      console.error('[Telegram Webhook] Send photo error:', error);
   126→      return false;
   127→    }
   128→
   129→    console.log('[Telegram Webhook] Sent photo to chat:', chatId);
   130→    return true;
   131→  } catch (error) {
   132→    console.error('[Telegram Webhook] Error sending photo:', error);
   133→    return false;
   134→  }
   135→}
   136→
   137→/**
   138→ * Send a message to a Telegram chat with thread support
   139→ */
   140→async function sendTelegramMessageToThread(chatId: string, threadId: number, text: string, parseMode: 'HTML' | 'Markdown' | '' = '') {
   141→  const botToken = process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN;
   142→  if (!botToken) {
   143→    console.error('[Telegram Webhook] Bot token not configured');
   144→    return false;
   145→  }
   146→
   147→  try {
   148→    const payload: any = {
   149→      chat_id: chatId,
   150→      message_thread_id: threadId,
   151→      text
   152→    };
   153→    if (parseMode) {
   154→      payload.parse_mode = parseMode;
   155→    }
   156→
   157→    const response = await fetch(
   158→      `https://api.telegram.org/bot${botToken}/sendMessage`,
   159→      {
   160→        method: 'POST',
   161→        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
   162→        body: JSON.stringify(payload)
   163→      }
   164→    );
   165→
   166→    if (!response.ok) {
   167→      const error = await response.json();
   168→      console.error('[Telegram Webhook] Send message to thread error:', error);
   169→      return false;
   170→    }
   171→
   172→    console.log('[Telegram Webhook] Sent message to thread:', { chatId, threadId });
   173→    return true;
   174→  } catch (error) {
   175→    console.error('[Telegram Webhook] Error sending message to thread:', error);
   176→    return false;
   177→  }
   178→}
   179→
   180→/**
   181→ * Detect and extract Telegram/Twitter links from text
   182→ */
   183→function extractSocialLinks(text: string): { telegram: string[]; twitter: string[] } {
   184→  const telegramRegex = /(https?:\/\/)?(t\.me|telegram\.me)\/[^\s]+/gi;
   185→  const twitterRegex = /(https?:\/\/)?(twitter\.com|x\.com)\/[^\s]+/gi;
   186→
   187→  const telegramMatches = text.match(telegramRegex) || [];
   188→  const twitterMatches = text.match(twitterRegex) || [];
   189→
   190→  return {
   191→    telegram: telegramMatches.map(url => url.startsWith('http') ? url : `https://${url}`),
   192→    twitter: twitterMatches.map(url => url.startsWith('http') ? url : `https://${url}`)
   193→  };
   194→}
   195→
   196→/**
   197→ * Forward social links from KOL chats to the Content thread
   198→ */
   199→async function forwardKolSocialLinks(
   200→  chatId: string,
   201→  chatTitle: string | undefined,
   202→  fromName: string,
   203→  messageText: string,
   204→  messageDate: Date
   205→) {
   206→  try {
   207→    // Check if this chat is linked to a KOL
   208→    const { data: chat, error } = await supabaseAdmin
   209→      .from('telegram_chats')
   210→      .select('master_kol_id, title, master_kols:master_kol_id(name)')
   211→      .eq('chat_id', chatId)
   212→      .single();
   213→
   214→    if (error || !chat || !chat.master_kol_id) {
   215→      // Not a KOL-linked chat, skip
   216→      return;
   217→    }
   218→
   219→    // Extract social links from the message
   220→    const links = extractSocialLinks(messageText);
   221→    const hasLinks = links.telegram.length > 0 || links.twitter.length > 0;
   222→
   223→    if (!hasLinks) {
   224→      return;
   225→    }
   226→
   227→    // Format the notification message
   228→    const kolName = (chat.master_kols as any)?.name || 'Unknown KOL';
   229→    const chatName = chat.title || chatTitle || 'Unknown Chat';
   230→    const dateStr = messageDate.toLocaleDateString('en-US', { month: 'short', day: 'numeric', year: 'numeric' });
   231→
   232→    let message = `🔗 New KOL Link Detected\n\n`;
   233→    message += `KOL: ${kolName}\n`;
   234→    message += `Chat: ${chatName}\n`;
   235→    message += `From: ${fromName}\n`;
   236→    message += `Date: ${dateStr}\n\n`;
   237→
   238→    if (links.telegram.length > 0) {
   239→      message += `📱 Telegram:\n`;
   240→      links.telegram.forEach(link => {
   241→        message += `${link}\n`;
   242→      });
   243→    }
   244→
   245→    if (links.twitter.length > 0) {
   246→      if (links.telegram.length > 0) message += `\n`;
   247→      message += `𝕏 Twitter/X:\n`;
   248→      links.twitter.forEach(link => {
   249→        message += `${link}\n`;
   250→      });
   251→    }
   252→
   253→    // Send to Content thread
   254→    await sendTelegramMessageToThread(CONTENT_THREAD_CHAT_ID, CONTENT_THREAD_ID, message);
   255→    console.log('[Telegram Webhook] Forwarded KOL social links to Content thread:', { kolName, links });
   256→  } catch (error) {
   257→    console.error('[Telegram Webhook] Error forwarding KOL social links:', error);
   258→    // Non-critical, don't throw
   259→  }
   260→}
   261→
   262→/**
   263→ * Handle bot commands - reads from database
   264→ */
   265→async function handleCommand(chatId: string, command: string, args: string[], message: any) {
   266→  // Remove leading slash and bot mention
   267→  const cmd = command.toLowerCase().replace(/^\//, '').replace('@holo_hive_bot', '');
   268→
   269→  // Built-in /test command - sends chat ID and thread ID to Andy Lee
   270→  if (cmd === 'test') {
   271→    const ANDY_LEE_TELEGRAM_ID = '6281931733';
   272→    const threadId = message.message_thread_id || null;
   273→    const chatType = message.chat?.type || 'unknown';
   274→    const chatTitle = message.chat?.title || 'Private Chat';
   275→    const fromUser = message.from?.first_name || message.from?.username || 'Unknown';
   276→
   277→    const response = `<b>🔧 /test Command Triggered</b>\n\n` +
   278→      `<b>Chat ID:</b> <code>${chatId}</code>\n` +
   279→      `<b>Thread ID:</b> <code>${threadId || 'N/A (not a forum topic)'}</code>\n` +
   280→      `<b>Chat Type:</b> ${chatType}\n` +
   281→      `<b>Chat Title:</b> ${chatTitle}\n` +
   282→      `<b>Triggered by:</b> ${fromUser}`;
   283→
   284→    // Send to Andy Lee's DM
   285→    await sendTelegramMessage(ANDY_LEE_TELEGRAM_ID, response);
   286→
   287→    // Send confirmation in the original chat
   288→    await sendTelegramMessage(chatId, '✅ Chat info sent to Andy Lee.');
   289→
   290→    console.log('[Telegram Webhook] Executed /test command:', { chatId, threadId, sentTo: 'Andy Lee' });
   291→    return;
   292→  }
   293→
   294→  try {
   295→    // Look up command in database
   296→    const { data: commandData, error } = await supabaseAdmin
   297→      .from('telegram_commands')
   298→      .select('response, image_url, team_only')
   299→      .eq('command', cmd)
   300→      .eq('is_active', true)
   301→      .single();
   302→
   303→    if (error || !commandData) {
   304→      console.log('[Telegram Webhook] Unknown command:', cmd);
   305→      return;
   306→    }
   307→
   308→    // Check if command is team-only
   309→    if (commandData.team_only) {
   310→      const fromUserId = message.from?.id?.toString();
   311→
   312→      if (!fromUserId) {
   313→        console.log('[Telegram Webhook] Team-only command rejected: no user ID');
   314→        await sendTelegramMessage(chatId, 'This command is only available to team members.');
   315→        return;
   316→      }
   317→
   318→      // Check if the user's telegram_id is in the users table (team member)
   319→      const { data: teamMember, error: teamError } = await supabaseAdmin
   320→        .from('users')
   321→        .select('id, name')
   322→        .eq('telegram_id', fromUserId)
   323→        .single();
   324→
   325→      if (teamError || !teamMember) {
   326→        console.log('[Telegram Webhook] Team-only command rejected: user not a team member', fromUserId);
   327→        await sendTelegramMessage(chatId, 'This command is only available to team members.');
   328→        return;
   329→      }
   330→
   331→      console.log('[Telegram Webhook] Team-only command authorized for:', teamMember.name);
   332→    }
   333→
   334→    // Send photo with caption if image_url exists, otherwise just send message
   335→    if (commandData.image_url) {
   336→      await sendTelegramPhoto(chatId, commandData.image_url, commandData.response);
   337→    } else {
   338→      await sendTelegramMessage(chatId, commandData.response);
   339→    }
   340→    console.log('[Telegram Webhook] Executed command:', cmd);
   341→  } catch (error) {
   342→    console.error('[Telegram Webhook] Error handling command:', error);
   343→  }
   344→}
   345→
   346→/**
   347→ * Handle incoming message from Telegram
   348→ */
   349→async function handleMessage(message: any) {
   350→  const chatId = message.chat?.id?.toString();
   351→  const chatTitle = message.chat?.title;
   352→  const chatType = message.chat?.type; // 'group', 'supergroup', 'private'
   353→  const messageId = message.message_id?.toString();
   354→  const fromId = message.from?.id?.toString();
   355→  const fromFirstName = message.from?.first_name;
   356→  const fromLastName = message.from?.last_name;
   357→  const fromUsername = message.from?.username;
   358→  const messageText = message.text || message.caption || '[Media]';
   359→  const messageDate = new Date(message.date * 1000); // Telegram sends Unix timestamp
   360→
   361→  if (!chatId) {
   362→    console.log('[Telegram Webhook] No chat ID in message');
   363→    return;
   364→  }
   365→
   366→  // Handle commands (messages starting with /)
   367→  if (messageText.startsWith('/')) {
   368→    const parts = messageText.split(' ');
   369→    const command = parts[0];
   370→    const args = parts.slice(1);
   371→    await handleCommand(chatId, command, args, message);
   372→  }
   373→
   374→  // Track all chat types (groups, supergroups, and private DMs)
   375→  // For DMs, use the user's name as the title
   376→  const title = chatType === 'private'
   377→    ? [fromFirstName, fromLastName].filter(Boolean).join(' ') || fromUsername || 'Unknown User'
   378→    : chatTitle;
   379→
   380→  // Track/update chat in telegram_chats table
   381→  await trackChat(chatId, title, chatType, messageDate);
   382→
   383→  // Store the message for chat identification
   384→  const fromName = [fromFirstName, fromLastName].filter(Boolean).join(' ') || 'Unknown';
   385→  await storeMessage(chatId, messageId, fromId, fromName, fromUsername, messageText, messageDate);
   386→
   387→  // Check for Telegram/X links in KOL chats and forward to Content thread
   388→  await forwardKolSocialLinks(chatId, chatTitle, fromName, messageText, messageDate);
   389→
   390→  // Check if this chat ID matches any opportunity's gc field
   391→  const { data: opportunities, error } = await supabaseAdmin
   392→    .from('crm_opportunities')
   393→    .select('id, name, gc')
   394→    .eq('gc', chatId);
   395→
   396→  if (error) {
   397→    console.error('[Telegram Webhook] Error querying opportunities:', error);
   398→    return;
   399→  }
   400→
   401→  // Check if the message is from our bot
   402→  const botToken = process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN;
   403→  const botId = botToken ? botToken.split(':')[0] : null;
   404→  const isFromBot = fromId === botId;
   405→
   406→  // Check if the sender is a team member (has telegram_id in users table)
   407→  let isTeamMember = false;
   408→  if (fromId && !isFromBot) {
   409→    const { data: teamMember } = await supabaseAdmin
   410→      .from('users')
   411→      .select('id')
   412→      .eq('telegram_id', fromId)
   413→      .single();
   414→    isTeamMember = !!teamMember;
   415→  }
   416→
   417→  // Determine which field to update:
   418→  // - Bot messages → last_reply_at
   419→  // - Team member messages → last_team_message_at
   420→  // - Lead/others messages → last_message_at
   421→  const getUpdateField = () => {
   422→    if (isFromBot) return 'last_reply_at';
   423→    if (isTeamMember) return 'last_team_message_at';
   424→    return 'last_message_at';
   425→  };
   426→
   427→  // Update all matching opportunities
   428→  if (opportunities && opportunities.length > 0) {
   429→    for (const opportunity of opportunities) {
   430→      const updateField = getUpdateField();
   431→      const senderType = isFromBot ? 'bot' : (isTeamMember ? 'team' : 'lead');
   432→
   433→      const { error: updateError } = await supabaseAdmin
   434→        .from('crm_opportunities')
   435→        .update({
   436→          [updateField]: messageDate.toISOString(),
   437→          updated_at: new Date().toISOString()
   438→        })
   439→        .eq('id', opportunity.id);
   440→
   441→      if (updateError) {
   442→        console.error('[Telegram Webhook] Error updating opportunity:', updateError);
   443→      } else {
   444→        console.log(`[Telegram Webhook] Updated ${updateField} for opportunity:`, {
   445→          id: opportunity.id,
   446→          name: opportunity.name,
   447→          chatId,
   448→          timestamp: messageDate.toISOString(),
   449→          senderType
   450→        });
   451→      }
   452→    }
   453→  }
   454→}
   455→
   456→/**
   457→ * Track/update chat info in telegram_chats table
   458→ */
   459→async function trackChat(chatId: string, title: string | null, chatType: string, messageDate: Date) {
   460→  try {
   461→    // Check if chat already exists
   462→    const { data: existingChat } = await supabaseAdmin
   463→      .from('telegram_chats')
   464→      .select('id, message_count')
   465→      .eq('chat_id', chatId)
   466→      .single();
   467→
   468→    if (existingChat) {
   469→      // Update existing chat
   470→      await supabaseAdmin
   471→        .from('telegram_chats')
   472→        .update({
   473→          title: title || undefined,
   474→          chat_type: chatType,
   475→          last_message_at: messageDate.toISOString(),
   476→          message_count: (existingChat.message_count || 0) + 1,
   477→          updated_at: new Date().toISOString()
   478→        })
   479→        .eq('id', existingChat.id);
   480→
   481→      console.log('[Telegram Webhook] Updated chat tracking:', { chatId, title });
   482→    } else {
   483→      // Insert new chat
   484→      await supabaseAdmin
   485→        .from('telegram_chats')
   486→        .insert({
   487→          chat_id: chatId,
   488→          title: title,
   489→          chat_type: chatType,
   490→          last_message_at: messageDate.toISOString(),
   491→          message_count: 1
   492→        });
   493→
   494→      console.log('[Telegram Webhook] New chat discovered:', { chatId, title });
   495→    }
   496→  } catch (error) {
   497→    console.error('[Telegram Webhook] Error tracking chat:', error);
   498→    // Don't throw - chat tracking is non-critical
   499→  }
   500→}
   501→
   502→/**
   503→ * Store message for chat identification
   504→ */
   505→async function storeMessage(
   506→  chatId: string,
   507→  messageId: string,
   508→  fromUserId: string | null,
   509→  fromUserName: string,
   510→  fromUsername: string | null,
   511→  text: string,
   512→  messageDate: Date
   513→) {
   514→  try {
   515→    // Truncate long messages
   516→    const truncatedText = text.length > 500 ? text.substring(0, 500) + '...' : text;
   517→
   518→    await supabaseAdmin
   519→      .from('telegram_messages')
   520→      .upsert({
   521→        chat_id: chatId,
   522→        message_id: messageId,
   523→        from_user_id: fromUserId,
   524→        from_user_name: fromUserName,
   525→        from_username: fromUsername,
   526→        text: truncatedText,
   527→        message_date: messageDate.toISOString()
   528→      }, {
   529→        onConflict: 'chat_id,message_id'
   530→      });
   531→
   532→    console.log('[Telegram Webhook] Stored message:', { chatId, messageId, from: fromUserName });
   533→  } catch (error) {
   534→    console.error('[Telegram Webhook] Error storing message:', error);
   535→    // Don't throw - message storage is non-critical
   536→  }
   537→}
   538→
   539→/**
   540→ * GET endpoint for webhook verification
   541→ * Telegram doesn't use GET, but useful for manual testing
   542→ */
   543→export async function GET() {
   544→  return NextResponse.json({
   545→    status: 'Telegram webhook endpoint active',
   546→    info: 'This endpoint receives POST requests from Telegram'
   547→  });
   548→}
   549→
```

```text
     1→import { supabase } from './supabase';
     2→
     3→// TypeScript types for Changelog
     4→export interface Changelog {
     5→  id: string;
     6→  version: string;
     7→  title: string;
     8→  content: string;
     9→  published_at: string | null;
    10→  is_published: boolean;
    11→  created_by: string | null;
    12→  created_at: string;
    13→  updated_at: string;
    14→}
    15→
    16→export interface CreateChangelogData {
    17→  version: string;
    18→  title: string;
    19→  content: string;
    20→  published_at?: string | null;
    21→  is_published?: boolean;
    22→}
    23→
    24→export interface UpdateChangelogData {
    25→  version?: string;
    26→  title?: string;
    27→  content?: string;
    28→  published_at?: string | null;
    29→  is_published?: boolean;
    30→}
    31→
    32→export class ChangelogService {
    33→  /**
    34→   * Get all changelogs (admin only - includes drafts)
    35→   */
    36→  static async getAllChangelogs(): Promise<Changelog[]> {
    37→    const { data, error } = await supabase
    38→      .from('changelogs')
    39→      .select('*')
    40→      .order('created_at', { ascending: false });
    41→
    42→    if (error) {
    43→      console.error('Error fetching changelogs:', error);
    44→      throw error;
    45→    }
    46→
    47→    return data || [];
    48→  }
    49→
    50→  /**
    51→   * Get all published changelogs (visible to all users)
    52→   */
    53→  static async getPublishedChangelogs(): Promise<Changelog[]> {
    54→    const { data, error } = await supabase
    55→      .from('changelogs')
    56→      .select('*')
    57→      .eq('is_published', true)
    58→      .or('published_at.is.null,published_at.lte.now()')
    59→      .order('published_at', { ascending: false, nullsFirst: false });
    60→
    61→    if (error) {
    62→      console.error('Error fetching published changelogs:', error);
    63→      throw error;
    64→    }
    65→
    66→    return data || [];
    67→  }
    68→
    69→  /**
    70→   * Get unread changelogs for the current user
    71→   */
    72→  static async getUnreadChangelogs(): Promise<Changelog[]> {
    73→    // Get current user
    74→    const { data: { user } } = await supabase.auth.getUser();
    75→    if (!user) return [];
    76→
    77→    // First, get all changelog IDs that the user has viewed
    78→    const { data: viewedData, error: viewedError } = await supabase
    79→      .from('changelog_views')
    80→      .select('changelog_id')
    81→      .eq('user_id', user.id);
    82→
    83→    if (viewedError) {
    84→      console.error('Error fetching viewed changelogs:', viewedError);
    85→      throw viewedError;
    86→    }
    87→
    88→    const viewedIds = (viewedData || []).map(v => v.changelog_id);
    89→
    90→    // Get all published changelogs
    91→    let query = supabase
    92→      .from('changelogs')
    93→      .select('*')
    94→      .eq('is_published', true)
    95→      .or('published_at.is.null,published_at.lte.now()')
    96→      .order('published_at', { ascending: false, nullsFirst: false });
    97→
    98→    // Exclude viewed changelogs if any
    99→    if (viewedIds.length > 0) {
   100→      query = query.not('id', 'in', `(${viewedIds.join(',')})`);
   101→    }
   102→
   103→    const { data, error } = await query;
   104→
   105→    if (error) {
   106→      console.error('Error fetching unread changelogs:', error);
   107→      throw error;
   108→    }
   109→
   110→    return data || [];
   111→  }
   112→
   113→  /**
   114→   * Get a single changelog by ID
   115→   */
   116→  static async getChangelogById(id: string): Promise<Changelog | null> {
   117→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   118→      .from('changelogs')
   119→      .select('*')
   120→      .eq('id', id)
   121→      .single();
   122→
   123→    if (error) {
   124→      if (error.code === 'PGRST116') return null; // Not found
   125→      console.error('Error fetching changelog:', error);
   126→      throw error;
   127→    }
   128→
   129→    return data;
   130→  }
   131→
   132→  /**
   133→   * Create a new changelog (admin only)
   134→   */
   135→  static async createChangelog(data: CreateChangelogData): Promise<Changelog> {
   136→    const { data: { user } } = await supabase.auth.getUser();
   137→
   138→    const { data: changelog, error } = await supabase
   139→      .from('changelogs')
   140→      .insert({
   141→        ...data,
   142→        created_by: user?.id,
   143→        created_at: new Date().toISOString(),
   144→        updated_at: new Date().toISOString()
   145→      })
   146→      .select()
   147→      .single();
   148→
   149→    if (error) {
   150→      console.error('Error creating changelog:', error);
   151→      throw error;
   152→    }
   153→
   154→    return changelog;
   155→  }
   156→
   157→  /**
   158→   * Update an existing changelog (admin only)
   159→   */
   160→  static async updateChangelog(id: string, data: UpdateChangelogData): Promise<Changelog> {
   161→    const { data: changelog, error } = await supabase
   162→      .from('changelogs')
   163→      .update({
   164→        ...data,
   165→        updated_at: new Date().toISOString()
   166→      })
   167→      .eq('id', id)
   168→      .select()
   169→      .single();
   170→
   171→    if (error) {
   172→      console.error('Error updating changelog:', error);
   173→      throw error;
   174→    }
   175→
   176→    return changelog;
   177→  }
   178→
   179→  /**
   180→   * Delete a changelog (admin only)
   181→   */
   182→  static async deleteChangelog(id: string): Promise<void> {
   183→    const { error } = await supabase
   184→      .from('changelogs')
   185→      .delete()
   186→      .eq('id', id);
   187→
   188→    if (error) {
   189→      console.error('Error deleting changelog:', error);
   190→      throw error;
   191→    }
   192→  }
   193→
   194→  /**
   195→   * Publish a changelog (admin only)
   196→   */
   197→  static async publishChangelog(id: string): Promise<Changelog> {
   198→    return this.updateChangelog(id, {
   199→      is_published: true,
   200→      published_at: new Date().toISOString()
   201→    });
   202→  }
   203→
   204→  /**
   205→   * Unpublish a changelog (admin only)
   206→   */
   207→  static async unpublishChangelog(id: string): Promise<Changelog> {
   208→    return this.updateChangelog(id, {
   209→      is_published: false
   210→    });
   211→  }
   212→
   213→  /**
   214→   * Mark a changelog as viewed by the current user
   215→   */
   216→  static async markAsViewed(changelogId: string): Promise<void> {
   217→    const { data: { user } } = await supabase.auth.getUser();
   218→    if (!user) return;
   219→
   220→    const { error } = await supabase
   221→      .from('changelog_views')
   222→      .upsert({
   223→        user_id: user.id,
   224→        changelog_id: changelogId,
   225→        viewed_at: new Date().toISOString()
   226→      }, {
   227→        onConflict: 'user_id,changelog_id'
   228→      });
   229→
   230→    if (error) {
   231→      console.error('Error marking changelog as viewed:', error);
   232→      throw error;
   233→    }
   234→  }
   235→
   236→  /**
   237→   * Mark multiple changelogs as viewed by the current user
   238→   */
   239→  static async markMultipleAsViewed(changelogIds: string[]): Promise<void> {
   240→    const { data: { user } } = await supabase.auth.getUser();
   241→    if (!user || changelogIds.length === 0) return;
   242→
   243→    const records = changelogIds.map(changelogId => ({
   244→      user_id: user.id,
   245→      changelog_id: changelogId,
   246→      viewed_at: new Date().toISOString()
   247→    }));
   248→
   249→    const { error } = await supabase
   250→      .from('changelog_views')
   251→      .upsert(records, {
   252→        onConflict: 'user_id,changelog_id'
   253→      });
   254→
   255→    if (error) {
   256→      console.error('Error marking changelogs as viewed:', error);
   257→      throw error;
   258→    }
   259→  }
   260→}
   261→
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server';
     2→
     3→export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic';
     4→
     5→/**
     6→ * Delete a message from a Telegram chat
     7→ * POST /api/telegram/delete
     8→ */
     9→export async function POST(request: NextRequest) {
    10→  try {
    11→    const body = await request.json();
    12→    const { chatId, messageId } = body;
    13→
    14→    if (!chatId || !messageId) {
    15→      return NextResponse.json(
    16→        { error: 'chatId and messageId are required' },
    17→        { status: 400 }
    18→      );
    19→    }
    20→
    21→    const botToken = process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN;
    22→    if (!botToken) {
    23→      console.error('[Telegram Delete] Bot token not configured');
    24→      return NextResponse.json(
    25→        { error: 'Telegram bot not configured' },
    26→        { status: 500 }
    27→      );
    28→    }
    29→
    30→    const response = await fetch(
    31→      `https://api.telegram.org/bot${botToken}/deleteMessage`,
    32→      {
    33→        method: 'POST',
    34→        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
    35→        body: JSON.stringify({
    36→          chat_id: chatId,
    37→          message_id: messageId
    38→        })
    39→      }
    40→    );
    41→
    42→    if (!response.ok) {
    43→      const error = await response.json();
    44→      console.error('[Telegram Delete] Error:', error);
    45→      return NextResponse.json(
    46→        { error: 'Failed to delete message', details: error },
    47→        { status: 500 }
    48→      );
    49→    }
    50→
    51→    const result = await response.json();
    52→    console.log('[Telegram Delete] Message deleted from chat:', chatId, 'message_id:', messageId);
    53→
    54→    return NextResponse.json({
    55→      success: true
    56→    });
    57→  } catch (error) {
    58→    console.error('[Telegram Delete] Error:', error);
    59→    return NextResponse.json(
    60→      { error: 'Internal server error' },
    61→      { status: 500 }
    62→    );
    63→  }
    64→}
    65→
```

```text
     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server';
     2→import { TelegramService } from '@/lib/telegramService';
     3→import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js';
     4→
     5→export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic';
     6→
     7→// Require admin authentication
     8→const supabase = createClient(
     9→  process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL!,
    10→  process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY!
    11→);
    12→
    13→/**
    14→ * GET /api/telegram/webhook/manage - Get webhook status
    15→ */
    16→export async function GET(request: NextRequest) {
    17→  try {
    18→    // Check auth
    19→    const authHeader = request.headers.get('authorization');
    20→    if (!authHeader) {
    21→      return NextResponse.json({ error: 'Unauthorized' }, { status: 401 });
    22→    }
    23→
    24→    const info = await TelegramService.getWebhookInfo();
    25→    return NextResponse.json(info);
    26→  } catch (error) {
    27→    console.error('Error getting webhook info:', error);
    28→    return NextResponse.json(
    29→      { error: 'Failed to get webhook info' },
    30→      { status: 500 }
    31→    );
    32→  }
    33→}
    34→
    35→/**
    36→ * POST /api/telegram/webhook/manage - Register webhook
    37→ * Body: { action: 'register' | 'delete', webhookUrl?: string }
    38→ */
    39→export async function POST(request: NextRequest) {
    40→  try {
    41→    // Check auth
    42→    const authHeader = request.headers.get('authorization');
    43→    if (!authHeader) {
    44→      return NextResponse.json({ error: 'Unauthorized' }, { status: 401 });
    45→    }
    46→
    47→    const { action, webhookUrl } = await request.json();
    48→
    49→    if (action === 'register') {
    50→      // Auto-construct webhook URL if not provided
    51→      const baseUrl = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL
    52→        || process.env.VERCEL_URL
    53→        || '';
    54→
    55→      const url = webhookUrl || `https://${baseUrl.replace(/^https?:\/\//, '')}/api/telegram/webhook`;
    56→
    57→      if (!url.startsWith('https://')) {
    58→        return NextResponse.json(
    59→          { error: 'Webhook URL must be HTTPS. Deploy to production first.' },
    60→          { status: 400 }
    61→        );
    62→      }
    63→
    64→      const result = await TelegramService.registerWebhook(url);
    65→
    66→      if (result.success) {
    67→        return NextResponse.json({
    68→          success: true,
    69→          message: 'Webhook registered successfully',
    70→          webhookUrl: url
    71→        });
    72→      } else {
    73→        return NextResponse.json(
    74→          { success: false, error: result.error },
    75→          { status: 400 }
    76→        );
    77→      }
    78→    } else if (action === 'delete') {
    79→      const result = await TelegramService.deleteWebhook();
    80→
    81→      if (result.success) {
    82→        return NextResponse.json({
    83→          success: true,
    84→          message: 'Webhook deleted successfully'
    85→        });
    86→      } else {
    87→        return NextResponse.json(
    88→          { success: false, error: result.error },
    89→          { status: 400 }
    90→        );
    91→      }
    92→    } else {
    93→      return NextResponse.json(
    94→        { error: 'Invalid action. Use "register" or "delete"' },
    95→        { status: 400 }
    96→      );
    97→    }
    98→  } catch (error) {
    99→    console.error('Error managing webhook:', error);
   100→    return NextResponse.json(
   101→      { error: 'Failed to manage webhook' },
   102→      { status: 500 }
   103→    );
   104→  }
   105→}
   106→
```

Perfect! I found the changelog-to-Telegram integration. Let me compile all the findings now with complete information.

## Summary of Telegram Bot Integration and Changelog Sending

I've found comprehensive Telegram bot integration in the KOL Campaign Manager codebase, including changelog sending to Telegram. Here are the exact file paths and implementation details:

### **Environment Configuration**
**File:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/.env.local`

```
# Telegram Bot Configuration (for form submission notifications)
# Get bot token from @BotFather on Telegram
# Get chat ID by adding bot to your group/channel and sending a message
# Thread ID is optional - use for sending to specific topics in groups
TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=7996189688:AAF73zNcxoStIGaNxzYdV_75XYd0LhQ6fZw
TELEGRAM_TERMINAL_CHAT_ID=-1002681460515
TELEGRAM_TERMINAL_THREAD_ID=2
```

---

### **1. Core Telegram Service Library**
**File:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/telegramService.ts`

This is the main service class that handles all Telegram operations:

**Key Methods:**
- `sendMessage(text, parseMode)` - Sends text messages to the configured terminal chat
- `sendFormSubmissionNotification(formName, formId, submittedBy, responseData)` - Sends form submission notifications
- `sendTestMessage()` - Sends a test message to verify bot is connected
- `sendToChat(chatId, text, parseMode)` - Sends to a specific chat ID
- `registerWebhook(webhookUrl)` - Registers webhook with Telegram API
- `deleteWebhook()` - Removes the webhook
- `getWebhookInfo()` - Gets current webhook status

**API Endpoint Used:** `https://api.telegram.org/bot{botToken}/sendMessage`

**Features:**
- HTML and Markdown formatting support
- Thread ID support for forum topics in groups
- Webhook secret token verification
- Environment variable runtime reading (avoiding serverless caching)

---

### **2. Telegram Send API Route**
**File:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/api/telegram/send/route.ts`

**Endpoint:** `POST /api/telegram/send`

**Request Body:**
```typescript
{
  message: string,
  chatId?: string,
  threadId?: number,
  useTerminalChat?: boolean
}
```

**Features:**
- Routes messages to terminal chat or operations chat
- Stores sent messages in Supabase `telegram_messages` table
- Defaults to HH Operations chat (`-1002636253963`)
- Database storage for message tracking

**Operations Chat ID:** `-1002636253963`

---

### **3. Telegram Webhook Route (Incoming Messages)**
**File:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/api/telegram/webhook/route.ts`

**Endpoint:** `POST /api/telegram/webhook`

**Key Features:**
- Receives updates from Telegram when messages are sent in group chats
- Handles commands (messages starting with `/`)
- Built-in `/test` command that sends chat/thread IDs to Andy Lee (Telegram ID: `6281931733`)
- Extracts Telegram and Twitter/X links from KOL chat messages
- Forwards social links to Content thread (`-1002636253963`, thread ID `4280`)
- Updates CRM opportunities' `last_message_at`, `last_team_message_at`, or `last_reply_at` fields
- Tracks chats in `telegram_chats` table
- Stores messages in `telegram_messages` table

**Content Thread Details:**
- Chat ID: `-1002636253963`
- Thread ID: `4280`
- Purpose: Receives forwarded KOL social links

---

### **4. Changelog to Telegram Integration**
**File:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/admin/changelog/page.tsx`

**Lines 110-123, 138-151, 202-214:**

When a changelog is published (either newly created or updated from draft), it automatically sends a notification to Telegram:

```typescript
// Send Telegram notification if switching from draft to published
if (!wasPublished && formData.is_published) {
  try {
    const notificationMessage = `<b>New Changelog Published</b>\n\n<b>Version:</b> v${formData.version}\n<b>Title:</b> ${formData.title}\n\n${formData.content.substring(0, 500)}${formData.content.length > 500 ? '...' : ''}`;

    await fetch('/api/telegram/send', {
      method: 'POST',
      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
      body: JSON.stringify({ message: notificationMessage })
    });
  } catch (telegramErr) {
    console.error('Failed to send Telegram notification:', telegramErr);
  }
}
```

**How it works:**
1. When a changelog status changes from draft to published, it sends an HTML-formatted message
2. Message includes version, title, and first 500 characters of content
3. Messages are sent via `/api/telegram/send` endpoint
4. Telegram errors are logged but don't prevent the changelog from being saved
5. Only super_admin role can create/publish changelogs

---

### **5. Telegram Delete Message Route**
**File:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/api/telegram/delete/route.ts`

**Endpoint:** `POST /api/telegram/delete`

**Request Body:**
```typescript
{
  chatId: string,
  messageId: string
}
```

**API Endpoint Used:** `https://api.telegram.org/bot{botToken}/deleteMessage`

---

### **6. Telegram Test Route**
**File:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/api/telegram/test/route.ts`

**Endpoint:** `GET /api/telegram/test`

Sends a test message: "✅ Telegram Bot Connected! Your Holo Hive form submission notifications are now active."

---

### **7. Webhook Management Route**
**File:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/api/telegram/webhook/manage/route.ts`

**Endpoints:**
- `GET /api/telegram/webhook/manage` - Get webhook status
- `POST /api/telegram/webhook/manage` - Register or delete webhook

**Request Body:**
```typescript
{
  action: 'register' | 'delete',
  webhookUrl?: string
}
```

**Webhook Features:**
- Auto-constructs webhook URL from environment
- Requires HTTPS (enforced)
- Supports optional webhook secret token validation
- Configurable allowed updates

---

### **Key Database Tables**

1. **telegram_messages** - Stores all sent and received messages
2. **telegram_chats** - Tracks chat metadata (title, type, message count, last activity)
3. **telegram_commands** - Database-driven bot commands with response and image_url
4. **crm_opportunities** - Updated with message timestamps via webhook

---

### **Telegram Message Flow Summary**

```
Changelog Published
         ↓
Admin publishes changelog in /app/admin/changelog/page.tsx
         ↓
Calls POST /api/telegram/send with formatted message
         ↓
Sends to TELEGRAM_TERMINAL_CHAT_ID (-1002681460515)
         ↓
In thread TELEGRAM_TERMINAL_THREAD_ID (2)
         ↓
Message stored in telegram_messages table
```

All sensitive information (bot token, chat IDs) is stored in `.env.local` and accessed via environment variables at runtime.

```text
Perfect! Now I have all the information. Let me create a comprehensive summary of my findings:

## Summary

I've successfully identified how link submissions send Telegram messages in the codebase. Here are the findings:

### File Paths and Code Locations

**1. Link Submission Page (Form Frontend):**
- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/public/links/submit/page.tsx` (lines 100-150)
  - Handles the form submission UI and sends POST request to `/api/links/submit`

**2. Link Submission API Endpoint:**
- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/api/links/submit/route.ts` (lines 80-93)
  - Receives the form data, inserts it into the database
  - Calls `TelegramService.sendMessage()` to send Telegram notification

**3. Telegram Service Implementation:**
- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/telegramService.ts` (lines 14-97)
  - Core service that sends messages to Telegram
  - Uses environment variables: `TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN`, `TELEGRAM_TERMINAL_CHAT_ID`, `TELEGRAM_TERMINAL_THREAD_ID`

### Telegram Configuration Details

**Chat ID:** `-1002681460515`
**Thread ID:** `2`
**Bot Token:** `7996189688:AAF73zNcxoStIGaNxzYdV_75XYd0LhQ6fZw`

These values are stored in `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/.env.local` (lines 17-19)

### Message Flow for Link Submissions

1. User fills form at `/app/public/links/submit/page.tsx` with: name, URL, client, link types, access level, description
2. Frontend calls `/api/links/submit` POST endpoint
3. API inserts link into Supabase `links` table
4. API calls `TelegramService.sendMessage(message)` with formatted message (lines 83-87 of route.ts):
   ```
   <b>New Link Submitted</b>
   
   <b>Name:</b> [name]
   <b>Client:</b> [client]
   <b>Type:</b> [link_types]
   
   <a href="[baseUrl]/links">View Links</a>
   ```
5. TelegramService sends message to chat `-1002681460515`, thread `2`

### Additional Link-Related Submissions

The same pattern is used for other submission types (all in `/app/api/*/submit/route.ts`):
- **Forms:** `/app/api/forms/submit/route.ts` - uses `TelegramService.sendFormSubmissionNotification()`
- **Leads:** `/app/api/leads/submit/route.ts` - uses `TelegramService.sendMessage()`
- **Partners:** `/app/api/partners/submit/route.ts` - uses `TelegramService.sendMessage()`

All these services target the same Telegram chat and thread configuration defined in environment variables.
```

```text
     1→'use client';
     2→
     3→import { useState, useEffect, useMemo } from 'react';
     4→import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button';
     5→import { Input } from '@/components/ui/input';
     6→import { Label } from '@/components/ui/label';
     7→import { Textarea } from '@/components/ui/textarea';
     8→import { Select, SelectContent, SelectItem, SelectTrigger, SelectValue } from '@/components/ui/select';
     9→import { Popover, PopoverContent, PopoverTrigger } from '@/components/ui/popover';
    10→import { Command, CommandEmpty, CommandGroup, CommandInput, CommandItem, CommandList } from '@/components/ui/command';
    11→import { Checkbox } from '@/components/ui/checkbox';
    12→import { Badge } from '@/components/ui/badge';
    13→import { Check, Loader2, ChevronsUpDown, X } from 'lucide-react';
    14→import Image from 'next/image';
    15→import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js';
    16→
    17→// Create a standalone Supabase client for public access
    18→const supabaseUrl = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL!;
    19→const supabaseAnonKey = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY!;
    20→const supabase = createClient(supabaseUrl, supabaseAnonKey);
    21→
    22→const LINK_TYPES = [
    23→  { value: 'client delivery', label: 'Client Delivery' },
    24→  { value: 'templates', label: 'Templates' },
    25→  { value: 'report/research', label: 'Report/Research' },
    26→  { value: 'operations', label: 'Operations' },
    27→  { value: 'public/pr', label: 'Public/PR' },
    28→  { value: 'resources', label: 'Resources' },
    29→  { value: 'list', label: 'List' },
    30→  { value: 'loom', label: 'Loom' },
    31→  { value: 'sales', label: 'Sales' },
    32→  { value: 'guide', label: 'Guide' },
    33→  { value: 'contract', label: 'Contract' },
    34→  { value: 'others', label: 'Others' }
    35→];
    36→
    37→const ACCESS_OPTIONS = [
    38→  { value: 'public', label: 'Public' },
    39→  { value: 'partners', label: 'Partners' },
    40→  { value: 'team', label: 'Team' },
    41→  { value: 'client', label: 'Client' }
    42→];
    43→
    44→export default function LinkSubmitPage() {
    45→  const [isSubmitting, setIsSubmitting] = useState(false);
    46→  const [isSubmitted, setIsSubmitted] = useState(false);
    47→  const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null);
    48→  const [linkTypesPopoverOpen, setLinkTypesPopoverOpen] = useState(false);
    49→  const [clientPopoverOpen, setClientPopoverOpen] = useState(false);
    50→  const [existingClients, setExistingClients] = useState<string[]>([]);
    51→
    52→  const [formData, setFormData] = useState({
    53→    name: '',
    54→    url: '',
    55→    description: '',
    56→    client: '',
    57→    link_types: [] as string[],
    58→    access: 'team'
    59→  });
    60→
    61→  // Fetch existing clients for suggestions
    62→  useEffect(() => {
    63→    const fetchClients = async () => {
    64→      const { data } = await supabase
    65→        .from('links')
    66→        .select('client')
    67→        .not('client', 'is', null);
    68→
    69→      if (data) {
    70→        const clients = new Set<string>();
    71→        data.forEach((link: { client: string | null }) => {
    72→          if (link.client && link.client.trim()) {
    73→            clients.add(link.client.trim());
    74→          }
    75→        });
    76→        clients.add('Holo Hive');
    77→        setExistingClients(Array.from(clients).sort());
    78→      }
    79→    };
    80→    fetchClients();
    81→  }, []);
    82→
    83→  const clientSuggestions = useMemo(() => {
    84→    return existingClients;
    85→  }, [existingClients]);
    86→
    87→  const toggleLinkType = (type: string) => {
    88→    setFormData(prev => ({
    89→      ...prev,
    90→      link_types: prev.link_types.includes(type)
    91→        ? prev.link_types.filter(t => t !== type)
    92→        : [...prev.link_types, type]
    93→    }));
    94→  };
    95→
    96→  const getLinkTypeLabel = (value: string) => {
    97→    return LINK_TYPES.find(t => t.value === value)?.label || value;
    98→  };
    99→
   100→  const handleSubmit = async (e: React.FormEvent) => {
   101→    e.preventDefault();
   102→    setError(null);
   103→
   104→    if (!formData.name.trim()) {
   105→      setError('Name is required');
   106→      return;
   107→    }
   108→    if (!formData.url.trim()) {
   109→      setError('URL is required');
   110→      return;
   111→    }
   112→
   113→    try {
   114→      new URL(formData.url);
   115→    } catch {
   116→      setError('Please enter a valid URL (include https://)');
   117→      return;
   118→    }
   119→
   120→    if (!formData.client.trim()) {
   121→      setError('Client is required');
   122→      return;
   123→    }
   124→    if (formData.link_types.length === 0) {
   125→      setError('At least one link type is required');
   126→      return;
   127→    }
   128→
   129→    setIsSubmitting(true);
   130→
   131→    try {
   132→      const response = await fetch('/api/links/submit', {
   133→        method: 'POST',
   134→        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
   135→        body: JSON.stringify(formData)
   136→      });
   137→
   138→      const result = await response.json();
   139→
   140→      if (!response.ok) {
   141→        throw new Error(result.error || 'Failed to submit link');
   142→      }
   143→
   144→      setIsSubmitted(true);
   145→    } catch (err: any) {
   146→      setError(err.message || 'An error occurred while submitting');
   147→    } finally {
   148→      setIsSubmitting(false);
   149→    }
   150→  };
   151→
   152→  if (isSubmitted) {
   153→    return (
   154→      <div className="min-h-screen bg-gradient-to-br from-gray-50 to-gray-100 flex items-center justify-center p-4">
   155→        <div className="w-full max-w-md bg-white rounded-2xl shadow-xl border border-gray-100 p-8">
   156→          <div className="text-center">
   157→            <div className="mx-auto w-16 h-16 bg-green-100 rounded-full flex items-center justify-center mb-6">
   158→              <Check className="h-8 w-8 text-green-600" />
   159→            </div>
   160→            <h2 className="text-2xl font-bold text-gray-900 mb-2">Link Submitted!</h2>
   161→            <p className="text-gray-500 mb-8">Your link has been successfully added to the repository.</p>
   162→            <Button
   163→              onClick={() => {
   164→                setIsSubmitted(false);
   165→                setFormData({
   166→                  name: '',
   167→                  url: '',
   168→                  description: '',
   169→                  client: '',
   170→                  link_types: [],
   171→                  access: 'team'
   172→                });
   173→              }}
   174→              variant="outline"
   175→              className="px-6"
   176→            >
   177→              Submit Another Link
   178→            </Button>
   179→          </div>
   180→        </div>
   181→      </div>
   182→    );
   183→  }
   184→
   185→  return (
   186→    <div className="min-h-screen bg-gradient-to-br from-gray-50 to-gray-100 flex items-center justify-center p-4">
   187→      <div className="w-full max-w-lg">
   188→        {/* Header with logo */}
   189→        <div className="text-center mb-6">
   190→          <div className="inline-flex items-center justify-center mb-4">
   191→            <Image
   192→              src="/images/logo.png"
   193→              alt="Logo"
   194→              width={48}
   195→              height={48}
   196→              className="rounded-lg"
   197→            />
   198→          </div>
   199→          <h1 className="text-2xl font-bold text-gray-900">Submit a Link</h1>
   200→          <p className="text-gray-500 mt-1">Add a new link to the repository</p>
   201→        </div>
   202→
   203→        {/* Form Card */}
   204→        <div className="bg-white rounded-2xl shadow-xl border border-gray-100 p-6 sm:p-8">
   205→          <form onSubmit={handleSubmit} className="space-y-5">
   206→            {error && (
   207→              <div className="bg-red-50 border border-red-200 text-red-700 px-4 py-3 rounded-lg text-sm">
   208→                {error}
   209→              </div>
   210→            )}
   211→
   212→            <div className="space-y-2">
   213→              <Label htmlFor="name">Name <span className="text-red-500">*</span></Label>
   214→              <Input
   215→                id="name"
   216→                placeholder="Enter link name"
   217→                value={formData.name}
   218→                onChange={e => setFormData(prev => ({ ...prev, name: e.target.value }))}
   219→                disabled={isSubmitting}
   220→                className="auth-input"
   221→              />
   222→            </div>
   223→
   224→            <div className="space-y-2">

   232→                disabled={isSubmitting}
   233→                className="auth-input"
   234→              />
   235→            </div>
   236→
   237→            <div className="space-y-2">
   238→              <Label>Client <span className="text-red-500">*</span></Label>
   239→              <Popover open={clientPopoverOpen} onOpenChange={setClientPopoverOpen}>

   241→                  <Button

   383→                  Submitting...
   384→                </>
   385→              ) : (
   386→                'Submit Link'
   387→              )}
   388→            </Button>
   389→          </form>
   390→        </div>
   391→
   392→        {/* Footer */}
   393→        <p className="text-center text-xs text-gray-400 mt-6">
   394→          Powered by Holo Hive
   395→        </p>
   396→      </div>
   397→    </div>
   398→  );
   399→}
   400→
```
</details></tool-use>

 `./app/api/links/submit/route.ts`

```text
     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server';
     2→import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js';
     3→import { Database } from '@/lib/database.types';
     4→import { TelegramService } from '@/lib/telegramService';
     5→
     6→export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic';
     7→
     8→export async function POST(request: NextRequest) {
     9→  try {
    10→    const body = await request.json();
    11→    const { name, url, description, client, link_types, access } = body;
    12→
    13→    // Validate required fields
    14→    if (!name || !name.trim()) {
    15→      return NextResponse.json(
    16→        { error: 'Name is required' },
    17→        { status: 400 }
    18→      );
    19→    }
    20→
    21→    if (!url || !url.trim()) {
    22→      return NextResponse.json(
    23→        { error: 'URL is required' },
    24→        { status: 400 }
    25→      );
    26→    }
    27→
    28→    // Validate URL format
    29→    try {
    30→      new URL(url);
    31→    } catch {
    32→      return NextResponse.json(
    33→        { error: 'Invalid URL format' },
    34→        { status: 400 }
    35→      );
    36→    }
    37→
    38→    // Create server-side Supabase client with service role key to bypass RLS
    39→    const supabaseUrl = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL;
    40→    const supabaseServiceKey = process.env.SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY;
    41→
    42→    if (!supabaseUrl || !supabaseServiceKey) {
    43→      console.error('Missing Supabase environment variables');
    44→      return NextResponse.json(
    45→        { error: 'Server configuration error' },
    46→        { status: 500 }
    47→      );
    48→    }
    49→
    50→    const supabaseAdmin = createClient<Database>(supabaseUrl, supabaseServiceKey, {
    51→      auth: {
    52→        autoRefreshToken: false,
    53→        persistSession: false
    54→      }
    55→    });
    56→
    57→    // Insert link
    58→    const { data: link, error } = await supabaseAdmin
    59→      .from('links')
    60→      .insert([{
    61→        name: name.trim(),
    62→        url: url.trim(),
    63→        description: description?.trim() || null,
    64→        client: client?.trim() || null,
    65→        link_types: link_types || [],
    66→        access: access || 'team',
    67→        status: 'active'
    68→      }])
    69→      .select()
    70→      .single();
    71→
    72→    if (error) {
    73→      console.error('Error inserting link:', error);
    74→      return NextResponse.json(
    75→        { error: 'Failed to save link' },
    76→        { status: 500 }
    77→      );
    78→    }
    79→
    80→    // Send Telegram notification (must await in serverless environment)
    81→    const baseUrl = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL || 'https://portal.holohive.agency';
    82→    const linkUrl = `${baseUrl}/links`;
    83→    const message = `<b>New Link Submitted</b>\n\n` +
    84→      `<b>Name:</b> ${name.trim()}\n` +
    85→      `<b>Client:</b> ${client?.trim() || 'N/A'}\n` +
    86→      `<b>Type:</b> ${link_types?.map((t: string) => t.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + t.slice(1)).join(', ') || 'N/A'}\n\n` +
    87→      `<a href="${linkUrl}">View Links</a>`;
    88→
    89→    try {
    90→      await TelegramService.sendMessage(message);
    91→    } catch (err) {
    92→      console.error('[Links Submit] Telegram notification error:', err);
    93→    }
    94→
    95→    return NextResponse.json({
    96→      success: true,
    97→      link
    98→    });
    99→
   100→  } catch (error) {
   101→    console.error('Error in link submission:', error);
   102→    return NextResponse.json(
   103→      { error: 'Internal server error' },
   104→      { status: 500 }
   105→    );
   106→  }
   107→}
   108→
```
</details></tool-use>
