```text
     1→/**
     2→ * Telegram Bot Service
     3→ * Sends notifications to Telegram chat rooms
     4→ */
     5→
     6→interface TelegramMessage {
     7→  chat_id: string;
     8→  text: string;
     9→  parse_mode?: 'HTML' | 'Markdown' | 'MarkdownV2';
    10→  disable_web_page_preview?: boolean;
    11→  message_thread_id?: number;
    12→}
    13→
    14→export class TelegramService {
    15→  // Read env vars at runtime via getters to avoid serverless caching issues
    16→  private static get botToken() {
    17→    return process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN;
    18→  }
    19→
    20→  private static get chatId() {
    21→    return process.env.TELEGRAM_TERMINAL_CHAT_ID;
    22→  }
    23→
    24→  private static get threadId() {
    25→    return process.env.TELEGRAM_TERMINAL_THREAD_ID;
    26→  }
    27→
    28→  /**
    29→   * Send a message to the configured Telegram chat
    30→   */
    31→  static async sendMessage(text: string, parseMode: 'HTML' | 'Markdown' = 'HTML'): Promise<boolean> {
    32→    const botToken = this.botToken;
    33→    const chatId = this.chatId;
    34→    const threadId = this.threadId;
    35→
    36→    if (!botToken || !chatId) {
    37→      console.error('[Telegram] Configuration missing:', {
    38→        hasToken: !!botToken,
    39→        hasChatId: !!chatId,
    40→        tokenPrefix: botToken ? botToken.substring(0, 10) + '...' : 'missing',
    41→        chatId: chatId || 'missing'
    42→      });
    43→      return false;
    44→    }
    45→
    46→    try {
    47→      const message: TelegramMessage = {
    48→        chat_id: chatId,
    49→        text,
    50→        parse_mode: parseMode,
    51→        disable_web_page_preview: false,
    52→      };
    53→
    54→      // Add thread ID if configured (for sending to specific topics in groups)
    55→      if (threadId) {
    56→        message.message_thread_id = parseInt(threadId);
    57→      }
    58→
    59→      console.log('[Telegram] Sending message:', {
    60→        chatId: chatId,
    61→        threadId: threadId,
    62→        messageLength: text.length,
    63→        parseMode
    64→      });
    65→
    66→      const response = await fetch(
    67→        `https://api.telegram.org/bot${botToken}/sendMessage`,
    68→        {
    69→          method: 'POST',
    70→          headers: {
    71→            'Content-Type': 'application/json',
    72→          },
    73→          body: JSON.stringify(message),
    74→        }
    75→      );
    76→
    77→      if (!response.ok) {
    78→        const error = await response.json();
    79→        console.error('[Telegram] API error:', {
    80→          status: response.status,
    81→          error
    82→        });
    83→        return false;
    84→      }
    85→
    86→      const result = await response.json();
    87→      console.log('[Telegram] Message sent successfully:', {
    88→        messageId: result.result?.message_id,
    89→        chatId: result.result?.chat?.id
    90→      });
    91→
    92→      return true;
    93→    } catch (error) {
    94→      console.error('[Telegram] Error sending message:', error);
    95→      return false;
    96→    }
    97→  }
    98→
    99→  /**
   100→   * Format and send form submission notification
   101→   */
   102→  static async sendFormSubmissionNotification(
   103→    formName: string,
   104→    formId: string,
   105→    submittedBy?: { name?: string; email?: string },
   106→    responseData?: Record<string, any>
   107→  ): Promise<boolean> {
   108→    // Construct the base URL
   109→    const baseUrl = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL
   110→      ? (process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL.startsWith('http')
   111→          ? process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL
   112→          : `https://${process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL}`)
   113→      : (process.env.VERCEL_URL ? `https://${process.env.VERCEL_URL}` : 'http://localhost:3000');
   114→
   115→    const formUrl = `${baseUrl}/forms/${formId}`;
   116→
   117→    const message = `${formName} Form has been submitted.\n<a href="${formUrl}">View Form</a>`;
   118→
   119→    return this.sendMessage(message);
   120→  }
   121→
   122→  /**
   123→   * Send a test message to verify configuration
   124→   */
   125→  static async sendTestMessage(): Promise<boolean> {
   126→    return this.sendMessage(
   127→      '<b>✅ Telegram Bot Connected!</b>\n\nYour Holo Hive form submission notifications are now active.'
   128→    );
   129→  }
   130→
   131→  // ============================================
   132→  // WEBHOOK MANAGEMENT
   133→  // ============================================
   134→
   135→  /**
   136→   * Register the webhook URL with Telegram
   137→   * Call this once after deploying to set up message tracking
   138→   */
   139→  static async registerWebhook(webhookUrl: string): Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string }> {
   140→    if (!this.botToken) {
   141→      return { success: false, error: 'TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN not configured' };
   142→    }
   143→
   144→    try {
   145→      const params: any = {
   146→        url: webhookUrl,
   147→        allowed_updates: ['message', 'edited_message'],
   148→        drop_pending_updates: true // Don't process old messages
   149→      };
   150→
   151→      // Add secret token for webhook verification if configured
   152→      const webhookSecret = process.env.TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET;
   153→      if (webhookSecret) {
   154→        params.secret_token = webhookSecret;
   155→      }
   156→
   157→      const response = await fetch(
   158→        `https://api.telegram.org/bot${this.botToken}/setWebhook`,
   159→        {
   160→          method: 'POST',
   161→          headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
   162→          body: JSON.stringify(params)
   163→        }
   164→      );
   165→
   166→      const result = await response.json();
   167→
   168→      if (!result.ok) {
   169→        console.error('[Telegram] Webhook registration failed:', result);
   170→        return { success: false, error: result.description };
   171→      }
   172→
   173→      console.log('[Telegram] Webhook registered successfully:', webhookUrl);
   174→      return { success: true };
   175→    } catch (error) {
   176→      console.error('[Telegram] Error registering webhook:', error);
   177→      return { success: false, error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Unknown error' };
   178→    }
   179→  }
   180→
   181→  /**
   182→   * Remove the webhook (stop receiving updates)
   183→   */
   184→  static async deleteWebhook(): Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string }> {
   185→    if (!this.botToken) {
   186→      return { success: false, error: 'TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN not configured' };
   187→    }
   188→
   189→    try {
   190→      const response = await fetch(
   191→        `https://api.telegram.org/bot${this.botToken}/deleteWebhook`,
   192→        { method: 'POST' }
   193→      );
   194→
   195→      const result = await response.json();
   196→
   197→      if (!result.ok) {
   198→        return { success: false, error: result.description };
   199→      }
   200→
   201→      console.log('[Telegram] Webhook deleted successfully');
   202→      return { success: true };
   203→    } catch (error) {
   204→      console.error('[Telegram] Error deleting webhook:', error);
   205→      return { success: false, error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Unknown error' };
   206→    }
   207→  }
   208→
   209→  /**
   210→   * Get current webhook info
   211→   */
   212→  static async getWebhookInfo(): Promise<any> {
   213→    if (!this.botToken) {
   214→      return { error: 'TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN not configured' };
   215→    }
   216→
   217→    try {
   218→      const response = await fetch(
   219→        `https://api.telegram.org/bot${this.botToken}/getWebhookInfo`
   220→      );
   221→
   222→      return await response.json();
   223→    } catch (error) {
   224→      console.error('[Telegram] Error getting webhook info:', error);
   225→      return { error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Unknown error' };
   226→    }
   227→  }
   228→
   229→  /**
   230→   * Send a message to a specific chat (for CRM integration)
   231→   */
   232→  static async sendToChat(
   233→    chatId: string,
   234→    text: string,
   235→    parseMode: 'HTML' | 'Markdown' = 'HTML'
   236→  ): Promise<boolean> {
   237→    if (!this.botToken) {
   238→      console.error('[Telegram] Bot token not configured');
   239→      return false;
   240→    }
   241→
   242→    try {
   243→      const response = await fetch(
   244→        `https://api.telegram.org/bot${this.botToken}/sendMessage`,
   245→        {
   246→          method: 'POST',
   247→          headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
   248→          body: JSON.stringify({
   249→            chat_id: chatId,
   250→            text,
   251→            parse_mode: parseMode
   252→          })
   253→        }
   254→      );
   255→
   256→      if (!response.ok) {
   257→        const error = await response.json();
   258→        console.error('[Telegram] Send to chat error:', error);
   259→        return false;
   260→      }
   261→
   262→      return true;
   263→    } catch (error) {
   264→      console.error('[Telegram] Error sending to chat:', error);
   265→      return false;
   266→    }
   267→  }
   268→}
   269→
```

```text
     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server';
     2→import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js';
     3→
     4→export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic';
     5→
     6→// Operations chat for internal notifications
     7→const OPERATIONS_CHAT_ID = '-1002636253963';
     8→
     9→// Initialize Supabase client
    10→const supabaseUrl = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL!;
    11→const supabaseKey = process.env.SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY || process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY!;
    12→const supabase = createClient(supabaseUrl, supabaseKey);
    13→
    14→/**
    15→ * Send a message to the HH Operations Telegram chat
    16→ * POST /api/telegram/send
    17→ */
    18→export async function POST(request: NextRequest) {
    19→  try {
    20→    const body = await request.json();
    21→    const { message, chatId, threadId, useTerminalChat } = body;
    22→
    23→    if (!message) {
    24→      return NextResponse.json(
    25→        { error: 'Message is required' },
    26→        { status: 400 }
    27→      );
    28→    }
    29→
    30→    const botToken = process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN;
    31→    if (!botToken) {
    32→      console.error('[Telegram Send] Bot token not configured');
    33→      return NextResponse.json(
    34→        { error: 'Telegram bot not configured' },
    35→        { status: 500 }
    36→      );
    37→    }
    38→
    39→    // Use terminal chat if requested, otherwise use provided chatId or default to operations chat
    40→    const terminalChatId = process.env.TELEGRAM_TERMINAL_CHAT_ID;
    41→    const targetChatId = useTerminalChat && terminalChatId ? terminalChatId : (chatId || OPERATIONS_CHAT_ID);
    42→
    43→    const messagePayload: any = {
    44→      chat_id: targetChatId,
    45→      text: message,
    46→      parse_mode: 'HTML'
    47→    };
    48→
    49→    // Add thread_id for topic-based messaging
    50→    // Use terminal thread ID if using terminal chat, otherwise use provided threadId
    51→    const terminalThreadId = process.env.TELEGRAM_TERMINAL_THREAD_ID;
    52→    const targetThreadId = useTerminalChat && terminalThreadId ? terminalThreadId : threadId;
    53→    if (targetThreadId) {
    54→      messagePayload.message_thread_id = parseInt(targetThreadId);
    55→    }
    56→
    57→    const response = await fetch(
    58→      `https://api.telegram.org/bot${botToken}/sendMessage`,
    59→      {
    60→        method: 'POST',
    61→        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
    62→        body: JSON.stringify(messagePayload)
    63→      }
    64→    );
    65→
    66→    if (!response.ok) {
    67→      const error = await response.json();
    68→      console.error('[Telegram Send] Error:', error);
    69→      return NextResponse.json(
    70→        { error: 'Failed to send message', details: error },
    71→        { status: 500 }
    72→      );
    73→    }
    74→
    75→    const result = await response.json();
    76→    console.log('[Telegram Send] Message sent to chat:', targetChatId);
    77→
    78→    // Store the sent message in the database
    79→    try {
    80→      const botInfo = result.result?.from;
    81→      await supabase.from('telegram_messages').insert({
    82→        chat_id: targetChatId,
    83→        message_id: String(result.result?.message_id),
    84→        from_user_id: botInfo?.id ? String(botInfo.id) : null,
    85→        from_user_name: botInfo?.first_name || 'Bot',
    86→        from_username: botInfo?.username || null,
    87→        text: message,
    88→        message_date: new Date().toISOString()
    89→      });
    90→      console.log('[Telegram Send] Message stored in database');
    91→    } catch (dbError) {
    92→      // Log but don't fail the request if storage fails
    93→      console.error('[Telegram Send] Failed to store message:', dbError);
    94→    }
    95→
    96→    return NextResponse.json({
    97→      success: true,
    98→      message_id: result.result?.message_id
    99→    });
   100→  } catch (error) {
   101→    console.error('[Telegram Send] Error:', error);
   102→    return NextResponse.json(
   103→      { error: 'Internal server error' },
   104→      { status: 500 }
   105→    );
   106→  }
   107→}
   108→
```

 `./app/api/forms/submit/route.ts`

```text
     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server';
     2→import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js';
     3→import { Database } from '@/lib/database.types';
     4→import { TelegramService } from '@/lib/telegramService';
     5→
     6→export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic';
     7→
     8→export async function POST(request: NextRequest) {
     9→  try {
    10→    const body = await request.json();
    11→    const { form_id, response_data, submitted_by_email, submitted_by_name } = body;
    12→
    13→    // Validate required fields
    14→    if (!form_id || !response_data) {
    15→      return NextResponse.json(
    16→        { error: 'Missing required fields: form_id and response_data' },
    17→        { status: 400 }
    18→      );
    19→    }
    20→
    21→    // Create server-side Supabase client with service role key to bypass RLS
    22→    const supabaseUrl = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL;
    23→    const supabaseServiceKey = process.env.SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY;
    24→
    25→    if (!supabaseUrl || !supabaseServiceKey) {
    26→      console.error('Missing Supabase environment variables');
    27→      return NextResponse.json(
    28→        { error: 'Server configuration error' },
    29→        { status: 500 }
    30→      );
    31→    }
    32→
    33→    const supabaseAdmin = createClient<Database>(supabaseUrl, supabaseServiceKey, {
    34→      auth: {
    35→        autoRefreshToken: false,
    36→        persistSession: false
    37→      }
    38→    });
    39→
    40→    // Insert form response
    41→    const { data: response, error } = await supabaseAdmin
    42→      .from('form_responses')
    43→      .insert([{
    44→        form_id,
    45→        response_data,
    46→        submitted_by_email: submitted_by_email || null,
    47→        submitted_by_name: submitted_by_name || null
    48→      }])
    49→      .select()
    50→      .single();
    51→
    52→    if (error) {
    53→      console.error('Error inserting form response:', error);
    54→      return NextResponse.json(
    55→        { error: 'Failed to submit form response' },
    56→        { status: 500 }
    57→      );
    58→    }
    59→
    60→    // Fetch form details for Telegram notification
    61→    const { data: form } = await supabaseAdmin
    62→      .from('forms')
    63→      .select('name')
    64→      .eq('id', form_id)
    65→      .single();
    66→
    67→    console.log('[Form Submit] Sending Telegram notification for form:', {
    68→      formId: form_id,
    69→      formName: form?.name,
    70→      hasSubmitterName: !!submitted_by_name,
    71→      hasSubmitterEmail: !!submitted_by_email
    72→    });
    73→
    74→    // Send Telegram notification (wait for it but don't fail if it errors)
    75→    try {
    76→      const telegramSuccess = await TelegramService.sendFormSubmissionNotification(
    77→        form?.name || 'Unknown Form',
    78→        form_id,
    79→        {
    80→          name: submitted_by_name,
    81→          email: submitted_by_email,
    82→        },
    83→        response_data
    84→      );
    85→
    86→      if (telegramSuccess) {
    87→        console.log('[Form Submit] Telegram notification sent successfully');
    88→      } else {
    89→        console.warn('[Form Submit] Telegram notification failed (returned false)');
    90→      }
    91→    } catch (err) {
    92→      console.error('[Form Submit] Exception while sending Telegram notification:', err);
    93→      // Don't fail the request if Telegram notification fails
    94→    }
    95→
    96→    return NextResponse.json(
    97→      { success: true, data: response },
    98→      { status: 200 }
    99→    );
   100→  } catch (error) {
   101→    console.error('Error in form submission API:', error);
   102→    return NextResponse.json(
   103→      { error: 'Internal server error' },
   104→      { status: 500 }
   105→    );
   106→  }
   107→}
   108→
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server';
     2→import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js';
     3→import { Database } from '@/lib/database.types';
     4→import { TelegramService } from '@/lib/telegramService';
     5→
     6→export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic';
     7→
     8→export async function POST(request: NextRequest) {
     9→  try {
    10→    const body = await request.json();
    11→    const {
    12→      name,
    13→      account_type,
    14→      scope,
    15→      deal_value,
    16→      currency,
    17→      source,
    18→      referrer,
    19→      gc,
    20→      notes,
    21→      contact_name,
    22→      contact_email,
    23→      contact_telegram
    24→    } = body;
    25→
    26→    // Validate required fields
    27→    if (!name || !name.trim()) {
    28→      return NextResponse.json(
    29→        { error: 'Lead name is required' },
    30→        { status: 400 }
    31→      );
    32→    }
    33→
    34→    if (!contact_name || !contact_name.trim()) {
    35→      return NextResponse.json(
    36→        { error: 'Contact name is required' },
    37→        { status: 400 }
    38→      );
    39→    }
    40→
    41→    // Validate at least one contact method
    42→    if ((!contact_email || !contact_email.trim()) && (!contact_telegram || !contact_telegram.trim())) {
    43→      return NextResponse.json(
    44→        { error: 'At least one contact method (email or Telegram) is required' },
    45→        { status: 400 }
    46→      );
    47→    }
    48→
    49→    // Validate email format if provided
    50→    if (contact_email && contact_email.trim()) {
    51→      const emailRegex = /^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/;
    52→      if (!emailRegex.test(contact_email.trim())) {
    53→        return NextResponse.json(
    54→          { error: 'Invalid email format' },
    55→          { status: 400 }
    56→        );
    57→      }
    58→    }
    59→
    60→    // Validate deal value if provided
    61→    if (deal_value && isNaN(parseFloat(deal_value))) {
    62→      return NextResponse.json(
    63→        { error: 'Deal value must be a valid number' },
    64→        { status: 400 }
    65→      );
    66→    }
    67→
    68→    // Create server-side Supabase client with service role key to bypass RLS
    69→    const supabaseUrl = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL;
    70→    const supabaseServiceKey = process.env.SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY;
    71→
    72→    if (!supabaseUrl || !supabaseServiceKey) {
    73→      console.error('Missing Supabase environment variables');
    74→      return NextResponse.json(
    75→        { error: 'Server configuration error' },
    76→        { status: 500 }
    77→      );
    78→    }
    79→
    80→    const supabaseAdmin = createClient<Database>(supabaseUrl, supabaseServiceKey, {
    81→      auth: {
    82→        autoRefreshToken: false,
    83→        persistSession: false
    84→      }
    85→    });
    86→
    87→    // Build notes with contact info
    88→    let combinedNotes = '';
    89→    if (contact_name || contact_email || contact_telegram) {
    90→      combinedNotes += `Contact: ${contact_name || 'N/A'}`;
    91→      if (contact_email) combinedNotes += ` | Email: ${contact_email}`;
    92→      if (contact_telegram) combinedNotes += ` | Telegram: ${contact_telegram}`;
    93→    }
    94→    if (notes?.trim()) {
    95→      combinedNotes += combinedNotes ? `\n\n${notes.trim()}` : notes.trim();
    96→    }
    97→
    98→    // Build scope string (join array into comma-separated string)
    99→    const scopeValue = Array.isArray(scope) && scope.length > 0 ? scope.join(', ') : null;
   100→
   101→    // Insert opportunity
   102→    const { data: opportunity, error } = await supabaseAdmin
   103→      .from('crm_opportunities')
   104→      .insert([{
   105→        name: name.trim(),
   106→        account_type: account_type || null,
   107→        scope: scopeValue,
   108→        deal_value: deal_value ? parseFloat(deal_value) : null,
   109→        currency: currency || 'USD',
   110→        source: source || null,
   111→        referrer: referrer?.trim() || null,
   112→        gc: gc?.trim() || null,
   113→        notes: combinedNotes || null,
   114→        stage: 'new'
   115→      }])
   116→      .select()
   117→      .single();
   118→
   119→    if (error) {
   120→      console.error('Error inserting opportunity:', error);
   121→      return NextResponse.json(
   122→        { error: 'Failed to save lead' },
   123→        { status: 500 }
   124→      );
   125→    }
   126→
   127→    // Send Telegram notification
   128→    const baseUrl = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL || 'https://portal.holohive.agency';
   129→    const pipelineUrl = `${baseUrl}/crm/pipeline`;
   130→    const message = `<b>New Lead Submitted</b>\n\n` +
   131→      `<b>Name:</b> ${name.trim()}\n` +
   132→      `<b>Type:</b> ${account_type || 'N/A'}\n` +
   133→      `<b>Scope:</b> ${Array.isArray(scope) ? scope.join(', ') : scope || 'N/A'}\n` +
   134→      `<b>Value:</b> ${deal_value ? `${currency || 'USD'} ${deal_value}` : 'N/A'}\n` +
   135→      `<b>Contact:</b> ${contact_name?.trim() || 'N/A'}\n` +
   136→      `<b>Source:</b> ${source || 'N/A'}\n\n` +
   137→      `<a href="${pipelineUrl}">View Pipeline</a>`;
   138→
   139→    try {
   140→      await TelegramService.sendMessage(message);
   141→    } catch (err) {
   142→      console.error('[Leads Submit] Telegram notification error:', err);
   143→    }
   144→
   145→    return NextResponse.json({
   146→      success: true,
   147→      opportunity
   148→    });
   149→
   150→  } catch (error) {
   151→    console.error('Error in lead submission:', error);
   152→    return NextResponse.json(
   153→      { error: 'Internal server error' },
   154→      { status: 500 }
   155→    );
   156→  }
   157→}
   158→
```
</details></tool-use>

Perfect! Now I have all the information. Let me create a comprehensive summary of my findings:

## Summary

I've successfully identified how link submissions send Telegram messages in the codebase. Here are the findings:

### File Paths and Code Locations

**1. Link Submission Page (Form Frontend):**
- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/public/links/submit/page.tsx` (lines 100-150)
  - Handles the form submission UI and sends POST request to `/api/links/submit`

**2. Link Submission API Endpoint:**
- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/api/links/submit/route.ts` (lines 80-93)
  - Receives the form data, inserts it into the database
  - Calls `TelegramService.sendMessage()` to send Telegram notification

**3. Telegram Service Implementation:**
- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/telegramService.ts` (lines 14-97)
  - Core service that sends messages to Telegram
  - Uses environment variables: `TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN`, `TELEGRAM_TERMINAL_CHAT_ID`, `TELEGRAM_TERMINAL_THREAD_ID`

### Telegram Configuration Details

**Chat ID:** `-1002681460515`
**Thread ID:** `2`
**Bot Token:** `7996189688:AAF73zNcxoStIGaNxzYdV_75XYd0LhQ6fZw`

These values are stored in `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/.env.local` (lines 17-19)

### Message Flow for Link Submissions

1. User fills form at `/app/public/links/submit/page.tsx` with: name, URL, client, link types, access level, description
2. Frontend calls `/api/links/submit` POST endpoint
3. API inserts link into Supabase `links` table
4. API calls `TelegramService.sendMessage(message)` with formatted message (lines 83-87 of route.ts):
   ```
   <b>New Link Submitted</b>
   
   <b>Name:</b> [name]
   <b>Client:</b> [client]
   <b>Type:</b> [link_types]
   
   <a href="[baseUrl]/links">View Links</a>
   ```
5. TelegramService sends message to chat `-1002681460515`, thread `2`

### Additional Link-Related Submissions

The same pattern is used for other submission types (all in `/app/api/*/submit/route.ts`):
- **Forms:** `/app/api/forms/submit/route.ts` - uses `TelegramService.sendFormSubmissionNotification()`
- **Leads:** `/app/api/leads/submit/route.ts` - uses `TelegramService.sendMessage()`
- **Partners:** `/app/api/partners/submit/route.ts` - uses `TelegramService.sendMessage()`

All these services target the same Telegram chat and thread configuration defined in environment variables.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-02-13 08:06:14Z)**_

Link submissions (and all other form submissions — leads, partners, forms) send Telegram notifications to the **Terminal Chat**:

- **Chat ID:** `-1002681460515` (`TELEGRAM_TERMINAL_CHAT_ID`)
- **Thread ID:** `2` (`TELEGRAM_TERMINAL_THREAD_ID`)

This is the same chat the changelog was just sent to. The message is sent via `TelegramService.sendMessage()` in `/app/api/links/submit/route.ts`, formatted as:

```
New Link Submitted

Name: [name]
Client: [client]
Type: [link_types]

View Links
```

```text
Excellent! Now I have a complete picture. Let me create the final report:

## Search Results: Meeting Document/File Upload Functionality

### Summary
The codebase has **partial meeting document/file upload functionality** that is implemented but not fully persisted to storage. Here's what I found:

---

### What EXISTS (Implemented)

#### 1. Meeting Notes Feature - FULLY IMPLEMENTED
**Location:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/clients/page.tsx`

**File Upload for Parsing (.docx files):**
- **Feature:** Gemini meeting notes document parser
- **File Types Supported:** `.docx` files only
- **Library Used:** `mammoth` (v1.11.0) - parses Word documents to text
- **Function:** `handleDocUpload()` (lines 451-564)
- **What it does:**
  - Users can upload Google Gemini meeting notes exported as `.docx` files
  - Extracts text and automatically parses:
    - Meeting date (first line)
    - Meeting title (second line)
    - Attendees (marked with "Invited" prefix)
    - Summary/Details (rich text content)
    - Suggested next steps (action items)
  - **NOTE:** The original document file is NOT stored in Supabase storage
  - Only the parsed data is stored in the database

#### 2. Meeting Notes Database Tables - DEFINED IN CODE
Three related tables are used in the app:
- **`client_meeting_notes`** - stores parsed meeting content
  - Fields: `id`, `client_id`, `title`, `content`, `meeting_date`, `attendees`, `action_items`, `created_by`, `created_at`, `updated_at`
  - No file attachment/URL field
- **`client_decision_log`** - stores decision tracking
- **`client_weekly_updates`** - stores weekly update notes
- **`client_context`** - stores client engagement context

#### 3. Form Attachments Storage - EXISTS (separate feature)
**Location:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/sql/migrations/018_create_form_attachments_storage.sql`

**Storage Bucket Configuration:**
- **Bucket Name:** `form-attachments`
- **Public:** Yes (anyone can read)
- **File Size Limit:** 10MB
- **Supported MIME Types:**
  - Images: `jpeg`, `png`, `gif`, `webp`
  - Documents: `pdf`, `docx`, `xlsx`, `doc`, `xls`, `txt`, `csv`
- **Policies:**
  - Anyone can upload (for public form submissions)
  - Form owners and admins can delete
  - Read access is public

**Note:** This is for general form submissions, not specifically for meeting documents.

---

### What DOES NOT EXIST

#### 1. File Attachment Storage for Meeting Notes
- No database fields for storing attachment URLs in `client_meeting_notes` table
- No file upload handler for meeting notes (unlike forms)
- No Supabase storage bucket specifically configured for meeting documents
- Users cannot attach files to created meeting notes (only parsed documents can populate content)

#### 2. Meeting Document Database Reference
- No migration files creating the `client_meeting_notes`, `client_context`, `client_decision_log`, or `client_weekly_updates` tables
- These tables are referenced in code but may not exist in production database yet
- No RLS policies defined for these tables

#### 3. Document Management Features
- No document versioning
- No file metadata tracking (file name, size, type, upload date)
- No public meeting note sharing portal
- No document expiration/deletion workflows

---

### UI Implementation (Implemented)

**Meeting Notes Modal in Clients Page:**
- Location: `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/clients/page.tsx` (lines 2248-2470)
- Features:
  - Upload button for `.docx` files with label "Gemini meeting notes only"
  - Rich text editor (React Quill) for content and action items
  - Meeting date picker
  - Attendees field
  - Full CRUD operations (Create, Read, Update, Delete)
  - Edit/delete buttons for existing notes

---

### File Paths Reference

**Source Files:**
- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/clients/page.tsx` - Main implementation

### Conclusion

**Status:** Partially Implemented
- Meeting notes feature exists and is functional for creating, editing, and deleting notes
- Document parsing from Gemini `.docx` exports works
- However, actual file attachment/storage is NOT implemented
- Only the parsed content is stored, not the original document files
- For full file management capabilities, additional development would be needed to add file storage, versioning, and attachment fields
```

```text
     1→import { createServerClient as createSupabaseServerClient } from '@supabase/ssr';
     2→import { cookies } from 'next/headers';
     3→import { Database } from './database.types';
     4→
     5→/**
     6→ * Create a Supabase client for Server Components and API routes
     7→ * This client can access cookies and retrieve the user session
     8→ */
     9→export async function createServerClient() {
    10→  const cookieStore = await cookies();
    11→  const supabaseUrl = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL!;
    12→  const supabaseAnonKey = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY!;
    13→
    14→  return createSupabaseServerClient<Database>(
    15→    supabaseUrl,
    16→    supabaseAnonKey,
    17→    {
    18→      cookies: {
    19→        getAll() {
    20→          return cookieStore.getAll();
    21→        },
    22→        setAll(cookiesToSet) {
    23→          try {
    24→            cookiesToSet.forEach(({ name, value, options }) =>
    25→              cookieStore.set(name, value, options)
    26→            );
    27→          } catch {
    28→            // The `setAll` method was called from a Server Component.
    29→            // This can be ignored if you have middleware refreshing user sessions.
    30→          }
    31→        },
    32→      },
    33→    }
    34→  );
    35→}
    36→
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
     1→-- CRM Database Schema
     2→-- Core Tables: Opportunities (Leads+Deals), Partners, Affiliates, Contacts
     3→-- Supporting: Stage History for analytics
     4→
     5→-- ============================================
     6→-- 1. AFFILIATES TABLE (created first for FK reference)
     7→-- KOLs, referrers with rev-share or commission
     8→-- Lifecycle: New → Active → Inactive
     9→-- ============================================
    10→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS crm_affiliates (
    11→  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    12→
    13→  -- Basic Info
    14→  name TEXT NOT NULL,
    15→  affiliation TEXT, -- Their affiliation/company
    16→  category TEXT, -- Type of affiliate
    17→
    18→  -- Lifecycle
    19→  status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'new', -- 'new', 'active', 'inactive'
    20→
    21→  -- Commission
    22→  commission_model TEXT, -- Description of commission/rev-share structure
    23→  commission_rate DECIMAL, -- Percentage if applicable
    24→
    25→  -- Tracking
    26→  last_contacted_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
    27→
    28→  -- Ownership
    29→  owner_id UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id),
    30→
    31→  -- Point of Contact (inline)
    32→  poc_name TEXT,
    33→  poc_email TEXT,
    34→  poc_telegram TEXT,
    35→
    36→  -- Notes
    37→  notes TEXT,
    38→
    39→  -- Timestamps
    40→  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
    41→  updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
    42→);
    43→
    44→-- ============================================
    45→-- 2. OPPORTUNITIES TABLE (Unified Leads + Deals)
    46→-- Single pipeline: New → Contacted → Qualified → Proposal → Contract → Closed
    47→-- ============================================
    48→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS crm_opportunities (
    49→  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    50→
    51→  -- Basic Info
    52→  name TEXT NOT NULL,
    53→
    54→  -- Pipeline Stage (unified lead + deal stages)
    55→  stage TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'new',
    56→  -- Lead stages: 'new', 'contacted', 'qualified', 'unqualified', 'nurture'
    57→  -- Deal stages: 'proposal', 'contract', 'closed_won', 'closed_lost'
    58→
    59→  -- Classification
    60→  account_type TEXT, -- 'general', 'channel', 'campaign', 'lite', 'ad_hoc' (set when qualified)
    61→
    62→  -- Value (set when becomes a deal)
    63→  deal_value DECIMAL,
    64→  currency TEXT DEFAULT 'USD',
    65→
    66→  -- Tracking
    67→  last_contacted_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
    68→
    69→  -- Ownership & Source
    70→  owner_id UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id),
    71→  source TEXT, -- 'referral', 'inbound', 'event', 'cold_outreach'
    72→  referrer TEXT, -- Who referred them
    73→
    74→  -- Communication
    75→  gc TEXT, -- Group Chat link/info
    76→
    77→  -- Affiliate connection (for commission tracking)
    78→  affiliate_id UUID REFERENCES crm_affiliates(id),
    79→
    80→  -- Notes
    81→  notes TEXT,
    82→
    83→  -- Timestamps
    84→  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
    85→  updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
    86→  qualified_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE, -- When moved from lead to deal stage
    87→  closed_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE -- When won or lost
    88→);
    89→
    90→-- ============================================
    91→-- 3. PARTNERS TABLE
    92→-- BD relationships, cross-partnerships
    93→-- Lifecycle: Active / Inactive
    94→-- ============================================
    95→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS crm_partners (
    96→  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    97→
    98→  -- Basic Info
    99→  name TEXT NOT NULL,
   100→
   101→  -- Classification
   102→  category TEXT, -- 'service_provider', 'investor_vc', 'project', 'individual'
   103→  focus TEXT, -- Area of focus/expertise
   104→
   105→  -- Lifecycle
   106→  status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active', -- 'active', 'inactive'
   107→
   108→  -- Tracking
   109→  last_contacted_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
   110→
   111→  -- Ownership
   112→  owner_id UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id),
   113→
   114→  -- Point of Contact (inline)
   115→  poc_name TEXT,
   116→  poc_email TEXT,
   117→  poc_telegram TEXT,
   118→
   119→  -- Affiliate connection (if partner is also an affiliate)
   120→  is_affiliate BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
   121→  affiliate_id UUID REFERENCES crm_affiliates(id),
   122→
   123→  -- Notes
   124→  notes TEXT,
   125→
   126→  -- Timestamps
   127→  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
   128→  updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
   129→);
   130→
   131→-- ============================================
   132→-- 4. CONTACTS TABLE
   133→-- Individual people linked to opportunities/partners/affiliates
   134→-- ============================================
   135→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS crm_contacts (
   136→  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   137→
   138→  -- Basic Info
   139→  name TEXT NOT NULL,
   140→  email TEXT,
   141→  telegram_id TEXT,
   142→  x_id TEXT,
   143→  role TEXT, -- Their role/title
   144→  category TEXT, -- Contact category
   145→
   146→  -- Ownership
   147→  owner_id UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id),
   148→
   149→  -- Notes
   150→  notes TEXT,
   151→
   152→  -- Timestamps
   153→  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
   154→  updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
   155→);
   156→
   157→-- ============================================
   158→-- 5. CONTACT LINKS (Junction Table)
   159→-- Links contacts to opportunities/partners/affiliates
   160→-- ============================================
   161→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS crm_contact_links (
   162→  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   163→  contact_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES crm_contacts(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   164→
   165→  -- Linked object type and ID
   166→  linked_type TEXT NOT NULL, -- 'opportunity', 'partner', 'affiliate'
   167→  opportunity_id UUID REFERENCES crm_opportunities(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   168→  partner_id UUID REFERENCES crm_partners(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   169→  affiliate_id UUID REFERENCES crm_affiliates(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   170→
   171→  -- Role in this relationship
   172→  role TEXT,
   173→  is_primary BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
   174→
   175→  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
   176→
   177→  -- Ensure only one link type is set
   178→  CONSTRAINT check_single_link CHECK (
   179→    (CASE WHEN opportunity_id IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END +
   180→     CASE WHEN partner_id IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END +
   181→     CASE WHEN affiliate_id IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 1
   182→  )
   183→);
   184→
   185→-- ============================================
   186→-- 6. STAGE HISTORY (For Analytics)
   187→-- Tracks all stage changes for conversion/velocity analysis
   188→-- ============================================
   189→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS crm_stage_history (
   190→  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   191→
   192→  -- Object reference
   193→  object_type TEXT NOT NULL, -- 'opportunity', 'partner', 'affiliate'
   194→  object_id UUID NOT NULL,
   195→
   196→  -- Stage change
   197→  from_stage TEXT,
   198→  to_stage TEXT NOT NULL,
   199→
   200→  -- Who and when
   201→  changed_by UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id),
   202→  changed_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
   203→
   204→  notes TEXT
   205→);
   206→
   207→-- ============================================
   208→-- INDEXES
   209→-- ============================================
   210→
   211→-- Opportunities
   212→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_opportunities_stage ON crm_opportunities(stage);
   213→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_opportunities_owner ON crm_opportunities(owner_id);
   214→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_opportunities_source ON crm_opportunities(source);
   215→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_opportunities_affiliate ON crm_opportunities(affiliate_id);
   216→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_opportunities_created ON crm_opportunities(created_at);
   217→
   218→-- Partners
   219→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_partners_status ON crm_partners(status);
   220→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_partners_category ON crm_partners(category);
   221→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_partners_owner ON crm_partners(owner_id);
   222→
   223→-- Affiliates
   224→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_affiliates_status ON crm_affiliates(status);
   225→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_affiliates_category ON crm_affiliates(category);
   226→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_affiliates_owner ON crm_affiliates(owner_id);
   227→
   228→-- Contacts
   229→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_contacts_owner ON crm_contacts(owner_id);
   230→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_contacts_email ON crm_contacts(email);
   231→
   232→-- Contact links
   233→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_contact_links_contact ON crm_contact_links(contact_id);
   234→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_contact_links_opportunity ON crm_contact_links(opportunity_id);
   235→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_contact_links_partner ON crm_contact_links(partner_id);
   236→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_contact_links_affiliate ON crm_contact_links(affiliate_id);
   237→
   238→-- Stage history
   239→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_stage_history_object ON crm_stage_history(object_type, object_id);
   240→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_stage_history_changed ON crm_stage_history(changed_at);
   241→
   242→-- ============================================
   243→-- COMMENTS
   244→-- ============================================
   245→
   246→COMMENT ON TABLE crm_opportunities IS 'Unified sales pipeline: Leads (new/contacted/qualified) → Deals (proposal/contract/closed). Single entity journey.';
   247→COMMENT ON TABLE crm_partners IS 'BD relationships. Categories: service_provider, investor_vc, project, individual. Can also be affiliates.';
   248→COMMENT ON TABLE crm_affiliates IS 'KOLs and referrers with commission/rev-share. Lifecycle: new → active → inactive.';
   249→COMMENT ON TABLE crm_contacts IS 'Individual people who can be linked to multiple CRM objects.';
   250→COMMENT ON TABLE crm_contact_links IS 'Links contacts to opportunities, partners, or affiliates with role info.';
   251→COMMENT ON TABLE crm_stage_history IS 'Audit trail of all stage changes for analytics (conversion rates, velocity).';
   252→
```

Now let me check what the handleDocUpload function actually does and whether it stores files to Supabase storage:

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 2026-02-13 11:56:39Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./app/clients/page.tsx`

```text
   450→  // Parse Gemini meeting notes .docx
   451→  const handleDocUpload = async (e: React.ChangeEvent<HTMLInputElement>) => {
   452→    const file = e.target.files?.[0];
   453→    if (!file) return;
   454→    // Reset input so the same file can be re-uploaded
   455→    e.target.value = '';
   456→
   457→    if (!file.name.endsWith('.docx')) {
   458→      toast({ title: 'Invalid file', description: 'Please upload a .docx file (Gemini meeting notes export).', variant: 'destructive' });
   459→      return;
   460→    }
   461→
   462→    setIsParsingDoc(true);
   463→    try {
   464→      const arrayBuffer = await file.arrayBuffer();
   465→      const result = await mammoth.extractRawText({ arrayBuffer });
   466→      const text = result.value;
   467→      const lines = text.split('\n').map(l => l.trim()).filter(l => l.length > 0);
   468→
   469→      // Parse Gemini meeting notes format
   470→      let title = '';
   471→      let meetingDate: Date | undefined;
   472→      let attendees = '';
   473→      let content = '';
   474→      let actionItems = '';
   475→
   476→      // Line 1: Date (e.g. "Feb 10, 2026")
   477→      // Line 2: Title
   478→      // Line starting with "Invited": attendees
   479→      // After "Summary" or "Details": content
   480→      // After "Suggested next steps": action items
   481→
   482→      let section = 'header'; // header | summary | details | actions | footer
   483→      let lineIndex = 0;
   484→
   485→      for (const line of lines) {
   486→        // Skip footer lines from Gemini
   487→        if (line.startsWith('You should review Gemini') || line.startsWith('Please provide feedback')) continue;
   488→        if (line.startsWith('Get tips and learn how Gemini')) continue;
   489→
   490→        if (lineIndex === 0) {
   491→          // First line is the date
   492→          const parsed = new Date(line);
   493→          if (!isNaN(parsed.getTime())) {
   494→            meetingDate = parsed;
   495→          }
   496→          lineIndex++;
   497→          continue;
   498→        }
   499→
   500→        if (lineIndex === 1) {
   501→          title = line;
   502→          lineIndex++;
   503→          continue;
   504→        }
   505→
   506→        if (line.startsWith('Invited ')) {
   507→          attendees = line.replace('Invited ', '').trim();
   508→          lineIndex++;
   509→          continue;
   510→        }
   511→
   512→        if (line.startsWith('Attachments ')) {
   513→          lineIndex++;
   514→          continue;
   515→        }
   516→
   517→        if (line === 'Summary') {
   518→          section = 'summary';
   519→          lineIndex++;
   520→          continue;
   521→        }
   522→
   523→        if (line === 'Details') {
   524→          section = 'details';
   525→          lineIndex++;
   526→          continue;
   527→        }
   528→
   529→        if (line === 'Suggested next steps') {
   530→          section = 'actions';
   531→          lineIndex++;
   532→          continue;
   533→        }
   534→
   535→        if (section === 'summary' || section === 'details') {
   536→          content += (content ? '\n\n' : '') + line;
   537→        } else if (section === 'actions') {
   538→          actionItems += (actionItems ? '\n' : '') + line;
   539→        }
   540→
   541→        lineIndex++;
   542→      }
   543→
   544→      // Convert content and action items to basic HTML for ReactQuill
   545→      const toHtml = (text: string) => text.split('\n').map(p => p.trim() ? `<p>${p.trim()}</p>` : '').join('');
   546→
   547→      setMeetingNoteForm({
   548→        title,
   549→        meeting_date: meetingDate,
   550→        attendees,
   551→        content: toHtml(content),
   552→        action_items: toHtml(actionItems),
   553→      });
   554→      setIsNoteFormOpen(true);
   555→      setEditingNoteId(null);
   556→
   557→      toast({ title: 'Document parsed', description: 'Meeting note fields have been filled from the document.' });
   558→    } catch (err) {
   559→      console.error('Error parsing .docx:', err);
   560→      toast({ title: 'Parse error', description: 'Failed to parse the document. Make sure it is a Gemini meeting notes .docx export.', variant: 'destructive' });
   561→    } finally {
   562→      setIsParsingDoc(false);
   563→    }
   564→  };
   565→
   566→  const handleNoteFormSubmit = async () => {
   567→    if (!meetingNotesModalClient || !meetingNoteForm.title.trim() || !meetingNoteForm.meeting_date) return;
   568→    const payload = {
   569→      title: meetingNoteForm.title.trim(),
   570→      content: meetingNoteForm.content.trim(),
   571→      meeting_date: meetingNoteForm.meeting_date.toISOString().split('T')[0],
   572→      attendees: meetingNoteForm.attendees.trim(),
   573→      action_items: meetingNoteForm.action_items.trim(),
   574→    };
   575→    try {
   576→      if (editingNoteId) {
   577→        await updateMeetingNote(editingNoteId, payload);
   578→      } else {
   579→        await addMeetingNote(meetingNotesModalClient.id, payload);
   580→      }
   581→      setIsNoteFormOpen(false);
   582→      setEditingNoteId(null);
   583→      setMeetingNoteForm({ title: '', content: '', meeting_date: undefined, attendees: '', action_items: '' });
   584→    } catch (err) {
   585→      console.error('Error saving meeting note:', err);
   586→    }
   587→  };
   588→
   589→  // Helper to get linked CRM account for a client
   590→  const getLinkedCRMAccount = (clientId: string) => {
   591→    const accounts = linkedAccounts[clientId];
   592→    // Get the first account-stage opportunity (account_active, account_at_risk, account_churned)
   593→    return accounts?.find(a => a.stage.startsWith('account_')) || accounts?.[0] || null;
   594→  };
   595→
   596→  // Scope options mapping (consistent with pipeline page)
   597→  const scopeLabels: Record<string, string> = {
   598→    'fundraising': 'Fundraising',
   599→    'advisory': 'Advisory',
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./sql/migrations/018_create_form_attachments_storage.sql`

```text
     1→-- Create storage bucket for form attachments
     2→-- This allows public form submissions to upload files
     3→
     4→-- Create the storage bucket
     5→INSERT INTO storage.buckets (id, name, public, file_size_limit, allowed_mime_types)
     6→VALUES (
     7→  'form-attachments',
     8→  'form-attachments',
     9→  true, -- Public bucket so anyone can read the files
    10→  10485760, -- 10MB file size limit
    11→  ARRAY[
    12→    'image/jpeg',
    13→    'image/png',
    14→    'image/gif',
    15→    'image/webp',
    16→    'application/pdf',
    17→    'application/msword',
    18→    'application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document',
    19→    'application/vnd.ms-excel',
    20→    'application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet',
    21→    'text/plain',
    22→    'text/csv'
    23→  ]
    24→)
    25→ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING;
    26→
    27→-- Create storage policies
    28→
    29→-- Allow anyone to upload files (for public form submissions)
    30→CREATE POLICY "Anyone can upload form attachments"
    31→  ON storage.objects FOR INSERT
    32→  WITH CHECK (
    33→    bucket_id = 'form-attachments'
    34→  );
    35→
    36→-- Allow anyone to read files (public bucket)
    37→CREATE POLICY "Anyone can read form attachments"
    38→  ON storage.objects FOR SELECT
    39→  USING (
    40→    bucket_id = 'form-attachments'
    41→  );
    42→
    43→-- Allow form owners and admins to delete attachments
    44→CREATE POLICY "Form owners and admins can delete attachments"
    45→  ON storage.objects FOR DELETE
    46→  USING (
    47→    bucket_id = 'form-attachments'
    48→    AND (
    49→      -- Check if user owns the form (path format: formId/fieldId/filename)
    50→      EXISTS (
    51→        SELECT 1 FROM forms
    52→        WHERE forms.id::text = split_part(name, '/', 1)
    53→        AND forms.user_id = auth.uid()
    54→      )
    55→      OR
    56→      -- Check if user is admin
    57→      EXISTS (
    58→        SELECT 1 FROM users
    59→        WHERE users.id = auth.uid()
    60→        AND users.role = 'admin'
    61→      )
    62→    )
    63→  );
    64→
    65→-- Add comment for documentation
    66→COMMENT ON POLICY "Anyone can upload form attachments" ON storage.objects IS
    67→  'Allows anyone (including anonymous users) to upload attachments when submitting forms';
    68→
    69→COMMENT ON POLICY "Anyone can read form attachments" ON storage.objects IS
    70→  'Allows public access to read form attachments since the bucket is public';
    71→
    72→COMMENT ON POLICY "Form owners and admins can delete attachments" ON storage.objects IS
    73→  'Allows form owners to delete attachments for their forms, and admins to delete any attachment';
    74→
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
type MeetingNote = {
  id: string;
  client_id: string;
  title: string;
  content: string | null;
  meeting_date: string;
  attendees: string | null;
  action_items: string | null;
  created_by: string | null;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
};

type ClientContext = {
  id: string;
  client_id: string;
  engagement_type: string | null;
  scope: string | null;
  start_date: string | null;
  milestones: string | null;
  client_contacts: string | null;
  holohive_contacts: string | null;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
};

type DecisionLogEntry = {
  id: string;
  client_id: string;
  decision_date: string;
  summary: string;
  created_by: string | null;
  created_at: string;
};

type WeeklyUpdate = {
  id: string;
  client_id: string;
  week_of: string;
  current_focus: string;
  active_initiatives: string | null;
  next_checkin: string | null;
  open_questions: string | null;
  created_by: string | null;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
};

type CampaignStatus = 'Planning' | 'Active' | 'Paused' | 'Completed';
type ClientWithStatus = ClientWithAccess & {
  campaignsByStatus?: Record<CampaignStatus, number>;
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 2026-02-18 15:46:22Z)**_

Excellent! Now I have a complete picture. Let me create the final report:

## Search Results: Meeting Document/File Upload Functionality

### Summary
The codebase has **partial meeting document/file upload functionality** that is implemented but not fully persisted to storage. Here's what I found:

---

### What EXISTS (Implemented)

#### 1. Meeting Notes Feature - FULLY IMPLEMENTED
**Location:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/clients/page.tsx`

**File Upload for Parsing (.docx files):**
- **Feature:** Gemini meeting notes document parser
- **File Types Supported:** `.docx` files only
- **Library Used:** `mammoth` (v1.11.0) - parses Word documents to text
- **Function:** `handleDocUpload()` (lines 451-564)
- **What it does:**
  - Users can upload Google Gemini meeting notes exported as `.docx` files
  - Extracts text and automatically parses:
    - Meeting date (first line)
    - Meeting title (second line)
    - Attendees (marked with "Invited" prefix)
    - Summary/Details (rich text content)
    - Suggested next steps (action items)
  - **NOTE:** The original document file is NOT stored in Supabase storage
  - Only the parsed data is stored in the database

#### 2. Meeting Notes Database Tables - DEFINED IN CODE
Three related tables are used in the app:
- **`client_meeting_notes`** - stores parsed meeting content
  - Fields: `id`, `client_id`, `title`, `content`, `meeting_date`, `attendees`, `action_items`, `created_by`, `created_at`, `updated_at`
  - No file attachment/URL field
- **`client_decision_log`** - stores decision tracking
- **`client_weekly_updates`** - stores weekly update notes
- **`client_context`** - stores client engagement context

#### 3. Form Attachments Storage - EXISTS (separate feature)
**Location:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/sql/migrations/018_create_form_attachments_storage.sql`

**Storage Bucket Configuration:**
- **Bucket Name:** `form-attachments`
- **Public:** Yes (anyone can read)
- **File Size Limit:** 10MB
- **Supported MIME Types:**
  - Images: `jpeg`, `png`, `gif`, `webp`
  - Documents: `pdf`, `docx`, `xlsx`, `doc`, `xls`, `txt`, `csv`
- **Policies:**
  - Anyone can upload (for public form submissions)
  - Form owners and admins can delete
  - Read access is public

**Note:** This is for general form submissions, not specifically for meeting documents.

---

### What DOES NOT EXIST

#### 1. File Attachment Storage for Meeting Notes
- No database fields for storing attachment URLs in `client_meeting_notes` table
- No file upload handler for meeting notes (unlike forms)
- No Supabase storage bucket specifically configured for meeting documents
- Users cannot attach files to created meeting notes (only parsed documents can populate content)

#### 2. Meeting Document Database Reference
- No migration files creating the `client_meeting_notes`, `client_context`, `client_decision_log`, or `client_weekly_updates` tables
- These tables are referenced in code but may not exist in production database yet
- No RLS policies defined for these tables

#### 3. Document Management Features
- No document versioning
- No file metadata tracking (file name, size, type, upload date)
- No public meeting note sharing portal
- No document expiration/deletion workflows

---

### UI Implementation (Implemented)

**Meeting Notes Modal in Clients Page:**
- Location: `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/clients/page.tsx` (lines 2248-2470)
- Features:
  - Upload button for `.docx` files with label "Gemini meeting notes only"
  - Rich text editor (React Quill) for content and action items
  - Meeting date picker
  - Attendees field
  - Full CRUD operations (Create, Read, Update, Delete)
  - Edit/delete buttons for existing notes

---

### File Paths Reference

**Source Files:**
- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/clients/page.tsx` - Main implementation
- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/public/portal/[id]/page.tsx` - Public view of meeting notes
- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/sql/migrations/018_create_form_attachments_storage.sql` - Storage bucket config
- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/supabase.ts` - Supabase client
- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/supabase-server.ts` - Server client

**Libraries:**
- `mammoth` v1.11.0 - For parsing `.docx` files
- `react-quill` - Rich text editor for meeting notes content

---

### Conclusion

**Status:** Partially Implemented
- Meeting notes feature exists and is functional for creating, editing, and deleting notes
- Document parsing from Gemini `.docx` exports works
- However, actual file attachment/storage is NOT implemented
- Only the parsed content is stored, not the original document files
- For full file management capabilities, additional development would be needed to add file storage, versioning, and attachment fields

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-02-18 15:46:35Z)**_

The meeting doc upload is **partially implemented**. Here's the current state:

**What works:**
- You can upload `.docx` files (Gemini meeting notes) on the Clients page
- The `mammoth` library parses the Word doc and auto-extracts: date, title, attendees, summary, and action items
- The parsed content is saved to the `client_meeting_notes` table

**What's missing:**
- The original `.docx` file itself is **not stored** — only the parsed text is saved
- No file attachment field in the database to reference uploaded documents
- No Supabase storage bucket for meeting documents (there is one for `form-attachments` but not meetings)

So the upload works as a **parser** (extract content from .docx into the form), but not as **file storage** (keeping the original document attached to the meeting note). Would you like me to add full file attachment storage for meeting documents?

---

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The issue is that the scrolling content is visible between the # and Name columns. The problem is that the # column is 48px wide, but there might be a gap between the two sticky columns where scrolling content shows through. 

The real issue is that both columns are sticky but the # column doesn't have a right border that's part of its sticky element, and the Name column's left border might not fully cover the gap either. The gap exists because the table cells have borders rendered by the table's border-collapse behavior, and sticky positioning creates a layering issue.

Let me think about this differently. The # column is `sticky left-0` and the Name column is `sticky left-[48px]`. 

The # column width might not actually be enforced at 48px everywhere—it's set on the cell but not as a class on the header, so the Name column's left position might not align perfectly with where the # column ends. Looking at the screenshot, I can see truncated link text showing through between the columns, which confirms the scrolling content is bleeding through that gap.

The real fix is to either make the # column slightly wider to overlap the Name column, or use a box-shadow on the # column's right edge to create a solid visual barrier that matches its background color.

Actually, I think the issue is that the width and left positioning don't align perfectly - the # column is set to 48px wide but the Name column's left position might not match exactly. I should use a fixed min-width approach and ensure the Name column's left value corresponds precisely to where the # column ends.

The cleanest solution is probably to add a right box-shadow to the # column that matches its background color, creating a visual seal over any gap that appears when scrolling. With Tailwind, I can apply this shadow effect directly to cover the space between the two sticky columns. I'm settling on forcing the # column to have both `min-w-[48px]` and `w-[48px]` to guarantee its width matches the `left-[48px]` positioning on the Name column, eliminating any sub-pixel gaps between them. I think the real issue is the `border-collapse` setting on the table—when borders are collapsed, they get shared between cells, which breaks the sticky positioning when adjacent cells aren't sticky too. Switching to `border-separate` with zero spacing should fix this.
</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-02-18 16:14:38Z)**_

The issue is `border-collapse: collapse` on the table — with collapsed borders, the shared border between sticky and non-sticky cells gets lost. I need to switch to `border-separate` with `border-spacing: 0`.

<summary>
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user's primary work involved building and iterating on a **Tasks page** for the KOL Campaign Manager, then making various fixes to the **KOL page** and **Clients page**. Specific requests included:
   - Tasks page: inline editing, status tracking, rich text descriptions, reorderable rows/columns, grouped by user, profile avatars, plus button for quick-add
   - Restricting Workspace sidebar section (Tasks + Daily Stand-Up) to Andy only
   - Committing, pushing, sending Telegram changelog, creating system changelog v0.1.9
   - Fixing meeting notes .docx upload button visibility in Clients page
   - KOL page: pagination spacing, sticky Name column, fixing border bleed-through between sticky columns

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Next.js App Router with `'use client'` pages
   - Supabase MCP for database migrations and queries
   - HTML5 native drag-and-drop for column reordering (persisted to localStorage)
   - Sticky table columns with `position: sticky` and `z-index` layering
   - `border-collapse: separate` vs `collapse` for sticky column border rendering
   - React Quill (dynamic import, SSR disabled) for rich text editing
   - Tooltip component for hover previews (replacing native `title` attribute)
   - Popover-based status dropdown with colored icons
   - Telegram bot API integration for changelog notifications
   - Optimistic UI updates with rollback on error
   - Global vs per-group task reordering via `sort_order`

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **`app/tasks/page.tsx`** (Created and heavily iterated)
     - Full task management page with 3 tabs, grouped tables, inline editing, status icons, rich text descriptions
     - Key constants:
       ```tsx
       const STATUSES = ['to_do', 'in_progress', 'paused', 'ready_for_feedback', 'complete'] as const;
       const STATUS_CONFIG: Record<string, { label: string; icon: typeof Circle; color: string; bg: string }> = {
         to_do: { label: 'To Do', icon: Circle, color: 'text-gray-400', bg: 'hover:bg-gray-100' },
         in_progress: { label: 'In Progress', icon: PlayCircle, color: 'text-blue-500', bg: 'hover:bg-blue-50' },
         paused: { label: 'Paused', icon: PauseCircle, color: 'text-amber-500', bg: 'hover:bg-amber-50' },
         ready_for_feedback: { label: 'Ready for Feedback', icon: MessageCircle, color: 'text-purple-500', bg: 'hover:bg-purple-50' },
         complete: { label: 'Complete', icon: CheckCircle2, color: 'text-green-500', bg: 'hover:bg-green-50' },
       };
       ```
     - Column order state with localStorage persistence:
       ```tsx
       type ColumnKey = 'taskName' | 'dueDate' | 'comment' | 'frequency' | 'type' | 'link' | 'createdBy' | 'created';
       const COLUMN_ORDER_KEY = 'tasks-column-order';
       const [columnOrder, setColumnOrder] = useState<ColumnKey[]>(DEFAULT_COLUMN_ORDER);
       ```
     - Global reorder (not per-group):
       ```tsx
       const globalSorted = useMemo(() => [...filtered].sort((a, b) => a.sort_order - b.sort_order), [filtered]);
       ```
     - `renderCell` switch function renders each column type dynamically based on `columnOrder`
     - Status dropdown using Popover in each row (between reorder arrows and dynamic columns)
     - Rich text description via ReactQuill (dynamic import), shown in tooltip on task name hover with `max-h-[200px] overflow-y-auto`
     - CreatedBy column shows avatar with Tooltip for full name
     - Avatar fallback uses gradient: `bg-gradient-to-br from-[#3e8692] to-[#2d6470]` with white text
     - Profile photos use `<img>` tag (not Next.js Image) with onError fallback
     - Database has columns: `description text`, `status text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'to_do'`

   - **`components/Sidebar.tsx`** (Modified)
     - Workspace section wrapped with Andy-only check:
       ```tsx
       {userProfile?.email === 'andy@holohive.io' && (
         <>
           {/* Workspace section divider + Daily Stand-Up + Tasks links */}
         </>
       )}
       ```

   - **`app/clients/page.tsx`** (Modified)
     - Added "Upload .docx" button inside the note form (not just outside)
     - Moved hidden file `<input ref={docUploadRef}>` outside the `{!isNoteFormOpen}` conditional block so it's always in DOM

   - **`app/kols/page.tsx`** (Modified)
     - Pagination spacing: `pb-14` added to pagination div
     - Sticky columns: # column `sticky left-0 z-20`, Name column `sticky left-[48px] z-20 shadow-[2px_0_4px_-2px_rgba(0,0,0,0.1)]`
     - Border fix: Removed `border-r` from # column, added `border-l border-l-gray-200` to Name column
     - Table changed from `borderCollapse: 'collapse'` to `borderCollapse: 'separate', borderSpacing: 0`
     - Both header and body cells updated, plus skeleton table headers

   - **Database migrations applied:**
     - `add_tasks_description`: `ALTER TABLE tasks ADD COLUMN description text`
     - `add_tasks_status`: `ALTER TABLE tasks ADD COLUMN status text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'to_do' CHECK (status IN ('to_do', 'paused', 'in_progress', 'ready_for_feedback', 'complete'))`

   - **Changelog v0.1.9** created in `changelogs` table and sent to Telegram terminal chat

4. Errors and Fixes:
   - **Profile picture rendering poorly**: Next.js `<Image>` component at 28px rendered incorrectly. Fixed by switching to `<img>` tag like the team page.
   - **Type field overflow**: Adding `overflow-visible` made the badge bleed into Link column. Fixed by removing it and using `truncate` + `max-w-full` instead.
   - **Reorder not working (per-group scope)**: With only 1 task per group, both arrows were always disabled. Fixed by changing reorder to work globally across all filtered tasks using `globalSorted` memo.
   - **`title` attribute tooltip not visible**: Native `title` tooltip is too slow/subtle. Replaced with Radix Tooltip component with `delayDuration={0}`.
   - **Description gradient indicator**: User said "Get rid of the weird gradient". Replaced with text hint, then user said remove that too, then made it scrollable instead.
   - **RichTextEditor component mismatch**: User wanted it to match clients page notes editor. Replaced custom `RichTextEditor` component with direct ReactQuill dynamic import using same toolbar config and CSS wrapper as clients page.
   - **Upload .docx button not working when form open**: Hidden file `<input ref={docUploadRef}>` was inside `{!isNoteFormOpen}` block, so it wasn't in DOM when form was open. Fixed by moving it outside the conditional.
   - **KOL pagination overlapping scrollbar**: Added `pb-14` to pagination controls.
   - **Sticky column border bleed-through**: Scrolling content visible between # and Name columns due to `border-collapse: collapse`. Fixed by removing `border-r` from #, adding `border-l` to Name, and changing table to `borderCollapse: 'separate', borderSpacing: 0`.

5. Problem Solving:
   - Solved global reorder architecture: `globalSorted` useMemo computes position across all filtered tasks, `handleReorder` swaps sort_order between adjacent tasks regardless of group, arrows disabled based on global position
   - Solved column reordering: HTML5 drag-and-drop on headers, `columnOrder` state persisted to localStorage, `renderCell` switch function renders cells dynamically
   - Solved rich text in tooltip: `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` with prose styling, empty check for Quill's `<p><br></p>`
   - **Ongoing**: Sticky column border bleed-through fix (changed to `border-separate`) - build NOT yet verified

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user's primary work involved building and iterating on a **Tasks page** for the KOL Campaign Manager, then making various fixes to the **KOL page** and **Clients page**. Specific requests included:
   - Tasks page: inline editing, status tracking, rich text descriptions, reorderable rows/columns, grouped by user, profile avatars, plus button for quick-add
   - Restricting Workspace sidebar section (Tasks + Daily Stand-Up) to Andy only
   - Committing, pushing, sending Telegram changelog, creating system changelog v0.1.9
   - Fixing meeting notes .docx upload button visibility in Clients page
   - KOL page: pagination spacing, sticky Name column, fixing border bleed-through between sticky columns

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Next.js App Router with `'use client'` pages
   - Supabase MCP for database migrations and queries
   - HTML5 native drag-and-drop for column reordering (persisted to localStorage)
   - Sticky table columns with `position: sticky` and `z-index` layering
   - `border-collapse: separate` vs `collapse` for sticky column border rendering
   - React Quill (dynamic import, SSR disabled) for rich text editing
   - Tooltip component for hover previews (replacing native `title` attribute)
   - Popover-based status dropdown with colored icons
   - Telegram bot API integration for changelog notifications
   - Optimistic UI updates with rollback on error
   - Global vs per-group task reordering via `sort_order`

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **`app/tasks/page.tsx`** (Created and heavily iterated)
     - Full task management page with 3 tabs, grouped tables, inline editing, status icons, rich text descriptions
     - Key constants:
       ```tsx
       const STATUSES = ['to_do', 'in_progress', 'paused', 'ready_for_feedback', 'complete'] as const;
       const STATUS_CONFIG: Record<string, { label: string; icon: typeof Circle; color: string; bg: string }> = {
         to_do: { label: 'To Do', icon: Circle, color: 'text-gray-400', bg: 'hover:bg-gray-100' },
         in_progress: { label: 'In Progress', icon: PlayCircle, color: 'text-blue-500', bg: 'hover:bg-blue-50' },
         paused: { label: 'Paused', icon: PauseCircle, color: 'text-amber-500', bg: 'hover:bg-amber-50' },
         ready_for_feedback: { label: 'Ready for Feedback', icon: MessageCircle, color: 'text-purple-500', bg: 'hover:bg-purple-50' },
         complete: { label: 'Complete', icon: CheckCircle2, color: 'text-green-500', bg: 'hover:bg-green-50' },
       };
       ```
     - Column order state with localStorage persistence:
       ```tsx
       type ColumnKey = 'taskName' | 'dueDate' | 'comment' | 'frequency' | 'type' | 'link' | 'createdBy' | 'created';
       const COLUMN_ORDER_KEY = 'tasks-column-order';
       const [columnOrder, setColumnOrder] = useState<ColumnKey[]>(DEFAULT_COLUMN_ORDER);
       ```
     - Global reorder (not per-group):
       ```tsx
       const globalSorted = useMemo(() => [...filtered].sort((a, b) => a.sort_order - b.sort_order), [filtered]);
       ```
     - `renderCell` switch function renders each column type dynamically based on `columnOrder`
     - Status dropdown using Popover in each row (between reorder arrows and dynamic columns)
     - Rich text description via ReactQuill (dynamic import), shown in tooltip on task name hover with `max-h-[200px] overflow-y-auto`
     - CreatedBy column shows avatar with Tooltip for full name
     - Avatar fallback uses gradient: `bg-gradient-to-br from-[#3e8692] to-[#2d6470]` with white text
     - Profile photos use `<img>` tag (not Next.js Image) with onError fallback
     - Database has columns: `description text`, `status text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'to_do'`

   - **`components/Sidebar.tsx`** (Modified)
     - Workspace section wrapped with Andy-only check:

       ```tsx
       {userProfile?.email === 'andy@holohive.io' && (

           {/* Workspace section divider + Daily Stand-Up + Tasks links */}
         </>
       )}
       ```

   - **`app/clients/page.tsx`** (Modified)
     - Added "Upload .docx" button inside the note form (not just outside)
     - Moved hidden file `<input ref={docUploadRef}>` outside the `{!isNoteFormOpen}` conditional block so it's always in DOM

   - **`app/kols/page.tsx`** (Modified)
     - Pagination spacing: `pb-14` added to pagination div
     - Sticky columns: # column `sticky left-0 z-20`, Name column `sticky left-[48px] z-20 shadow-[2px_0_4px_-2px_rgba(0,0,0,0.1)]`
     - Border fix: Removed `border-r` from # column, added `border-l border-l-gray-200` to Name column

   - **Database migrations applied:**
     - `add_tasks_description`: `ALTER TABLE tasks ADD COLUMN description text`
     - `add_tasks_status`: `ALTER TABLE tasks ADD COLUMN status text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'to_do' CHECK (status IN ('to_do', 'paused', 'in_progress', 'ready_for_feedback', 'complete'))`

   - **Changelog v0.1.9** created in `changelogs` table and sent to Telegram terminal chat

4. Errors and Fixes:
   - **Profile picture rendering poorly**: Next.js `<Image>` component at 28px rendered incorrectly. Fixed by switching to `<img>` tag like the team page.
   - **Type field overflow**: Adding `overflow-visible` made the badge bleed into Link column. Fixed by removing it and using `truncate` + `max-w-full` instead.
   - **Reorder not working (per-group scope)**: With only 1 task per group, both arrows were always disabled. Fixed by changing reorder to work globally across all filtered tasks using `globalSorted` memo.
   - **`title` attribute tooltip not visible**: Native `title` tooltip is too slow/subtle. Replaced with Radix Tooltip component with `delayDuration={0}`.
   - **Description gradient indicator**: User said "Get rid of the weird gradient". Replaced with text hint, then user said remove that too, then made it scrollable instead.
   - **RichTextEditor component mismatch**: User wanted it to match clients page notes editor. Replaced custom `RichTextEditor` component with direct ReactQuill dynamic import using same toolbar config and CSS wrapper as clients page.
   - **Upload .docx button not working when form open**: Hidden file `<input ref={docUploadRef}>` was inside `{!isNoteFormOpen}` block, so it wasn't in DOM when form was open. Fixed by moving it outside the conditional.
   - **KOL pagination overlapping scrollbar**: Added `pb-14` to pagination controls.
   - **Sticky column border bleed-through**: Scrolling content visible between # and Name columns due to `border-collapse: collapse`. Fixed by removing `border-r` from #, adding `border-l` to Name, and changing table to `borderCollapse: 'separate', borderSpacing: 0`.

5. Problem Solving:
   - Solved global reorder architecture: `globalSorted` useMemo computes position across all filtered tasks, `handleReorder` swaps sort_order between adjacent tasks regardless of group, arrows disabled based on global position
   - Solved column reordering: HTML5 drag-and-drop on headers, `columnOrder` state persisted to localStorage, `renderCell` switch function renders cells dynamically
   - Solved rich text in tooltip: `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` with prose styling, empty check for Quill's `<p><br></p>`
   - **Ongoing**: Sticky column border bleed-through fix (changed to `border-separate`) - build NOT yet verified

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
I can see the issue. With `border-collapse: separate`, the borders between cells aren't shared anymore. The # column has `border-r-0` and the Name column has `border-l border-l-gray-200`. But the problem is that when content scrolls behind the sticky columns, the gap between them can show the scrolling content.

The key issue is that the # column needs a solid background, and the Name column also needs a solid background. Both already have backgrounds set (`bg-gray-50` for headers, and conditional `bg-white`/`bg-gray-50` for body cells). 

The real fix needs to ensure there's no transparent gap between the two sticky columns. 

I'm wondering if the # column's 48px width is actually covering its full area, or if there's a small gap forming between the cells that's letting content peek through. I should check how the skeleton and loading state headers are styled too.
</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-02-18 16:19:12Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
I see the issue. The `Table` component wraps the `<table>` in a `<div className="relative w-full overflow-auto">`. But the outer container at line 2336 also has `overflow-auto`. So there's a double overflow wrapper, which may cause issues.

But more importantly, the real problem with the border bleed-through between sticky columns is that with `border-collapse: separate`, there's no gap between cells (since `border-spacing: 0`), so the border should work. But the # column has `border-r-0` (removing the right border), and the Name column has `border-l border-l-gray-200`. 


Actually, I think the core issue is that the scrolling container is the inner div from the Table component, not the outer one at line 2336. When the # and Name columns are both sticky with backgrounds and shadows, the scrolling content passes behind them, but the borders don't align properly because each cell maintains its own individual borders with `border-collapse: separate`. The # column might not be rendering at exactly 48px in the header, creating a misalignment with the body cells and leaving a gap where the Name column's `left-[48px]` positioning doesn't fully cover the scrolling content underneath. I think the most reliable solution is to enforce an exact width on the # column in both header and body cells using min and max width constraints, remove the borders between the columns entirely, and rely on the existing shadow on the Name column to create visual separation. I need to fix the line disappearing during scroll by using box-shadow instead of borders—I'll set the # column to a fixed 48px width and add a shadow-based border to the Name column that creates the visual separator without relying on border-collapse behavior. Defining the sticky column styles...
</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-02-18 16:19:14Z)**_

Now I understand the issue. The border between `#` and `Name` bleeds through because standard CSS borders don't render reliably between sticky cells. The fix is to use `box-shadow` to simulate the border on the Name column's left side, which sticks with the element. I also need to enforce an exact width on the `#` column.
