```text
  1529→  <div class="callout callout-info">
  1530→    <strong>Audit Fix: Split by Account</strong>
  1531→    "DMs Sent = 73" &mdash; from which account? The dashboard adds <code>dm_account</code> as a filter dimension so the team can compare Path A vs Path B performance separately. Each metric card can be toggled to show totals or per-account breakdown.
  1532→  </div>
  1533→
  1534→  <h3>No-Show &amp; Contract Alerts</h3>
  1535→  <table>
  1536→    <thead>
  1537→      <tr>
  1538→        <th>Alert</th>
  1539→        <th>Trigger</th>
  1540→        <th>Action</th>
  1541→      </tr>
  1542→    </thead>
  1543→    <tbody>
  1544→      <tr>
  1545→        <td><strong>No-Show</strong></td>
  1546→        <td>Booked lead doesn't show up for discovery call</td>
  1547→        <td>3-day reschedule window (auto-task). Day 3 no response &rarr; Orbit (reason: ghost). Field: <code>no_show</code> flag.</td>
  1548→      </tr>
  1549→      <tr>
  1550→        <td><strong>Contract Unsigned</strong></td>
  1551→        <td>Contract sent but not signed after 3 days</td>
  1552→        <td>Alert Closer + Founder. Auto-task to follow up. Fields: <code>contract_sent_date</code>, <code>contract_signed_date</code>.</td>
  1553→      </tr>
  1554→    </tbody>
  1555→  </table>
  1556→
  1557→  <h3>Weekly Rhythm Report (Auto-generated every Monday)</h3>
  1558→  <p>The playbook defines a weekly rhythm (Section 16). Every Monday morning, the CRM auto-generates a report sent to the team TG channel:</p>
  1559→  <ul>
  1560→    <li><strong>Last week:</strong> Deals closed, revenue, conversion rates, DMs sent (by account), meetings run</li>
  1561→    <li><strong>This week:</strong> Scheduled calls, proposals due, nurture touches queued, follow-ups overdue</li>
  1562→    <li><strong>Alerts:</strong> Stale leads (no contact in 7+ days), BAMFAM violations, no-shows pending reschedule, unsigned contracts, Bucket B leads ready for re-assessment</li>
  1563→  </ul>
  1564→</div>
  1565→
  1566→<!-- SECTION 8: AI INTEGRATION -->
  1567→<div class="section" id="section-8">
  1568→  <div class="section-header">
  1569→    <div class="section-num">08</div>
  1570→    <div>
  1571→      <div class="section-title">AI Integration</div>
  1572→      <div class="section-subtitle">Making Holo GPT the playbook's co-pilot</div>
  1573→    </div>
  1574→  </div>
  1575→
  1576→  <p>We already have Holo GPT with agent orchestration. Here's how we level it up with playbook knowledge:</p>
  1577→
  1578→  <div class="card-grid">
  1579→    <div class="card">
  1580→      <div class="card-header">
  1581→        <div class="card-icon" style="background: var(--accent-light); color: var(--accent);">&#129302;</div>
  1582→        <div class="card-title">Pre-Call Research Agent</div>
  1583→      </div>
  1584→      <p>Before a discovery call, Holo GPT auto-generates the pre-call prep doc: project basics (from CRM data), token data (from external APIs), competitor Korea presence, 2-3 suggested insight drops, and recommended bucket prediction.</p>
  1585→    </div>
  1586→
  1587→    <div class="card">
  1588→      <div class="card-header">
  1589→        <div class="card-icon" style="background: var(--green-light); color: var(--green);">&#128172;</div>
  1590→        <div class="card-title">Message Composer</div>
  1591→      </div>
  1592→      <p>Given the opportunity context and playbook stage, AI drafts personalized messages using the playbook templates as a base &mdash; filling in project name, competitor intel, market data. Phil reviews and sends.</p>
  1593→    </div>
  1594→
  1595→    <div class="card">
  1596→      <div class="card-header">
  1597→        <div class="card-icon" style="background: var(--amber-light); color: var(--amber);">&#128200;</div>
  1598→        <div class="card-title">Bucket Scoring Engine</div>
  1599→      </div>
```

```text
  1725→<div class="section" id="section-10">
  1726→  <div class="section-header">

  1728→    <div>
  1729→      <div class="section-title">Implementation Roadmap</div>
  1730→      <div class="section-subtitle">Four phases (1a, 1b, 2, 3), incremental value at each step</div>
  1731→    </div>
  1732→  </div>
  1733→
  1734→  <div class="timeline">
  1735→    <div class="timeline-item">
  1736→      <div class="timeline-dot"></div>
  1737→      <div class="timeline-label">Phase 1a &mdash; Correct Pipeline + Core Fields</div>
  1738→      <div class="timeline-title">Two-Path Routing + Stages + Cold Sequence</div>
  1739→      <div class="timeline-desc">
  1740→        <ul style="margin-top: 8px; font-size: 13px; color: var(--gray-500);">
  1741→          <li>Add <code>dm_account</code>, <code>bump_number</code>, <code>last_bump_date</code>, <code>warm_sub_state</code>, <code>tg_handle</code>, <code>calendly_sent_via/date</code>, <code>gc_opened</code>, <code>dedup_key</code> to opportunities</li>
  1742→          <li>Implement correct pipeline stages: Cold DM &rarr; Warm (Interested/Silent) &rarr; TG (Path B) &rarr; Booked &rarr; Discovery Done &rarr; Proposal Sent/Call &rarr; Contract &rarr; Closed Won</li>
  1743→          <li>Add Orbit exit lane with <code>orbit_reason</code> and Closed Lost with <code>closed_lost_reason</code></li>
  1744→          <li>Add bucket field + temperature score to opportunities</li>
  1745→          <li>Build cold outreach 5-bump sequence (highest-volume automation)</li>
  1746→          <li>Build <code>crm_activities</code> table and activity timeline UI</li>
  1747→          <li>Add BAMFAM next_meeting tracking with warning badges</li>
  1748→          <li>Add decision-maker flag to contacts</li>
  1749→          <li>Dedup check on lead creation (project name + handle)</li>
  1750→        </ul>
  1751→        <p style="margin-top: 8px;"><strong>Value delivered:</strong> Pipeline accurately reflects the two-path reality. Cold outreach is tracked and automated. Duplicates are prevented. Every opportunity has a bucket, activity history, and BAMFAM compliance.</p>
  1752→      </div>
  1753→    </div>
  1754→
  1755→    <div class="timeline-item">
  1756→      <div class="timeline-dot" style="background: #6366F1; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px #6366F1;"></div>
  1757→      <div class="timeline-label" style="color: #6366F1;">Phase 1b &mdash; Calendly + Orbit + Templates</div>
  1758→      <div class="timeline-title">Complete the Foundation</div>
  1759→      <div class="timeline-desc">
  1760→        <ul style="margin-top: 8px; font-size: 13px; color: var(--gray-500);">
  1761→          <li>Calendly webhook integration (<code>invitee.created</code> &rarr; auto-move to Booked)</li>
  1762→          <li>TG booking nudge sequence (Path B: Day 2, 5, 10, 14 &rarr; Orbit)</li>
  1763→          <li>Full Orbit system: 5 reasons, two cadences (45d/60d), resurrection flow</li>
  1764→          <li>No-show tracking (<code>no_show</code> flag, 3-day reschedule window)</li>
  1765→          <li>Contract alerts (<code>contract_sent_date</code>, <code>contract_signed_date</code>, 3-day unsigned alert)</li>
  1766→          <li>Link message templates to stages + buckets + <code>dm_account</code> for path-specific surfacing</li>
  1767→          <li>Build pre-call prep checklist form</li>
  1768→          <li>Dashboard: split metrics by <code>dm_account</code> dimension</li>
  1769→        </ul>
  1770→        <p style="margin-top: 8px;"><strong>Value delivered:</strong> Calendly auto-updates pipeline. Orbit nurtures cold leads long-term. Templates surface at the right stage for the right path. No-shows and stale contracts are flagged automatically.</p>
  1771→      </div>
  1772→    </div>
  1773→

  1794→      <div class="timeline-title">AI Co-pilot + Trigger Monitoring + Proposal Generator</div>
  1795→      <div class="timeline-desc">
  1796→        <ul style="margin-top: 8px; font-size: 13px; color: var(--gray-500);">

  1798→          <li>Auto-generate pre-call research docs via AI</li>

  1806→      </div>
  1807→    </div>
  1808→  </div>
  1809→
  1810→  <h3>Summary: Before &amp; After</h3>
  1811→  <div class="compare-grid">
  1812→    <div class="compare-col">
  1813→      <div class="compare-header current">Before (Playbook as Document)</div>
  1814→      <div class="compare-body">
  1815→        <ul>
  1816→          <li>Team reads 17-section PDF and tries to remember</li>
  1817→          <li>Follow-ups tracked in heads or scattered notes</li>
  1818→          <li>No visibility into DM volume, response rates, or meeting counts</li>
  1819→          <li>Proposals built from scratch each time</li>
  1820→          <li>Bucket classification is a mental exercise</li>
  1821→          <li>Trigger events noticed by chance</li>
  1822→          <li>BAMFAM is aspirational, not enforced</li>
  1823→          <li>Weekly rhythm depends on discipline</li>
  1824→          <li>No way to measure playbook compliance</li>
  1825→        </ul>
  1826→      </div>
  1827→    </div>
  1828→    <div class="compare-col">
  1829→      <div class="compare-header proposed">After (Playbook in CRM)</div>
  1830→      <div class="compare-body">
  1831→        <ul>
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-02-20 13:52:19Z)**_

Here's a brief implementation breakdown based on the document:

---

**Phase 1a — Foundation (Start here)**

1. **Database migrations first** — Add all the new fields to `crm_opportunities` (dm_account, bump_number, warm_sub_state, tg_handle, calendly fields, orbit_reason, dedup_key, etc.) + create `crm_activities` and `crm_sequences`/`crm_sequence_steps` tables
2. **Pipeline stage enum update** — Replace current stages (new/contacted/qualified/etc.) with the corrected flow (Cold DM → Warm → TG → Booked → Discovery Done → Proposal Sent → Proposal Call → Contract → Closed Won + Orbit/Closed Lost exits)
3. **Pipeline UI update** — Update the CRM pipeline page to render new stages, show bucket badges (A/B/C), and support the two-path routing (dm_account selector on opportunity creation)
4. **Activity timeline component** — Build the per-opportunity activity log + BAMFAM next_meeting tracking with warning badges
5. **Cold outreach sequence** — Build the 5-bump automation (cron Edge Function that checks `bump_number` + `last_bump_date`, creates tasks when due)
6. **Dedup check** — Add a unique constraint or check on lead creation (project name + handle combo)

**Phase 1b — Complete Foundation**

7. **Calendly webhook** — Edge Function to receive `invitee.created`, match to opportunity, auto-move to Booked
8. **TG nudge sequence** — Path B only, Day 2/5/10/14 nudge cadence
9. **Orbit system** — Orbit reason selector, cadence config (45d/60d), resurrection back to pipeline
10. **Template linking** — Add stage/bucket/dm_account columns to `message_templates`, build UI to surface right template at right time
11. **No-show + contract alerts** — Flag fields + auto-task creation

**Phase 2 — Automation**

12. **Full sequence engine** — Generic cron runner for all 7 sequence types
13. **Sales Dashboard page** — New route with Phil/Jdot metrics, pipeline summary, Path A vs B filter
14. **Weekly report** — Edge Function on Monday cron → TG channel
15. **Proposal tracker** — `crm_proposals` table + basic UI

**Phase 3 — AI**

16. **New Holo GPT agent tools** — Pre-call research, message composer, bucket scorer, proposal generator, trigger scanner

---

**Practically, the order of work would be:**
- Start with **database migrations** (everything downstream depends on the schema)
- Then **pipeline stage updates** (UI + enum) since it's the most visible change
- Then **activity log + BAMFAM** (gives immediate value)
- Then **cold outreach automation** (highest-volume workflow)
- Everything else layers on incrementally

Want me to start implementing Phase 1a, or would you like to drill into any specific part first?

---

```text
Perfect! Now let me compile all my findings into a comprehensive summary:

## Detailed Summary of Current CRM System

Based on my thorough exploration of the codebase, here's what currently exists in the KOL Campaign Manager CRM system:

### 1. CURRENT PIPELINE STAGES

**Unified Opportunity Pipeline** - The system treats leads and deals as a single unified entity with multiple stage progressions:

**Lead Stages:**
- `new` - Initial leads
- `contacted` - Have been reached out to
- `qualified` - Qualified for follow-up
- `unqualified` - Not a fit
- `nurture` - Ongoing nurture
- `dead` - Lost/dead leads

**Deal Stages:**
- `deal_qualified` - Qualified to deal stage
- `proposal` - Proposal sent
- `negotiation` - Active negotiation
- `contract` - In contract review
- `closed_won` - Won deals
- `closed_lost` - Lost deals

**Account Stages:** (for existing customers)
- `account_active` - Active accounts
- `account_at_risk` - At risk of churn
- `account_churned` - Churned/inactive

**File locations:**
- Type definitions: `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/crmService.ts` (lines 8-11)
- Pipeline page: `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/crm/pipeline/page.tsx`

### 2. DATABASE TABLES (CRM-RELATED)

**Core CRM Tables:**
- `crm_opportunities` - Main sales pipeline (opportunities/deals)
- `crm_affiliates` - KOLs, referrers with commission/rev-share
- `crm_partners` - BD relationships, cross-partnerships
- `crm_contacts` - Individual people linked to opportunities
- `crm_contact_links` - Junction table linking contacts to opportunities/partners/affiliates
- `crm_stage_history` - Audit trail of all stage changes for analytics

**Campaign/KOL-Related Tables:**
- `campaigns` - Campaign records
- `campaign_kols` - KOLs in campaigns
- `campaign_templates` - Campaign templates for reuse
- `campaign_reports` - Campaign report metadata
- `campaign_report_files` - Files attached to campaign reports
- `contents` - Content deliverables (social media posts, etc.)
- `master_kols` - Master KOL database

**Message/Communication Tables:**
- `message_templates` - Message templates for communication
- `chat_sessions` - Chat conversation sessions
- `chat_messages` - Individual chat messages

**Supporting Tables:**
- `clients` - Client/brand information
- `users` - Team members
- `payments` - Payment tracking

**File location:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/sql/migrations/022_create_crm_tables.sql`

### 3. CURRENT CRM UI FEATURES

**Pipeline Page** (`/app/crm/pipeline/`)
- **Multiple View Modes:**
  - Kanban board view (drag-and-drop between stages)
  - Table view (spreadsheet-like)
- **Tabs:**
  - Outreach tab (cold outreach leads)
  - Leads tab (new → dead stages)
  - Deals tab (deal_qualified → closed_lost stages)
  - Accounts tab (account_active, account_churned)
- **Features:**
  - Drag-and-drop between stages with position tracking
  - Create new opportunities inline or via dialog
  - Edit opportunity details (name, stage, deal value, source, etc.)
  - Link contacts/affiliates to opportunities
  - View stage history timeline
  - Bulk operations (select multiple)
  - Search and filter (by source, account type, affiliate)
  - Sort options
  - Metrics dashboard (total leads, deals, values, conversion rates)
  - Stage collapse/expand
  - Add and manage contact links
  - Convert leads to deals
  - Convert deals to accounts
  - Delete opportunities (with confirmation)

**Contacts Page** (`/app/crm/contacts/`)
- Create/edit/delete contacts
- Link contacts to opportunities, partners, and affiliates
- Filter by category and link status
- View linked entities for each contact
- Card and table view modes
- Search functionality

**Network Page** (`/app/crm/network/`)
- **Partners Tab:**
  - CRUD for partners
  - Category: service_provider, investor_vc, project, individual
  - Link to affiliates and contacts
  - Track status (active/inactive)
  - Contact POC management
  - Stage history
- **Affiliates Tab:**
  - CRUD for affiliates (KOLs, referrers)
  - Commission model and rate tracking
  - Affiliation types (kol_affiliate, apac_affiliate, general_affiliate)
  - Terms of interest (mutual_deal_flow, referral, portco_support)
  - Link to partners and contacts
  - Status tracking (new, active, inactive)

**Telegram Page** (`/app/crm/telegram/`)
- Track Telegram group chats
- Link chats to opportunities and KOLs
- Message history tracking
- Commands for Telegram bot integration
- Current implementation has sample data for demo

### 4. EXISTING AUTOMATION & INTEGRATIONS

**CRM Service Methods** (in `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/crmService.ts`):
- `getOpportunityMetrics()` - Calculates conversion rates, total values, won value
- `sendTelegramMessage()` - Send messages via Telegram
- `getStaleOpportunities(daysThreshold)` - Find opportunities not contacted in N days
- `updateLastContacted()` - Track last contact datetime
- `recordStageHistory()` - Automatic audit trail of stage changes
- Position-based ordering for drag-and-drop

**AI Integration** (in `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/aiService.ts`):
- AI response generation with fallback to rule-based responses
- Campaign suggestion AI
- List suggestion AI
- Message drafting assistance
- Budget calculation suggestions

**No Current Features:**
- No cron jobs or scheduled automation
- No sequences/workflows (this is empty)
- No email automation
- No API integrations beyond Telegram

### 5. CURRENT TEMPLATE SYSTEM

**Message Templates Table:**
- `message_templates` - Stores reusable message templates
- Fields: `category`, `content`, `name`, `target_audience`, `tone`, `tags`, `usage_count`, `created_at`, `updated_at`
- Used in chat and message composition

**Campaign Templates:**
- `campaign_templates` - Templates for campaign creation
- Can be cloned to create new campaigns
- Includes: budget, dates, KOL selections, region mappings
- Usage count tracking
- Public/private visibility

**Message Training:**
- `messageTrainingService.ts` - Service for learning from message interactions
- Can be used to improve AI message suggestions

### 6. CURRENT DASHBOARD/METRICS

**Pipeline Metrics** (calculated in real-time):
- Total leads count
- Total deals count
- Total pipeline value (USD)
- Total won value
- Conversion rate (qualified to won)
- Metrics updated when data changes

**Campaign Reporting:**
- `campaign_reports` - Metadata about reports
- `campaign_report_files` - Uploaded report files with:
  - Public/private visibility
  - File tracking (name, size, type, URL)
  - Display ordering
  - Uploader tracking
  - Sharing links

**No Dashboard UI Currently:**
- No dedicated analytics/metrics dashboard page
- Metrics are only shown on Pipeline page
- No KPI tracking or forecasting
- No stage velocity analytics (though stage_history exists for this)
- No funnel visualization

### 7. ADDITIONAL FEATURES

**Opportunity Fields:**
- `client_id` - Link to client/brand for account opportunities
- `scope` - Account scope type (fundraising, advisory, kol_activation, gtm, bd_partnerships, apac)
- `gc` - Telegram group chat link
- `last_contacted_at` - When last contacted
- `last_message_at` - When lead messaged (in TG)
- `last_reply_at` - When bot replied (in TG)
- `last_team_message_at` - When team member messaged (in TG)
- `position` - Custom sort order (for drag-drop)
- `qualified_at` - Timestamp when moved to deal stage
- `closed_at` - Timestamp when won/lost

**Row Level Security (RLS):**
- All CRM tables have RLS enabled
- Users can view all CRM data
- Users can create/edit own records or admins can edit all
- Stage history is read-only (audit trail)

**Recent Migrations:**
- Dec 11, 2024: Added position column for ordering
- Dec 5, 2024: Added campaigns RLS policies
- Dec 3, 2024: Created CRM tables
- Jan 21, 2025: Added client logo and slug fields
- Jan 21, 2025: Added last_team_message_at field

This is a fairly complete CRM system for pipeline management but lacks advanced automation, sequences, and comprehensive analytics dashboarding.
```

```text
     1→-- Add logo_url field to clients table for client branding
     2→ALTER TABLE clients ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS logo_url TEXT;
     3→
     4→-- Add comment
     5→COMMENT ON COLUMN clients.logo_url IS 'URL to client logo stored in Supabase storage';
```

```text
     2→ALTER TABLE clients ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS slug VARCHAR(100) UNIQUE;
     3→
     4→-- Add index for faster slug lookups
     5→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_clients_slug ON clients(slug);
     6→
     7→-- Add comment
     8→COMMENT ON COLUMN clients.slug IS 'URL-friendly slug for client portal access';
     9→
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./supabase/migrations/20250131_create_campaign_reports.sql`

```text
     1→-- Create campaign_reports table
     2→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.campaign_reports (
     3→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
     4→    campaign_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.campaigns(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
     5→    custom_message TEXT,
     6→    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT timezone('utc'::text, now()) NOT NULL,
     7→    updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT timezone('utc'::text, now()) NOT NULL
     8→);
     9→
    10→-- Create campaign_report_files table
    11→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.campaign_report_files (
    12→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    13→    campaign_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.campaigns(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    14→    file_name TEXT NOT NULL,
    15→    file_url TEXT NOT NULL,
    16→    file_type TEXT NOT NULL,
    17→    file_size BIGINT NOT NULL,
    18→    is_public BOOLEAN DEFAULT false NOT NULL,
    19→    uploaded_by UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
    20→    display_order INTEGER DEFAULT 0 NOT NULL,
    21→    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT timezone('utc'::text, now()) NOT NULL
    22→);
    23→
    24→-- Add columns to campaigns table
    25→ALTER TABLE public.campaigns
    26→ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS share_report_publicly BOOLEAN DEFAULT false,
    27→ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS report_share_link TEXT;
    28→
    29→-- Create indexes for better performance
    30→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_campaign_reports_campaign_id ON public.campaign_reports(campaign_id);
    31→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_campaign_report_files_campaign_id ON public.campaign_report_files(campaign_id);
    32→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_campaign_report_files_is_public ON public.campaign_report_files(is_public);
    33→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_campaign_report_files_display_order ON public.campaign_report_files(display_order);
    34→
    35→-- Enable Row Level Security
    36→ALTER TABLE public.campaign_reports ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
    37→ALTER TABLE public.campaign_report_files ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
    38→
    39→-- RLS Policies for campaign_reports
    40→CREATE POLICY "Users can view reports for their campaigns" ON public.campaign_reports
    41→    FOR SELECT USING (
    42→        EXISTS (
    43→            SELECT 1 FROM public.campaigns
    44→            WHERE campaigns.id = campaign_reports.campaign_id
    45→        )
    46→    );
    47→
    48→CREATE POLICY "Users can insert reports for their campaigns" ON public.campaign_reports
    49→    FOR INSERT WITH CHECK (
    50→        EXISTS (
    51→            SELECT 1 FROM public.campaigns
    52→            WHERE campaigns.id = campaign_reports.campaign_id
    53→        )
    54→    );
    55→
    56→CREATE POLICY "Users can update reports for their campaigns" ON public.campaign_reports
    57→    FOR UPDATE USING (
    58→        EXISTS (
    59→            SELECT 1 FROM public.campaigns
    60→            WHERE campaigns.id = campaign_reports.campaign_id
    61→        )
    62→    );
    63→
    64→-- RLS Policies for campaign_report_files
    65→CREATE POLICY "Users can view files for their campaigns" ON public.campaign_report_files
    66→    FOR SELECT USING (
    67→        EXISTS (
    68→            SELECT 1 FROM public.campaigns
    69→            WHERE campaigns.id = campaign_report_files.campaign_id
    70→        )
    71→        OR is_public = true
    72→    );
    73→
    74→CREATE POLICY "Authenticated users can insert files" ON public.campaign_report_files
    75→    FOR INSERT WITH CHECK (
    76→        auth.uid() IS NOT NULL
    77→        AND EXISTS (
    78→            SELECT 1 FROM public.campaigns
    79→            WHERE campaigns.id = campaign_report_files.campaign_id
    80→        )
    81→    );
    82→
    83→CREATE POLICY "Users can update their campaign files" ON public.campaign_report_files
    84→    FOR UPDATE USING (
    85→        EXISTS (
    86→            SELECT 1 FROM public.campaigns
    87→            WHERE campaigns.id = campaign_report_files.campaign_id
    88→        )
    89→    );
    90→
    91→CREATE POLICY "Users can delete their campaign files" ON public.campaign_report_files
    92→    FOR DELETE USING (
    93→        EXISTS (
    94→            SELECT 1 FROM public.campaigns
    95→            WHERE campaigns.id = campaign_report_files.campaign_id
    96→        )
    97→    );
    98→
    99→-- Create updated_at trigger for campaign_reports
   100→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.update_campaign_reports_updated_at()
   101→RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
   102→BEGIN
   103→    NEW.updated_at = timezone('utc'::text, now());
   104→    RETURN NEW;
   105→END;
   106→$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
   107→
   108→CREATE TRIGGER update_campaign_reports_updated_at
   109→    BEFORE UPDATE ON public.campaign_reports
   110→    FOR EACH ROW
   111→    EXECUTE FUNCTION public.update_campaign_reports_updated_at();
   112→
   113→-- Grant permissions
   114→GRANT ALL ON public.campaign_reports TO authenticated;
   115→GRANT ALL ON public.campaign_report_files TO authenticated;
   116→GRANT SELECT ON public.campaign_report_files TO anon;
   117→
```

```text
     1→import { supabase } from './supabase';

     3→// ============================================
     4→// TypeScript Types for CRM Entities
     5→// ============================================
     6→
     7→// Opportunity Stages
     8→export type OpportunityStage =
     9→  | 'new' | 'contacted' | 'qualified' | 'unqualified' | 'nurture' | 'dead'  // Lead stages
    10→  | 'deal_qualified' | 'proposal' | 'negotiation' | 'contract' | 'closed_won' | 'closed_lost'  // Deal stages
    11→  | 'account_active' | 'account_at_risk' | 'account_churned';  // Account stages
    12→
    13→export type AccountType = 'general' | 'channel' | 'campaign' | 'lite' | 'ad_hoc';
    14→export type OpportunitySource = 'referral' | 'inbound' | 'event' | 'cold_outreach';
    15→
    16→export type AffiliateStatus = 'new' | 'active' | 'inactive';
    17→export type PartnerStatus = 'active' | 'inactive';
    18→export type PartnerCategory = 'service_provider' | 'investor_vc' | 'project' | 'individual';
    19→
    20→export type LinkedType = 'opportunity' | 'partner' | 'affiliate';
    21→
    22→// ============================================
    23→// Interfaces
    24→// ============================================
    25→
    26→export interface CRMAffiliate {
    27→  id: string;
    28→  name: string;
    29→  affiliation: string | null;
    30→  category: string | null;
    31→  status: AffiliateStatus;
    32→  commission_model: string | null;
    33→  commission_rate: number | null;
    34→  terms_of_interest: string | null;
    35→  last_contacted_at: string | null;
    36→  owner_id: string | null;
    37→  poc_name: string | null;
    38→  poc_email: string | null;
    39→  poc_telegram: string | null;
    40→  notes: string | null;
    41→  created_at: string;
    42→  updated_at: string;
    43→}
    44→
    45→export interface CRMOpportunity {
    46→  id: string;
    47→  name: string;
    48→  stage: OpportunityStage;
    49→  account_type: AccountType | null;
    50→  deal_value: number | null;
    51→  currency: string;
    52→  last_contacted_at: string | null;
    53→  last_message_at: string | null;       // When lead/others last messaged in TG group
    54→  last_reply_at: string | null;          // When our bot last messaged in TG group
    55→  last_team_message_at: string | null;   // When a team member last messaged in TG group
    56→  owner_id: string | null;
    57→  source: OpportunitySource | null;
    58→  referrer: string | null;
    59→  gc: string | null;               // Telegram group chat ID
    60→  affiliate_id: string | null;
    61→  client_id: string | null;        // Links account-stage opportunities to clients
    62→  scope: string | null;            // Scope for accounts: fundraising, advisory, kol_activation, gtm, bd_partnerships, apac
    63→  notes: string | null;
    64→  position: number;
    65→  created_at: string;
    66→  updated_at: string;
    67→  qualified_at: string | null;
    68→  closed_at: string | null;
    69→  // Joined data
    70→  affiliate?: CRMAffiliate | null;
    71→  contacts?: CRMContact[];
    72→  client?: { id: string; name: string } | null;
    73→}
    74→
    75→export interface CRMPartner {
    76→  id: string;
    77→  name: string;
    78→  category: PartnerCategory | null;
    79→  focus: string | null;
    80→  status: PartnerStatus;
    81→  last_contacted_at: string | null;
    82→  owner_id: string | null;
    83→  poc_name: string | null;
    84→  poc_email: string | null;
    85→  poc_telegram: string | null;
    86→  is_affiliate: boolean;
    87→  affiliate_id: string | null;
    88→  notes: string | null;
    89→  created_at: string;
    90→  updated_at: string;
    91→  // Joined data

    93→  contacts?: CRMContact[];
    94→}
    95→
    96→export interface CRMContact {

    99→  email: string | null;
   100→  telegram_id: string | null;
```

```text
   200→export class CRMService {
   201→  // ----------------------------------------
   202→  // AFFILIATES
   203→  // ----------------------------------------
   204→
   205→  static async getAllAffiliates(): Promise<CRMAffiliate[]> {
   206→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   207→      .from('crm_affiliates')
   208→      .select('*')
   209→      .order('created_at', { ascending: false });
   210→
   211→    if (error) throw error;
   212→    return data || [];
   213→  }
   214→
   215→  static async getAffiliateById(id: string): Promise<CRMAffiliate | null> {
   216→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   217→      .from('crm_affiliates')
   218→      .select('*')
   219→      .eq('id', id)
   220→      .single();
   221→
   222→    if (error) throw error;
   223→    return data;
   224→  }
   225→
   226→  static async createAffiliate(affiliateData: CreateAffiliateData): Promise<CRMAffiliate> {
   227→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   228→      .from('crm_affiliates')
   229→      .insert([affiliateData])
   230→      .select()
   231→      .single();
   232→
   233→    if (error) throw error;
   234→
   235→    // Record stage history
   236→    await this.recordStageHistory('affiliate', data.id, null, affiliateData.status || 'new');
   237→
   238→    return data;
   239→  }
   240→
   241→  static async updateAffiliate(id: string, updates: Partial<CreateAffiliateData>): Promise<CRMAffiliate> {
   242→    // Get current state for stage history
   243→    const current = await this.getAffiliateById(id);
   244→
   245→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   246→      .from('crm_affiliates')
   247→      .update({ ...updates, updated_at: new Date().toISOString() })
   248→      .eq('id', id)
   249→      .select()
   250→      .single();
   251→
   252→    if (error) throw error;
   253→
   254→    // Record stage change if status changed
   255→    if (updates.status && current && current.status !== updates.status) {
   256→      await this.recordStageHistory('affiliate', id, current.status, updates.status);
   257→    }
   258→
   259→    return data;
   260→  }
   261→
   262→  static async deleteAffiliate(id: string): Promise<void> {
   263→    const { error } = await supabase
   264→      .from('crm_affiliates')
   265→      .delete()
   266→      .eq('id', id);
   267→
   268→    if (error) throw error;
   269→  }
   270→
   271→  // ----------------------------------------
   272→  // OPPORTUNITIES
   273→  // ----------------------------------------
   274→
   275→  static async getAllOpportunities(): Promise<CRMOpportunity[]> {
   276→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   277→      .from('crm_opportunities')
   278→      .select(`
   279→        *,
   280→        affiliate:crm_affiliates(*),
   281→        client:clients!crm_opportunities_client_id_fkey(id, name)
   282→      `)
   283→      .order('position', { ascending: true })
   284→      .order('created_at', { ascending: false });
   285→
   286→    if (error) {
   287→      console.error('Error fetching opportunities:', error);
   288→      throw error;
   289→    }
   290→    return data || [];
   291→  }
   292→
   293→  static async getOpportunitiesByStage(stages: OpportunityStage[]): Promise<CRMOpportunity[]> {
   294→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   295→      .from('crm_opportunities')
   296→      .select(`
   297→        *,
   298→        affiliate:crm_affiliates(*),
   299→        client:clients!crm_opportunities_client_id_fkey(id, name)
   300→      `)
   301→      .in('stage', stages)
   302→      .order('created_at', { ascending: false });
   303→
   304→    if (error) throw error;
   305→    return data || [];
   306→  }
   307→
   308→  static async getOpportunityById(id: string): Promise<CRMOpportunity | null> {
   309→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   310→      .from('crm_opportunities')
   311→      .select(`
   312→        *,
   313→        affiliate:crm_affiliates(*),
   314→        client:clients!crm_opportunities_client_id_fkey(id, name)
   315→      `)
   316→      .eq('id', id)
   317→      .single();
   318→
   319→    if (error) throw error;
   320→    return data;
   321→  }
   322→
   323→  static async createOpportunity(opportunityData: CreateOpportunityData): Promise<CRMOpportunity> {
   324→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   325→      .from('crm_opportunities')
   326→      .insert([opportunityData])
   327→      .select()
   328→      .single();
   329→
   330→    if (error) throw error;
   331→
   332→    // Record stage history
   333→    await this.recordStageHistory('opportunity', data.id, null, opportunityData.stage || 'new');
   334→
   335→    return data;
   336→  }
   337→
   338→  static async updateOpportunity(id: string, updates: Partial<CreateOpportunityData>): Promise<CRMOpportunity> {
   339→    // Get current state for stage history
   340→    const current = await this.getOpportunityById(id);
   341→
   342→    const updateData: any = { ...updates, updated_at: new Date().toISOString() };
   343→
   344→    // Set qualified_at when moving to deal stages
   345→    if (updates.stage && ['proposal', 'contract', 'closed_won', 'closed_lost'].includes(updates.stage)) {
   346→      if (current && !current.qualified_at && ['new', 'contacted', 'qualified', 'unqualified', 'nurture'].includes(current.stage)) {
   347→        updateData.qualified_at = new Date().toISOString();
   348→      }
   349→    }
   350→
   351→    // Set closed_at when closing
   352→    if (updates.stage && ['closed_won', 'closed_lost'].includes(updates.stage)) {
   353→      if (current && !current.closed_at) {
   354→        updateData.closed_at = new Date().toISOString();
   355→      }
   356→    }
   357→
   358→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   359→      .from('crm_opportunities')
   360→      .update(updateData)
   361→      .eq('id', id)
   362→      .select()
   363→      .single();
   364→
   365→    if (error) throw error;
   366→
   367→    // Record stage change if stage changed
   368→    if (updates.stage && current && current.stage !== updates.stage) {
   369→      await this.recordStageHistory('opportunity', id, current.stage, updates.stage);
   370→    }
   371→
   372→    return data;
   373→  }
   374→
   375→  static async deleteOpportunity(id: string): Promise<void> {
   376→    const { error } = await supabase
   377→      .from('crm_opportunities')
   378→      .delete()
   379→      .eq('id', id);
   380→
   381→    if (error) throw error;
   382→  }
   383→
   384→  static async updateOpportunityPositions(positions: { id: string; position: number }[]): Promise<void> {
   385→    // Update each opportunity's position
   386→    const updates = positions.map(({ id, position }) =>
   387→      supabase
   388→        .from('crm_opportunities')
   389→        .update({ position, updated_at: new Date().toISOString() })
   390→        .eq('id', id)
   391→    );
   392→
   393→    const results = await Promise.all(updates);
   394→    const errors = results.filter(r => r.error);
   395→    if (errors.length > 0) {
   396→      console.error('Errors updating positions:', errors);
   397→      throw errors[0].error;
   398→    }
   399→  }
   400→
   401→  // ----------------------------------------
   402→  // PARTNERS
   403→  // ----------------------------------------
   404→
   405→  static async getAllPartners(): Promise<CRMPartner[]> {
   406→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   407→      .from('crm_partners')
   408→      .select(`
   409→        *,
   410→        affiliate:crm_affiliates(*)
   411→      `)
   412→      .order('created_at', { ascending: false });
   413→
   414→    if (error) throw error;
   415→    return data || [];
   416→  }
   417→
   418→  static async getPartnerById(id: string): Promise<CRMPartner | null> {
   419→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   420→      .from('crm_partners')
   421→      .select(`
   422→        *,
   423→        affiliate:crm_affiliates(*)
   424→      `)
   425→      .eq('id', id)
   426→      .single();
   427→
   428→    if (error) throw error;
   429→    return data;
   430→  }
   431→
   432→  static async createPartner(partnerData: CreatePartnerData): Promise<CRMPartner> {
   433→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   434→      .from('crm_partners')
   435→      .insert([partnerData])
   436→      .select()
   437→      .single();
   438→
   439→    if (error) throw error;
   440→
   441→    // Record stage history (for status)
   442→    await this.recordStageHistory('partner', data.id, null, partnerData.status || 'active');
   443→
   444→    return data;
   445→  }
   446→
   447→  static async updatePartner(id: string, updates: Partial<CreatePartnerData>): Promise<CRMPartner> {
   448→    // Get current state for stage history
   449→    const current = await this.getPartnerById(id);
   450→
   451→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   452→      .from('crm_partners')
   453→      .update({ ...updates, updated_at: new Date().toISOString() })
   454→      .eq('id', id)
   455→      .select()
   456→      .single();
   457→
   458→    if (error) throw error;
   459→
   460→    // Record status change if changed
   461→    if (updates.status && current && current.status !== updates.status) {
   462→      await this.recordStageHistory('partner', id, current.status, updates.status);
   463→    }
   464→
   465→    return data;
   466→  }
   467→
   468→  static async deletePartner(id: string): Promise<void> {
   469→    const { error } = await supabase
   470→      .from('crm_partners')
   471→      .delete()
   472→      .eq('id', id);
   473→
   474→    if (error) throw error;
   475→  }
   476→
   477→  // ----------------------------------------
   478→  // CONTACTS
   479→  // ----------------------------------------
   480→
   481→  static async getAllContacts(): Promise<CRMContact[]> {
   482→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   483→      .from('crm_contacts')
   484→      .select('*')
   485→      .order('created_at', { ascending: false });
   486→
   487→    if (error) throw error;
   488→    return data || [];
   489→  }
   490→
   491→  static async getContactById(id: string): Promise<CRMContact | null> {
   492→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   493→      .from('crm_contacts')
   494→      .select('*')
   495→      .eq('id', id)
   496→      .single();
   497→
   498→    if (error) throw error;
   499→    return data;
```
</details></tool-use>

 `./sql/migrations/022_create_crm_tables.sql`

```text
     1→-- CRM Database Schema
     2→-- Core Tables: Opportunities (Leads+Deals), Partners, Affiliates, Contacts
     3→-- Supporting: Stage History for analytics
     4→
     5→-- ============================================
     6→-- 1. AFFILIATES TABLE (created first for FK reference)
     7→-- KOLs, referrers with rev-share or commission
     8→-- Lifecycle: New → Active → Inactive
     9→-- ============================================
    10→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS crm_affiliates (
    11→  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    12→
    13→  -- Basic Info
    14→  name TEXT NOT NULL,
    15→  affiliation TEXT, -- Their affiliation/company
    16→  category TEXT, -- Type of affiliate
    17→
    18→  -- Lifecycle
    19→  status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'new', -- 'new', 'active', 'inactive'
    20→
    21→  -- Commission
    22→  commission_model TEXT, -- Description of commission/rev-share structure
    23→  commission_rate DECIMAL, -- Percentage if applicable
    24→
    25→  -- Tracking
    26→  last_contacted_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
    27→
    28→  -- Ownership
    29→  owner_id UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id),
    30→
    31→  -- Point of Contact (inline)
    32→  poc_name TEXT,
    33→  poc_email TEXT,
    34→  poc_telegram TEXT,
    35→
    36→  -- Notes
    37→  notes TEXT,
    38→
    39→  -- Timestamps
    40→  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
    41→  updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
    42→);
    43→
    44→-- ============================================
    45→-- 2. OPPORTUNITIES TABLE (Unified Leads + Deals)
    46→-- Single pipeline: New → Contacted → Qualified → Proposal → Contract → Closed
    47→-- ============================================
    48→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS crm_opportunities (
    49→  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    50→
    51→  -- Basic Info
    52→  name TEXT NOT NULL,
    53→
    54→  -- Pipeline Stage (unified lead + deal stages)
    55→  stage TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'new',
    56→  -- Lead stages: 'new', 'contacted', 'qualified', 'unqualified', 'nurture'
    57→  -- Deal stages: 'proposal', 'contract', 'closed_won', 'closed_lost'
    58→
    59→  -- Classification
    60→  account_type TEXT, -- 'general', 'channel', 'campaign', 'lite', 'ad_hoc' (set when qualified)
    61→
    62→  -- Value (set when becomes a deal)
    63→  deal_value DECIMAL,
    64→  currency TEXT DEFAULT 'USD',
    65→
    66→  -- Tracking
    67→  last_contacted_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
    68→
    69→  -- Ownership & Source
    70→  owner_id UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id),
    71→  source TEXT, -- 'referral', 'inbound', 'event', 'cold_outreach'
    72→  referrer TEXT, -- Who referred them
    73→
    74→  -- Communication
    75→  gc TEXT, -- Group Chat link/info
    76→
    77→  -- Affiliate connection (for commission tracking)
    78→  affiliate_id UUID REFERENCES crm_affiliates(id),
    79→
    80→  -- Notes
    81→  notes TEXT,
    82→
    83→  -- Timestamps
    84→  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
    85→  updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
    86→  qualified_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE, -- When moved from lead to deal stage
    87→  closed_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE -- When won or lost
    88→);
    89→
    90→-- ============================================
    91→-- 3. PARTNERS TABLE
    92→-- BD relationships, cross-partnerships
    93→-- Lifecycle: Active / Inactive
    94→-- ============================================
    95→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS crm_partners (
    96→  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    97→
    98→  -- Basic Info
    99→  name TEXT NOT NULL,
   100→
   101→  -- Classification
   102→  category TEXT, -- 'service_provider', 'investor_vc', 'project', 'individual'
   103→  focus TEXT, -- Area of focus/expertise
   104→
   105→  -- Lifecycle
   106→  status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active', -- 'active', 'inactive'
   107→
   108→  -- Tracking
   109→  last_contacted_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
   110→
   111→  -- Ownership
   112→  owner_id UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id),
   113→
   114→  -- Point of Contact (inline)
   115→  poc_name TEXT,
   116→  poc_email TEXT,
   117→  poc_telegram TEXT,
   118→
   119→  -- Affiliate connection (if partner is also an affiliate)
   120→  is_affiliate BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
   121→  affiliate_id UUID REFERENCES crm_affiliates(id),
   122→
   123→  -- Notes
   124→  notes TEXT,
   125→
   126→  -- Timestamps
   127→  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
   128→  updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
   129→);
   130→
   131→-- ============================================
   132→-- 4. CONTACTS TABLE
   133→-- Individual people linked to opportunities/partners/affiliates
   134→-- ============================================
   135→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS crm_contacts (
   136→  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   137→
   138→  -- Basic Info
   139→  name TEXT NOT NULL,
   140→  email TEXT,
   141→  telegram_id TEXT,
   142→  x_id TEXT,
   143→  role TEXT, -- Their role/title
   144→  category TEXT, -- Contact category
   145→
   146→  -- Ownership
   147→  owner_id UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id),
   148→
   149→  -- Notes
   150→  notes TEXT,
   151→
   152→  -- Timestamps
   153→  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
   154→  updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
   155→);
   156→
   157→-- ============================================
   158→-- 5. CONTACT LINKS (Junction Table)
   159→-- Links contacts to opportunities/partners/affiliates
   160→-- ============================================
   161→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS crm_contact_links (
   162→  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   163→  contact_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES crm_contacts(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   164→
   165→  -- Linked object type and ID
   166→  linked_type TEXT NOT NULL, -- 'opportunity', 'partner', 'affiliate'
   167→  opportunity_id UUID REFERENCES crm_opportunities(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   168→  partner_id UUID REFERENCES crm_partners(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   169→  affiliate_id UUID REFERENCES crm_affiliates(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   170→
   171→  -- Role in this relationship
   172→  role TEXT,
   173→  is_primary BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
   174→
   175→  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
   176→
   177→  -- Ensure only one link type is set
   178→  CONSTRAINT check_single_link CHECK (
   179→    (CASE WHEN opportunity_id IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END +
   180→     CASE WHEN partner_id IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END +
   181→     CASE WHEN affiliate_id IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 1
   182→  )
   183→);
   184→
   185→-- ============================================
   186→-- 6. STAGE HISTORY (For Analytics)
   187→-- Tracks all stage changes for conversion/velocity analysis
   188→-- ============================================
   189→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS crm_stage_history (
   190→  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   191→
   192→  -- Object reference
   193→  object_type TEXT NOT NULL, -- 'opportunity', 'partner', 'affiliate'
   194→  object_id UUID NOT NULL,
   195→
   196→  -- Stage change
   197→  from_stage TEXT,
   198→  to_stage TEXT NOT NULL,
   199→
   200→  -- Who and when
   201→  changed_by UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id),
   202→  changed_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
   203→
   204→  notes TEXT
   205→);
   206→
   207→-- ============================================
   208→-- INDEXES
   209→-- ============================================
   210→
   211→-- Opportunities
   212→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_opportunities_stage ON crm_opportunities(stage);
   213→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_opportunities_owner ON crm_opportunities(owner_id);
   214→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_opportunities_source ON crm_opportunities(source);
   215→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_opportunities_affiliate ON crm_opportunities(affiliate_id);
   216→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_opportunities_created ON crm_opportunities(created_at);
   217→
   218→-- Partners
   219→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_partners_status ON crm_partners(status);
   220→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_partners_category ON crm_partners(category);
   221→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_partners_owner ON crm_partners(owner_id);
   222→
   223→-- Affiliates
   224→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_affiliates_status ON crm_affiliates(status);
   225→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_affiliates_category ON crm_affiliates(category);
   226→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_affiliates_owner ON crm_affiliates(owner_id);
   227→
   228→-- Contacts
   229→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_contacts_owner ON crm_contacts(owner_id);
   230→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_contacts_email ON crm_contacts(email);
   231→
   232→-- Contact links
   233→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_contact_links_contact ON crm_contact_links(contact_id);
   234→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_contact_links_opportunity ON crm_contact_links(opportunity_id);
   235→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_contact_links_partner ON crm_contact_links(partner_id);
   236→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_contact_links_affiliate ON crm_contact_links(affiliate_id);
   237→
   238→-- Stage history
   239→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_stage_history_object ON crm_stage_history(object_type, object_id);
   240→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_stage_history_changed ON crm_stage_history(changed_at);
   241→
   242→-- ============================================
   243→-- COMMENTS
   244→-- ============================================
   245→
   246→COMMENT ON TABLE crm_opportunities IS 'Unified sales pipeline: Leads (new/contacted/qualified) → Deals (proposal/contract/closed). Single entity journey.';
   247→COMMENT ON TABLE crm_partners IS 'BD relationships. Categories: service_provider, investor_vc, project, individual. Can also be affiliates.';
   248→COMMENT ON TABLE crm_affiliates IS 'KOLs and referrers with commission/rev-share. Lifecycle: new → active → inactive.';
   249→COMMENT ON TABLE crm_contacts IS 'Individual people who can be linked to multiple CRM objects.';
   250→COMMENT ON TABLE crm_contact_links IS 'Links contacts to opportunities, partners, or affiliates with role info.';
   251→COMMENT ON TABLE crm_stage_history IS 'Audit trail of all stage changes for analytics (conversion rates, velocity).';
   252→
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>

 `./sql/migrations/023_crm_rls_policies.sql`

```text
     1→-- CRM Row Level Security Policies
     2→-- Enables access control for all CRM tables
     3→
     4→-- ============================================
     5→-- Enable RLS on all CRM tables
     6→-- ============================================
     7→
     8→ALTER TABLE crm_affiliates ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
     9→ALTER TABLE crm_opportunities ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
    10→ALTER TABLE crm_partners ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
    11→ALTER TABLE crm_contacts ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
    12→ALTER TABLE crm_contact_links ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
    13→ALTER TABLE crm_stage_history ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
    14→
    15→-- ============================================
    16→-- CRM AFFILIATES POLICIES
    17→-- ============================================
    18→
    19→-- All authenticated users can view affiliates
    20→CREATE POLICY "Users can view all affiliates"
    21→  ON crm_affiliates FOR SELECT
    22→  TO authenticated
    23→  USING (true);
    24→
    25→-- Users can create affiliates
    26→CREATE POLICY "Users can create affiliates"
    27→  ON crm_affiliates FOR INSERT
    28→  TO authenticated
    29→  WITH CHECK (true);
    30→
    31→-- Users can update affiliates they own or if they're admin
    32→CREATE POLICY "Users can update own affiliates or admin can update all"
    33→  ON crm_affiliates FOR UPDATE
    34→  TO authenticated
    35→  USING (
    36→    owner_id = auth.uid() OR
    37→    EXISTS (
    38→      SELECT 1 FROM users
    39→      WHERE users.id = auth.uid()
    40→      AND users.role = 'admin'
    41→    )
    42→  );
    43→
    44→-- Users can delete affiliates they own or if they're admin
    45→CREATE POLICY "Users can delete own affiliates or admin can delete all"
    46→  ON crm_affiliates FOR DELETE
    47→  TO authenticated
    48→  USING (
    49→    owner_id = auth.uid() OR
    50→    EXISTS (
    51→      SELECT 1 FROM users
    52→      WHERE users.id = auth.uid()
    53→      AND users.role = 'admin'
    54→    )
    55→  );
    56→
    57→-- ============================================
    58→-- CRM OPPORTUNITIES POLICIES
    59→-- ============================================
    60→
    61→-- All authenticated users can view opportunities
    62→CREATE POLICY "Users can view all opportunities"
    63→  ON crm_opportunities FOR SELECT
    64→  TO authenticated
    65→  USING (true);
    66→
    67→-- Users can create opportunities
    68→CREATE POLICY "Users can create opportunities"
    69→  ON crm_opportunities FOR INSERT
    70→  TO authenticated
    71→  WITH CHECK (true);
    72→
    73→-- Users can update opportunities they own or if they're admin
    74→CREATE POLICY "Users can update own opportunities or admin can update all"
    75→  ON crm_opportunities FOR UPDATE
    76→  TO authenticated
    77→  USING (
    78→    owner_id = auth.uid() OR
    79→    EXISTS (
    80→      SELECT 1 FROM users
    81→      WHERE users.id = auth.uid()
    82→      AND users.role = 'admin'
    83→    )
    84→  );
    85→
    86→-- Users can delete opportunities they own or if they're admin
    87→CREATE POLICY "Users can delete own opportunities or admin can delete all"
    88→  ON crm_opportunities FOR DELETE
    89→  TO authenticated
    90→  USING (
    91→    owner_id = auth.uid() OR
    92→    EXISTS (
    93→      SELECT 1 FROM users
    94→      WHERE users.id = auth.uid()
    95→      AND users.role = 'admin'
    96→    )
    97→  );
    98→
    99→-- ============================================
   100→-- CRM PARTNERS POLICIES
   101→-- ============================================
   102→
   103→-- All authenticated users can view partners
   104→CREATE POLICY "Users can view all partners"
   105→  ON crm_partners FOR SELECT
   106→  TO authenticated
   107→  USING (true);
   108→
   109→-- Users can create partners
   110→CREATE POLICY "Users can create partners"
   111→  ON crm_partners FOR INSERT
   112→  TO authenticated
   113→  WITH CHECK (true);
   114→
   115→-- Users can update partners they own or if they're admin
   116→CREATE POLICY "Users can update own partners or admin can update all"
   117→  ON crm_partners FOR UPDATE
   118→  TO authenticated
   119→  USING (
   120→    owner_id = auth.uid() OR
   121→    EXISTS (
   122→      SELECT 1 FROM users
   123→      WHERE users.id = auth.uid()
   124→      AND users.role = 'admin'
   125→    )
   126→  );
   127→
   128→-- Users can delete partners they own or if they're admin
   129→CREATE POLICY "Users can delete own partners or admin can delete all"

   132→  USING (

   135→      SELECT 1 FROM users

   140→
   141→-- ============================================
   142→-- CRM CONTACTS POLICIES
   143→-- ============================================
   144→
   145→-- All authenticated users can view contacts
   146→CREATE POLICY "Users can view all contacts"
   147→  ON crm_contacts FOR SELECT
   148→  TO authenticated
   149→  USING (true);
   150→
   151→-- Users can create contacts
   152→CREATE POLICY "Users can create contacts"
   153→  ON crm_contacts FOR INSERT
   154→  TO authenticated
   155→  WITH CHECK (true);
   156→
   157→-- Users can update contacts they own or if they're admin
   158→CREATE POLICY "Users can update own contacts or admin can update all"

   168→  );
   169→
   170→-- Users can delete contacts they own or if they're admin
   171→CREATE POLICY "Users can delete own contacts or admin can delete all"
   172→  ON crm_contacts FOR DELETE
   173→  TO authenticated
   174→  USING (
   175→    owner_id = auth.uid() OR
   176→    EXISTS (
   177→      SELECT 1 FROM users

   182→
   183→-- ============================================

   185→-- ============================================
   186→
   187→-- All authenticated users can view contact links
   188→CREATE POLICY "Users can view all contact links"
   189→  ON crm_contact_links FOR SELECT
   190→  TO authenticated
   191→  USING (true);
   192→
   193→-- Users can create contact links
   194→CREATE POLICY "Users can create contact links"
   195→  ON crm_contact_links FOR INSERT
   196→  TO authenticated
   197→  WITH CHECK (true);
   198→
   199→-- Users can update contact links
   200→CREATE POLICY "Users can update contact links"
   201→  ON crm_contact_links FOR UPDATE
   202→  TO authenticated
   203→  USING (true);
   204→
   205→-- Users can delete contact links
   206→CREATE POLICY "Users can delete contact links"
   207→  ON crm_contact_links FOR DELETE
   208→  TO authenticated
   209→  USING (true);
   210→
   211→-- ============================================
   212→-- CRM STAGE HISTORY POLICIES
   213→-- ============================================
   214→
   215→-- All authenticated users can view stage history
   216→CREATE POLICY "Users can view all stage history"
   217→  ON crm_stage_history FOR SELECT
   218→  TO authenticated
   219→  USING (true);
   220→
   221→-- Users can create stage history entries
   222→CREATE POLICY "Users can create stage history"
   223→  ON crm_stage_history FOR INSERT
   224→  TO authenticated
   225→  WITH CHECK (true);
   226→
   227→-- Stage history should not be updated (audit trail)
   228→-- No UPDATE policy
   229→
   230→-- Stage history should not be deleted (audit trail)
   231→-- No DELETE policy
   232→
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
    11→import { useAuth } from '@/contexts/AuthContext';
    12→import { Skeleton } from '@/components/ui/skeleton';
    13→import {
    14→  Plus, Search, Edit, Trash2, UserPlus,
    15→  Mail, MessageSquare, MoreHorizontal, Building2, Handshake, Users, TrendingUp,
    16→  Filter, ArrowUpDown, X, Link2, Table as TableIcon, LayoutGrid
    17→} from 'lucide-react';

    21→import { DropdownMenu, DropdownMenuContent, DropdownMenuItem, DropdownMenuTrigger } from '@/components/ui/dropdown-menu';
    22→import { Popover, PopoverContent, PopoverTrigger } from '@/components/ui/popover';
    23→import { Command, CommandEmpty, CommandGroup, CommandInput, CommandItem, CommandList } from '@/components/ui/command';
    24→import {
    25→  CRMService,
    26→  CRMContact,
    27→  CRMContactLink,
    28→  CRMOpportunity,
    29→  CRMPartner,
    30→  CRMAffiliate,
    31→  CreateContactData,
    32→} from '@/lib/crmService';
    33→
    34→export default function ContactsPage() {
    35→  const { user } = useAuth();
    36→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);
    37→  const [searchTerm, setSearchTerm] = useState('');
    38→  const [contacts, setContacts] = useState<CRMContact[]>([]);
    39→  const [allContactLinks, setAllContactLinks] = useState<CRMContactLink[]>([]);
    40→  const [opportunities, setOpportunities] = useState<CRMOpportunity[]>([]);
    41→  const [partners, setPartners] = useState<CRMPartner[]>([]);
    42→  const [affiliates, setAffiliates] = useState<CRMAffiliate[]>([]);
    43→  const [isNewContactOpen, setIsNewContactOpen] = useState(false);
    44→  const [isSubmitting, setIsSubmitting] = useState(false);
    45→  const [editingContact, setEditingContact] = useState<CRMContact | null>(null);
    46→  const [contactForm, setContactForm] = useState<CreateContactData>({
    47→    name: '',
    48→  });
    49→
    50→  // Delete confirmation dialog state
    51→  const [isDeleteDialogOpen, setIsDeleteDialogOpen] = useState(false);
    52→  const [contactToDelete, setContactToDelete] = useState<{ id: string; name: string } | null>(null);
    53→
    54→  // Link to network state
    55→  const [linkToAffiliate, setLinkToAffiliate] = useState(false);
    56→  const [selectedAffiliateId, setSelectedAffiliateId] = useState<string>('');
    57→  const [linkToPartner, setLinkToPartner] = useState(false);
    58→  const [selectedPartnerId, setSelectedPartnerId] = useState<string>('');
    59→  const [existingAffiliateLink, setExistingAffiliateLink] = useState<CRMContactLink | null>(null);
    60→  const [existingPartnerLink, setExistingPartnerLink] = useState<CRMContactLink | null>(null);
    61→
    62→  // Filter state
    63→  const [filterCategory, setFilterCategory] = useState<string>('all');
    64→  const [filterLinked, setFilterLinked] = useState<string>('all');
    65→
    66→  // Category combobox state
    67→  const [categoryPopoverOpen, setCategoryPopoverOpen] = useState(false);
    68→
    69→  // Sort state
    70→  const [sortBy, setSortBy] = useState<string>('created_desc');
    71→
    72→  // View mode state
    73→  const [viewMode, setViewMode] = useState<'table' | 'cards'>('cards');
    74→
    75→  useEffect(() => {
    76→    fetchContacts();
    77→  }, []);
    78→
    79→  const fetchContacts = async () => {
    80→    setLoading(true);
    81→    try {
    82→      const [conts, contactLinks, opps, parts, affs] = await Promise.all([
    83→        CRMService.getAllContacts(),
    84→        CRMService.getAllContactLinks(),
    85→        CRMService.getAllOpportunities(),
    86→        CRMService.getAllPartners(),
    87→        CRMService.getAllAffiliates()
    88→      ]);
    89→      setContacts(conts);
    90→      setAllContactLinks(contactLinks);
    91→      setOpportunities(opps);
    92→      setPartners(parts);
    93→      setAffiliates(affs);
    94→    } catch (error) {
    95→      console.error('Error fetching contacts:', error);
    96→    } finally {
    97→      setLoading(false);
    98→    }
    99→  };
   100→
   101→  // Get linked entities for a contact
   102→  const getContactLinks = (contactId: string) => {
   103→    return allContactLinks.filter(link => link.contact_id === contactId);
   104→  };
   105→
   106→  // Get linked opportunities for a contact
   107→  const getLinkedOpportunities = (contactId: string) => {
   108→    const links = getContactLinks(contactId).filter(l => l.opportunity_id);
   109→    return links.map(l => opportunities.find(o => o.id === l.opportunity_id)).filter(Boolean) as CRMOpportunity[];
   110→  };
   111→
   112→  // Get linked partners for a contact
   113→  const getLinkedPartners = (contactId: string) => {
   114→    const links = getContactLinks(contactId).filter(l => l.partner_id);
   115→    return links.map(l => partners.find(p => p.id === l.partner_id)).filter(Boolean) as CRMPartner[];
   116→  };
   117→
   118→  // Get linked affiliates for a contact
   119→  const getLinkedAffiliates = (contactId: string) => {
   120→    const links = getContactLinks(contactId).filter(l => l.affiliate_id);
   121→    return links.map(l => affiliates.find(a => a.id === l.affiliate_id)).filter(Boolean) as CRMAffiliate[];
   122→  };
   123→
   124→  const handleCreateContact = async () => {
   125→    if (!contactForm.name.trim()) return;
   126→    setIsSubmitting(true);
   127→    try {
   128→      let contactId: string;
   129→
   130→      if (editingContact) {
   131→        await CRMService.updateContact(editingContact.id, contactForm);
   132→        contactId = editingContact.id;
   133→      } else {
   134→        const newContact = await CRMService.createContact({
   135→          ...contactForm,
   136→          owner_id: user?.id
   137→        });
   138→        contactId = newContact.id;
   139→      }
   140→
   141→      // Handle affiliate link
   142→      if (linkToAffiliate && selectedAffiliateId) {
   143→        if (existingAffiliateLink) {
   144→          // If affiliate changed, delete old and create new
   145→          if (existingAffiliateLink.affiliate_id !== selectedAffiliateId) {
   146→            await CRMService.unlinkContact(existingAffiliateLink.id);
   147→            await CRMService.linkContactToAffiliate(contactId, selectedAffiliateId);
   148→          }
   149→        } else {
   150→          await CRMService.linkContactToAffiliate(contactId, selectedAffiliateId);
```

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 2026-02-20 14:10:12Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./sql/migrations/024_opportunity_position.sql`

```text
     1→-- Add position column to crm_opportunities for custom ordering
     2→ALTER TABLE crm_opportunities ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS position INTEGER DEFAULT 0;
     3→
     4→-- Set initial positions based on created_at (newest first = lowest position)
     5→WITH numbered AS (
     6→  SELECT id, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY created_at DESC) as rn
     7→  FROM crm_opportunities
     8→)
     9→UPDATE crm_opportunities
    10→SET position = numbered.rn
    11→FROM numbered
    12→WHERE crm_opportunities.id = numbered.id;
    13→
    14→-- Create index for faster ordering
    15→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_crm_opportunities_position ON crm_opportunities(position);
    16→
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
   100→  const [opportunityContacts, setOpportunityContacts] = useState<CRMContactLink[]>([]);
   101→  const [selectedContactId, setSelectedContactId] = useState<string>('');
   102→  const [contactRole, setContactRole] = useState<string>('');
   103→  const [isPrimaryContact, setIsPrimaryContact] = useState(false);
   104→
   105→  // New contact creation state (within manage contacts dialog)
   106→  const [contactMode, setContactMode] = useState<'link' | 'create'>('link');
   107→  const [newContactForm, setNewContactForm] = useState<CreateContactData>({ name: '' });
   108→
   109→  // Stage history state
   110→  const [isHistoryOpen, setIsHistoryOpen] = useState(false);
   111→  const [historyOpportunity, setHistoryOpportunity] = useState<CRMOpportunity | null>(null);
   112→  const [stageHistory, setStageHistory] = useState<CRMStageHistory[]>([]);
   113→
   114→  // Delete confirmation dialog state
   115→  const [isDeleteDialogOpen, setIsDeleteDialogOpen] = useState(false);
   116→  const [opportunityToDelete, setOpportunityToDelete] = useState<{ id: string; name: string } | null>(null);
   117→  const [isBulkDeleteDialogOpen, setIsBulkDeleteDialogOpen] = useState(false);
   118→
   119→  // Convert to deal dialog state
   120→  const [isConvertDialogOpen, setIsConvertDialogOpen] = useState(false);
   121→  const [opportunityToConvert, setOpportunityToConvert] = useState<CRMOpportunity | null>(null);
   122→
   123→  // Convert to account dialog state
   124→  const [isConvertToAccountDialogOpen, setIsConvertToAccountDialogOpen] = useState(false);
   125→  const [dealToConvertToAccount, setDealToConvertToAccount] = useState<CRMOpportunity | null>(null);
   126→
   127→  // Analytics state
   128→  const [metrics, setMetrics] = useState<{
   129→    totalLeads: number;
   130→    totalDeals: number;
   131→    totalValue: number;
   132→    wonValue: number;
   133→    conversionRate: number;
   134→  } | null>(null);
   135→
   136→  // Filter state
   137→  const [filterSource, setFilterSource] = useState<string>('all');
   138→  const [filterAccountType, setFilterAccountType] = useState<string>('all');
   139→  const [filterAffiliate, setFilterAffiliate] = useState<string>('all');
   140→
   141→  // Sort state
   142→  const [sortBy, setSortBy] = useState<string>('position');
   143→
   144→  // Drag and drop state
   145→  const [activeId, setActiveId] = useState<string | null>(null);
   146→  const [activeOpportunity, setActiveOpportunity] = useState<CRMOpportunity | null>(null);
   147→
   148→  // Collapsed stages state (dead is collapsed by default)
   149→  const [collapsedStages, setCollapsedStages] = useState<Set<OpportunityStage>>(new Set(['dead']));
   150→
   151→  // Adding new row state
   152→  const [addingToStage, setAddingToStage] = useState<OpportunityStage | null>(null);
   153→  const [newRowName, setNewRowName] = useState('');
   154→  const savedScrollPositionRef = useRef<number>(0);
   155→
   156→  // Bulk edit state
   157→  const [selectedOpportunities, setSelectedOpportunities] = useState<string[]>([]);
   158→  const [bulkEdit, setBulkEdit] = useState<Partial<CRMOpportunity>>({});
   159→
   160→  // Helper to get scroll container
   161→  const getScrollContainer = () => {
   162→    return document.querySelector('[data-radix-scroll-area-viewport]') || document.scrollingElement;
   163→  };
   164→
   165→  // Helper to restore scroll position
   166→  const restoreScrollPosition = () => {
   167→    const scrollContainer = getScrollContainer();
   168→    if (scrollContainer) {
   169→      requestAnimationFrame(() => {
   170→        scrollContainer.scrollTop = savedScrollPositionRef.current;
   171→      });
   172→    }
   173→  };
   174→
   175→  const handleAddNewRow = (stage: OpportunityStage) => {
   176→    // Capture scroll position before any state changes
   177→    const scrollContainer = getScrollContainer();
   178→    savedScrollPositionRef.current = scrollContainer?.scrollTop || 0;
   179→
   180→    // Expand the stage if collapsed
   181→    if (collapsedStages.has(stage)) {
   182→      setCollapsedStages(prev => {
   183→        const newSet = new Set(prev);
   184→        newSet.delete(stage);
   185→        return newSet;
   186→      });
   187→    }
   188→    setAddingToStage(stage);
   189→    setNewRowName('');
   190→
   191→    // Restore scroll position after React renders
   192→    requestAnimationFrame(() => {
   193→      requestAnimationFrame(() => {
   194→        if (scrollContainer) scrollContainer.scrollTop = savedScrollPositionRef.current;
   195→      });
   196→    });
   197→  };
   198→
   199→  const handleSaveNewRow = async () => {
   200→    if (!addingToStage || !newRowName.trim()) {
   201→      setAddingToStage(null);
   202→      setNewRowName('');
   203→      restoreScrollPosition();
   204→      return;
   205→    }
   206→
   207→    const stageSaving = addingToStage;
   208→    const nameSaving = newRowName.trim();
   209→
   210→    // Clear input states first
   211→    setAddingToStage(null);
   212→    setNewRowName('');
   213→    restoreScrollPosition();
   214→
   215→    try {
   216→      const newOpp = await CRMService.createOpportunity({
   217→        name: nameSaving,
   218→        stage: stageSaving,
   219→        owner_id: user?.id,
   220→        // For outreach tab, set source to cold_outreach
   221→        source: activeTab === 'outreach' ? 'cold_outreach' : undefined
   222→      });
   223→
   224→      // Optimistic update - add to state directly
   225→      if (newOpp) {
   226→        setOpportunities(prev => [newOpp, ...prev]);
   227→        restoreScrollPosition();
   228→      }
   229→    } catch (error) {
   230→      console.error('Error creating opportunity:', error);
   231→    }
   232→  };
   233→
   234→  const handleNewRowKeyDown = (e: React.KeyboardEvent) => {
   235→    if (e.key === 'Enter') {
   236→      e.preventDefault();
   237→      handleSaveNewRow();
   238→    } else if (e.key === 'Escape') {
   239→      setAddingToStage(null);
   240→      setNewRowName('');
   241→      restoreScrollPosition();
   242→    }
   243→  };
   244→
   245→  const toggleStageCollapse = (stage: OpportunityStage) => {
   246→    setCollapsedStages(prev => {
   247→      const newSet = new Set(prev);
   248→      if (newSet.has(stage)) {
   249→        newSet.delete(stage);
   250→      } else {
   251→        newSet.add(stage);
   252→      }
   253→      return newSet;
   254→    });
   255→  };
   256→
   257→  // Drag and drop sensors
   258→  const sensors = useSensors(
   259→    useSensor(PointerSensor, {
   260→      activationConstraint: {
   261→        distance: 8,
   262→      },
   263→    }),
   264→    useSensor(KeyboardSensor)
   265→  );
   266→
   267→  const outreachStages: OpportunityStage[] = ['new'];  // Outreach shows only 'new' stage with source 'cold_outreach'
   268→  const leadStages: OpportunityStage[] = ['new', 'contacted', 'qualified', 'unqualified', 'nurture', 'dead'];
   269→  const dealStages: OpportunityStage[] = ['deal_qualified', 'proposal', 'negotiation', 'contract', 'closed_won', 'closed_lost'];
   270→  const accountStages: OpportunityStage[] = ['account_active', 'account_churned'];

   288→    dead: 'Dead',

   300→  const stageColors: Record<OpportunityStage, { bg: string; text: string; border: string; solid: string }> = {
```

```text
     7→export interface CampaignSuggestion {
     8→  name: string;
     9→  description: string;
    10→  budget: number;
    11→  duration: string;
    12→  targetRegions: string[];

    14→  budgetAllocation: { region: string; amount: number }[];

    18→
    19→export interface ListSuggestion {
    20→  name: string;
    21→  description: string;
    22→  kolCount: number;
    23→  criteria: {
    24→    regions?: string[];
    25→    platforms?: string[];
    26→    minFollowers?: number;
    27→    maxFollowers?: number;
    28→    contentTypes?: string[];
    29→    creatorTypes?: string[];
    30→  };
    31→  suggestedKOLs: any[];
    32→  reasoning: string;
    33→}
    34→
    35→export class AIService {
    36→  // Enhanced AI response with context awareness
    37→  static async getAIResponse(messages: any[], context?: AIContext): Promise<string> {
    38→    try {
    39→      // Use OpenAI service for real AI responses
    40→      const response = await OpenAIService.getAIResponse(messages, context);
    41→      
    42→      // Log usage for monitoring
    43→      console.log('AI Usage:', {
    44→        tokens: response.tokens,
    45→        cost: response.cost,
    46→        model: 'gpt-3.5-turbo'
    47→      });
    48→      
    49→      return response.content;
    50→    } catch (error) {
    51→      console.error('Error getting AI response:', error);
    52→      
    53→      // Fallback to rule-based responses if OpenAI fails
    54→      const lastMessage = messages[messages.length - 1];
    55→      const userInput = lastMessage?.content?.toLowerCase() || '';
    56→
    57→      // Campaign-related queries
    58→      if (userInput.includes('campaign') || userInput.includes('create campaign')) {
    59→        return this.handleCampaignQuery(userInput, context);
    60→      }
    61→
    62→      // List-related queries
    63→      if (userInput.includes('list') || userInput.includes('create list') || userInput.includes('kol list')) {
    64→        return this.handleListQuery(userInput, context);
    65→      }
    66→
    67→      // Budget-related queries
    68→      if (userInput.includes('budget') || userInput.includes('cost') || userInput.includes('pricing')) {
    69→        return this.handleBudgetQuery(userInput, context);
    70→      }
    71→
    72→      // KOL-related queries
    73→      if (userInput.includes('kol') || userInput.includes('influencer') || userInput.includes('creator')) {
    74→        return this.handleKOLQuery(userInput, context);
    75→      }
    76→
    77→      // Message-related queries
    78→      if (userInput.includes('message') || userInput.includes('template') || userInput.includes('write') || userInput.includes('draft')) {
    79→        return this.handleMessageQuery(userInput, context);
    80→      }
    81→
    82→      // Template management queries
    83→      if (userInput.includes('template') || userInput.includes('manage templates') || userInput.includes('create template')) {
    84→        return this.handleTemplateQuery(userInput, context);
    85→      }
    86→
    87→      // Greetings
    88→      if (userInput.includes('hello') || userInput.includes('hi') || userInput.includes('hey')) {
    89→        return this.getWelcomeMessage();
    90→      }
    91→
    92→      // General help
    93→      if (userInput.includes('help') || userInput.includes('what can you do')) {
    94→        return this.getHelpMessage();
    95→      }
    96→
    97→      // Default response
    98→      return this.getDefaultResponse();
    99→    }
   100→  }
```
