```text
     1→'use client';
     2→
     3→import { useState, useEffect, useMemo } from 'react';
     4→import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button';
     5→import { Input } from '@/components/ui/input';
     6→import { Label } from '@/components/ui/label';
     7→import { Textarea } from '@/components/ui/textarea';
     8→import { Select, SelectContent, SelectItem, SelectTrigger, SelectValue } from '@/components/ui/select';
     9→import { Dialog, DialogContent, DialogDescription, DialogFooter, DialogHeader, DialogTitle } from '@/components/ui/dialog';
    10→import { Skeleton } from '@/components/ui/skeleton';
    11→import { Tabs, TabsList, TabsTrigger } from '@/components/ui/tabs';
    12→import { useAuth } from '@/contexts/AuthContext';
    13→import { supabase } from '@/lib/supabase';
    14→import { UserService } from '@/lib/userService';
    15→import { useToast } from '@/hooks/use-toast';
    16→import Image from 'next/image';
    17→import {
    18→  Plus,
    19→  CheckCircle,
    20→  Search,
    21→  Trash2,
    22→  Edit,
    23→  Calendar as CalendarIcon,
    24→  ClipboardList,
    25→  X,
    26→  Check,
    27→  XCircle,
    28→  ExternalLink,
    29→} from 'lucide-react';
    30→
    31→type DailyStandup = {
    32→  id: string;
    33→  user_id: string;
    34→  user_name: string;
    35→  completed_yesterday: 'Yes' | 'No';
    36→  priorities: string;
    37→  output_goal: string;
    38→  blockers: string | null;
    39→  submitted_at: string;
    40→  submission_date: string;
    41→  created_at: string;
    42→};
    43→
    44→type TeamMember = {
    45→  id: string;
    46→  name: string;
    47→  email: string;
    48→  role: string;
    49→  profile_photo_url: string | null;
    50→};
    51→
    52→export default function DailyStandupPage() {
    53→  const { user, userProfile } = useAuth();
    54→  const { toast } = useToast();
    55→
    56→  const [standups, setStandups] = useState<DailyStandup[]>([]);
    57→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);
    58→  const [teamMembers, setTeamMembers] = useState<TeamMember[]>([]);
    59→  const [selectedUserId, setSelectedUserId] = useState<string>('all');
    60→  const [searchTerm, setSearchTerm] = useState('');
    61→
    62→  // Form state
    63→  const [isFormOpen, setIsFormOpen] = useState(false);
    64→  const [editingId, setEditingId] = useState<string | null>(null);
    65→  const [deletingId, setDeletingId] = useState<string | null>(null);
    66→  const [submitting, setSubmitting] = useState(false);
    67→  const [form, setForm] = useState({
    68→    completed_yesterday: '' as string,
    69→    priorities: '',
    70→    output_goal: '',
    71→    blockers: '',
    72→  });
    73→
    74→  useEffect(() => {
    75→    fetchStandups();
    76→    UserService.getAllUsers().then((users) => {
    77→      setTeamMembers(
    78→        users
    79→          .filter(u => u.role !== 'client')
    80→          .map(u => ({ id: u.id, name: u.name || u.email, email: u.email, role: u.role, profile_photo_url: u.profile_photo_url || null }))
    81→      );
    82→    });
    83→  }, []);
    84→
    85→  const fetchStandups = async () => {
    86→    setLoading(true);
    87→    const { data } = await supabase
    88→      .from('daily_standups')
    89→      .select('*')
    90→      .order('submission_date', { ascending: false })
    91→      .order('submitted_at', { ascending: false });
    92→    setStandups(data || []);
    93→    setLoading(false);
    94→  };
    95→
    96→  // Check if user already submitted today
    97→  const todayStr = new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0];
    98→  const hasSubmittedToday = useMemo(() => {
    99→    return standups.some(s => s.user_id === user?.id && s.submission_date === todayStr);
   100→  }, [standups, user?.id, todayStr]);
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
     1→'use client';
     2→
     3→import React, { useState } from 'react';
     4→import Link from 'next/link';
     5→import { usePathname, useRouter } from 'next/navigation';
     6→import Image from 'next/image';
     7→import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button';
     8→import { Users, Megaphone, Crown, List, Building2, PanelLeftClose, PanelLeftOpen, Settings, LogOut, Shield, MessageSquare, Zap, User, FileText, ClipboardList, Sliders, DollarSign, TrendingUp, Handshake, UserPlus, Archive, Sparkles, Link2, ChevronLeft, ChevronRight, BookOpen, CheckCircle, Briefcase, ListTodo } from 'lucide-react';
     9→import { useAuth } from '@/contexts/AuthContext';
    10→import { useChangelog } from '@/contexts/ChangelogContext';
    11→import { DropdownMenu, DropdownMenuTrigger, DropdownMenuContent, DropdownMenuItem, DropdownMenuSeparator } from '@/components/ui/dropdown-menu';
    12→import { Avatar, AvatarImage, AvatarFallback } from '@/components/ui/avatar';
    13→import { Dialog, DialogContent, DialogHeader, DialogTitle, DialogDescription } from '@/components/ui/dialog';
    14→import { Badge } from '@/components/ui/badge';
    15→import { ScrollArea } from '@/components/ui/scroll-area';
    16→
    17→interface SidebarProps {
    18→  children: React.ReactNode;
    19→}
    20→
    21→export default function Sidebar({ children }: SidebarProps) {
    22→  const pathname = usePathname();
    23→  const router = useRouter();
    24→  const { userProfile, signOut } = useAuth();
    25→  const [isSidebarCollapsed, setIsSidebarCollapsed] = React.useState(() => {
    26→    if (typeof window !== 'undefined') {
    27→      const stored = localStorage.getItem('sidebarCollapsed');
    28→      return stored === 'true';
    29→    }
    30→    return false;
    31→  });
    32→  const [isChangelogOpen, setIsChangelogOpen] = useState(false);
    33→  const [changelogPage, setChangelogPage] = useState(0);
    34→  const changelogsPerPage = 3;
    35→
    36→  // Get changelog data from context (fetched once at app level)
    37→  const { changelogs, latestVersion } = useChangelog();
    38→
    39→  // Format date for changelog display
    40→  const formatChangelogDate = (dateString: string | null) => {
    41→    if (!dateString) return '';
    42→    return new Date(dateString).toLocaleDateString('en-US', {
    43→      year: 'numeric',
    44→      month: 'long',
    45→      day: 'numeric'
    46→    });
    47→  };
    48→
    49→  // Render changelog content
    50→  const renderChangelogContent = (content: string) => {
    51→    return content.split('\n').map((line, index) => {
    52→      if (line.trim().startsWith('- ') || line.trim().startsWith('* ')) {
    53→        return (
    54→          <li key={index} className="ml-4 text-gray-700 text-sm">
    55→            {line.trim().substring(2)}
    56→          </li>
    57→        );
    58→      }
    59→      if (line.trim().startsWith('### ')) {
    60→        return (
    61→          <h4 key={index} className="font-semibold text-gray-900 mt-3 mb-1 text-sm">
    62→            {line.trim().substring(4)}
    63→          </h4>
    64→        );
    65→      }
    66→      if (line.trim().startsWith('## ')) {
    67→        return (
    68→          <h3 key={index} className="font-bold text-gray-900 mt-4 mb-2 text-sm">
    69→            {line.trim().substring(3)}
    70→          </h3>
    71→        );
    72→      }
    73→      if (line.trim() === '') {
    74→        return <div key={index} className="h-2" />;
    75→      }
    76→      return (
    77→        <p key={index} className="text-gray-700 text-sm">
    78→          {line}
    79→        </p>
    80→      );
    81→    });
    82→  };
    83→
    84→  const handleSidebarToggle = () => {
    85→    setIsSidebarCollapsed(prev => {
    86→      localStorage.setItem('sidebarCollapsed', String(!prev));
    87→      return !prev;
    88→    });
    89→  };
    90→
    91→  // Helper function to get user initials
    92→  const getUserInitials = () => {
    93→    if (userProfile?.name) {
    94→      return userProfile.name
    95→        .split(' ')
    96→        .map(word => word.charAt(0).toUpperCase())
    97→        .join('')
    98→        .slice(0, 2);
    99→    }
   100→    return userProfile?.email?.charAt(0).toUpperCase() || 'U';
   101→  };
   102→
   103→  return (
   104→    <div className="h-screen bg-gray-50 flex flex-col">
   105→      {/* Header */}
   106→      <header className="bg-white border-b border-gray-200 px-6 py-2 flex-shrink-0">
   107→        <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
   108→          <div className="flex items-center space-x-4">
   109→            <div className="flex items-center">
   110→              <Image src="/images/logo.png" alt="Logo" width={36} height={36} />
   111→              <span className="ml-2 text-xl font-semibold text-gray-800">Holo Hive</span>
   112→              {latestVersion && (
   113→                <Badge
   114→                  variant="secondary"
   115→                  className="ml-2 cursor-pointer bg-[#3e8692]/10 text-[#3e8692] hover:bg-[#3e8692]/20 transition-colors"
   116→                  onClick={() => setIsChangelogOpen(true)}
   117→                >
   118→                  v{latestVersion}
   119→                </Badge>
   120→              )}
   121→            </div>
   122→          </div>
   123→          <div className="flex items-center space-x-4">
   124→              <DropdownMenu>
   125→              <DropdownMenuTrigger asChild>
   126→                <Button
   127→                  variant="ghost"
   128→                  className="h-8 w-8 p-0 rounded-full hover:bg-transparent active:bg-transparent focus:bg-transparent focus-visible:ring-0 data-[state=open]:bg-transparent"
   129→                >
   130→                  <Avatar className="h-8 w-8">
   131→                    {userProfile?.profile_photo_url ? (
   132→                      <AvatarImage src={userProfile.profile_photo_url} alt={userProfile?.name || userProfile?.email || 'User'} />
   133→                    ) : null}
   134→                    <AvatarFallback className="bg-gray-200 text-gray-800 text-xs font-semibold">
   135→                      {getUserInitials()}
   136→                    </AvatarFallback>
   137→                  </Avatar>
   138→                </Button>
   139→              </DropdownMenuTrigger>
   140→              <DropdownMenuContent align="end">
   141→                <DropdownMenuItem onClick={() => router.push('/settings')}>
   142→                  <Settings className="h-4 w-4 mr-2" />
   143→                  Settings
   144→                </DropdownMenuItem>
   145→                <DropdownMenuSeparator />
   146→                <DropdownMenuItem onClick={signOut}>
   147→                  <LogOut className="h-4 w-4 mr-2" />
   148→                  Sign out
   149→                </DropdownMenuItem>
   150→              </DropdownMenuContent>
   151→            </DropdownMenu>
   152→          </div>
   153→        </div>
   154→      </header>
   155→      <div className="flex flex-1 overflow-hidden">
   156→        {/* Sidebar */}
   157→        <aside className={`${isSidebarCollapsed ? 'w-16' : 'w-64'} bg-white border-r border-gray-200 flex-shrink-0 transition-all duration-300 ease-in-out`}>
   158→          <div className="flex flex-col h-full">
   159→            {/* Navigation */}
   160→            <nav className="p-4 space-y-4 flex-1 overflow-y-auto">
   161→              {/* Holo GPT - Top of sidebar */}
   162→              <div className="space-y-2">
   163→                <Link href="/chat" legacyBehavior>
   164→                  <Button
   165→                    asChild
   166→                    variant={pathname.startsWith('/chat') ? 'default' : 'ghost'}
   167→                    className={`w-full ${isSidebarCollapsed ? 'justify-center px-0' : 'justify-start'} hover:opacity-90`}
   168→                    style={pathname.startsWith('/chat') ? { backgroundColor: '#3e8692', color: 'white' } : {}}
   169→                    title={isSidebarCollapsed ? 'Holo GPT' : undefined}
   170→                  >
   171→                    <span>
   172→                      <Sparkles className={`h-4 w-4 ${!isSidebarCollapsed ? 'mr-2' : ''}`} />
   173→                      {!isSidebarCollapsed && 'Holo GPT'}
   174→                    </span>
   175→                  </Button>
   176→                </Link>
   177→              </div>
   178→
   179→              {/* People Section */}
   180→              {!isSidebarCollapsed && (
   181→                <div className="flex items-center space-x-2">
   182→                  <User className="h-4 w-4 text-gray-400" />
   183→                  <div className="flex-1 h-px bg-gray-200"></div>
   184→                </div>
   185→              )}
   186→              <div className="space-y-2">
   187→                {/* Team tab */}
   188→                <Link href="/team" legacyBehavior>
   189→                  <Button
   190→                    asChild
   191→                    variant={pathname.startsWith('/team') ? 'default' : 'ghost'}
   192→                    className={`w-full ${isSidebarCollapsed ? 'justify-center px-0' : 'justify-start'} hover:opacity-90`}
   193→                    style={pathname.startsWith('/team') ? { backgroundColor: '#3e8692', color: 'white' } : {}}
   194→                    title={isSidebarCollapsed ? 'Team' : undefined}
   195→                  >
   196→                    <span>
   197→                      <Shield className={`h-4 w-4 ${!isSidebarCollapsed ? 'mr-2' : ''}`} />
   198→                      {!isSidebarCollapsed && 'Team'}
   199→                    </span>
   200→                  </Button>
```

   393→                  <FileText className="h-4 w-4 text-gray-400" />
   394→                  <div className="flex-1 h-px bg-gray-200"></div>
   395→                </div>
   396→              )}
   397→              <div className="space-y-2">
   398→                {/* Delivery Logs tab */}
   399→                <Link href="/delivery-logs" legacyBehavior>
```
</details></tool-use>

   915→  const formatCurrency = (value: number) => {
   916→    return new Intl.NumberFormat('en-US', {
   917→      style: 'currency',
   918→      currency: 'USD',
   919→      minimumFractionDigits: 0,
   920→      maximumFractionDigits: 0
   921→    }).format(value);
   922→  };
   923→
   924→  const formatDate = (dateString: string) => {
   925→    return new Date(dateString).toLocaleString('en-US', {
   926→      month: 'short',
   927→      day: 'numeric',
   928→      year: 'numeric',
   929→      hour: '2-digit',
   930→      minute: '2-digit'
   931→    });
   932→  };
   933→
   934→  const formatShortDate = (dateString: string) => {
   935→    return new Date(dateString).toLocaleDateString('en-US', {
   936→      month: 'short',
   937→      day: 'numeric'
   938→    });
   939→  };
   940→
   941→  const formatSource = (source: string) => {
   942→    return source
   943→      .split('_')
   944→      .map(word => word.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + word.slice(1))
   945→      .join(' ');
   946→  };
   947→
   948→  const getNextStage = (currentStage: OpportunityStage): OpportunityStage | null => {
   949→    const leadIndex = leadStages.indexOf(currentStage);
   950→    const dealIndex = dealStages.indexOf(currentStage);
   951→
   952→    if (leadIndex >= 0 && leadIndex < leadStages.length - 1) {
   953→      return leadStages[leadIndex + 1];
   954→    }
   955→    if (dealIndex >= 0 && dealIndex < dealStages.length - 1) {
   956→      return dealStages[dealIndex + 1];
   957→    }
   958→    return null;
   959→  };
   960→
   961→  // Get linked contacts for an opportunity

   963→    return allContactLinks.filter(link => link.opportunity_id === oppId);
   964→  };
   965→
   966→  // Get primary contact for an opportunity
   967→  const getPrimaryContact = (oppId: string) => {
   968→    const links = getOpportunityContacts(oppId);
   969→    return links.find(l => l.is_primary)?.contact || links[0]?.contact || null;
   970→  };

   975→      case 'outreach': return outreachStages;
   976→      case 'leads': return leadStages;
   977→      case 'deals': return dealStages;
   978→      case 'accounts': return accountStages;
   979→      default: return leadStages;
   980→    }
   981→  })();
   982→
   983→  // Get current view mode based on active tab
   984→  const currentViewMode = (() => {
   985→    switch (activeTab) {
   986→      case 'outreach': return outreachViewMode;
   987→      case 'leads': return leadsViewMode;
   988→      case 'deals': return dealsViewMode;
   989→      case 'accounts': return accountsViewMode;
   990→      default: return leadsViewMode;
   991→    }
   992→  })();
   993→
   994→  const setCurrentViewMode = (mode: 'kanban' | 'table') => {

   996→      case 'outreach': setOutreachViewMode(mode); break;
   997→      case 'leads': setLeadsViewMode(mode); break;
   998→      case 'deals': setDealsViewMode(mode); break;
   999→      case 'accounts': setAccountsViewMode(mode); break;
  1000→    }
  1001→  };
  1002→
  1003→  const currentOpportunities = filteredOpportunities.filter(o => {
  1004→    // Outreach tab: show only 'new' stage with source 'cold_outreach'
  1005→    if (activeTab === 'outreach') {
  1006→      return o.stage === 'new' && o.source === 'cold_outreach';
  1007→    }
  1008→    // Leads tab: show lead stages but exclude cold_outreach new leads (they're in outreach)

  1011→        return false;
  1012→      }
  1013→      return leadStages.includes(o.stage);
  1014→    }
  1015→    // Deals and Accounts: filter by their respective stages
  1016→    return currentStages.includes(o.stage);

  1020→  // RENDER: Opportunity Card Content
  1021→  // ============================================
  1022→  const renderOpportunityCardContent = (opp: CRMOpportunity, isDeal: boolean = false, isDragging: boolean = false) => {
  1023→    const colors = stageColors[opp.stage] || stageColors.new;
  1024→
  1025→    return (
  1026→      <Card
  1027→        className={`group hover:shadow-md transition-all duration-200 border-l-4 ${colors.border} ${isDragging ? 'shadow-lg ring-2 ring-blue-400 opacity-90' : ''}`}
  1028→      >
  1029→        <CardContent className="p-4">
  1030→          <div className="flex items-start justify-between gap-2">
  1031→            <div className="flex-1 min-w-0">
  1032→              {/* Name with drag handle */}
  1033→              <div className="flex items-center gap-2">
  1034→                <GripVertical className="h-4 w-4 text-gray-300 flex-shrink-0 cursor-grab active:cursor-grabbing" />
  1035→                <Building2 className="h-4 w-4 text-gray-400 flex-shrink-0" />
  1036→                <p className="font-medium text-gray-900 truncate">{opp.name}</p>
  1037→              </div>
  1038→
  1039→              {/* Deal Value */}
  1040→              {isDeal && opp.deal_value && (
  1041→                <p className="text-lg font-semibold text-emerald-600 mt-2">
  1042→                  {formatCurrency(opp.deal_value)}
  1043→                </p>
  1044→              )}
  1045→
  1046→              {/* Badges: Source, Account Type, Affiliate */}
  1047→              <div className="flex flex-wrap gap-1.5 mt-2">
  1048→                {opp.source && (
  1049→                  <Badge variant="outline" className="text-xs bg-white">
  1050→                    {formatSource(opp.source)}
  1051→                  </Badge>
  1052→                )}
  1053→                {opp.account_type && (
  1054→                  <Badge variant="outline" className="text-xs bg-white">
  1055→                    {formatSource(opp.account_type)}
  1056→                  </Badge>
  1057→                )}
  1058→                {opp.affiliate && (
  1059→                  <Badge
  1060→                    className="text-xs"
  1061→                    style={{ backgroundColor: '#3e8692', color: 'white' }}
  1062→                  >
  1063→                    {opp.affiliate.name}
  1064→                  </Badge>
  1065→                )}
  1066→              </div>
  1067→
  1068→              {/* Details: Referrer, Group Chat, Notes */}
  1069→              <div className="mt-3 space-y-1.5 text-xs text-gray-500">
  1070→                {opp.referrer && (
  1071→                  <div className="flex items-center gap-1.5">
  1072→                    <Users className="h-3 w-3 flex-shrink-0" />
  1073→                    <span>Referred by: {opp.referrer}</span>
  1074→                  </div>
  1075→                )}
  1076→                {opp.gc && (
  1077→                  <div className="flex flex-col gap-1">
  1078→                    <div className="flex items-center gap-1.5">
  1079→                      <MessageSquare className="h-3 w-3 flex-shrink-0 text-blue-500" />
  1080→                      <span className="font-medium">TG Connected</span>
  1081→                    </div>
  1082→                    <div className="ml-4 text-xs space-y-0.5">
  1083→                      <div className="flex items-center gap-1 text-gray-500">
  1084→                        <span>Lead:</span>
  1085→                        <span className={opp.last_message_at ? 'text-gray-700' : 'text-gray-400'}>
  1086→                          {opp.last_message_at ? formatShortDate(opp.last_message_at) : '-'}
  1087→                        </span>
  1088→                      </div>
  1089→                      <div className="flex items-center gap-1 text-gray-500">
  1090→                        <span>Team:</span>
  1091→                        <span className={opp.last_team_message_at ? 'text-gray-700' : 'text-gray-400'}>
  1092→                          {opp.last_team_message_at ? formatShortDate(opp.last_team_message_at) : '-'}
  1093→                        </span>
  1094→                      </div>
  1095→                      <div className="flex items-center gap-1 text-gray-500">
  1096→                        <span>Bot:</span>
  1097→                        <span className={opp.last_reply_at ? 'text-gray-700' : 'text-gray-400'}>
  1098→                          {opp.last_reply_at ? formatShortDate(opp.last_reply_at) : '-'}
  1099→                        </span>
  1100→                      </div>
  1101→                    </div>
  1102→                  </div>
  1103→                )}
  1104→                {opp.notes && (
  1105→                  <div className="flex items-start gap-1.5">
  1106→                    <Edit className="h-3 w-3 flex-shrink-0 mt-0.5" />
  1107→                    <span className="line-clamp-2">{opp.notes}</span>
  1108→                  </div>
  1109→                )}
  1110→              </div>
  1111→
  1112→              {/* Linked Contacts */}
  1113→              {(() => {
  1114→                const oppContacts = getOpportunityContacts(opp.id);
  1115→                if (oppContacts.length === 0) return null;
  1116→                const primaryContact = oppContacts.find(l => l.is_primary)?.contact || oppContacts[0]?.contact;
  1117→                return (
  1118→                  <div className="mt-3 pt-2 border-t border-gray-100">
  1119→                    <div className="flex items-center gap-2 text-xs">
  1120→                      <Users className="h-3 w-3 text-blue-500 flex-shrink-0" />
  1121→                      <span className="font-medium text-gray-700">{primaryContact?.name}</span>
  1122→                      {oppContacts.length > 1 && (
  1123→                        <Badge variant="secondary" className="text-xs px-1.5 py-0">
  1124→                          +{oppContacts.length - 1}
  1125→                        </Badge>
  1126→                      )}
  1127→                    </div>
  1128→                    {primaryContact?.email && (
  1129→                      <div className="flex items-center gap-1.5 text-xs text-gray-500 mt-1 ml-5">
  1130→                        <Mail className="h-3 w-3" />
  1131→                        <span className="truncate">{primaryContact.email}</span>
  1132→                      </div>
  1133→                    )}
  1134→                    {primaryContact?.telegram_id && !primaryContact?.email && (
  1135→                      <div className="flex items-center gap-1.5 text-xs text-gray-500 mt-1 ml-5">
  1136→                        <MessageSquare className="h-3 w-3" />
  1137→                        <span>@{primaryContact.telegram_id}</span>
  1138→                      </div>
  1139→                    )}
  1140→                  </div>
  1141→                );
  1142→              })()}
  1143→
  1144→            </div>
  1145→
  1146→            {!isDragging && renderActionMenu(opp)}
  1147→          </div>
  1148→        </CardContent>
  1149→      </Card>
  1150→    );
  1151→  };
  1152→
  1153→  // ============================================
  1154→  // RENDER: Draggable Opportunity Card
  1155→  // ============================================
  1156→  const DraggableCard = ({ opp, isDeal }: { opp: CRMOpportunity; isDeal: boolean }) => {
  1157→    const {
  1158→      attributes,
  1159→      listeners,
  1160→      setNodeRef,
  1161→      transform,
  1162→      transition,
  1163→      isDragging,
  1164→    } = useSortable({ id: opp.id });
  1165→
  1166→    const style = {
  1167→      transform: CSS.Transform.toString(transform),
  1168→      transition,
  1169→      opacity: isDragging ? 0.5 : 1,
  1170→    };
  1171→
  1172→    return (
  1173→      <div ref={setNodeRef} style={style} {...attributes} {...listeners}>
  1174→        {renderOpportunityCardContent(opp, isDeal, isDragging)}
  1175→      </div>
  1176→    );
  1177→  };
  1178→
  1179→  // ============================================
  1180→  // RENDER: Action Menu (shared across views)
  1181→  // ============================================
  1182→  const renderActionMenu = (opp: CRMOpportunity) => (
  1183→    <DropdownMenu>
  1184→      <DropdownMenuTrigger asChild>
  1185→        <Button
  1186→          variant="ghost"
  1187→          size="sm"
  1188→          className="h-8 w-8 p-0"
  1189→        >
  1190→          <MoreHorizontal className="h-4 w-4" />
  1191→        </Button>
  1192→      </DropdownMenuTrigger>
  1193→      <DropdownMenuContent align="end" className="w-48">
  1194→        <DropdownMenuItem onClick={() => handleEditOpportunity(opp)}>
  1195→          <Edit className="h-4 w-4 mr-2" />
  1196→          Edit
  1197→        </DropdownMenuItem>
  1198→        <DropdownMenuItem onClick={() => handleOpenContactLink(opp)}>
  1199→          <Users className="h-4 w-4 mr-2" />
```
</details></tool-use>

  1329→    const renderEditableText = (opp: CRMOpportunity, field: string, value: string | null | undefined, className?: string) => {

  1333→        return (
  1334→          <Input

  1336→            onChange={(e) => setEditingValue(e.target.value)}
  1337→            onBlur={() => handleInlineUpdate(opp.id, field, editingValue)}
  1338→            onKeyDown={(e) => handleKeyDown(e, opp.id, field)}
  1339→            className="h-8 text-sm auth-input"
  1340→            autoFocus
  1341→          />
  1342→        );
  1343→      }

  1345→      return (
  1346→        <div
  1347→          onClick={() => startEditing(opp.id, field, value)}

  1349→        >
  1350→          {value || <span className="text-gray-400">-</span>}
  1351→        </div>
  1352→      );
  1353→    };
  1354→

  1365→            onKeyDown={(e) => handleKeyDown(e, opp.id, field)}
  1366→            className="h-8 text-sm text-right auth-input"
  1367→            autoFocus
  1368→          />
  1369→        );
  1370→      }
  1371→
  1372→      return (

  1376→        >

  1381→          )}
  1382→        </div>
  1383→      );
  1384→    };
  1385→
  1386→    // Draggable table row component
  1387→    const DraggableTableRow = ({ opp, index }: { opp: CRMOpportunity; index: number }) => {
  1388→      const {
  1389→        attributes,
  1390→        listeners,
  1391→        setNodeRef,
  1392→        transform,
  1393→        transition,
  1394→        isDragging,
  1395→      } = useSortable({ id: opp.id });
  1396→
  1397→      const style: React.CSSProperties = {
  1398→        transform: CSS.Translate.toString(transform),
  1399→        transition,
  1400→        opacity: isDragging ? 0.5 : 1,
  1401→        position: 'relative' as const,
  1402→        zIndex: isDragging ? 1 : 0,
  1403→      };
  1404→
  1405→      return (
  1406→        <TableRow ref={setNodeRef} style={style} className={`group hover:bg-gray-50 ${isDragging ? 'bg-blue-50 shadow-lg' : ''}`}>
  1407→          <TableCell className="w-10">
  1408→            <div
  1409→              {...attributes}
  1410→              {...listeners}
  1411→              className="cursor-grab active:cursor-grabbing p-1 hover:bg-gray-100 rounded"
  1412→            >
  1413→              <GripVertical className="h-4 w-4 text-gray-400" />
  1414→            </div>
  1415→          </TableCell>
  1416→          <TableCell className="text-gray-500 text-sm w-10">
  1417→            {(() => {
  1418→              const isChecked = selectedOpportunities.includes(opp.id);
  1419→              return (
  1420→                <div className="flex items-center justify-center">
  1421→                  {isChecked ? (
  1422→                    <Checkbox
  1423→                      checked={true}
  1424→                      onCheckedChange={(checked) => {
  1425→                        if (checked) {
  1426→                          setSelectedOpportunities(prev => [...prev, opp.id]);
  1427→                        } else {
  1428→                          setSelectedOpportunities(prev => prev.filter(id => id !== opp.id));
  1429→                        }
  1430→                      }}
  1431→                    />
  1432→                  ) : (
  1433→                    <>
  1434→                      <span className="block group-hover:hidden">{index + 1}</span>
  1435→                      <span className="hidden group-hover:flex">
  1436→                        <Checkbox
  1437→                          checked={false}
  1438→                          onCheckedChange={(checked) => {
  1439→                            if (checked) {
  1440→                              setSelectedOpportunities(prev => [...prev, opp.id]);
  1441→                            }
  1442→                          }}
  1443→                        />
  1444→                      </span>
  1445→                    </>
  1446→                  )}
  1447→                </div>
  1448→              );
  1449→            })()}
  1450→          </TableCell>
  1451→          <TableCell>
  1452→            {editingCell?.id === opp.id && editingCell?.field === 'name' ? (
  1453→              <Input
  1454→                value={editingValue}
  1455→                onChange={(e) => setEditingValue(e.target.value)}
  1456→                onBlur={() => handleInlineUpdate(opp.id, 'name', editingValue)}
  1457→                onKeyDown={(e) => handleKeyDown(e, opp.id, 'name')}
  1458→                className="h-8 text-sm font-medium auth-input"
  1459→                autoFocus
  1460→              />
  1461→            ) : (
  1462→              <div
  1463→                onClick={() => startEditing(opp.id, 'name', opp.name)}
  1464→                className="flex items-center gap-2 cursor-pointer hover:bg-gray-100 rounded px-2 py-1 -mx-2 -my-1"
  1465→              >
  1466→                <Building2 className="h-4 w-4 text-gray-400" />
  1467→                <span className="font-medium">{opp.name}</span>
  1468→              </div>
  1469→            )}
  1470→          </TableCell>
  1471→          <TableCell>
  1472→            <Select
  1473→              value={opp.stage}
  1474→              onValueChange={(v) => handleMoveStage(opp, v as OpportunityStage)}
  1475→            >
  1476→              <SelectTrigger className={`w-32 h-8 ${stageColors[opp.stage].bg} ${stageColors[opp.stage].text} border-none text-xs font-medium`}>
  1477→                <SelectValue />
  1478→              </SelectTrigger>
  1479→              <SelectContent>
  1480→                {currentStages.map((stage) => (
  1481→                  <SelectItem key={stage} value={stage}>
  1482→                    {stageLabels[stage]}
  1483→                  </SelectItem>
  1484→                ))}
  1485→              </SelectContent>
  1486→            </Select>
  1487→          </TableCell>
  1488→          <TableCell>
  1489→            <Select
  1490→              value={opp.owner_id || 'none'}
  1491→              onValueChange={(v) => handleInlineUpdate(opp.id, 'owner_id', v === 'none' ? null : v)}
  1492→            >
  1493→              <SelectTrigger className="w-32 h-8 text-xs auth-input">
  1494→                <SelectValue placeholder="Select">
  1495→                  {opp.owner_id ? (users.find(u => u.id === opp.owner_id)?.name || users.find(u => u.id === opp.owner_id)?.email || '-') : <span className="text-gray-400">-</span>}
  1496→                </SelectValue>
  1497→              </SelectTrigger>
  1498→              <SelectContent>
  1499→                <SelectItem value="none">None</SelectItem>
```

## App Page Creation Patterns Analysis

I've completed a thorough exploration of your app's navigation and page structure. Here's what I found:

---

### 1. Main Layout Structure (`/app/layout.tsx`)

**Root Layout File:**
- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/layout.tsx` (1133 bytes)
- Uses Next.js 13+ App Router
- Sets up global providers:
  - `AuthProvider` - manages authentication
  - `ChangelogProvider` - handles changelog display
  - `Toaster` - for toast notifications
- Applies `inter` font from Google Fonts
- Sets metadata (title: "Holo Hive Portal")

---

### 2. Navigation & Sidebar Component

**Key File:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/components/Sidebar.tsx` (30KB)

The Sidebar is the central navigation hub. It includes:

**Features:**
- Toggle collapse/expand functionality (saves to localStorage)
- Context-aware active route highlighting using `usePathname()`
- User profile dropdown in header
- Organized into semantic sections with dividers

**Navigation Sections (in order):**

1. **Holo GPT** (top)
   - `/chat` - AI assistant

2. **People Section**
   - `/team` - Team members
   - `/clients` - Client management

3. **KOLs Section**
   - `/kols` - KOL database
   - `/lists` - List management
   - `/campaigns` - Campaign management

4. **CRM Section**
   - `/crm/pipeline` - Deal pipeline
   - `/crm/network` - Relationship network
   - `/crm/contacts` - Contact management
   - `/crm/telegram` - Telegram chats (super_admin only)

5. **Workspace Section** (Andy testing only)
   - `/daily-standup` - Daily stand-ups
   - `/tasks` - Task management

6. **Documents Section**
   - `/delivery-logs` - Delivery tracking
   - `/forms` - Forms management (admin+ only)
   - `/links` - Link management
   - `/templates` - Message templates
   - `/sops` - Standard operating procedures (admin+ only)

7. **Admin Section**
   - `/admin/field-options` - Field configuration
   - `/admin/changelog` - Changelog management (super_admin only)
   - `/archive` - Archived items

---

### 3. Page Creation Pattern

All protected pages follow this consistent structure:

**Layout File Pattern:** `app/[route]/layout.tsx`
```tsx
'use client';
import { ProtectedRoute } from '@/components/auth/ProtectedRoute';
import Sidebar from '@/components/Sidebar';

export default function SectionLayout({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {
  return (
    <ProtectedRoute>
      <Sidebar>{children}</Sidebar>
    </ProtectedRoute>
  );
}
```

**Page File Pattern:** `app/[route]/page.tsx`
```tsx
'use client';

import { useState, useEffect } from 'react';
// Import UI components from '@/components/ui/'
// Import services from '@/lib/'
// Import hooks from '@/hooks/'

export default function PageName() {
  const { user, userProfile } = useAuth();
  const { toast } = useToast();
  // ... component logic
}
```

**Common Imports in Page Files:**
- React hooks: `useState`, `useEffect`, `useMemo`, `useCallback`
- UI components from `/components/ui/` (button, dialog, input, etc.)
- Icons from `lucide-react`
- Auth context: `useAuth` from `@/contexts/AuthContext`
- Toast: `useToast` from `@/hooks/use-toast`
- Services: `supabase`, `UserService`, `ClientService`, etc. from `/lib/`

---

### 4. Complete App Directory Structure

All top-level routes under `app/`:

```
/app
├── admin/                    (admin pages)
├── ai-insights/             (AI insights)
├── api/                      (API routes)
├── archive/                  (archived items)
├── auth/                     (authentication pages)
├── campaigns/                (campaign management)
├── channel-partners/         (partner management)
├── chat/                     (AI chat/Holo GPT)
├── clients/                  (client management with sub-pages)
├── crm/                      (CRM with sub-routes: pipeline, contacts, network, telegram)
├── daily-standup/            (daily stand-up tracking)
├── delivery-logs/            (delivery tracking)
├── forms/                    (form management)
├── kols/                     (KOL/influencer management)
├── links/                    (link management)
├── lists/                    (list management)
├── public/                   (public-facing pages)
├── settings/                 (user settings)
├── sops/                     (standard operating procedures)
├── tasks/                    (task management)
├── team/                     (team management)
├── templates/                (message templates)
├── test-ai/                  (AI testing)
├── layout.tsx               (root layout)
└── page.tsx                 (home/redirect page)
```

---

### 5. Shared UI Components

Located in `/components/ui/` (51 components available):

**Most Used in Pages:**
- `button.tsx` - Primary UI button
- `dialog.tsx` / `alert-dialog.tsx` - Modal dialogs
- `input.tsx` / `textarea.tsx` - Form inputs
- `select.tsx` - Dropdown selects
- `table.tsx` - Data tables
- `card.tsx` - Card containers
- `badge.tsx` - Badge labels
- `tabs.tsx` - Tab navigation
- `checkbox.tsx` - Checkboxes
- `popover.tsx` - Popover menus
- `command.tsx` - Command palettes
- `dropdown-menu.tsx` - Dropdown menus
- `calendar.tsx` - Date pickers
- `skeleton.tsx` - Loading placeholders
- `scroll-area.tsx` - Scrollable areas
- `tooltip.tsx` - Tooltips
- `toast.tsx` / `toaster.tsx` - Notifications

**Custom Components:**
- `/components/auth/ProtectedRoute.tsx` - Auth wrapper
- `/components/Sidebar.tsx` - Main navigation
- `/components/ChunkErrorHandler.tsx` - Error handling
- `/components/changelog/ChangelogModal.tsx` - Changelog display

---

### 6. Key Libraries & Patterns

**State Management:**
- React hooks (useState, useEffect, useMemo, useCallback)
- Context API (AuthProvider, ChangelogProvider)

**UI Framework:**
- shadcn/ui components (Radix UI based)
- Tailwind CSS for styling
- Lucide icons

**Data Fetching:**
- Supabase client (`@/lib/supabase`)
- Service classes from `/lib/` (UserService, ClientService, KOLService, CRMService, etc.)

**Form Handling:**
- React state management
- Controlled inputs with useState

**Authentication:**
- Protected routes via `ProtectedRoute` component
- Role-based access (super_admin, admin, client)
- User profile from `useAuth()` hook

---

### Summary for Adding a New Page

To add a new page, follow these steps:

1. **Create directory structure:**
   ```
   app/your-new-page/
   ├── layout.tsx
   └── page.tsx
   ```

2. **Create `layout.tsx`:**
   - Wrap with `<ProtectedRoute>`
   - Include `<Sidebar>{children}</Sidebar>`
   - Export as default function component

3. **Create `page.tsx`:**
   - Mark with `'use client'`
   - Import necessary UI components from `@/components/ui/`
   - Use `useAuth()` for user context
   - Use `useToast()` for notifications
   - Follow the component pattern with hooks and services

4. **Add navigation item to Sidebar.tsx:**
   - Add to appropriate section (or create new section with divider)
   - Use `Link` with `legacyBehavior` and `Button` as child
   - Style active state using `pathname.startsWith('/your-new-page')`
   - Add icon from lucide-react
   - Consider role-based visibility if needed

5. **Add Sidebar UI components** as needed from `/components/ui/`

The architecture is very consistent, making it straightforward to add new pages following the established patterns.

Perfect! Now I have enough information to provide a comprehensive report. Let me compile my findings:

## DETAILED CRM PIPELINE STRUCTURE ANALYSIS

Based on my thorough exploration, here's the complete CRM pipeline architecture:

---

### 1. PAGE STRUCTURE: `/app/crm/pipeline/page.tsx`

**File Location:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/crm/pipeline/page.tsx`
**File Size:** 3,082 lines | **Client Component:** Yes (uses 'use client')

#### Core Organization:

**State Management (Lines 65-159):**
- Main opportunity data: `opportunities`, `affiliates`, `contacts`, `users`, `clients`
- UI modes: 4 independent view modes (kanban/table) for each tab (outreach, leads, deals, accounts)
- 7 dialogs for: new opportunity, contact linking, history, delete confirmation, bulk delete, convert to deal, convert to account
- Inline editing state and bulk edit state for table view
- Collapsed stages state (`collapsedStages` - Set<OpportunityStage>)
- Drag-and-drop active state

**Stage Definitions (Lines 267-298):**
```
outreachStages:   ['new']                                                    (cold_outreach source)
leadStages:       ['new', 'contacted', 'qualified', 'unqualified', 'nurture', 'dead']
dealStages:       ['deal_qualified', 'proposal', 'negotiation', 'contract', 'closed_won', 'closed_lost']
accountStages:    ['account_active', 'account_churned']
```

**Stage Colors (Lines 300-316):**
Each stage has a color palette with `bg`, `text`, `border`, and `solid` properties for visual distinction.

**Tab-Based Filtering (Lines 1003-1017):**
The page supports 4 tabs that control which opportunities are displayed:
- `outreach`: Only new stage with cold_outreach source
- `leads`: Lead stages (excluding cold_outreach)
- `deals`: Deal stages
- `accounts`: Account stages

---

### 2. KEY COMPONENTS & VIEW RENDERING

#### Kanban View (Lines 1944+)
- **Component:** `DroppableColumn` (Lines 1242-1318)
- **Features:**
  - Collapsible stage columns
  - Drag-and-drop between stages
  - Stage totals and deal values displayed
  - DndKit integration with vertical sorting
  - Empty state handling
  
#### Table View (Lines 1323+)
- **Component:** `DraggableTableRow` (Lines 1387-1670)
- **Features:**
  - Inline editing (name, notes, deal_value, etc.)
  - Dropdown selectors for stage and owner
  - Checkboxes for bulk selection
  - Row reordering with drag-and-drop
  - Cross-stage dragging support

#### Opportunity Card Content (Lines 1022-1151)
- **Display Fields:**
  - Name with drag handle
  - Deal value (for deals)
  - Badges: Source, Account Type, Affiliate
  - Referrer, Group Chat (Telegram), Notes
  - Linked contacts (primary contact highlighted)
  - Telegram integration indicators (last_message_at, last_team_message_at, last_reply_at)

#### Action Menu (Lines 1182-1237)
Dropdown actions:
- Edit
- Manage Contacts
- View History
- Mark as Contacted
- Move to next stage
- Mark as Won/Lost (contract stage only)
- Delete

---

### 3. CRM SERVICE DEFINITION: `/lib/crmService.ts`

**File Location:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/crmService.ts`
**Organization:** TypeScript types, interfaces, and static service class

#### Type Definitions (Lines 8-21):

**OpportunityStage Union Type:**
```typescript
'new' | 'contacted' | 'qualified' | 'unqualified' | 'nurture' | 'dead' |
'deal_qualified' | 'proposal' | 'negotiation' | 'contract' | 'closed_won' | 'closed_lost' |
'account_active' | 'account_at_risk' | 'account_churned'
```

**AccountType:** `'general' | 'channel' | 'campaign' | 'lite' | 'ad_hoc'`
**OpportunitySource:** `'referral' | 'inbound' | 'event' | 'cold_outreach'`

#### Interface: CRMOpportunity (Lines 45-73)

**Complete Field List (21 fields):**
| Field | Type | Notes |
|-------|------|-------|
| `id` | UUID | Primary key |
| `name` | TEXT | Required |
| `stage` | OpportunityStage | Required, default: 'new' |
| `account_type` | AccountType \| null | Set when qualified |
| `deal_value` | DECIMAL \| null | Set when becomes deal |
| `currency` | TEXT | Default: 'USD' |
| `last_contacted_at` | TIMESTAMP \| null | Manual contact tracking |
| `last_message_at` | TIMESTAMP \| null | Lead messaged in TG group |
| `last_reply_at` | TIMESTAMP \| null | Bot replied in TG group |
| `last_team_message_at` | TIMESTAMP \| null | Team messaged in TG group |
| `owner_id` | UUID \| null | Assigned user |
| `source` | OpportunitySource \| null | How it came in |
| `referrer` | TEXT \| null | Who referred |
| `gc` | TEXT \| null | Telegram group chat ID |
| `affiliate_id` | UUID \| null | FK to crm_affiliates |
| `client_id` | UUID \| null | Links to clients (accounts) |
| `scope` | TEXT \| null | For accounts: fundraising, advisory, kol_activation, gtm, bd_partnerships, apac |
| `notes` | TEXT \| null | Custom notes |
| `position` | NUMBER | For ordering within stage |
| `created_at` | TIMESTAMP | Auto |
| `updated_at` | TIMESTAMP | Auto |
| `qualified_at` | TIMESTAMP \| null | When transitioned to deal |
| `closed_at` | TIMESTAMP \| null | When won/lost |

**Joined Data:**
- `affiliate?: CRMAffiliate | null`
- `contacts?: CRMContact[]`
- `client?: { id: string; name: string } | null`

#### CRUD Methods (CRMService class):

**Opportunities (Lines 275-400):**
- `getAllOpportunities()` - Joins with affiliate & client
- `getOpportunitiesByStage(stages: OpportunityStage[])` - Filter by stages
- `getOpportunityById(id: string)`
- `createOpportunity(data: CreateOpportunityData)` - Records stage history
- `updateOpportunity(id: string, updates)` - Auto-sets qualified_at, closed_at; records history
- `deleteOpportunity(id: string)`
- `updateOpportunityPositions(positions)` - Batch update for drag-reorder

**Contact Linking (Lines 538-651):**
- `linkContactToOpportunity(contactId, opportunityId, role?, isPrimary?)`
- `getContactsForOpportunity(opportunityId)` - Joins with contact data
- `unlinkContact(linkId)`
- `getAllContactLinks()` - Fetches all with contact data

**Stage History (Lines 657-693):**
- `recordStageHistory(objectType, objectId, fromStage, toStage, notes)`
- `getStageHistory(objectType, objectId)`

**Analytics (Lines 794-826):**
- `getOpportunityMetrics()` - Returns: totalLeads, totalDeals, totalValue, wonValue, conversionRate

**Telegram Integration (Lines 836-890):**
- `sendTelegramMessage(opportunityId, message)` - Updates last_reply_at

**Other Utilities (Lines 699-715):**
- `updateLastContacted(type, id)` - Updates last_contacted_at

**Static Options (Lines 721-788):**
- `getOpportunityStages()` - Grouped by lead/deal/account
- `getAccountTypes()`
- `getOpportunitySources()`
- `getPartnerCategories()`
- `getAffiliateStatuses()`
- `getPartnerStatuses()`

---

### 4. DATABASE SCHEMA: `/sql/migrations/022_create_crm_tables.sql`

**Tables Created:**

#### crm_opportunities (Lines 48-88)
21 columns as listed above. Key constraints:
- Stage default: 'new'
- Currency default: 'USD'
- Position for ordering
- Indexes on: stage, owner_id, source, affiliate_id, created_at

#### crm_affiliates (Lines 10-42)
Statuses: new, active, inactive
Fields include commission_model, commission_rate, terms_of_interest, POC fields

#### crm_partners (Lines 95-129)
Categories: service_provider, investor_vc, project, individual
Can be linked to affiliates

#### crm_contacts (Lines 135-155)
Individual people: name, email, telegram_id, x_id, role, category

#### crm_contact_links (Lines 161-183)
Junction table linking contacts to opportunities/partners/affiliates
Constraint: only one link type per row

#### crm_stage_history (Lines 189-205)
Audit trail: from_stage, to_stage, changed_by, changed_at

---

### 5. ROUTING & NAVIGATION

**File:** `/components/Sidebar.tsx` (Lines 274-343)

**CRM Section in Navigation:**
```
CRM (Dollar Sign icon section):
├─ Pipeline  → /crm/pipeline    [TrendingUp icon]
├─ Network   → /crm/network     [Handshake icon]
├─ Contacts  → /crm/contacts    [UserPlus icon]
└─ TG Chats  → /crm/telegram    [MessageSquare icon] (super_admin only)
```

**Layout Structure:**
- Main layout: `/app/crm/pipeline/layout.tsx` (Lines 1-10)
  - Uses `<ProtectedRoute>` wrapper
  - Wraps children with `<Sidebar>` component
- All CRM pages have similar layout structure

---

### 6. COMPONENT DEPENDENCIES & IMPORTS

**UI Components Used:**
- Button, Card, Badge, Input, Dialog, Select, Tabs, Table, Checkbox, HoverCard, ScrollArea, Popover, Command, DropdownMenu, Textarea, Skeleton

**Icons (Lucide):**
- Plus, Search, Edit, Trash2, DollarSign, ArrowRight, Building2, GripVertical, Mail, MessageSquare, Filter, Phone, MoreHorizontal, History, TrendingUp, Target, Award, etc.

**Drag-and-Drop:**
- @dnd-kit/core (DndContext, DragOverlay, useSensor, useDroppable)
- @dnd-kit/sortable (useSortable, SortableContext, verticalListSortingStrategy)
- @dnd-kit/utilities (CSS transforms)
- @dnd-kit/modifiers (restrictToVerticalAxis)

**External Services:**
- Supabase for database operations
- UserService for fetching users
- ClientService for fetching clients

---

### 7. KEY FEATURES IMPLEMENTED

1. **Multi-Tab Pipeline** - Separate pipelines for outreach, leads, deals, accounts
2. **Dual View Modes** - Kanban (drag-drop columns) and Table (inline editable rows)
3. **Stage Auto-Actions** - Prompts to convert qualified leads to deals; closed deals to accounts
4. **Inline Editing** - Name, notes, deal value directly in table
5. **Bulk Operations** - Select multiple opportunities, bulk delete/edit
6. **Contact Linking** - Create/link/manage contacts per opportunity with primary contact
7. **Stage History Audit Trail** - Track all stage transitions with timestamps
8. **Telegram Integration** - Track last_message_at, last_team_message_at, last_reply_at
9. **Drag-Reordering** - Within-stage and cross-stage opportunity movement
10. **Metrics Dashboard** - Total leads, deals, values, won value, conversion rate
11. **Owner Assignment** - Dropdown to assign/change opportunity owner
12. **Source Tracking** - Filter by referral, inbound, event, cold_outreach
13. **Account Type Classification** - general, channel, campaign, lite, ad_hoc
14. **Affiliate Association** - Link opportunities to affiliates for commission tracking

---

### 8. FILTERING & SEARCH

**Implemented Filters (Lines 831-885):**
- Search term (case-insensitive opportunity name)
- Source filter (all, referral, inbound, event, cold_outreach)
- Account type filter (all, general, channel, campaign, lite, ad_hoc)
- Affiliate filter (all, or specific affiliate_id)

**Sorting Options (Lines 851-871):**
- position (default, drag-order)
- created_desc/created_asc
- name_asc/name_desc
- value_desc/value_asc

---

This comprehensive pipeline supports a complex sales funnel with leads graduating to deals and eventually to customer accounts, with full audit trails, contact management, and Telegram integration!

---

# Sales Pipeline v2 - Phase 1a Implementation Plan

## Overview

This plan adds a new "Sales Pipeline v2" page at `/crm/sales-pipeline` that implements the Sales Playbook CRM integration. The existing `/crm/pipeline` page remains untouched. Both pages share the same `crm_opportunities` table -- v2 adds new columns but never removes or renames existing ones.

---

## Architecture Decisions

**Route:** `/app/crm/sales-pipeline/page.tsx` -- separate Next.js page, not modifying the existing pipeline.

**Service layer:** Create a new file `lib/salesPipelineService.ts` rather than modifying `lib/crmService.ts`. The new service imports `supabase` from `lib/supabase.ts` directly and defines its own types, queries, and business logic for v2 stages. The existing `CRMService` class continues to work unchanged because the new columns are all nullable with defaults.

**Shared table, separate views:** Both pipelines query `crm_opportunities`. The v2 page filters to its own stages (`cold_dm`, `warm`, `tg_intro`, `booked`, `discovery_done`, `proposal_sent`, `proposal_call`, `v2_contract`, `v2_closed_won`). Old opportunities in old stages simply do not appear on the v2 page (and vice versa). The v2 page also shows `orbit` and `v2_closed_lost` as exit lanes.

**Stage naming convention:** New stages are prefixed or namespaced to avoid collision with existing stages. For example, use `v2_contract` and `v2_closed_won` / `v2_closed_lost` instead of reusing `contract`, `closed_won`, `closed_lost`. This prevents any filtering ambiguity. The `orbit` stage is entirely new so no prefix needed.

---

## Step 1: Database Migration (New Columns on `crm_opportunities`)

Create migration file: `sql/migrations/026_sales_pipeline_v2_columns.sql`

Add these nullable columns to `crm_opportunities`:

```sql
-- Sales Pipeline v2 fields (Phase 1a)
ALTER TABLE crm_opportunities
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS bucket TEXT,                          -- 'A', 'B', 'C'
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS temperature_score INTEGER DEFAULT 50, -- 0-100
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS dm_account TEXT DEFAULT 'sdr',        -- 'closer', 'sdr', 'other'
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS bump_number INTEGER DEFAULT 0,        -- 0-4
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS last_bump_date TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS warm_sub_state TEXT,                  -- 'interested', 'silent'
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS tg_handle TEXT,
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS calendly_sent_via TEXT,               -- 'dm', 'tg', 'not_yet'
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS calendly_sent_date TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS calendly_booked_date TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS gc_opened TEXT DEFAULT 'na',          -- 'na', 'not_yet', 'opened'
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS orbit_reason TEXT,                    -- 'no_response','not_now','ghost','has_team','hard_no'
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS closed_lost_reason TEXT,
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS dedup_key TEXT,                       -- lowercase(name + tg_handle or x_id)
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS next_meeting_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS next_meeting_type TEXT,               -- 'discovery','proposal_call','contract_review'
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS bucket_changed_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS discovery_call_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS proposal_sent_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE;

-- Indexes for v2 queries
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_crm_opportunities_bucket ON crm_opportunities(bucket);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_crm_opportunities_dm_account ON crm_opportunities(dm_account);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_crm_opportunities_dedup_key ON crm_opportunities(dedup_key);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_crm_opportunities_next_meeting ON crm_opportunities(next_meeting_at);
```

All new columns are nullable or have defaults, so existing data and the existing pipeline page are unaffected.

---

## Step 2: Database Migration (New `crm_activities` Table)

Create migration file: `sql/migrations/027_create_crm_activities.sql`

```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS crm_activities (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  opportunity_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES crm_opportunities(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  type TEXT NOT NULL,             -- 'call', 'message', 'meeting', 'proposal', 'note', 'bump'
  title TEXT NOT NULL,
  description TEXT,
  outcome TEXT,                   -- what happened
  next_step TEXT,                 -- BAMFAM: what's the next action
  next_step_date TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,  -- BAMFAM: when
  owner_id UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id),
  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_crm_activities_opportunity ON crm_activities(opportunity_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_crm_activities_type ON crm_activities(type);
CREATE INDEX idx_crm_activities_created ON crm_activities(created_at);

-- RLS policies (matching existing crm_ table pattern from migration 023)
ALTER TABLE crm_activities ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

CREATE POLICY "Authenticated users can read activities"
  ON crm_activities FOR SELECT
  TO authenticated
  USING (true);

CREATE POLICY "Authenticated users can insert activities"
  ON crm_activities FOR INSERT
  TO authenticated
  WITH CHECK (true);

CREATE POLICY "Authenticated users can update activities"
  ON crm_activities FOR UPDATE
  TO authenticated
  USING (true);

CREATE POLICY "Authenticated users can delete activities"
  ON crm_activities FOR DELETE
  TO authenticated
  USING (true);
```

Note: `crm_sequences` and `crm_sequence_steps` tables are deferred to Phase 1b/2. For Phase 1a, bump tracking is done via the `bump_number` and `last_bump_date` fields on the opportunity itself, not via a full sequence engine.

---

## Step 3: New Service File -- `lib/salesPipelineService.ts`

This file defines:

### Types

```typescript
// New v2 stage type (separate from OpportunityStage to avoid breaking existing type)
export type SalesPipelineStage =
  | 'cold_dm' | 'warm' | 'tg_intro' | 'booked'
  | 'discovery_done' | 'proposal_sent' | 'proposal_call'
  | 'v2_contract' | 'v2_closed_won'
  | 'orbit' | 'v2_closed_lost' | 'nurture';

export type Bucket = 'A' | 'B' | 'C';
export type DmAccount = 'closer' | 'sdr' | 'other';
export type WarmSubState = 'interested' | 'silent';
export type CalendlySentVia = 'dm' | 'tg' | 'not_yet';
export type GcOpened = 'na' | 'not_yet' | 'opened';
export type OrbitReason = 'no_response' | 'not_now' | 'ghost' | 'has_team' | 'hard_no';

export interface SalesPipelineOpportunity extends CRMOpportunity {
  // v2 fields (all from the same crm_opportunities row)
  bucket: Bucket | null;
  temperature_score: number;
  dm_account: DmAccount;
  bump_number: number;
  last_bump_date: string | null;
  warm_sub_state: WarmSubState | null;
  tg_handle: string | null;
  calendly_sent_via: CalendlySentVia | null;
  calendly_sent_date: string | null;
  calendly_booked_date: string | null;
  gc_opened: GcOpened;
  orbit_reason: OrbitReason | null;
  closed_lost_reason: string | null;
  dedup_key: string | null;
  next_meeting_at: string | null;
  next_meeting_type: string | null;
  bucket_changed_at: string | null;
  discovery_call_at: string | null;
  proposal_sent_at: string | null;
}

export interface CRMActivity {
  id: string;
  opportunity_id: string;
  type: 'call' | 'message' | 'meeting' | 'proposal' | 'note' | 'bump';
  title: string;
  description: string | null;
  outcome: string | null;
  next_step: string | null;
  next_step_date: string | null;
  owner_id: string | null;
  created_at: string;
}

export interface CreateSalesPipelineOpportunityData {
  name: string;
  stage?: SalesPipelineStage;
  dm_account?: DmAccount;
  bucket?: Bucket;
  temperature_score?: number;
  source?: string;
  owner_id?: string;
  tg_handle?: string;
  notes?: string;
  deal_value?: number;
  referrer?: string;
  affiliate_id?: string;
}
```

### Key Methods

```typescript
export class SalesPipelineService {
  static readonly V2_STAGES: SalesPipelineStage[] = [
    'cold_dm', 'warm', 'tg_intro', 'booked', 'discovery_done',
    'proposal_sent', 'proposal_call', 'v2_contract', 'v2_closed_won',
    'orbit', 'v2_closed_lost', 'nurture'
  ];

  // Fetch only v2 pipeline opportunities
  static async getAll(): Promise<SalesPipelineOpportunity[]>
  // Uses .in('stage', V2_STAGES)

  // Create with dedup check
  static async create(data: CreateSalesPipelineOpportunityData): Promise<SalesPipelineOpportunity>
  // Generates dedup_key = lowercase(name + tg_handle)
  // Checks for existing dedup_key before insert
  // Records stage history via CRMService.recordStageHistory

  // Update with stage-transition logic
  static async update(id: string, updates: Partial<...>): Promise<SalesPipelineOpportunity>
  // Sets discovery_call_at when moving to discovery_done
  // Sets proposal_sent_at when moving to proposal_sent
  // Sets closed_at when moving to v2_closed_won or v2_closed_lost
  // Records stage history

  // Bump tracking
  static async recordBump(id: string): Promise<SalesPipelineOpportunity>
  // Increments bump_number, sets last_bump_date
  // If bump_number reaches 4 and no reply after 7 days, suggests orbit

  // Activities CRUD
  static async getActivities(opportunityId: string): Promise<CRMActivity[]>
  static async createActivity(data: Omit<CRMActivity, 'id' | 'created_at'>): Promise<CRMActivity>

  // BAMFAM check
  static async getBAMFAMViolations(): Promise<SalesPipelineOpportunity[]>
  // Returns opportunities past discovery_done with no next_meeting_at or overdue next_meeting_at

  // Path routing helper
  static getAvailableStages(dmAccount: DmAccount): SalesPipelineStage[]
  // Path A (closer): cold_dm -> warm -> booked -> discovery_done -> proposal_sent -> proposal_call -> v2_contract -> v2_closed_won
  // Path B (sdr):    cold_dm -> warm -> tg_intro -> booked -> discovery_done -> proposal_sent -> proposal_call -> v2_contract -> v2_closed_won
  // Both can exit to orbit or v2_closed_lost at any stage
}
```

The service imports `supabase` from `@/lib/supabase` and the existing `CRMService` class only for calling `recordStageHistory`. It does not modify `CRMService`.

---

## Step 4: New Page -- `app/crm/sales-pipeline/page.tsx`

### Layout File: `app/crm/sales-pipeline/layout.tsx`

Identical pattern to the existing pipeline layout:

```typescript
import { ProtectedRoute } from '@/components/auth/ProtectedRoute';
import Sidebar from '@/components/Sidebar';

export default function SalesPipelineLayout({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {
  return (
    <ProtectedRoute>
      <Sidebar>{children}</Sidebar>
    </ProtectedRoute>
  );
}
```

### Page Structure

The page follows the exact same pattern as `/app/crm/pipeline/page.tsx` (`'use client'`, `useAuth()`, service calls in `useEffect`, etc.) but with these differences:

**Tabs:** Two tabs instead of four:
1. **Pipeline** -- the main kanban/table view with the v2 stages
2. **Orbit** -- shows opportunities in `orbit` stage, grouped by `orbit_reason`

**Kanban Columns (Pipeline tab):**
- Path A view: `cold_dm` -> `warm` -> `booked` -> `discovery_done` -> `proposal_sent` -> `proposal_call` -> `v2_contract` -> `v2_closed_won`
- Path B view: `cold_dm` -> `warm` -> `tg_intro` -> `booked` -> `discovery_done` -> `proposal_sent` -> `proposal_call` -> `v2_contract` -> `v2_closed_won`
- Toggle or filter at the top: "All Paths" | "Path A (Closer)" | "Path B (SDR)"
- When "All Paths" is selected, show all columns (including `tg_intro`)

**Opportunity Card (in Kanban):** Each card shows:
- Name (bold)
- Bucket badge: colored A (green), B (amber), C (gray)
- `dm_account` indicator: small icon/badge showing "Closer" or "SDR"
- Bump progress: for `cold_dm` stage, show "Bump 2/4" with progress dots
- Temperature score: small colored bar (green >70, amber 40-70, red <40)
- BAMFAM warning: if `next_meeting_at` is null and stage is past `discovery_done`, show orange warning icon
- `warm_sub_state`: if in `warm` stage, show "Interested" or "Silent" sub-badge
- Last contacted relative time

**DnD:** Same `@dnd-kit/core` pattern as existing pipeline. Drag between columns to change stage. When dragging to `orbit`, prompt for `orbit_reason`. When dragging to `v2_closed_lost`, prompt for `closed_lost_reason`.

**Create/Edit Dialog:** New opportunity form includes:
- Name (text input)
- DM Account (select: Closer / SDR / Other) -- this is the routing key
- Bucket (select: A / B / C)
- Temperature Score (slider or number input, 0-100)
- Source (select: referral / inbound / event / cold_outreach)
- TG Handle (text input)
- Owner (user select, same pattern as existing)
- Referrer (text input)
- Affiliate (combobox, same pattern as existing)
- Deal Value + Currency
- Notes (textarea)

**Activity Panel:** When clicking an opportunity card, open a slide-over or dialog that shows:
- Opportunity details (editable)
- Activity timeline (list of `crm_activities` for this opp, newest first)
- "Add Activity" button that opens a form: type (call/message/meeting/proposal/note/bump), title, description, outcome, next_step, next_step_date
- BAMFAM indicator at top: shows next meeting date or warning if missing

**Estimated size:** The page will likely be 1500-2000 lines initially. Can be broken into components later but for Phase 1a, a single-file approach matches the existing pipeline pattern.

---

## Step 5: Sidebar Navigation Update

In `components/Sidebar.tsx`, add a new link in the CRM section, between "Pipeline" and "Network":

```tsx
{/* Sales Pipeline v2 tab */}
<Link href="/crm/sales-pipeline" legacyBehavior>
  <Button
    asChild
    variant={pathname.startsWith('/crm/sales-pipeline') ? 'default' : 'ghost'}
    className={`w-full ${isSidebarCollapsed ? 'justify-center px-0' : 'justify-start'} hover:opacity-90`}
    style={pathname.startsWith('/crm/sales-pipeline') ? { backgroundColor: '#3e8692', color: 'white' } : {}}
    title={isSidebarCollapsed ? 'Sales' : undefined}
  >
    <span>
      <Target className={`h-4 w-4 ${!isSidebarCollapsed ? 'mr-2' : ''}`} />
      {!isSidebarCollapsed && 'Sales'}
    </span>
  </Button>
</Link>
```

The `Target` icon from Lucide is already imported in Sidebar.tsx (visible in the imports). The label "Sales" is short and distinguishes from the existing "Pipeline" link.

**Important:** The `pathname.startsWith('/crm/sales-pipeline')` check must come before the existing `/crm/pipeline` check in the rendered JSX, OR the existing pipeline check must be updated to use an exact match. Currently the sidebar uses `pathname.startsWith('/crm/pipeline')` which would also match `/crm/pipeline-v2` but NOT `/crm/sales-pipeline` (different prefix). So the current routing is safe as-is because the new path `/crm/sales-pipeline` does not start with `/crm/pipeline`.

---

## Step 6: Update `OpportunityStage` Type (Additive Only)

In `lib/crmService.ts`, extend the `OpportunityStage` type union to include the new stages. This is additive and does not break existing code:

```typescript
export type OpportunityStage =
  | 'new' | 'contacted' | 'qualified' | 'unqualified' | 'nurture' | 'dead'
  | 'deal_qualified' | 'proposal' | 'negotiation' | 'contract' | 'closed_won' | 'closed_lost'
  | 'account_active' | 'account_at_risk' | 'account_churned'
  // Sales Pipeline v2 stages
  | 'cold_dm' | 'warm' | 'tg_intro' | 'booked' | 'discovery_done'
  | 'proposal_sent' | 'proposal_call' | 'v2_contract' | 'v2_closed_won'
  | 'orbit' | 'v2_closed_lost';
```

The existing pipeline page uses `leadStages`, `dealStages`, `accountStages` arrays to filter which stages to show. Since the new stages are not in those arrays, they will never appear on the old page. The `stageLabels` and `stageColors` records will get TypeScript errors because they don't include the new stages -- fix by adding entries for the new stages to those records. The existing page will never render those entries since it never encounters those stages in its filtered arrays.

Alternatively, the `stageLabels` and `stageColors` records can be typed with `Partial<Record<OpportunityStage, ...>>` or the new stage labels/colors can be defined only in the v2 page. The simplest approach: add entries to `stageLabels` and `stageColors` in the existing page file for the new stages. They are inert -- never displayed -- but satisfy TypeScript.

---

## Potential Challenges

1. **TypeScript strictness on Record types:** Adding new stages to `OpportunityStage` will cause compile errors in the existing pipeline page's `stageLabels` and `stageColors` records (which are typed `Record<OpportunityStage, ...>`). These need entries added for the new stages. Alternatively, change the record types to use `Partial<Record<...>>` with appropriate null checks.

2. **Dedup key uniqueness:** The `dedup_key` field should have a unique index, but only for non-null values. Use `CREATE UNIQUE INDEX ... WHERE dedup_key IS NOT NULL` in the migration.

3. **Stage transition validation:** The service should validate that stage transitions follow the two-path routing rules (e.g., Path A cannot go through `tg_intro`). This is business logic in `SalesPipelineService.update()`.

4. **Existing opportunities:** Old opportunities (in stages like `new`, `contacted`, etc.) will not appear on the v2 page. A future "migration assistant" could help move them, but for Phase 1a, new opportunities are created directly in v2 stages.

5. **File size:** The existing pipeline page is 3,082 lines. The v2 page should aim for better modularity from the start, potentially extracting the Kanban board, opportunity cards, and activity panel into separate component files under a `components/sales-pipeline/` directory. However, following the existing pattern of a single page file is also acceptable for Phase 1a.

---

## Implementation Sequence

1. Run database migration (Step 1 + Step 2) -- add columns and create `crm_activities` table
2. Create `lib/salesPipelineService.ts` (Step 3) -- types and service methods
3. Update `OpportunityStage` in `lib/crmService.ts` (Step 6) -- additive type change
4. Create `app/crm/sales-pipeline/layout.tsx` and `app/crm/sales-pipeline/page.tsx` (Step 4)
5. Update `components/Sidebar.tsx` (Step 5) -- add nav link
6. Test that existing pipeline page still works unchanged

---

## What Phase 1a Does NOT Include (Deferred)

- Calendly webhook integration (Phase 1b)
- `crm_sequences` / `crm_sequence_steps` tables and sequence engine (Phase 2)
- Auto-bump automation (Phase 2 -- bumps are manually recorded in 1a)
- Sales Dashboard metrics page (Phase 2)
- Message template linking to stages/buckets (Phase 1b)
- AI integration (Phase 3)
- Trigger event scanner (Phase 3)
- Weekly rhythm report (Phase 2)

---

### Critical Files for Implementation

- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/crmService.ts` - Must extend OpportunityStage type union with new v2 stages; contains CRMService.recordStageHistory that v2 service will reuse
- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/crm/pipeline/page.tsx` - Reference pattern for the new page (kanban, DnD, dialogs, inline editing); also needs stageLabels/stageColors entries for new stages to satisfy TypeScript
- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/components/Sidebar.tsx` - Add new "Sales" nav link in CRM section (line ~282-296 area)
- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/sql/migrations/022_create_crm_tables.sql` - Reference for existing table schema and migration patterns
- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/docs/sales-playbook-crm-integration.html` - Authoritative spec for stages, fields, two-path routing, bucket system, and BAMFAM rules (lines 1634-1721 for schema, lines 1734-1808 for phase roadmap)

_**Agent - sidechain (2026-02-23 12:37:01Z)**_

Design a detailed implementation plan for adding a new "Sales Pipeline v2" page to the KOL Campaign Manager app. The goal is to implement the Sales Playbook CRM integration WITHOUT breaking the existing pipeline page.

## Current System Summary

**Existing Pipeline:** `/app/crm/pipeline/page.tsx` (3,082 lines)
- 4 tabs: Outreach, Leads, Deals, Accounts
- Stages: new → contacted → qualified → unqualified → nurture → dead → deal_qualified → proposal → negotiation → contract → closed_won → closed_lost → account_active → account_at_risk → account_churned
- Kanban + Table views with drag-and-drop (DndKit)
- Stage history tracking, contact linking, TG integration
- Service: `lib/crmService.ts` with CRMOpportunity interface (21 fields)

**Existing DB Table: `crm_opportunities`** - 21 columns: id, name, stage, account_type, deal_value, currency, last_contacted_at, last_message_at, last_reply_at, last_team_message_at, owner_id, source, referrer, gc, affiliate_id, client_id, scope, notes, position, created_at, updated_at, qualified_at, closed_at

**App Pattern:** 
- Layout: `ProtectedRoute` + `Sidebar` wrapper
- Pages: `'use client'`, useAuth(), useToast(), service classes
- UI: shadcn/ui + Tailwind + Lucide icons
- Nav: Sidebar.tsx has CRM section with Pipeline, Network, Contacts, TG Chats
- Supabase for all DB operations

## What Needs to Be Built (from sales-playbook-crm-integration.html)

**New Pipeline Stages:** Cold DM (5 bumps) → Warm (Interested/Silent) → TG (Path B only) → Booked → Discovery Done → Proposal Sent → Proposal Call → Contract → Closed Won
- Exit lanes: Orbit (5 reasons) and Closed Lost (with reason)
- Nurture side-track for Bucket B

**New Fields on crm_opportunities (~25 fields):**
Phase 1a: bucket (A/B/C), temperature_score (0-100), dm_account (closer/sdr/other), bump_number (0-4), last_bump_date, warm_sub_state (interested/silent), tg_handle, calendly_sent_via (dm/tg/not_yet), calendly_sent_date, calendly_booked_date, gc_opened (na/not_yet/opened), orbit_reason, closed_lost_reason, dedup_key, next_meeting_at, next_meeting_type, bucket_changed_at, discovery_call_at, proposal_sent_at

**New Tables:**
- crm_activities (activity timeline + BAMFAM)
- crm_sequences + crm_sequence_steps (automation engine)

**Key Features for new page:**
- Two-path routing (Path A: Closer's account, Path B: SDR's account)
- Bucket system (A/B/C) with temperature scoring
- Activity log per opportunity
- BAMFAM enforcement (next_meeting tracking with warning badges)
- Stage-linked templates

## Design Constraints
1. Must NOT break existing pipeline page - both should work simultaneously
2. Same crm_opportunities table (add new columns, don't change existing ones)
3. Follow existing app patterns (layout, sidebar, shadcn/ui)
4. New page route: suggest `/crm/sales-pipeline` or similar

## Plan Requirements
Design a phased implementation plan. Phase 1a should be the first deliverable - focused on:
1. Database migration (new columns + new tables)
2. New service file or extended service for v2 pipeline types
3. New page with Kanban view showing new stages
4. Opportunity cards showing bucket, dm_account, bump info
5. Create/edit opportunity with new fields
6. Sidebar nav update

Keep it practical - what's the minimum viable implementation that shows the new pipeline working with new stages, two-path routing, and bucket system? Don't try to build everything at once.

     9→  | 'new' | 'contacted' | 'qualified' | 'unqualified' | 'nurture' | 'dead'  // Lead stages
    10→  | 'deal_qualified' | 'proposal' | 'negotiation' | 'contract' | 'closed_won' | 'closed_lost'  // Deal stages

    15→
    16→export type AffiliateStatus = 'new' | 'active' | 'inactive';
    17→export type PartnerStatus = 'active' | 'inactive';
    18→export type PartnerCategory = 'service_provider' | 'investor_vc' | 'project' | 'individual';

    66→  updated_at: string;

    68→  closed_at: string | null;

```text
     1→'use client';
     2→
     3→import React, { useState } from 'react';
     4→import Link from 'next/link';
     5→import { usePathname, useRouter } from 'next/navigation';
     6→import Image from 'next/image';
     7→import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button';
     8→import { Users, Megaphone, Crown, List, Building2, PanelLeftClose, PanelLeftOpen, Settings, LogOut, Shield, MessageSquare, Zap, User, FileText, ClipboardList, Sliders, DollarSign, TrendingUp, Handshake, UserPlus, Archive, Sparkles, Link2, ChevronLeft, ChevronRight, BookOpen, CheckCircle, Briefcase, ListTodo } from 'lucide-react';
     9→import { useAuth } from '@/contexts/AuthContext';
    10→import { useChangelog } from '@/contexts/ChangelogContext';
    11→import { DropdownMenu, DropdownMenuTrigger, DropdownMenuContent, DropdownMenuItem, DropdownMenuSeparator } from '@/components/ui/dropdown-menu';
    12→import { Avatar, AvatarImage, AvatarFallback } from '@/components/ui/avatar';
    13→import { Dialog, DialogContent, DialogHeader, DialogTitle, DialogDescription } from '@/components/ui/dialog';
    14→import { Badge } from '@/components/ui/badge';
    15→import { ScrollArea } from '@/components/ui/scroll-area';
    16→
    17→interface SidebarProps {
    18→  children: React.ReactNode;
    19→}
    20→
    21→export default function Sidebar({ children }: SidebarProps) {
    22→  const pathname = usePathname();
    23→  const router = useRouter();
    24→  const { userProfile, signOut } = useAuth();
    25→  const [isSidebarCollapsed, setIsSidebarCollapsed] = React.useState(() => {
    26→    if (typeof window !== 'undefined') {
    27→      const stored = localStorage.getItem('sidebarCollapsed');
    28→      return stored === 'true';
    29→    }
    30→    return false;
    31→  });
    32→  const [isChangelogOpen, setIsChangelogOpen] = useState(false);
    33→  const [changelogPage, setChangelogPage] = useState(0);
    34→  const changelogsPerPage = 3;
    35→
    36→  // Get changelog data from context (fetched once at app level)
    37→  const { changelogs, latestVersion } = useChangelog();
    38→
    39→  // Format date for changelog display
    40→  const formatChangelogDate = (dateString: string | null) => {
    41→    if (!dateString) return '';
    42→    return new Date(dateString).toLocaleDateString('en-US', {
    43→      year: 'numeric',
    44→      month: 'long',
    45→      day: 'numeric'
    46→    });
    47→  };
    48→
    49→  // Render changelog content
    50→  const renderChangelogContent = (content: string) => {
    51→    return content.split('\n').map((line, index) => {
    52→      if (line.trim().startsWith('- ') || line.trim().startsWith('* ')) {
    53→        return (
    54→          <li key={index} className="ml-4 text-gray-700 text-sm">
    55→            {line.trim().substring(2)}
    56→          </li>
    57→        );
    58→      }
    59→      if (line.trim().startsWith('### ')) {
    60→        return (
    61→          <h4 key={index} className="font-semibold text-gray-900 mt-3 mb-1 text-sm">
    62→            {line.trim().substring(4)}
    63→          </h4>
    64→        );
    65→      }
    66→      if (line.trim().startsWith('## ')) {
    67→        return (
    68→          <h3 key={index} className="font-bold text-gray-900 mt-4 mb-2 text-sm">
    69→            {line.trim().substring(3)}
    70→          </h3>
    71→        );
    72→      }
    73→      if (line.trim() === '') {
    74→        return <div key={index} className="h-2" />;
    75→      }
    76→      return (
    77→        <p key={index} className="text-gray-700 text-sm">
    78→          {line}
    79→        </p>
    80→      );
```
</details></tool-use>
