```text
   195→
   196→// ============================================
   197→// CRM Service Class
   198→// ============================================
   199→
   200→export class CRMService {
   201→  // ----------------------------------------
   202→  // AFFILIATES
   203→  // ----------------------------------------
   204→
   205→  static async getAllAffiliates(): Promise<CRMAffiliate[]> {
   206→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   207→      .from('crm_affiliates')
   208→      .select('*')
   209→      .order('created_at', { ascending: false });
   210→
   211→    if (error) throw error;
   212→    return data || [];
   213→  }
   214→
   215→  static async getAffiliateById(id: string): Promise<CRMAffiliate | null> {
   216→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   217→      .from('crm_affiliates')
   218→      .select('*')
   219→      .eq('id', id)
   220→      .single();
   221→
   222→    if (error) throw error;
   223→    return data;
   224→  }
   225→
   226→  static async createAffiliate(affiliateData: CreateAffiliateData): Promise<CRMAffiliate> {
   227→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   228→      .from('crm_affiliates')
   229→      .insert([affiliateData])
   230→      .select()
   231→      .single();
   232→
   233→    if (error) throw error;
   234→
   235→    // Record stage history
   236→    await this.recordStageHistory('affiliate', data.id, null, affiliateData.status || 'new');
   237→
   238→    return data;
   239→  }
   240→
   241→  static async updateAffiliate(id: string, updates: Partial<CreateAffiliateData>): Promise<CRMAffiliate> {
   242→    // Get current state for stage history
   243→    const current = await this.getAffiliateById(id);
   244→
   245→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   246→      .from('crm_affiliates')
   247→      .update({ ...updates, updated_at: new Date().toISOString() })
   248→      .eq('id', id)
   249→      .select()
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./lib/crmService.ts`

```text
   270→
   271→  // ----------------------------------------
   272→  // OPPORTUNITIES
   273→  // ----------------------------------------
   274→
   275→  static async getAllOpportunities(): Promise<CRMOpportunity[]> {
   276→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   277→      .from('crm_opportunities')
   278→      .select(`
   279→        *,
   280→        affiliate:crm_affiliates(*),
   281→        client:clients!crm_opportunities_client_id_fkey(id, name)
   282→      `)
   283→      .order('position', { ascending: true })
   284→      .order('created_at', { ascending: false });
   285→
   286→    if (error) {
   287→      console.error('Error fetching opportunities:', error);
   288→      throw error;
   289→    }
   290→    return data || [];
   291→  }
   292→
   293→  static async getOpportunitiesByStage(stages: OpportunityStage[]): Promise<CRMOpportunity[]> {
   294→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   295→      .from('crm_opportunities')
   296→      .select(`
   297→        *,
   298→        affiliate:crm_affiliates(*),
   299→        client:clients!crm_opportunities_client_id_fkey(id, name)
   300→      `)
   301→      .in('stage', stages)
   302→      .order('created_at', { ascending: false });
   303→
   304→    if (error) throw error;
   305→    return data || [];
   306→  }
   307→
   308→  static async getOpportunityById(id: string): Promise<CRMOpportunity | null> {
   309→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   310→      .from('crm_opportunities')
   311→      .select(`
   312→        *,
   313→        affiliate:crm_affiliates(*),
   314→        client:clients!crm_opportunities_client_id_fkey(id, name)
   315→      `)
   316→      .eq('id', id)
   317→      .single();
   318→
   319→    if (error) throw error;
   320→    return data;
   321→  }
   322→
   323→  static async createOpportunity(opportunityData: CreateOpportunityData): Promise<CRMOpportunity> {
   324→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   325→      .from('crm_opportunities')
   326→      .insert([opportunityData])
   327→      .select()
   328→      .single();
   329→
   330→    if (error) throw error;
   331→
   332→    // Record stage history
   333→    await this.recordStageHistory('opportunity', data.id, null, opportunityData.stage || 'new');
   334→
   335→    return data;
   336→  }
   337→
   338→  static async updateOpportunity(id: string, updates: Partial<CreateOpportunityData>): Promise<CRMOpportunity> {
   339→    // Get current state for stage history
   340→    const current = await this.getOpportunityById(id);
   341→
   342→    const updateData: any = { ...updates, updated_at: new Date().toISOString() };
   343→
   344→    // Set qualified_at when moving to deal stages
   345→    if (updates.stage && ['proposal', 'contract', 'closed_won', 'closed_lost'].includes(updates.stage)) {
   346→      if (current && !current.qualified_at && ['new', 'contacted', 'qualified', 'unqualified', 'nurture'].includes(current.stage)) {
   347→        updateData.qualified_at = new Date().toISOString();
   348→      }
   349→    }
   350→
   351→    // Set closed_at when closing
   352→    if (updates.stage && ['closed_won', 'closed_lost'].includes(updates.stage)) {
   353→      if (current && !current.closed_at) {
   354→        updateData.closed_at = new Date().toISOString();
   355→      }
   356→    }
   357→
   358→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   359→      .from('crm_opportunities')
   360→      .update(updateData)
   361→      .eq('id', id)
   362→      .select()
   363→      .single();
   364→
   365→    if (error) throw error;
   366→
   367→    // Record stage change if stage changed
   368→    if (updates.stage && current && current.stage !== updates.stage) {
   369→      await this.recordStageHistory('opportunity', id, current.stage, updates.stage);
   370→    }
   371→
   372→    return data;
   373→  }
   374→
   375→  static async deleteOpportunity(id: string): Promise<void> {
   376→    const { error } = await supabase
   377→      .from('crm_opportunities')
   378→      .delete()
   379→      .eq('id', id);
   380→
   381→    if (error) throw error;
   382→  }
   383→
   384→  static async updateOpportunityPositions(positions: { id: string; position: number }[]): Promise<void> {
   385→    // Update each opportunity's position
   386→    const updates = positions.map(({ id, position }) =>
   387→      supabase
   388→        .from('crm_opportunities')
   389→        .update({ position, updated_at: new Date().toISOString() })
   390→        .eq('id', id)
   391→    );
   392→
   393→    const results = await Promise.all(updates);
   394→    const errors = results.filter(r => r.error);
   395→    if (errors.length > 0) {
   396→      console.error('Errors updating positions:', errors);
   397→      throw errors[0].error;
   398→    }
   399→  }
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
   243→    .card p {
   244→      font-size: 13px;
   245→      color: var(--gray-500);
   246→      margin: 0;
   247→    }
   248→
   249→    /* STATUS BADGES */
   250→    .badge {
   251→      display: inline-flex;
   252→      align-items: center;
   253→      gap: 6px;
   254→      padding: 3px 10px;
   255→      border-radius: 6px;
   256→      font-size: 12px;
   257→      font-weight: 600;
   258→      white-space: nowrap;
   259→    }
   260→    .badge-green { background: var(--green-light); color: var(--green); }
   261→    .badge-amber { background: var(--amber-light); color: var(--amber); }
   262→    .badge-red { background: var(--red-light); color: var(--red); }
   263→    .badge-blue { background: var(--blue-light); color: var(--blue); }
   264→    .badge-gray { background: var(--gray-100); color: var(--gray-500); }
   265→
   266→    /* TABLES */
   267→    table {
   268→      width: 100%;
   269→      border-collapse: collapse;
   270→      margin: 16px 0;
   271→      font-size: 14px;
   272→    }
   273→    th {
   274→      background: var(--gray-50);
   275→      padding: 10px 14px;
   276→      text-align: left;
   277→      font-weight: 600;
   278→      color: var(--gray-500);
   279→      font-size: 12px;
   280→      text-transform: uppercase;
   281→      letter-spacing: 0.5px;
   282→      border-bottom: 2px solid var(--gray-200);
   283→    }
   284→    td {
   285→      padding: 12px 14px;
   286→      border-bottom: 1px solid var(--gray-100);
   287→      vertical-align: top;
   288→    }
   289→    tr:hover td { background: var(--gray-50); }
   290→
   291→    /* CALLOUTS */
   292→    .callout {
   293→      border-radius: 10px;
   294→      padding: 20px 24px;
   295→      margin: 16px 0;
   296→      font-size: 14px;
   297→      border-left: 4px solid;
   298→    }
   299→    .callout-info {
   300→      background: var(--blue-light);
   301→      border-color: var(--blue);
   302→    }
   303→    .callout-success {
   304→      background: var(--green-light);
   305→      border-color: var(--green);
   306→    }
   307→    .callout-warning {
   308→      background: var(--amber-light);
   309→      border-color: var(--amber);
   310→    }
   311→    .callout-primary {
   312→      background: var(--primary-light);
   313→      border-color: var(--primary);
   314→    }
   315→    .callout strong { display: block; margin-bottom: 4px; }
   316→
   317→    /* FLOW DIAGRAMS */
   318→    .flow {
   319→      display: flex;
   320→      align-items: center;
   321→      gap: 8px 0;
   322→      margin: 20px 0;
   323→      flex-wrap: wrap;
   324→    }
   325→    .flow-step {
   326→      background: var(--white);
   327→      border: 2px solid var(--gray-200);
   328→      border-radius: 10px;
   329→      padding: 12px 18px;
   330→      font-size: 13px;
   331→      font-weight: 600;
   332→      color: var(--gray-700);
   333→      text-align: center;
   334→      min-width: 120px;
   335→    }
   336→    .flow-step.active {
   337→      background: var(--primary);
   338→      border-color: var(--primary);
   339→      color: white;
   340→    }
   341→    .flow-step.new {
   342→      background: var(--accent-light);
   343→      border-color: var(--accent);
   344→      color: var(--accent);
   345→    }
   346→    .flow-arrow {
   347→      font-size: 18px;
   348→      color: var(--gray-400);
   349→      padding: 0 6px;
   350→    }
   351→
   352→    /* COMPARISON */
   353→    .compare-grid {
   354→      display: grid;
   355→      grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr;
   356→      gap: 16px;
   357→      margin: 16px 0;
   358→    }
   359→    .compare-col {
   360→      border: 1px solid var(--gray-200);
   361→      border-radius: 10px;
   362→      overflow: hidden;
   363→    }
   364→    .compare-header {
   365→      padding: 14px 20px;
   366→      font-weight: 700;
   367→      font-size: 14px;
   368→    }
   369→    .compare-header.current {
   370→      background: var(--gray-100);
   371→      color: var(--gray-700);
   372→    }
   373→    .compare-header.proposed {
   374→      background: var(--primary);
   375→      color: white;
   376→    }
   377→    .compare-body {
   378→      padding: 16px 20px;
   379→    }
   380→    .compare-body li {
   381→      font-size: 13px;
   382→      margin-bottom: 6px;
   383→    }
   384→
   385→    /* PHASE TIMELINE */
   386→    .timeline {
   387→      position: relative;
   388→      margin: 24px 0;
   389→      padding-left: 32px;
   390→    }
   391→    .timeline::before {
   392→      content: '';
   393→      position: absolute;
   394→      left: 12px;
   395→      top: 0;
   396→      bottom: 0;
   397→      width: 2px;
   398→      background: var(--gray-200);
   399→    }
   400→    .timeline-item {
   401→      position: relative;
   402→      margin-bottom: 24px;
   403→    }
   404→    .timeline-dot {
   405→      position: absolute;
   406→      left: -26px;
   407→      top: 4px;
   408→      width: 14px;
   409→      height: 14px;
   410→      border-radius: 50%;
   411→      background: var(--primary);
   412→      border: 3px solid var(--white);
   413→      box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px var(--primary);
   414→    }
   415→    .timeline-label {
   416→      font-size: 12px;
   417→      font-weight: 700;
   418→      text-transform: uppercase;
   419→      letter-spacing: 0.5px;
   420→      color: var(--primary);
   421→      margin-bottom: 4px;
   422→    }
   423→    .timeline-title {
   424→      font-size: 16px;
   425→      font-weight: 700;
   426→      color: var(--gray-900);
   427→      margin-bottom: 4px;
   428→    }
   429→    .timeline-desc {
   430→      font-size: 13px;
   431→      color: var(--gray-500);
   432→    }
   433→
   434→    /* FEATURE LIST */
   435→    .feature-list {
   436→      list-style: none;
   437→      margin: 12px 0;
   438→      padding: 0;
   439→    }
   440→    .feature-list li {
   441→      display: flex;
   442→      align-items: flex-start;
   443→      gap: 10px;
   444→      padding: 8px 0;
   445→      border-bottom: 1px solid var(--gray-100);
   446→      font-size: 14px;
   447→    }
   448→    .feature-list li:last-child { border-bottom: none; }
   449→    .feature-check {
   450→      color: var(--green);
   451→      font-weight: 700;
   452→      flex-shrink: 0;
   453→      margin-top: 1px;
   454→    }
   455→    .feature-new {
   456→      color: var(--accent);
   457→      font-weight: 700;
   458→      flex-shrink: 0;
   459→      margin-top: 1px;
   460→    }
   461→
   462→    /* MOCKUP FRAMES */
   463→    .mockup {
   464→      border: 1px solid var(--gray-200);
   465→      border-radius: 10px;
   466→      overflow: hidden;
   467→      margin: 16px 0;
   468→    }
   469→    .mockup-bar {
   470→      background: var(--gray-100);
   471→      padding: 8px 14px;
   472→      display: flex;
   473→      align-items: center;
   474→      gap: 6px;
   475→      border-bottom: 1px solid var(--gray-200);
   476→    }
   477→    .mockup-dot {
   478→      width: 10px;
   479→      height: 10px;
   480→      border-radius: 50%;
   481→      background: var(--gray-300);
   482→    }
   483→    .mockup-body {
   484→      padding: 20px;
   485→      background: var(--white);
   486→      font-size: 13px;
   487→    }
   488→
   489→    /* KPI CARDS */
   490→    .kpi-grid {
   491→      display: grid;
   492→      grid-template-columns: repeat(4, 1fr);
   493→      gap: 12px;
   494→      margin: 16px 0;
   495→    }
   496→    .kpi {
   497→      text-align: center;
   498→      padding: 20px 12px;
   499→      border: 1px solid var(--gray-200);
```

```text
606:    The SDR (Phil) now DMs from the Closer's (Jdot's) account in addition to his own. This creates <strong>two booking paths</strong> &mdash; Path A (Closer's account, Calendly in DM, no TG/GC needed) and Path B (SDR's account, requires TG handoff + GC creation). This two-path model is the routing key for the entire pipeline and affects stages, sequences, and templates.
611:    The playbook says "Phil sends DMs, books calls, Jdot runs discovery, follows BAMFAM." The CRM should enforce this rhythm automatically &mdash; surfacing the right action, for the right lead, at the right time &mdash; so the team executes instead of remembering.
638:        <td><strong>Lead Buckets (A/B/C)</strong></td>
641:        <td>No bucket classification or temperature scoring</td>
650:        <td><strong>BAMFAM (Next Meeting)</strong></td>
734:        <td><strong>Calendly Integration</strong></td>
737:        <td>No Calendly tracking, webhook, or sent-vs-booked visibility</td>
746:        <td><strong>Orbit / Exit System</strong></td>
749:        <td>No orbit reasons, resurrection cadences, or exit tracking</td>
762:    Data infrastructure is at ~70% (templates, contacts, TG bot, stage history exist). But the playbook-driven <em>workflow</em> &mdash; two-path routing, bump sequences, Calendly tracking, Orbit system &mdash; is new work. Phase 1 is larger than initially scoped. We've split it into Phase 1a and 1b to manage complexity.
780:        <div class="card-title">1. Lead Scoring &amp; Bucket System</div>
782:      <p>Classify every opportunity as Bucket A/B/C with auto-scoring based on signals (budget mentioned, timeline given, decision-maker present). Drive all downstream automation from the bucket.</p>
790:      <p>Log calls, meetings, and messages tied to opportunities. Track BAMFAM compliance (did the call end with a booked next step?). Surface overdue follow-ups.</p>
798:      <p>Bucket-driven follow-up cadences. When a lead enters Bucket B, the 21-day nurture sequence auto-queues. When a proposal is sent, the Day 3/7/14 follow-ups queue automatically.</p>
806:      <p>Real-time metrics: Phil's DMs sent, response rate, meetings booked. Jdot's close rate, avg deal size, pipeline value. Weekly rhythm scorecards.</p>
822:      <p>Track competitor movements, exchange listings, funding rounds, and team hires. Auto-flag relevant Bucket B/C leads for re-engagement when triggers fire.</p>
830:      <p><span class="badge badge-red" style="margin-bottom: 6px;">CRITICAL</span> SDR DMs from Closer's account (Path A: Calendly in DM, no TG/GC) vs SDR's own account (Path B: get TG, send Calendly in TG, open GC). <code>dm_account</code> is the routing key for the whole pipeline.</p>
838:      <p><span class="badge badge-red" style="margin-bottom: 6px;">CRITICAL</span> The highest-volume automation: 5 bumps over ~19 days (Opener &rarr; Value &rarr; Credibility &rarr; Direct Ask &rarr; Breakup). Bump 4 + 7 days silence auto-moves to Orbit. Currently zero pre-discovery tracking.</p>
844:        <div class="card-title">9. Calendly Integration</div>
846:      <p><span class="badge badge-red" style="margin-bottom: 6px;">CRITICAL</span> Calendly is the booking mechanism. Track where it was sent (DM vs TG), when it was sent, and when the lead booked. Webhook (<code>invitee.created</code>) auto-moves lead to Booked stage. Dashboard: "Calendly sent but not booked = follow up."</p>
861:  <!-- 4A: BUCKET SYSTEM -->
862:  <h3>4A. Lead Bucket System</h3>
863:  <p>Every opportunity gets a <strong>bucket</strong> field and a <strong>temperature score</strong> (0-100). The bucket determines the playbook track.</p>
878:      <div class="compare-header proposed">Proposed: Stages + Buckets</div>
881:          <li>Same stages, PLUS a <strong>bucket</strong> field (A/B/C) on every opportunity</li>
883:          <li>Bucket drives which <strong>sequence</strong> fires and which <strong>templates</strong> surface</li>
884:          <li>Bucket can change as new signals arrive</li>
907:            <div style="font-size: 11px; color: var(--green); margin-top: 4px;">&#10003; BAMFAM: Proposal call Thu 2pm</div>
911:              <span style="font-weight: 600; font-size: 13px;">Orbit Finance</span>
954:          <li>Cold DM &rarr; Warm &rarr; <strong>Calendly sent in DM</strong> &rarr; Booked</li>
966:          <li>Cold DM &rarr; Warm &rarr; <strong>Get TG</strong> &rarr; Calendly in TG &rarr; Booked &rarr; <strong>Open GC</strong> (add Closer)</li>
967:          <li>TG handoff required before Calendly</li>
978:    A <code>dm_account</code> field on every opportunity is the routing key. Pipeline logic branches on this value: Path A skips the TG and GC stages entirely. Path B requires them. Templates also branch &mdash; Path A sends Calendly link directly in DM, Path B asks "drop your TG" first.
983:  <p>Every opportunity gets an activity timeline. Calls, messages, proposals, and meetings are logged with metadata. BAMFAM compliance is tracked automatically.</p>
986:    <li><span class="feature-new">NEW</span> <strong>Activity log per opportunity:</strong> Call happened, message sent, proposal delivered, meeting booked &mdash; each with timestamp, owner, and notes</li>
987:    <li><span class="feature-new">NEW</span> <strong>BAMFAM tracker:</strong> Every call/meeting must have a "Next Step" field. If empty, the card shows a warning badge.</li>
1001:        <th>Bucket</th>
1016:        <td>Send Calendly link in DM</td>
1022:        <td>"Drop your TG" &rarr; then send Calendly in TG</td>
1026:        <td>Booked</td>
1034:        <td>Bucket A: same-day follow-up + case studies</td>
1040:        <td>Bucket B: same-day follow-up + data walkthrough offer</td>
1046:        <td>Bucket C: soft close, keep in loop</td>
1102:    Templates already exist in the <code>message_templates</code> table. We add <code>pipeline_stage</code>, <code>bucket</code>, <code>dm_account</code> (for path-specific templates), and <code>sequence_day</code> fields. When a user opens an opportunity, the relevant templates auto-appear based on current stage, bucket, and path. Warm-stage templates branch on <code>dm_account</code>: Path A surfaces "send Calendly in DM," Path B surfaces "drop your TG."
1143:    <div class="flow-step new">Booked</div>
1158:    Exit lanes at every stage: <strong>Orbit</strong> (with reason) or <strong>Closed Lost</strong> (with reason)
1161:  <h4 style="margin-top: 24px;">Nurture side-track (Bucket B after Discovery)</h4>
1167:    <div class="flow-step" style="background: var(--gray-100); border-color: var(--gray-400); color: var(--gray-500);">Orbit<br><small>Monthly touch</small></div>
1192:        <td>Path B requires TG handoff before Calendly. Own nudge cadence: Day 2, 5, 10. Day 14 no booking &rarr; Orbit.</td>
1196:        <td>Add <strong>Booked</strong> stage (via Calendly)</td>
1197:        <td>Calendly link sent (DM or TG) &rarr; Lead books via Calendly &rarr; auto-move to Booked. Tracks Calendly sent-vs-booked gap.</td>
1198:        <td>Audit #4: Calendly tracking</td>
1201:        <td>Add <strong>Orbit</strong> exit lane (with reason)</td>
1203:        <td>Audit #5: Full Orbit system</td>
1220:    Existing opportunities in old stages will be auto-mapped to the nearest new stage. No data loss. Stage history will preserve the original stage names. <code>dm_account</code> defaults to "sdr" for existing leads (can be bulk-updated).
1255:      <tr><td>&mdash;</td><td>Day 26</td><td>No response after Bump 4 &rarr; auto-move to <strong>Orbit</strong> (reason: no_response)</td><td><span class="badge badge-green">Yes</span></td></tr>
1258:  <p style="font-size: 13px; color: var(--gray-500);"><strong>Fields needed:</strong> <code>bump_number</code> (0-4), <code>last_bump_date</code> on each opportunity. If lead replies at any bump &rarr; auto-move to <strong>Warm</strong> and stop the bump sequence.</p>
```

```text
  1392→      <tr><td>3</td><td>Content piece OR KOL signal</td><td><span class="badge badge-green">Yes</span></td></tr>
  1393→      <tr><td>4-5 (no response)</td><td>Breakup message. Pause nurture. Door stays open.</td><td><span class="badge badge-green">Yes</span></td></tr>
  1394→    </tbody>
  1395→  </table>
  1396→
  1397→  <h3>Sequence 5: Orbit (Bucket B/C &mdash; Long-Term)</h3>
  1398→  <table>
  1399→    <thead>
  1400→      <tr>
  1401→        <th>Orbit Reason</th>
  1402→        <th>Cadence</th>
  1403→        <th>Touch Type</th>
  1404→      </tr>
  1405→    </thead>
  1406→    <tbody>
  1407→      <tr><td>No Response</td><td>Every 60 days</td><td>Value touch (market data, competitor move)</td></tr>
  1408→      <tr><td>Not Now</td><td>Every 45 days</td><td>Value touch + soft re-engagement</td></tr>
  1409→      <tr><td>Ghost (booked but no-showed or went silent)</td><td>Every 60 days</td><td>Light touch, breakup after 3 attempts</td></tr>
  1410→      <tr><td>Has Team (doing it in-house)</td><td>Every 60 days</td><td>Case study results, "how's it going?"</td></tr>
  1411→      <tr><td>Hard No</td><td>None</td><td>No further outreach. Monitor for trigger events only.</td></tr>
  1412→    </tbody>
  1413→  </table>
  1414→  <p style="font-size: 13px; color: var(--gray-500);"><strong>Fields:</strong> <code>orbit_reason</code>, <code>orbit_cadence_days</code>, <code>orbit_touch_number</code>, <code>resurrect_count</code>. When a trigger event matches, lead can be resurrected back into the pipeline.</p>
  1415→
  1416→  <div class="callout callout-primary">
  1417→    <strong>How Sequences Work Technically</strong>
  1418→    A new <code>crm_sequences</code> table stores queued actions per opportunity. A cron job (Supabase Edge Function) runs daily, checks for due actions, and creates tasks in the Tasks system with the appropriate template pre-loaded. Phil/Jdot see these as action items on their dashboard.<br><br>
  1419→    <strong>7 sequence types:</strong> <code>cold_outreach</code> (5 bumps), <code>tg_booking</code> (Path B nudges), <code>fast_track</code>, <code>nurture_21</code>, <code>post_proposal</code>, <code>monthly</code>, <code>orbit</code>.
  1420→  </div>
  1421→</div>
  1422→
  1423→<!-- SECTION 7: SALES DASHBOARD -->
  1424→<div class="section" id="section-7">
  1425→  <div class="section-header">
  1426→    <div class="section-num">07</div>
  1427→    <div>
  1428→      <div class="section-title">Sales Dashboard</div>
  1429→      <div class="section-subtitle">The metrics from Section 15 of the playbook, live in the CRM</div>
  1430→    </div>
  1431→  </div>
  1432→
  1433→  <p>The playbook defines exact metrics for Phil, Jdot, and the overall business. Today these aren't tracked anywhere. The Sales Dashboard makes them real-time.</p>
  1434→

  1528→
  1529→  <div class="callout callout-info">
  1530→    <strong>Audit Fix: Split by Account</strong>
  1531→    "DMs Sent = 73" &mdash; from which account? The dashboard adds <code>dm_account</code> as a filter dimension so the team can compare Path A vs Path B performance separately. Each metric card can be toggled to show totals or per-account breakdown.
  1532→  </div>
  1533→
  1534→  <h3>No-Show &amp; Contract Alerts</h3>
  1535→  <table>
  1536→    <thead>
  1537→      <tr>
  1538→        <th>Alert</th>
  1539→        <th>Trigger</th>
  1540→        <th>Action</th>
  1541→      </tr>
  1542→    </thead>
  1543→    <tbody>
  1544→      <tr>
  1545→        <td><strong>No-Show</strong></td>
  1546→        <td>Booked lead doesn't show up for discovery call</td>
  1547→        <td>3-day reschedule window (auto-task). Day 3 no response &rarr; Orbit (reason: ghost). Field: <code>no_show</code> flag.</td>
  1548→      </tr>
  1549→      <tr>
  1550→        <td><strong>Contract Unsigned</strong></td>
  1551→        <td>Contract sent but not signed after 3 days</td>
  1552→        <td>Alert Closer + Founder. Auto-task to follow up. Fields: <code>contract_sent_date</code>, <code>contract_signed_date</code>.</td>
  1553→      </tr>
  1554→    </tbody>
  1555→  </table>
  1556→
  1557→  <h3>Weekly Rhythm Report (Auto-generated every Monday)</h3>
  1558→  <p>The playbook defines a weekly rhythm (Section 16). Every Monday morning, the CRM auto-generates a report sent to the team TG channel:</p>
  1559→  <ul>
  1560→    <li><strong>Last week:</strong> Deals closed, revenue, conversion rates, DMs sent (by account), meetings run</li>
  1561→    <li><strong>This week:</strong> Scheduled calls, proposals due, nurture touches queued, follow-ups overdue</li>
  1562→    <li><strong>Alerts:</strong> Stale leads (no contact in 7+ days), BAMFAM violations, no-shows pending reschedule, unsigned contracts, Bucket B leads ready for re-assessment</li>
  1563→  </ul>
  1564→</div>
  1565→
  1566→<!-- SECTION 8: AI INTEGRATION -->
  1567→<div class="section" id="section-8">
  1568→  <div class="section-header">
  1569→    <div class="section-num">08</div>
  1570→    <div>
  1571→      <div class="section-title">AI Integration</div>
  1572→      <div class="section-subtitle">Making Holo GPT the playbook's co-pilot</div>
  1573→    </div>
  1574→  </div>
  1575→
  1576→  <p>We already have Holo GPT with agent orchestration. Here's how we level it up with playbook knowledge:</p>
  1577→
  1578→  <div class="card-grid">
  1579→    <div class="card">
  1580→      <div class="card-header">
  1581→        <div class="card-icon" style="background: var(--accent-light); color: var(--accent);">&#129302;</div>
  1582→        <div class="card-title">Pre-Call Research Agent</div>
  1583→      </div>
  1584→      <p>Before a discovery call, Holo GPT auto-generates the pre-call prep doc: project basics (from CRM data), token data (from external APIs), competitor Korea presence, 2-3 suggested insight drops, and recommended bucket prediction.</p>
  1585→    </div>
  1586→
  1587→    <div class="card">
  1588→      <div class="card-header">
  1589→        <div class="card-icon" style="background: var(--green-light); color: var(--green);">&#128172;</div>
  1590→        <div class="card-title">Message Composer</div>
  1591→      </div>
  1592→      <p>Given the opportunity context and playbook stage, AI drafts personalized messages using the playbook templates as a base &mdash; filling in project name, competitor intel, market data. Phil reviews and sends.</p>
  1593→    </div>
  1594→
  1595→    <div class="card">
  1596→      <div class="card-header">
  1597→        <div class="card-icon" style="background: var(--amber-light); color: var(--amber);">&#128200;</div>
  1598→        <div class="card-title">Bucket Scoring Engine</div>
  1599→      </div>
```

```text
     1→-- CRM Database Schema
     2→-- Core Tables: Opportunities (Leads+Deals), Partners, Affiliates, Contacts
     3→-- Supporting: Stage History for analytics
     4→
     5→-- ============================================
     6→-- 1. AFFILIATES TABLE (created first for FK reference)
     7→-- KOLs, referrers with rev-share or commission
     8→-- Lifecycle: New → Active → Inactive
     9→-- ============================================
    10→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS crm_affiliates (
    11→  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    12→
    13→  -- Basic Info
    14→  name TEXT NOT NULL,
    15→  affiliation TEXT, -- Their affiliation/company
    16→  category TEXT, -- Type of affiliate
    17→
    18→  -- Lifecycle
    19→  status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'new', -- 'new', 'active', 'inactive'
    20→
    21→  -- Commission
    22→  commission_model TEXT, -- Description of commission/rev-share structure
    23→  commission_rate DECIMAL, -- Percentage if applicable
    24→
    25→  -- Tracking
    26→  last_contacted_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
    27→
    28→  -- Ownership
    29→  owner_id UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id),
    30→
    31→  -- Point of Contact (inline)
    32→  poc_name TEXT,
    33→  poc_email TEXT,
    34→  poc_telegram TEXT,
    35→
    36→  -- Notes
    37→  notes TEXT,
    38→
    39→  -- Timestamps
    40→  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
    41→  updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
    42→);
    43→
    44→-- ============================================
    45→-- 2. OPPORTUNITIES TABLE (Unified Leads + Deals)
    46→-- Single pipeline: New → Contacted → Qualified → Proposal → Contract → Closed
    47→-- ============================================
    48→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS crm_opportunities (
    49→  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    50→
    51→  -- Basic Info
    52→  name TEXT NOT NULL,
    53→
    54→  -- Pipeline Stage (unified lead + deal stages)
    55→  stage TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'new',
    56→  -- Lead stages: 'new', 'contacted', 'qualified', 'unqualified', 'nurture'
    57→  -- Deal stages: 'proposal', 'contract', 'closed_won', 'closed_lost'
    58→
    59→  -- Classification
    60→  account_type TEXT, -- 'general', 'channel', 'campaign', 'lite', 'ad_hoc' (set when qualified)
    61→
    62→  -- Value (set when becomes a deal)
    63→  deal_value DECIMAL,
    64→  currency TEXT DEFAULT 'USD',
    65→
    66→  -- Tracking
    67→  last_contacted_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
    68→
    69→  -- Ownership & Source
    70→  owner_id UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id),
    71→  source TEXT, -- 'referral', 'inbound', 'event', 'cold_outreach'
    72→  referrer TEXT, -- Who referred them
    73→
    74→  -- Communication
    75→  gc TEXT, -- Group Chat link/info
    76→
    77→  -- Affiliate connection (for commission tracking)
    78→  affiliate_id UUID REFERENCES crm_affiliates(id),
    79→
    80→  -- Notes
    81→  notes TEXT,
    82→
    83→  -- Timestamps
    84→  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
    85→  updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
    86→  qualified_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE, -- When moved from lead to deal stage
    87→  closed_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE -- When won or lost
    88→);
    89→
    90→-- ============================================
    91→-- 3. PARTNERS TABLE
    92→-- BD relationships, cross-partnerships
    93→-- Lifecycle: Active / Inactive
    94→-- ============================================
    95→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS crm_partners (
    96→  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    97→
    98→  -- Basic Info
    99→  name TEXT NOT NULL,
   100→
   101→  -- Classification
   102→  category TEXT, -- 'service_provider', 'investor_vc', 'project', 'individual'
   103→  focus TEXT, -- Area of focus/expertise
   104→
   105→  -- Lifecycle
   106→  status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active', -- 'active', 'inactive'
   107→
   108→  -- Tracking
   109→  last_contacted_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
   110→
   111→  -- Ownership
   112→  owner_id UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id),
   113→
   114→  -- Point of Contact (inline)
   115→  poc_name TEXT,
   116→  poc_email TEXT,
   117→  poc_telegram TEXT,
   118→
   119→  -- Affiliate connection (if partner is also an affiliate)
   120→  is_affiliate BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
   121→  affiliate_id UUID REFERENCES crm_affiliates(id),
   122→
   123→  -- Notes
   124→  notes TEXT,
   125→
   126→  -- Timestamps
   127→  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
   128→  updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
   129→);
   130→
   131→-- ============================================
   132→-- 4. CONTACTS TABLE
   133→-- Individual people linked to opportunities/partners/affiliates
   134→-- ============================================
   135→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS crm_contacts (
   136→  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   137→
   138→  -- Basic Info
   139→  name TEXT NOT NULL,
   140→  email TEXT,
   141→  telegram_id TEXT,
   142→  x_id TEXT,
   143→  role TEXT, -- Their role/title
   144→  category TEXT, -- Contact category
   145→
   146→  -- Ownership
   147→  owner_id UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id),
   148→
   149→  -- Notes
   150→  notes TEXT,
   151→
   152→  -- Timestamps
   153→  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
   154→  updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
   155→);
   156→
   157→-- ============================================
   158→-- 5. CONTACT LINKS (Junction Table)
   159→-- Links contacts to opportunities/partners/affiliates
   160→-- ============================================
   161→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS crm_contact_links (
   162→  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   163→  contact_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES crm_contacts(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   164→
   165→  -- Linked object type and ID
   166→  linked_type TEXT NOT NULL, -- 'opportunity', 'partner', 'affiliate'
   167→  opportunity_id UUID REFERENCES crm_opportunities(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   168→  partner_id UUID REFERENCES crm_partners(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   169→  affiliate_id UUID REFERENCES crm_affiliates(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   170→
   171→  -- Role in this relationship
   172→  role TEXT,
   173→  is_primary BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
   174→
   175→  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
   176→
   177→  -- Ensure only one link type is set
   178→  CONSTRAINT check_single_link CHECK (
   179→    (CASE WHEN opportunity_id IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END +
   180→     CASE WHEN partner_id IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END +
   181→     CASE WHEN affiliate_id IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 1
   182→  )
   183→);
   184→
   185→-- ============================================
   186→-- 6. STAGE HISTORY (For Analytics)
   187→-- Tracks all stage changes for conversion/velocity analysis
   188→-- ============================================
   189→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS crm_stage_history (
   190→  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   191→
   192→  -- Object reference
   193→  object_type TEXT NOT NULL, -- 'opportunity', 'partner', 'affiliate'
   194→  object_id UUID NOT NULL,
   195→
   196→  -- Stage change
   197→  from_stage TEXT,
   198→  to_stage TEXT NOT NULL,
   199→
   200→  -- Who and when
   201→  changed_by UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id),
   202→  changed_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
   203→
   204→  notes TEXT
   205→);
   206→
   207→-- ============================================
   208→-- INDEXES
   209→-- ============================================
   210→
   211→-- Opportunities
   212→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_opportunities_stage ON crm_opportunities(stage);
   213→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_opportunities_owner ON crm_opportunities(owner_id);
   214→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_opportunities_source ON crm_opportunities(source);
   215→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_opportunities_affiliate ON crm_opportunities(affiliate_id);
   216→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_opportunities_created ON crm_opportunities(created_at);
   217→
   218→-- Partners
   219→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_partners_status ON crm_partners(status);
   220→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_partners_category ON crm_partners(category);
   221→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_partners_owner ON crm_partners(owner_id);
   222→
   223→-- Affiliates
   224→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_affiliates_status ON crm_affiliates(status);
   225→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_affiliates_category ON crm_affiliates(category);
   226→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_affiliates_owner ON crm_affiliates(owner_id);
   227→
   228→-- Contacts
   229→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_contacts_owner ON crm_contacts(owner_id);
   230→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_contacts_email ON crm_contacts(email);
   231→
   232→-- Contact links
   233→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_contact_links_contact ON crm_contact_links(contact_id);
   234→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_contact_links_opportunity ON crm_contact_links(opportunity_id);
   235→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_contact_links_partner ON crm_contact_links(partner_id);
   236→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_contact_links_affiliate ON crm_contact_links(affiliate_id);
   237→
   238→-- Stage history
   239→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_stage_history_object ON crm_stage_history(object_type, object_id);
   240→CREATE INDEX idx_crm_stage_history_changed ON crm_stage_history(changed_at);
   241→
   242→-- ============================================
   243→-- COMMENTS
   244→-- ============================================
   245→
   246→COMMENT ON TABLE crm_opportunities IS 'Unified sales pipeline: Leads (new/contacted/qualified) → Deals (proposal/contract/closed). Single entity journey.';
   247→COMMENT ON TABLE crm_partners IS 'BD relationships. Categories: service_provider, investor_vc, project, individual. Can also be affiliates.';
   248→COMMENT ON TABLE crm_affiliates IS 'KOLs and referrers with commission/rev-share. Lifecycle: new → active → inactive.';
   249→COMMENT ON TABLE crm_contacts IS 'Individual people who can be linked to multiple CRM objects.';
   250→COMMENT ON TABLE crm_contact_links IS 'Links contacts to opportunities, partners, or affiliates with role info.';
   251→COMMENT ON TABLE crm_stage_history IS 'Audit trail of all stage changes for analytics (conversion rates, velocity).';
   252→
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
  1600→      <p>After call notes are logged, AI analyzes the notes against playbook criteria (budget mentioned? timeline given? decision-maker present?) and suggests a bucket classification with reasoning.</p>

  1618→
  1619→    <div class="card">

  1623→      </div>

We add new tools: <code>getOpportunityContext</code>, <code>draftPlaybookMessage</code>, <code>scoreBucket</code>, <code>generateProposal</code>, <code>scanTriggers</code>. The chat interface (<code>/chat</code>) becomes the playbook co-pilot.

  1658→          <strong>Phase 1b:</strong> <code>orbit_cadence_days</code>, <code>orbit_touch_number</code>, <code>resurrect_count</code>, <code>gc_opened_date</code>, <code>no_show</code> (boolean), <code>contract_sent_date</code>, <code>contract_signed_date</code>, <code>kickoff_date</code>

  1725→<div class="section" id="section-10">
  1726→  <div class="section-header">
  1727→    <div class="section-num">10</div>
  1728→    <div>
  1729→      <div class="section-title">Implementation Roadmap</div>
  1730→      <div class="section-subtitle">Four phases (1a, 1b, 2, 3), incremental value at each step</div>
  1731→    </div>
  1732→  </div>
  1733→
  1734→  <div class="timeline">
  1735→    <div class="timeline-item">
  1736→      <div class="timeline-dot"></div>
  1737→      <div class="timeline-label">Phase 1a &mdash; Correct Pipeline + Core Fields</div>
  1738→      <div class="timeline-title">Two-Path Routing + Stages + Cold Sequence</div>
  1739→      <div class="timeline-desc">
  1740→        <ul style="margin-top: 8px; font-size: 13px; color: var(--gray-500);">
  1741→          <li>Add <code>dm_account</code>, <code>bump_number</code>, <code>last_bump_date</code>, <code>warm_sub_state</code>, <code>tg_handle</code>, <code>calendly_sent_via/date</code>, <code>gc_opened</code>, <code>dedup_key</code> to opportunities</li>
  1742→          <li>Implement correct pipeline stages: Cold DM &rarr; Warm (Interested/Silent) &rarr; TG (Path B) &rarr; Booked &rarr; Discovery Done &rarr; Proposal Sent/Call &rarr; Contract &rarr; Closed Won</li>
  1743→          <li>Add Orbit exit lane with <code>orbit_reason</code> and Closed Lost with <code>closed_lost_reason</code></li>
  1744→          <li>Add bucket field + temperature score to opportunities</li>
  1745→          <li>Build cold outreach 5-bump sequence (highest-volume automation)</li>
  1746→          <li>Build <code>crm_activities</code> table and activity timeline UI</li>
  1747→          <li>Add BAMFAM next_meeting tracking with warning badges</li>
  1748→          <li>Add decision-maker flag to contacts</li>
  1749→          <li>Dedup check on lead creation (project name + handle)</li>
  1750→        </ul>
  1751→        <p style="margin-top: 8px;"><strong>Value delivered:</strong> Pipeline accurately reflects the two-path reality. Cold outreach is tracked and automated. Duplicates are prevented. Every opportunity has a bucket, activity history, and BAMFAM compliance.</p>
  1752→      </div>
  1753→    </div>
  1754→
  1755→    <div class="timeline-item">
  1756→      <div class="timeline-dot" style="background: #6366F1; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px #6366F1;"></div>
  1757→      <div class="timeline-label" style="color: #6366F1;">Phase 1b &mdash; Calendly + Orbit + Templates</div>
  1758→      <div class="timeline-title">Complete the Foundation</div>
  1759→      <div class="timeline-desc">
  1760→        <ul style="margin-top: 8px; font-size: 13px; color: var(--gray-500);">
  1761→          <li>Calendly webhook integration (<code>invitee.created</code> &rarr; auto-move to Booked)</li>
  1762→          <li>TG booking nudge sequence (Path B: Day 2, 5, 10, 14 &rarr; Orbit)</li>
  1763→          <li>Full Orbit system: 5 reasons, two cadences (45d/60d), resurrection flow</li>
  1764→          <li>No-show tracking (<code>no_show</code> flag, 3-day reschedule window)</li>
  1765→          <li>Contract alerts (<code>contract_sent_date</code>, <code>contract_signed_date</code>, 3-day unsigned alert)</li>
  1766→          <li>Link message templates to stages + buckets + <code>dm_account</code> for path-specific surfacing</li>
  1767→          <li>Build pre-call prep checklist form</li>
  1768→          <li>Dashboard: split metrics by <code>dm_account</code> dimension</li>
  1769→        </ul>
  1770→        <p style="margin-top: 8px;"><strong>Value delivered:</strong> Calendly auto-updates pipeline. Orbit nurtures cold leads long-term. Templates surface at the right stage for the right path. No-shows and stale contracts are flagged automatically.</p>
  1771→      </div>
  1772→    </div>
  1773→
  1774→    <div class="timeline-item">
  1775→      <div class="timeline-dot" style="background: var(--accent); box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px var(--accent);"></div>

  1777→      <div class="timeline-title">Post-Discovery Sequences + Dashboard + Weekly Reports</div>
  1778→      <div class="timeline-desc">

  1781→          <li>Implement remaining sequences: Fast Track, 21-Day Nurture, Post-Proposal Silence, Monthly Nurture, Orbit touches</li>
  1782→          <li>Build Sales Dashboard page with Phil/Jdot metrics, pipeline summary, and Path A vs B comparison</li>
  1783→          <li>Build <code>sales_metrics_daily</code> table with auto-snapshot Edge Function</li>
  1784→          <li>Auto-generate weekly Monday report to TG channel (including account breakdown)</li>
  1785→          <li>Build proposal tracker (<code>crm_proposals</code> table + UI)</li>
  1786→        </ul>
  1787→        <p style="margin-top: 8px;"><strong>Value delivered:</strong> Follow-ups happen automatically. No lead falls through the cracks. Metrics are real-time with Path A/B comparison. Monday standups have data.</p>
  1788→      </div>
  1789→    </div>
  1790→
  1791→    <div class="timeline-item">
  1792→      <div class="timeline-dot" style="background: var(--amber); box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px var(--amber);"></div>
  1793→      <div class="timeline-label" style="color: var(--amber);">Phase 3 &mdash; Intelligence</div>
  1794→      <div class="timeline-title">AI Co-pilot + Trigger Monitoring + Proposal Generator</div>
  1795→      <div class="timeline-desc">
  1796→        <ul style="margin-top: 8px; font-size: 13px; color: var(--gray-500);">
  1797→          <li>Add playbook knowledge to Holo GPT (new agent tools)</li>
  1798→          <li>Auto-generate pre-call research docs via AI</li>
  1799→          <li>AI-assisted bucket scoring from call notes</li>
  1800→          <li>Build trigger event scanner (RSS/API monitoring)</li>
  1801→          <li>Auto-match triggers to Bucket B/C leads and create re-engagement tasks</li>
  1802→          <li>AI proposal generator from opportunity context</li>
  1803→          <li>Objection handling coach (real-time script surfacing)</li>
  1804→        </ul>
  1805→        <p style="margin-top: 8px;"><strong>Value delivered:</strong> The CRM doesn't just track &mdash; it thinks. Prep time drops 80%. Proposals are drafted in minutes. No market signal is missed.</p>
  1806→      </div>
  1807→    </div>
  1808→  </div>
  1809→
  1810→  <h3>Summary: Before &amp; After</h3>
  1811→  <div class="compare-grid">
  1812→    <div class="compare-col">
  1813→      <div class="compare-header current">Before (Playbook as Document)</div>
  1814→      <div class="compare-body">
  1815→        <ul>
  1816→          <li>Team reads 17-section PDF and tries to remember</li>
  1817→          <li>Follow-ups tracked in heads or scattered notes</li>
  1818→          <li>No visibility into DM volume, response rates, or meeting counts</li>
  1819→          <li>Proposals built from scratch each time</li>
  1820→          <li>Bucket classification is a mental exercise</li>
  1821→          <li>Trigger events noticed by chance</li>
  1822→          <li>BAMFAM is aspirational, not enforced</li>
  1823→          <li>Weekly rhythm depends on discipline</li>
  1824→          <li>No way to measure playbook compliance</li>
  1825→        </ul>
  1826→      </div>
  1827→    </div>
  1828→    <div class="compare-col">
  1829→      <div class="compare-header proposed">After (Playbook in CRM)</div>
  1830→      <div class="compare-body">
  1831→        <ul>
  1832→          <li>Two-path routing (Path A / Path B) baked into every stage</li>
  1833→          <li>5-bump cold outreach runs automatically per lead</li>
```

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-6 2026-02-23 12:39:48Z)**_

I now have a thorough understanding of the codebase. Let me compile the implementation plan.

---

# Sales Pipeline v2 - Phase 1a Implementation Plan

## Overview

This plan adds a new "Sales Pipeline v2" page at `/crm/sales-pipeline` that implements the Sales Playbook CRM integration. The existing `/crm/pipeline` page remains untouched. Both pages share the same `crm_opportunities` table -- v2 adds new columns but never removes or renames existing ones.

---

## Architecture Decisions

**Route:** `/app/crm/sales-pipeline/page.tsx` -- separate Next.js page, not modifying the existing pipeline.

**Service layer:** Create a new file `lib/salesPipelineService.ts` rather than modifying `lib/crmService.ts`. The new service imports `supabase` from `lib/supabase.ts` directly and defines its own types, queries, and business logic for v2 stages. The existing `CRMService` class continues to work unchanged because the new columns are all nullable with defaults.

**Shared table, separate views:** Both pipelines query `crm_opportunities`. The v2 page filters to its own stages (`cold_dm`, `warm`, `tg_intro`, `booked`, `discovery_done`, `proposal_sent`, `proposal_call`, `v2_contract`, `v2_closed_won`). Old opportunities in old stages simply do not appear on the v2 page (and vice versa). The v2 page also shows `orbit` and `v2_closed_lost` as exit lanes.

**Stage naming convention:** New stages are prefixed or namespaced to avoid collision with existing stages. For example, use `v2_contract` and `v2_closed_won` / `v2_closed_lost` instead of reusing `contract`, `closed_won`, `closed_lost`. This prevents any filtering ambiguity. The `orbit` stage is entirely new so no prefix needed.

---

## Step 1: Database Migration (New Columns on `crm_opportunities`)

Create migration file: `sql/migrations/026_sales_pipeline_v2_columns.sql`

Add these nullable columns to `crm_opportunities`:

```sql
-- Sales Pipeline v2 fields (Phase 1a)
ALTER TABLE crm_opportunities
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS bucket TEXT,                          -- 'A', 'B', 'C'
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS temperature_score INTEGER DEFAULT 50, -- 0-100
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS dm_account TEXT DEFAULT 'sdr',        -- 'closer', 'sdr', 'other'
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS bump_number INTEGER DEFAULT 0,        -- 0-4
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS last_bump_date TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS warm_sub_state TEXT,                  -- 'interested', 'silent'
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS tg_handle TEXT,
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS calendly_sent_via TEXT,               -- 'dm', 'tg', 'not_yet'
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS calendly_sent_date TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS calendly_booked_date TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS gc_opened TEXT DEFAULT 'na',          -- 'na', 'not_yet', 'opened'
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS orbit_reason TEXT,                    -- 'no_response','not_now','ghost','has_team','hard_no'
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS closed_lost_reason TEXT,
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS dedup_key TEXT,                       -- lowercase(name + tg_handle or x_id)
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS next_meeting_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS next_meeting_type TEXT,               -- 'discovery','proposal_call','contract_review'
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS bucket_changed_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS discovery_call_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS proposal_sent_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE;

-- Indexes for v2 queries
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_crm_opportunities_bucket ON crm_opportunities(bucket);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_crm_opportunities_dm_account ON crm_opportunities(dm_account);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_crm_opportunities_dedup_key ON crm_opportunities(dedup_key);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_crm_opportunities_next_meeting ON crm_opportunities(next_meeting_at);
```

All new columns are nullable or have defaults, so existing data and the existing pipeline page are unaffected.

---

## Step 2: Database Migration (New `crm_activities` Table)

Create migration file: `sql/migrations/027_create_crm_activities.sql`

```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS crm_activities (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  opportunity_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES crm_opportunities(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  type TEXT NOT NULL,             -- 'call', 'message', 'meeting', 'proposal', 'note', 'bump'
  title TEXT NOT NULL,
  description TEXT,
  outcome TEXT,                   -- what happened
  next_step TEXT,                 -- BAMFAM: what's the next action
  next_step_date TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,  -- BAMFAM: when
  owner_id UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id),
  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_crm_activities_opportunity ON crm_activities(opportunity_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_crm_activities_type ON crm_activities(type);
CREATE INDEX idx_crm_activities_created ON crm_activities(created_at);

-- RLS policies (matching existing crm_ table pattern from migration 023)
ALTER TABLE crm_activities ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

CREATE POLICY "Authenticated users can read activities"
  ON crm_activities FOR SELECT
  TO authenticated
  USING (true);

CREATE POLICY "Authenticated users can insert activities"
  ON crm_activities FOR INSERT
  TO authenticated
  WITH CHECK (true);

CREATE POLICY "Authenticated users can update activities"
  ON crm_activities FOR UPDATE
  TO authenticated
  USING (true);

CREATE POLICY "Authenticated users can delete activities"
  ON crm_activities FOR DELETE
  TO authenticated
  USING (true);
```

Note: `crm_sequences` and `crm_sequence_steps` tables are deferred to Phase 1b/2. For Phase 1a, bump tracking is done via the `bump_number` and `last_bump_date` fields on the opportunity itself, not via a full sequence engine.

---

## Step 3: New Service File -- `lib/salesPipelineService.ts`

This file defines:

### Types

```typescript
// New v2 stage type (separate from OpportunityStage to avoid breaking existing type)
export type SalesPipelineStage =
  | 'cold_dm' | 'warm' | 'tg_intro' | 'booked'
  | 'discovery_done' | 'proposal_sent' | 'proposal_call'
  | 'v2_contract' | 'v2_closed_won'
  | 'orbit' | 'v2_closed_lost' | 'nurture';

export type Bucket = 'A' | 'B' | 'C';
export type DmAccount = 'closer' | 'sdr' | 'other';
export type WarmSubState = 'interested' | 'silent';
export type CalendlySentVia = 'dm' | 'tg' | 'not_yet';
export type GcOpened = 'na' | 'not_yet' | 'opened';
export type OrbitReason = 'no_response' | 'not_now' | 'ghost' | 'has_team' | 'hard_no';

export interface SalesPipelineOpportunity extends CRMOpportunity {
  // v2 fields (all from the same crm_opportunities row)
  bucket: Bucket | null;
  temperature_score: number;
  dm_account: DmAccount;
  bump_number: number;
  last_bump_date: string | null;
  warm_sub_state: WarmSubState | null;
  tg_handle: string | null;
  calendly_sent_via: CalendlySentVia | null;
  calendly_sent_date: string | null;
  calendly_booked_date: string | null;
  gc_opened: GcOpened;
  orbit_reason: OrbitReason | null;
  closed_lost_reason: string | null;
  dedup_key: string | null;
  next_meeting_at: string | null;
  next_meeting_type: string | null;
  bucket_changed_at: string | null;
  discovery_call_at: string | null;
  proposal_sent_at: string | null;
}

export interface CRMActivity {
  id: string;
  opportunity_id: string;
  type: 'call' | 'message' | 'meeting' | 'proposal' | 'note' | 'bump';
  title: string;
  description: string | null;
  outcome: string | null;
  next_step: string | null;
  next_step_date: string | null;
  owner_id: string | null;
  created_at: string;
}

export interface CreateSalesPipelineOpportunityData {
  name: string;
  stage?: SalesPipelineStage;
  dm_account?: DmAccount;
  bucket?: Bucket;
  temperature_score?: number;
  source?: string;
  owner_id?: string;
  tg_handle?: string;
  notes?: string;
  deal_value?: number;
  referrer?: string;
  affiliate_id?: string;
}
```

### Key Methods

```typescript
export class SalesPipelineService {
  static readonly V2_STAGES: SalesPipelineStage[] = [
    'cold_dm', 'warm', 'tg_intro', 'booked', 'discovery_done',
    'proposal_sent', 'proposal_call', 'v2_contract', 'v2_closed_won',
    'orbit', 'v2_closed_lost', 'nurture'
  ];

  // Fetch only v2 pipeline opportunities
  static async getAll(): Promise<SalesPipelineOpportunity[]>
  // Uses .in('stage', V2_STAGES)

  // Create with dedup check
  static async create(data: CreateSalesPipelineOpportunityData): Promise<SalesPipelineOpportunity>
  // Generates dedup_key = lowercase(name + tg_handle)
  // Checks for existing dedup_key before insert
  // Records stage history via CRMService.recordStageHistory

  // Update with stage-transition logic
  static async update(id: string, updates: Partial<...>): Promise<SalesPipelineOpportunity>
  // Sets discovery_call_at when moving to discovery_done
  // Sets proposal_sent_at when moving to proposal_sent
  // Sets closed_at when moving to v2_closed_won or v2_closed_lost
  // Records stage history

  // Bump tracking
  static async recordBump(id: string): Promise<SalesPipelineOpportunity>
  // Increments bump_number, sets last_bump_date
  // If bump_number reaches 4 and no reply after 7 days, suggests orbit

  // Activities CRUD
  static async getActivities(opportunityId: string): Promise<CRMActivity[]>
  static async createActivity(data: Omit<CRMActivity, 'id' | 'created_at'>): Promise<CRMActivity>

  // BAMFAM check
  static async getBAMFAMViolations(): Promise<SalesPipelineOpportunity[]>
  // Returns opportunities past discovery_done with no next_meeting_at or overdue next_meeting_at

  // Path routing helper
  static getAvailableStages(dmAccount: DmAccount): SalesPipelineStage[]
  // Path A (closer): cold_dm -> warm -> booked -> discovery_done -> proposal_sent -> proposal_call -> v2_contract -> v2_closed_won
  // Path B (sdr):    cold_dm -> warm -> tg_intro -> booked -> discovery_done -> proposal_sent -> proposal_call -> v2_contract -> v2_closed_won
  // Both can exit to orbit or v2_closed_lost at any stage
}
```

The service imports `supabase` from `@/lib/supabase` and the existing `CRMService` class only for calling `recordStageHistory`. It does not modify `CRMService`.

---

## Step 4: New Page -- `app/crm/sales-pipeline/page.tsx`

### Layout File: `app/crm/sales-pipeline/layout.tsx`

Identical pattern to the existing pipeline layout:

```typescript
import { ProtectedRoute } from '@/components/auth/ProtectedRoute';
import Sidebar from '@/components/Sidebar';

export default function SalesPipelineLayout({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {
  return (
    <ProtectedRoute>
      <Sidebar>{children}</Sidebar>
    </ProtectedRoute>
  );
}
```

### Page Structure

The page follows the exact same pattern as `/app/crm/pipeline/page.tsx` (`'use client'`, `useAuth()`, service calls in `useEffect`, etc.) but with these differences:

**Tabs:** Two tabs instead of four:
1. **Pipeline** -- the main kanban/table view with the v2 stages
2. **Orbit** -- shows opportunities in `orbit` stage, grouped by `orbit_reason`

**Kanban Columns (Pipeline tab):**
- Path A view: `cold_dm` -> `warm` -> `booked` -> `discovery_done` -> `proposal_sent` -> `proposal_call` -> `v2_contract` -> `v2_closed_won`
- Path B view: `cold_dm` -> `warm` -> `tg_intro` -> `booked` -> `discovery_done` -> `proposal_sent` -> `proposal_call` -> `v2_contract` -> `v2_closed_won`
- Toggle or filter at the top: "All Paths" | "Path A (Closer)" | "Path B (SDR)"
- When "All Paths" is selected, show all columns (including `tg_intro`)

**Opportunity Card (in Kanban):** Each card shows:
- Name (bold)
- Bucket badge: colored A (green), B (amber), C (gray)
- `dm_account` indicator: small icon/badge showing "Closer" or "SDR"
- Bump progress: for `cold_dm` stage, show "Bump 2/4" with progress dots
- Temperature score: small colored bar (green >70, amber 40-70, red <40)
- BAMFAM warning: if `next_meeting_at` is null and stage is past `discovery_done`, show orange warning icon
- `warm_sub_state`: if in `warm` stage, show "Interested" or "Silent" sub-badge
- Last contacted relative time

**DnD:** Same `@dnd-kit/core` pattern as existing pipeline. Drag between columns to change stage. When dragging to `orbit`, prompt for `orbit_reason`. When dragging to `v2_closed_lost`, prompt for `closed_lost_reason`.

**Create/Edit Dialog:** New opportunity form includes:
- Name (text input)
- DM Account (select: Closer / SDR / Other) -- this is the routing key
- Bucket (select: A / B / C)
- Temperature Score (slider or number input, 0-100)
- Source (select: referral / inbound / event / cold_outreach)
- TG Handle (text input)
- Owner (user select, same pattern as existing)
- Referrer (text input)
- Affiliate (combobox, same pattern as existing)
- Deal Value + Currency
- Notes (textarea)

**Activity Panel:** When clicking an opportunity card, open a slide-over or dialog that shows:
- Opportunity details (editable)
- Activity timeline (list of `crm_activities` for this opp, newest first)
- "Add Activity" button that opens a form: type (call/message/meeting/proposal/note/bump), title, description, outcome, next_step, next_step_date
- BAMFAM indicator at top: shows next meeting date or warning if missing

**Estimated size:** The page will likely be 1500-2000 lines initially. Can be broken into components later but for Phase 1a, a single-file approach matches the existing pipeline pattern.

---

## Step 5: Sidebar Navigation Update

In `components/Sidebar.tsx`, add a new link in the CRM section, between "Pipeline" and "Network":

```tsx
{/* Sales Pipeline v2 tab */}
<Link href="/crm/sales-pipeline" legacyBehavior>
  <Button
    asChild
    variant={pathname.startsWith('/crm/sales-pipeline') ? 'default' : 'ghost'}
    className={`w-full ${isSidebarCollapsed ? 'justify-center px-0' : 'justify-start'} hover:opacity-90`}
    style={pathname.startsWith('/crm/sales-pipeline') ? { backgroundColor: '#3e8692', color: 'white' } : {}}
    title={isSidebarCollapsed ? 'Sales' : undefined}
  >
    <span>
      <Target className={`h-4 w-4 ${!isSidebarCollapsed ? 'mr-2' : ''}`} />
      {!isSidebarCollapsed && 'Sales'}
    </span>
  </Button>
</Link>
```

The `Target` icon from Lucide is already imported in Sidebar.tsx (visible in the imports). The label "Sales" is short and distinguishes from the existing "Pipeline" link.

**Important:** The `pathname.startsWith('/crm/sales-pipeline')` check must come before the existing `/crm/pipeline` check in the rendered JSX, OR the existing pipeline check must be updated to use an exact match. Currently the sidebar uses `pathname.startsWith('/crm/pipeline')` which would also match `/crm/pipeline-v2` but NOT `/crm/sales-pipeline` (different prefix). So the current routing is safe as-is because the new path `/crm/sales-pipeline` does not start with `/crm/pipeline`.

---

## Step 6: Update `OpportunityStage` Type (Additive Only)

In `lib/crmService.ts`, extend the `OpportunityStage` type union to include the new stages. This is additive and does not break existing code:

```typescript
export type OpportunityStage =
  | 'new' | 'contacted' | 'qualified' | 'unqualified' | 'nurture' | 'dead'
  | 'deal_qualified' | 'proposal' | 'negotiation' | 'contract' | 'closed_won' | 'closed_lost'
  | 'account_active' | 'account_at_risk' | 'account_churned'
  // Sales Pipeline v2 stages
  | 'cold_dm' | 'warm' | 'tg_intro' | 'booked' | 'discovery_done'
  | 'proposal_sent' | 'proposal_call' | 'v2_contract' | 'v2_closed_won'
  | 'orbit' | 'v2_closed_lost';
```

The existing pipeline page uses `leadStages`, `dealStages`, `accountStages` arrays to filter which stages to show. Since the new stages are not in those arrays, they will never appear on the old page. The `stageLabels` and `stageColors` records will get TypeScript errors because they don't include the new stages -- fix by adding entries for the new stages to those records. The existing page will never render those entries since it never encounters those stages in its filtered arrays.

Alternatively, the `stageLabels` and `stageColors` records can be typed with `Partial<Record<OpportunityStage, ...>>` or the new stage labels/colors can be defined only in the v2 page. The simplest approach: add entries to `stageLabels` and `stageColors` in the existing page file for the new stages. They are inert -- never displayed -- but satisfy TypeScript.

---

## Potential Challenges

1. **TypeScript strictness on Record types:** Adding new stages to `OpportunityStage` will cause compile errors in the existing pipeline page's `stageLabels` and `stageColors` records (which are typed `Record<OpportunityStage, ...>`). These need entries added for the new stages. Alternatively, change the record types to use `Partial<Record<...>>` with appropriate null checks.

2. **Dedup key uniqueness:** The `dedup_key` field should have a unique index, but only for non-null values. Use `CREATE UNIQUE INDEX ... WHERE dedup_key IS NOT NULL` in the migration.

3. **Stage transition validation:** The service should validate that stage transitions follow the two-path routing rules (e.g., Path A cannot go through `tg_intro`). This is business logic in `SalesPipelineService.update()`.

4. **Existing opportunities:** Old opportunities (in stages like `new`, `contacted`, etc.) will not appear on the v2 page. A future "migration assistant" could help move them, but for Phase 1a, new opportunities are created directly in v2 stages.

5. **File size:** The existing pipeline page is 3,082 lines. The v2 page should aim for better modularity from the start, potentially extracting the Kanban board, opportunity cards, and activity panel into separate component files under a `components/sales-pipeline/` directory. However, following the existing pattern of a single page file is also acceptable for Phase 1a.

---

## Implementation Sequence

1. Run database migration (Step 1 + Step 2) -- add columns and create `crm_activities` table
2. Create `lib/salesPipelineService.ts` (Step 3) -- types and service methods
3. Update `OpportunityStage` in `lib/crmService.ts` (Step 6) -- additive type change
4. Create `app/crm/sales-pipeline/layout.tsx` and `app/crm/sales-pipeline/page.tsx` (Step 4)
5. Update `components/Sidebar.tsx` (Step 5) -- add nav link
6. Test that existing pipeline page still works unchanged

---

## What Phase 1a Does NOT Include (Deferred)

- Calendly webhook integration (Phase 1b)
- `crm_sequences` / `crm_sequence_steps` tables and sequence engine (Phase 2)
- Auto-bump automation (Phase 2 -- bumps are manually recorded in 1a)
- Sales Dashboard metrics page (Phase 2)
- Message template linking to stages/buckets (Phase 1b)
- AI integration (Phase 3)
- Trigger event scanner (Phase 3)
- Weekly rhythm report (Phase 2)

---

### Critical Files for Implementation

- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/crmService.ts` - Must extend OpportunityStage type union with new v2 stages; contains CRMService.recordStageHistory that v2 service will reuse
- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/crm/pipeline/page.tsx` - Reference pattern for the new page (kanban, DnD, dialogs, inline editing); also needs stageLabels/stageColors entries for new stages to satisfy TypeScript
- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/components/Sidebar.tsx` - Add new "Sales" nav link in CRM section (line ~282-296 area)
- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/sql/migrations/022_create_crm_tables.sql` - Reference for existing table schema and migration patterns
- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/docs/sales-playbook-crm-integration.html` - Authoritative spec for stages, fields, two-path routing, bucket system, and BAMFAM rules (lines 1634-1721 for schema, lines 1734-1808 for phase roadmap)

```md
# Sales Pipeline v2 — Phase 1a Implementation Plan

## Context

The Sales Playbook v2.0 defines a proven sales process (cold DM → signed deal) that currently lives as a static document. The existing pipeline page at `/crm/pipeline` has generic stages (new → contacted → qualified → etc.) that don't match how the team actually works. We're building a **new page** at `/crm/sales-pipeline` that implements the playbook-driven pipeline — without touching the existing pipeline page.

---

## Step 1: Database Migrations (via Supabase MCP)

### Migration 1: Add v2 columns to `crm_opportunities`

All nullable with defaults — existing data unaffected:
- `bucket` (text: A/B/C), `temperature_score` (int, default 50)
- `dm_account` (text: closer/sdr/other, default 'sdr')
- `bump_number` (int, default 0), `last_bump_date` (timestamptz)
- `warm_sub_state` (text: interested/silent)
- `tg_handle`, `calendly_sent_via` (dm/tg/not_yet), `calendly_sent_date`, `calendly_booked_date`
- `gc_opened` (text, default 'na'), `orbit_reason`, `closed_lost_reason`, `dedup_key`
- `next_meeting_at`, `next_meeting_type`
- `bucket_changed_at`, `discovery_call_at`, `proposal_sent_at`
- Indexes on: bucket, dm_account, dedup_key (unique where not null), next_meeting_at

### Migration 2: Create `crm_activities` table

- id, opportunity_id (FK), type (call/message/meeting/proposal/note/bump), title, description, outcome, next_step, next_step_date, owner_id, created_at
- RLS: authenticated users full CRUD (matches existing crm_ table pattern)

---

## Step 2: New Service — `lib/salesPipelineService.ts`

**Types:**
- `SalesPipelineStage`: cold_dm | warm | tg_intro | booked | discovery_done | proposal_sent | proposal_call | v2_contract | v2_closed_won | orbit | v2_closed_lost | nurture
- `Bucket`, `DmAccount`, `WarmSubState`, `OrbitReason` etc. type aliases
- `SalesPipelineOpportunity` — extends CRMOpportunity with all v2 fields
- `CRMActivity` interface

**Methods:**
- `getAll()` — fetch opportunities filtered to v2 stages, join affiliate + client
- `create(data)` — dedup check (name+tg_handle), insert, record stage history
- `update(id, updates)` — auto-set discovery_call_at/proposal_sent_at/closed_at on stage transitions, record stage history
- `delete(id)`
- `recordBump(id)` — increment bump_number, set last_bump_date
- `getActivities(opportunityId)` / `createActivity(data)`
- `getBAMFAMViolations()` — past discovery_done with no/overdue next_meeting
- `getAvailableStages(dmAccount)` — valid stages for Path A vs Path B
- `getMetrics()` — pipeline counts, values, bucket distribution

Reuses `CRMService.recordStageHistory()` from `lib/crmService.ts`.

---

## Step 3: Extend `OpportunityStage` in `lib/crmService.ts`

Additive only — append new v2 stages to the union type. Update the existing pipeline page's `stageLabels`/`stageColors` records to include entries for new stages (they'll never render but satisfies TypeScript), or change to `Partial<Record<...>>`.

---

## Step 4: New Page — `app/crm/sales-pipeline/`

### `layout.tsx` — standard ProtectedRoute + Sidebar pattern

### `page.tsx` — Full Phase 1a

**Header:** Metrics bar — total pipeline count, Bucket A/B/C counts, active value, BAMFAM violations

**Tabs:**
1. **Pipeline** — Kanban + Table views
2. **Orbit** — Orbit opportunities grouped by reason

**Path Filter:** "All" | "Path A (Closer)" | "Path B (SDR)" — controls which columns show

**Kanban Columns:**
Cold DM → Warm → TG* (Path B only) → Booked → Discovery Done → Proposal Sent → Proposal Call → Contract → Closed Won
- DndKit drag-and-drop (same pattern as existing pipeline)
- Drag to orbit → prompt for orbit_reason select
- Drag to closed lost → prompt for closed_lost_reason

**Table View:**
- All columns sortable, inline editing for key fields
- Shows bucket, dm_account, bump progress, temperature, BAMFAM status

**Opportunity Card:**
- Name, bucket badge (A=green, B=amber, C=gray)
- DM account indicator (Closer/SDR)
- Bump progress "Bump 2/4" with dots (cold_dm stage only)
- Warm sub-state badge (warm stage only)
- Temperature score bar
- BAMFAM warning (past discovery_done, no next_meeting)
- Deal value, last contacted, linked contacts

**Create/Edit Dialog:**
- Name, DM Account, Bucket, Temperature Score (slider)
- Source, TG Handle, Owner, Referrer, Affiliate
- Deal Value + Currency, Notes

**Activity Slide-Over (click opportunity):**
- Opportunity details (editable)
- BAMFAM indicator at top
- Activity timeline (newest first)
- "Add Activity" form: type, title, description, outcome, next_step, next_step_date
- "Record Bump" quick button (cold_dm stage)

**Orbit Tab:**
- Grouped by orbit_reason (5 groups)
- Shows: name, bucket, time in orbit, last touch
- "Resurrect" action → move back to pipeline

---

## Step 5: Sidebar Update — `components/Sidebar.tsx`

Add "Sales" link in CRM section (between Pipeline and Network):
- Route: `/crm/sales-pipeline`
- Icon: `Target` from lucide-react
- No path collision with existing `/crm/pipeline`

---

## Files Summary

| File | Action |
|---|---|
| `lib/crmService.ts` | Extend OpportunityStage type (additive) |
| `lib/salesPipelineService.ts` | **NEW** — v2 types + service |
| `app/crm/sales-pipeline/layout.tsx` | **NEW** |
| `app/crm/sales-pipeline/page.tsx` | **NEW** — full pipeline page |
| `components/Sidebar.tsx` | Add "Sales" nav link |
| `app/crm/pipeline/page.tsx` | Minor: handle new stages in type records |

---

## NOT in Phase 1a (Deferred)

- Calendly webhook (1b), Sequence engine (Phase 2), Auto-bump cron (Phase 2)
- Sales Dashboard page (Phase 2), Template linking (1b), AI integration (Phase 3)
- Bumps are manual in Phase 1a (click "Record Bump" button)

---

## Verification

1. `/crm/pipeline` still works unchanged
2. `/crm/sales-pipeline` loads with Kanban columns
3. Create opportunity with dm_account, bucket, tg_handle — dedup works
4. Drag between stages, stage history recorded
5. Path filter hides TG column for Path A
6. Activity panel: add activities, BAMFAM warning shows
7. Orbit tab groups by reason, resurrect works
8. Table view with inline editing
9. `npm run build` passes

```
